verses.hebrew_full: מִ֭שְׁלֵי שְׁלֹמֹ֣ה בֶן־דָּוִ֑ד מֶ֝֗לֶךְ יִשְׂרָאֵֽל
verses.translation: The proverbs of Solomon son of David, king of Israel:
verses.rashi: The proverbs of All his words are illustrations and allegories. He compared the Torah to a good woman, and he compared idolatry to a harlot.

verses.hebrew_full: לָדַ֣עַת חׇכְמָ֣ה וּמוּסָ֑ר לְ֝הָבִ֗ין אִמְרֵ֥י בִינָֽה
verses.translation: For learning wisdom and discipline;For understanding words of discernment;
verses.rashi: To know wisdom and discipline He stated these proverbs to make known to the people [wisdom and discipline], that they toil in the Torah, which is wisdom, discipline and understanding.

verses.hebrew_full: לָ֭קַחַת מוּסַ֣ר הַשְׂכֵּ֑ל צֶ֥דֶק וּ֝מִשְׁפָּ֗ט וּמֵשָׁרִֽים
verses.translation: For acquiring the discipline for success,Righteousness, justice, and equity;
verses.rashi: righteousness, justice, and equity “Righteousness” denotes charity from his money; “justice” means to judge honestly, and “equity” denotes compromise—the smooth and straight road, equal to this one and to that one.

verses.hebrew_full: לָתֵ֣ת לִפְתָאיִ֣ם עׇרְמָ֑ה לְ֝נַ֗עַר דַּ֣עַת וּמְזִמָּֽה
verses.translation: For endowing the simple with shrewdness,The young with knowledge and foresight.
verses.rashi: To give prudence to the simple Koheleth stated these proverbs so that the simple should gain prudence. knowledge...to the youth And also to the youth, who is devoid of all [knowledge], who has not yet learned anything. and discretion Thoughts of counsel.

verses.hebrew_full: יִשְׁמַ֣ע חָ֭כָם וְי֣וֹסֶף לֶ֑קַח וְ֝נָב֗וֹן תַּחְבֻּל֥וֹת יִקְנֶֽה
verses.translation: —The wise, hearing them, will gain more wisdom;The discerning will learn to be adroit;
verses.rashi: Let the wise man hear these proverbs. and increase to his wisdom. learning Heb. לקח, learning. the wise man This is the one who has heard wisdom. The understanding man who has knowledge superior to the knowledge of the wise man, for he knows how to derive one idea from another, and he adds to what he [has] heard.

verses.hebrew_full: לְהָבִ֣ין מָ֭שָׁל וּמְלִיצָ֑ה דִּבְרֵ֥י חֲ֝כָמִ֗ים וְחִידֹתָֽם
verses.translation: For understanding proverb and epigram,The words of the wise and their riddles.
verses.rashi: to understand an allegory and a figure That they should direct their attention to understand the verses through two methods: the allegory and the figure. They should understand what he compares to the figure, but they should not neglect the figure itself, for that, too, requires understanding. When he states, “To save you from a strange woman and a foreign one” (2:16), idolatry is meant; this is the allegory, and also the figure—for he expressed his allegory in terms of a woman—should be understood by it, [meaning that] you shall beware of a strange woman (other editions: a harlot). the words of the wise and their riddles Those who interpret the Torah metaphorically, full verses and elliptical ones, allusions, comparisons and riddles.

verses.hebrew_full: יִרְאַ֣ת יְ֭הֹוָה רֵאשִׁ֣ית דָּ֑עַת חׇכְמָ֥ה וּ֝מוּסָ֗ר אֱוִילִ֥ים בָּֽזוּ
verses.translation: The fear of GOD is the beginning of knowledge;Fools despise wisdom and discipline.
verses.rashi: The fear of the Lord is the beginning of knowledge Until here, he explained for what purpose Solomon composed this book, and now the book commences. The fear of the Lord is the beginning of knowledge This is the separation of the fundamentals of knowledge, and what shall be for you first, preceding knowledge: Before your wisdom, first fear your Creator, and that will give your heart the desire to engage in wisdom and in knowledge, for the fools, who do not fear the Lord, despise wisdom and discipline.

verses.hebrew_full: שְׁמַ֣ע בְּ֭נִי מוּסַ֣ר אָבִ֑יךָ וְאַל־תִּ֝טֹּ֗שׁ תּוֹרַ֥ת אִמֶּֽךָ
verses.translation: My son, heed the discipline of your father,And do not forsake the instruction of your mother;
verses.rashi: Hearken, my son, to the discipline of your father What the Holy One, blessed be He, gave Moses in writing and orally. your mother Heb. אמך [like אמתך], your nation, the nation of Israel, as in (Ezek. 19:2): “What a lioness was your mother [meaning your nation]!” These are the words of the Scribes, which they innovated and added and made safeguards for the Torah.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֤י לִוְיַ֤ת חֵ֓ן הֵ֬ם לְרֹאשֶׁ֑ךָ וַ֝עֲנָקִ֗ים לְגַרְגְּרֹתֶֽךָ
verses.translation: For they are a graceful wreath upon your head,A necklace about your throat.
verses.rashi: a wreath of grace They are a wreath of grace for your head; i.e., the instruction and the discipline will be a wreath of grace for your head, and they shall be like the rings of a golden ornament. for your neck Heb. לגרגרתיך. Since the trachea is composed of many rings, he refers to the neck in the plural form.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּנִ֡י אִם־יְפַתּ֥וּךָ חַ֝טָּאִ֗ים אַל־תֹּבֵֽא
verses.translation: My son, if sinners entice you, do not yield;
verses.rashi: sinners Heb. חטאים, sinners. do not consent Heb. אל תבא, do not consent to them.

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־יֹאמְרוּ֮ לְכָ֢ה אִ֫תָּ֥נוּ נֶאֶרְבָ֥ה לְדָ֑ם נִצְפְּנָ֖ה לְנָקִ֣י חִנָּֽם
verses.translation: If they say, “Come with us,Let us set an ambush to shed blood,Let us lie in wait for the innocent(Without cause!)
verses.rashi: let us lie in wait for blood to shed blood. let us hide Heb. נצפנה, an expression of ambush. let us hide for the innocent, without cause Scripture states that their hiding for the innocent is without cause.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object

verses.hebrew_full: נִ֭בְלָעֵם כִּשְׁא֣וֹל חַיִּ֑ים וּ֝תְמִימִ֗ים כְּי֣וֹרְדֵי בֽוֹר
verses.translation: Like Sheol, let us swallow them alive;Whole, like those who go down into the Pit.
verses.rashi: let us swallow them up The innocent when they are alive. like the grave which swallows up the entire body. and the whole ones Heb. ותמימים. This is not an expression of righteous men, but an expression of whole ones. Let us swallow them up when they are whole, like a man who descends into a pit when he is whole; i.e., when they are still wealthy, let us slay them and inherit their property.

verses.hebrew_full: כׇּל־ה֣וֹן יָקָ֣ר נִמְצָ֑א נְמַלֵּ֖א בָתֵּ֣ינוּ שָׁלָֽל
verses.translation: We shall obtain every precious treasure;We shall fill our homes with loot.

verses.hebrew_full: גּ֭וֹרָ֣לְךָ תַּפִּ֣יל בְּתוֹכֵ֑נוּ כִּ֥יס אֶ֝חָ֗ד יִהְיֶ֥ה לְכֻלָּֽנוּ
verses.translation: Throw in your lot with us;We shall all have a common purse.”
verses.rashi: Cast your lot among us If you wish, you may share; if you wish, it will be common property. we will all have one purse together.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּנִ֗י אַל־תֵּלֵ֣ךְ בְּדֶ֣רֶךְ אִתָּ֑ם מְנַ֥ע רַ֝גְלְךָ֗ מִנְּתִיבָתָֽם
verses.translation: My son, do not set out with them;Keep your feet from their path.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֣י רַ֭גְלֵיהֶם לָרַ֣ע יָר֑וּצוּ וִ֝ימַהֲר֗וּ לִשְׁפׇּךְ־דָּֽם
verses.translation: For their feet run to evil;They hurry to shed blood.
verses.rashi: run to evil They run to their own harm, and they do not know to pay heed to the matter.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־חִ֭נָּם מְזֹרָ֣ה הָרָ֑שֶׁת בְּ֝עֵינֵ֗י כׇּל־בַּ֥עַל כָּנָֽף
verses.translation: In the eyes of every winged creatureThe outspread net means nothing.
verses.rashi: For the net is scattered without cause For the fowl that see wheat and legumes spread out upon the net—it appears to them that it is without cause; they do not realize for what reason they are spread, and they go down into it and eat.

verses.hebrew_full: וְ֭הֵם לְדָמָ֣ם יֶאֱרֹ֑בוּ יִ֝צְפְּנ֗וּ לְנַפְשֹׁתָֽם
verses.translation: But they lie in ambush for their own blood;They lie in wait for their own lives.
verses.rashi: but they lie in wait for their blood But the hunters lie in wait for the blood of the birds.

verses.hebrew_full: כֵּ֗ן אׇ֭רְחוֹת כׇּל־בֹּ֣צֵֽעַ בָּ֑צַע אֶת־נֶ֖פֶשׁ בְּעָלָ֣יו יִקָּֽח
verses.translation: Such is the fate of all who pursue unjust gain;It takes the life of its possessor.
verses.rashi: So are the ways of everyone who commits robbery Heb. בצע בצע, who commits robbery. It is beautiful and precious to him, and it is free to him. But his end will be that... it will take away the life of its owner His own life, for he has now become the owner of the money that he stole from his neighbor.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·soul

verses.hebrew_full: חׇ֭כְמוֹת בַּח֣וּץ תָּרֹ֑נָּה בָּ֝רְחֹב֗וֹת תִּתֵּ֥ן קוֹלָֽהּ
verses.translation: Wisdom cries aloud in the streets,Raises her voice in the squares.
verses.rashi: Wisdoms shout in the street Behold, the wisdoms of the Torah cry out in her streets to admonish [the people] to turn to them [the streets]. Now what are her streets? The study halls. in the squares Heb. ברחבות, in the place where it is broadened [in the place where Torah is studied intensively and elaborated upon]. In this manner, Rabbi Tanhuma expounded [upon this verse].

words.eng: broad or open place or plaza

verses.hebrew_full: בְּרֹ֥אשׁ הֹמִיּ֗וֹת תִּ֫קְרָ֥א בְּפִתְחֵ֖י שְׁעָרִ֥ים בָּעִ֗יר אֲמָרֶ֥יהָ תֹאמֵֽר
verses.translation: At the head of the busy streets she calls;At the entrance of the gates, in the city, she speaks out:
verses.rashi: She calls at the head of the noisy streets In the place where she is heard and called [and proclaimed Vilna, Warsaw ed.] there she calls out and makes the following statement. “How long will you naive ones, etc?” at the entrances of the gates They are the place where the elders sit.

verses.hebrew_full: עַד־מָתַ֣י פְּתָיִם֮ תְּֽאֵהֲב֫וּ־פֶ֥תִי וְלֵצִ֗ים לָ֭צוֹן חָמְד֣וּ לָהֶ֑ם וּ֝כְסִילִ֗ים יִשְׂנְאוּ־דָֽעַת
verses.translation: “How long will you simple ones love simplicity,You scoffers be eager to scoff,You dullards hate knowledge?
verses.rashi: you naive ones Heb. פתים. Those who are enticed by enticers and sectarians. naivete Heb. פתי, enticement, the noun for simplicity; like כלי, a vessel, קרי, a happening, שפי, silence.

verses.hebrew_full: תָּשׁ֗וּבוּ לְֽת֫וֹכַחְתִּ֥י הִנֵּ֤ה אַבִּ֣יעָה לָכֶ֣ם רוּחִ֑י אוֹדִ֖יעָה דְבָרַ֣י אֶתְכֶֽם
verses.translation: You are indifferent to my rebuke;I will now speak my mind to you,And let you know my thoughts.

words.eng: inflected m. pers. pron. meaning ‘to you’.
sign of the definite direct object

verses.hebrew_full: יַ֣עַן קָ֭רָאתִי וַתְּמָאֵ֑נוּ נָטִ֥יתִי יָ֝דִ֗י וְאֵ֣ין מַקְשִֽׁיב
verses.translation: Since you refused me when I called,And paid no heed when I extended my hand,
verses.rashi: I stretched out my hand to beckon to you to turn to me, like a man who beckons to his friend with his hand, stretching out his hand to him to turn to him.

verses.hebrew_full: וַתִּפְרְע֥וּ כׇל־עֲצָתִ֑י וְ֝תוֹכַחְתִּ֗י לֹ֣א אֲבִיתֶֽם
verses.translation: You spurned all my advice,And would not hear my rebuke,
verses.rashi: and you have made nothing Heb. ותפרעו, and you nullified. all my advice that I advised, in order to aggrandize you in the world.

verses.hebrew_full: גַּם־אֲ֭נִי בְּאֵידְכֶ֣ם אֶשְׂחָ֑ק אֶ֝לְעַ֗ג בְּבֹ֣א פַחְדְּכֶֽם
verses.translation: I will laugh at your calamity,And mock when terror comes upon you,

words.eng: also·I (first pers. sing. -usually used for emphasis)

verses.hebrew_full: בְּבֹ֤א (כשאוה) כְשׁוֹאָ֨ה פַּחְדְּכֶ֗ם וְֽ֭אֵידְכֶם כְּסוּפָ֣ה יֶאֱתֶ֑ה בְּבֹ֥א עֲ֝לֵיכֶ֗ם צָרָ֥ה וְצוּקָֽה
verses.translation: When terror comes like a disaster,And calamity arrives like a whirlwind,When trouble and distress come upon you.
verses.rashi: when your fear comes like a storm Like a cloud that comes up suddenly. like a whirlwind Tourbillon in Old French, in German wirbelwind (Cf. Rashi, Jer. 4:13, Ps. 83:16). comes Heb. יאתה, it comes.

verses.hebrew_full: אָ֣ז יִ֭קְרָאֻנְנִי וְלֹ֣א אֶעֱנֶ֑ה יְ֝שַׁחֲרֻ֗נְנִי וְלֹ֣א יִמְצָאֻֽנְנִי
verses.translation: Then they shall call me but I will not answer;They shall seek me but not find me.
verses.rashi: they shall seek me Heb. ישחרנני, they shall look for me.

verses.hebrew_full: תַּ֭חַת כִּֽי־שָׂ֣נְאוּ דָ֑עַת וְיִרְאַ֥ת יְ֝הֹוָ֗ה לֹ֣א בָחָֽרוּ
verses.translation: Because they hated knowledge,And did not choose fear of GOD;

verses.hebrew_full: לֹא־אָב֥וּ לַעֲצָתִ֑י נָ֝אֲצ֗וּ כׇּל־תּוֹכַחְתִּֽי
verses.translation: They refused my advice,And disdained all my rebukes,

verses.hebrew_full: וְֽ֭יֹאכְלוּ מִפְּרִ֣י דַרְכָּ֑ם וּֽמִמֹּעֲצֹ֖תֵיהֶ֣ם יִשְׂבָּֽעוּ
verses.translation: They shall eat the fruit of their ways,And have their fill of their own counsels.
verses.rashi: of the fruit of their way The fruit of the troubles that befall them they eat in their lifetime, and the principal is preserved for them in Gehinnom.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֤י מְשׁוּבַ֣ת פְּתָיִ֣ם תַּהַרְגֵ֑ם וְשַׁלְוַ֖ת כְּסִילִ֣ים תְּאַבְּדֵֽם
verses.translation: The tranquility of the simple will kill them,And the complacency of dullards will destroy them.
verses.rashi: for the backsliding of the naive Heb. משובת פתים. Insofar as their heart backslides. and the tranquility of the fools shall cause them to perish Since they see the wicked who prosper, they adhere to their evil and do not repent.

verses.hebrew_full: וְשֹׁמֵ֣עַֽ לִ֭י יִשְׁכׇּן־בֶּ֑טַח וְ֝שַׁאֲנַ֗ן מִפַּ֥חַד רָעָֽה
verses.translation: But the one who listens to me will dwell in safety,Untroubled by the terror of misfortune.”
verses.rashi: shall dwell confidently in this world. and shall be tranquil in the world to come. from the fear of harm From the punishment of Gehinnom. and shall be tranquil Heb. ושאנן and shall be tranquil and at rest. The word ושאנן is in the future tense; therefore, [the “nun”] is vowelized with a “pattah.”

verses.hebrew_full: בְּ֭נִי אִם־תִּקַּ֣ח אֲמָרָ֑י וּ֝מִצְוֺתַ֗י תִּצְפֹּ֥ן אִתָּֽךְ
verses.translation: My son, if you accept my wordsAnd treasure up my commandments;
verses.rashi: My son, if you accept my words You will be my son if you accept my words.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object

verses.hebrew_full: לְהַקְשִׁ֣יב לַחׇכְמָ֣ה אׇזְנֶ֑ךָ תַּטֶּ֥ה לִ֝בְּךָ֗ לַתְּבוּנָֽה
verses.translation: If you make your ear attentive to wisdomAnd your mind open to discernment;
verses.rashi: to make...attentive to wisdom to toil in [learning] Torah.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֤י אִ֣ם לַבִּינָ֣ה תִקְרָ֑א לַ֝תְּבוּנָ֗ה תִּתֵּ֥ן קוֹלֶֽךָ
verses.translation: If you call to understandingAnd cry aloud to discernment,

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־תְּבַקְשֶׁ֥נָּה כַכָּ֑סֶף וְֽכַמַּטְמוֹנִ֥ים תַּחְפְּשֶֽׂנָּה
verses.translation: If you seek it as you do silverAnd search for it as for treasures,

verses.hebrew_full: אָ֗ז תָּ֭בִין יִרְאַ֣ת יְהֹוָ֑ה וְדַ֖עַת אֱלֹהִ֣ים תִּמְצָֽא
verses.translation: Then you will understand the fear of GOD And attain knowledge of God.
verses.rashi: then you will understand the fear of the Lord This refers back to the topic above, in which he said, “For if you call for understanding.”

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־יְ֭הֹוָה יִתֵּ֣ן חׇכְמָ֑ה מִ֝פִּ֗יו דַּ֣עַת וּתְבוּנָֽה
verses.translation: For GOD grants wisdom;Knowledge and discernment are by God’s decree.
verses.rashi: For the Lord gives wisdom Here you have learned that it [wisdom] is great, for it was given from the mouth of the Holy One, blessed be He. Therefore, you must acquire it.

verses.hebrew_full: (וצפן) יִצְפֹּ֣ן לַ֭יְשָׁרִים תּוּשִׁיָּ֑ה מָ֝גֵ֗ן לְהֹ֣לְכֵי תֹֽם
verses.translation: Ability is reserved for the uprightAnd [God] is a shield for those who live blamelessly,
verses.rashi: He lays up sound wisdom for the upright The Holy One, blessed be He, hid it [wisdom] with Him for twenty-six generations until He gave it to the generation of the desert. a shield for those who walk in integrity (He lays up a shield for those who walk with integrity); i.e., it will be a shield for you.

verses.hebrew_full: לִ֭נְצֹר אׇרְח֣וֹת מִשְׁפָּ֑ט וְדֶ֖רֶךְ חֲסִידָ֣ו יִשְׁמֹֽר
verses.translation: Guarding the paths of justice,Protecting the way of those who are loyal.
verses.rashi: to keep the paths of justice For through it, they will keep the paths of justice, and He will guard the way of His pious so that they do not stumble.

verses.hebrew_full: אָ֗ז תָּ֭בִין צֶ֣דֶק וּמִשְׁפָּ֑ט וּ֝מֵישָׁרִ֗ים כׇּל־מַעְגַּל־טֽוֹב
verses.translation: You will then understand what is right, just,And equitable—every good course.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־תָב֣וֹא חׇכְמָ֣ה בְלִבֶּ֑ךָ וְ֝דַ֗עַת לְֽנַפְשְׁךָ֥ יִנְעָֽם
verses.translation: For wisdom will enter your mindAnd knowledge will delight you.

verses.hebrew_full: מְ֭זִמָּה תִּשְׁמֹ֥ר עָלֶ֗יךָ תְּבוּנָ֥ה תִנְצְרֶֽכָּה
verses.translation: Foresight will protect you,And discernment will guard you.
verses.rashi: thought Heb. מזמה. The Torah shall watch over you.

verses.hebrew_full: לְ֭הַצִּ֣ילְךָ מִדֶּ֣רֶךְ רָ֑ע מֵ֝אִ֗ישׁ מְדַבֵּ֥ר תַּהְפֻּכֽוֹת
verses.translation: It will save you from an evildoer’s ways,From a man who speaks duplicity—
verses.rashi: who speaks perversity Those are the people who distort the thing. [Rashi as it appears in Lublin and Waxman ed.] Malbim ed. reads: who distorts the words of the Torah after his erroneous view. Vilna and Warsaw editions read: They are the apostates who distort the words of the Torah to evil.

verses.hebrew_full: הַ֭עֹ֣זְבִים אׇרְח֣וֹת יֹ֑שֶׁר לָ֝לֶ֗כֶת בְּדַרְכֵי־חֹֽשֶׁךְ
verses.translation: Those who leave the paths of rectitudeTo follow the ways of darkness,

verses.hebrew_full: הַ֭שְּׂמֵחִים לַעֲשׂ֥וֹת רָ֑ע יָ֝גִ֗ילוּ בְּֽתַהְפֻּכ֥וֹת רָֽע
verses.translation: Who rejoice in doing evilAnd exult in an evildoer’s duplicity,

verses.hebrew_full: אֲשֶׁ֣ר אׇרְחֹתֵיהֶ֣ם עִקְּשִׁ֑ים וּ֝נְלוֹזִ֗ים בְּמַעְגְּלוֹתָֽם
verses.translation: All whose paths are crookedAnd who are devious in their course.
verses.rashi: and perverse in their paths Heb. ונלוזים. Every expression of נלוז is an expression of crookedness, as it is always next to עקש, and עקש is an expression of crookedness, as it is stated (Isa. 42:16): “and crooked paths (ומעקשים) into straight ones.” and perverse in their paths They are crooked in their corrupt ways.

verses.hebrew_full: לְ֭הַצִּ֣ילְךָ מֵאִשָּׁ֣ה זָרָ֑ה מִ֝נׇּכְרִיָּ֗ה אֲמָרֶ֥יהָ הֶחֱלִֽיקָה
verses.translation: It will save you from the forbidden woman,From the alien woman whose talk is smooth,
verses.rashi: To save you from a strange woman From the assembly of idolatry [apostasy], which is sectarianism. It cannot be said that he spoke only of the actual adulteress, for what is the praise of the Torah, that he says here, “to save you from a strange woman,” and not from any other sin. Rather, this is the casting off of the yoke of all the commandments.

verses.hebrew_full: הַ֭עֹזֶבֶת אַלּ֣וּף נְעוּרֶ֑יהָ וְאֶת־בְּרִ֖ית אֱלֹהֶ֣יהָ שָׁכֵֽחָה
verses.translation: Who forsakes the companion of her youthAnd disregards the covenant of her God.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·covenant

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֤י שָׁ֣חָה אֶל־מָ֣וֶת בֵּיתָ֑הּ וְאֶל־רְ֝פָאִ֗ים מַעְגְּלֹתֶֽיהָ
verses.translation: Her house sinks down to Death,And her course leads to the shades.
verses.rashi: for her house sinks to death This refers back to “to save you, etc.” for whoever comes to her house will sink and slip as if down an incline that leads to death, and the Torah will guard you from this fall. Hence, it is a great thing for you. the dead Heb. רפאים, those who neglect (נרפים) the way of goodness and are forsaken without support until they fall into Gehinnom.

verses.hebrew_full: כׇּל־בָּ֭אֶיהָ לֹ֣א יְשׁוּב֑וּן וְלֹֽא־יַ֝שִּׂ֗יגוּ אׇרְח֥וֹת חַיִּֽים
verses.translation: All who go to her cannot returnAnd find again the paths of life.
verses.rashi: none...return It is hard for them to part with it and to repent.

verses.hebrew_full: לְמַ֗עַן תֵּ֭לֵךְ בְּדֶ֣רֶךְ טוֹבִ֑ים וְאׇרְח֖וֹת צַדִּיקִ֣ים תִּשְׁמֹֽר
verses.translation: So follow the way of the goodAnd keep to the paths of the just.
verses.rashi: in order that you go This refers back to the above verse (verse 12): “To save you from an evil way,” in order to lead you in the way of the good.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־יְשָׁרִ֥ים יִשְׁכְּנוּ־אָ֑רֶץ וּ֝תְמִימִ֗ים יִוָּ֥תְרוּ בָֽהּ
verses.translation: For the upright will inhabit the earth,The blameless will remain in it.
verses.rashi: For the upright shall dwell in the land In the world to come. shall remain therein when the wicked descend to Gehinnom.

verses.hebrew_full: וּ֭רְשָׁעִים מֵאֶ֣רֶץ יִכָּרֵ֑תוּ וּ֝בוֹגְדִ֗ים יִסְּח֥וּ מִמֶּֽנָּה
verses.translation: While the wicked will vanish from the landAnd the treacherous will be rooted out of it.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּ֭נִי תּוֹרָתִ֣י אַל־תִּשְׁכָּ֑ח וּ֝מִצְוֺתַ֗י יִצֹּ֥ר לִבֶּֽךָ
verses.translation: My son, do not forget my teaching,But let your mind retain my commandments;

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֤י אֹ֣רֶךְ יָ֭מִים וּשְׁנ֣וֹת חַיִּ֑ים וְ֝שָׁל֗וֹם יוֹסִ֥יפוּ לָֽךְ
verses.translation: For they will bestow on you length of days,Years of life and well-being.
verses.rashi: they shall add length of days...and peace The Torah and the commandments.

verses.hebrew_full: חֶ֥סֶד וֶאֱמֶ֗ת אַֽל־יַ֫עַזְבֻ֥ךָ קׇשְׁרֵ֥ם עַל־גַּרְגְּרוֹתֶ֑יךָ כׇּ֝תְבֵ֗ם עַל־ל֥וּחַ לִבֶּֽךָ
verses.translation: Let fidelity and steadfastness not leave you;Bind them about your throat,Write them on the tablet of your mind,

verses.hebrew_full: וּמְצָא־חֵ֥ן וְשֵֽׂכֶל־ט֑וֹב בְּעֵינֵ֖י אֱלֹהִ֣ים וְאָדָֽם
verses.translation: And you will find favor and approbationIn the eyes of God and human beings.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּטַ֣ח אֶל־יְ֭הֹוָה בְּכׇל־לִבֶּ֑ךָ וְאֶל־בִּ֥֝ינָתְךָ֗ אַל־תִּשָּׁעֵֽן
verses.translation: Trust in GOD with all your heart,And do not rely on your own understanding.
verses.rashi: Trust in the Lord and squander your money to seek for yourself a teacher from whom to learn, and do not rely on your understanding.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּכׇל־דְּרָכֶ֥יךָ דָעֵ֑הוּ וְ֝ה֗וּא יְיַשֵּׁ֥ר אֹֽרְחֹתֶֽיךָ
verses.translation: In all your ways acknowledge [God],And your paths will be made smooth.

verses.hebrew_full: אַל־תְּהִ֣י חָכָ֣ם בְּעֵינֶ֑יךָ יְרָ֥א אֶת־יְ֝הֹוָ֗ה וְס֣וּר מֵרָֽע
verses.translation: Do not be wise in your own eyes;Fear GOD and shun evil.
verses.rashi: Do not be wise in your own sight to despise the word of the one who reproves you.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·Commonly transcribed YHWH

verses.hebrew_full: רִ֭פְאוּת תְּהִ֣י לְשׇׁרֶּ֑ךָ וְ֝שִׁקּ֗וּי לְעַצְמוֹתֶֽיךָ
verses.translation: It will be a cure for your body, A tonic for your bones.
verses.rashi: It shall be healing for your navel Heb. לשרך. The wisdom shall be healing. שרך is like (Song 7:4): שררך, which means, your navel. and marrow for your bones Heb. ושקוי, lit. moisture. That is the marrow, as the matter is stated (Job 21:24): “and the marrow of his bones is moistened.”

verses.hebrew_full: כַּבֵּ֣ד אֶת־יְ֭הֹוָה מֵהוֹנֶ֑ךָ וּ֝מֵרֵאשִׁ֗ית כׇּל־תְּבוּאָתֶֽךָ
verses.translation: Honor GOD with your wealth,With the best of all your income,
verses.rashi: from your substance From whatever He favored you [with], even from a pleasant voice. (Do not read: from your substance [מהונך] but: from your throat [מגרונך].) and from the first (These are the terumoth and the tithes.)

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·Commonly transcribed YHWH

verses.hebrew_full: וְיִמָּלְא֣וּ אֲסָמֶ֣יךָ שָׂבָ֑ע וְ֝תִיר֗וֹשׁ יְקָבֶ֥יךָ יִפְרֹֽצוּ
verses.translation: And your barns will be filled with grain,Your vats will burst with new wine.

words.eng: to break through or down or over

verses.hebrew_full: מוּסַ֣ר יְ֭הֹוָה בְּנִ֣י אַל־תִּמְאָ֑ס וְאַל־תָּ֝קֹ֗ץ בְּתוֹכַחְתּֽוֹ
verses.translation: Do not reject GOD’s discipline, my son;Do not abhor such rebuke.
verses.rashi: My son, despise not the discipline of the Lord Should pains come upon you, they should be dear to you. and do not abhor Heb. תקץ, from the expression of (Gen. 27:46) “I abhor (קצתי) my life.”

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֤י אֶ֥ת־אֲשֶׁ֣ר יֶאֱהַ֣ב יְהֹוָ֣ה יוֹכִ֑יחַ וּ֝כְאָ֗ב אֶת־בֵּ֥ן יִרְצֶֽה
verses.translation: For whom GOD loves, [God] rebukes,As a father the son whom he favors.
verses.rashi: as a father placates his son He desires to benefit his son and he placates him after striking him with the staff. So will the benefit be pleasant to you after the smiting.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·(relative part.)
sign of the definite direct object·between

verses.hebrew_full: אַשְׁרֵ֣י אָ֭דָם מָצָ֣א חׇכְמָ֑ה וְ֝אָדָ֗ם יָפִ֥יק תְּבוּנָֽה
verses.translation: Happy is the one who finds wisdom,The one who attains understanding.
verses.rashi: Fortunate is the man who has found wisdom and a man who gives forth discernment One who learned wisdom until he is so accustomed to it that he can express it with his mouth.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֤י ט֣וֹב סַ֭חְרָהּ מִסְּחַר־כָּ֑סֶף וּ֝מֵחָר֗וּץ תְּבוּאָתָֽהּ
verses.translation: Her value in trade is better than silver,Her yield, greater than gold.
verses.rashi: for its commerce is better than the commerce of silver In any exchange, when a person exchanges [something] for merchandise, this one takes this, and that one takes that; but if one says to his friend, “Teach me your chapter, and I will teach you my chapter,” [then] both chapters are found in the hand of each of them. than fine gold Heb. מחרוץ. That is a type of gold.

verses.hebrew_full: יְקָ֣רָה הִ֭יא (מפניים) מִפְּנִינִ֑ים וְכׇל־חֲ֝פָצֶ֗יךָ לֹ֣א יִֽשְׁווּ־בָֽהּ
verses.translation: She is more precious than rubies;All of your goods cannot equal her.
verses.rashi: and all your desirable things Heb. חפציך all that you desire. cannot be compared to it They are not equal to its worth, nor their price to its price.

verses.hebrew_full: אֹ֣רֶךְ יָ֭מִים בִּֽימִינָ֑הּ בִּ֝שְׂמֹאולָ֗הּ עֹ֣שֶׁר וְכָבֽוֹד
verses.translation: In her right hand is length of days,In her left, riches and honor.
verses.rashi: in its right hand Those who make the right use of it and engage in it for its own sake will have length of days and surely riches and honor, but those who make the wrong [lit. left] use of it, who engage in it not for its own sake, will still have riches and honor.

verses.hebrew_full: דְּרָכֶ֥יהָ דַרְכֵי־נֹ֑עַם וְֽכׇל־נְתִ֖יבוֹתֶ֣יהָ שָׁלֽוֹם
verses.translation: Her ways are pleasant ways,And all her paths, peaceful.

verses.hebrew_full: עֵץ־חַיִּ֣ים הִ֭יא לַמַּחֲזִיקִ֣ים בָּ֑הּ וְֽתֹמְכֶ֥יהָ מְאֻשָּֽׁר
verses.translation: She is a tree of life to those who grasp her,And whoever holds on to her is happy.
verses.rashi: for those who grasp it Heb. למחזיקים for those who hold onto it, as in (Ex. 4:4): “and he extended his hand and grasped (ויחזק) it.” and those who draw near it are fortunate Heb. ותמכיה, those who draw near it. Likewise, every expression of תמיכה in this Book means that he holds onto it.

verses.hebrew_full: יְֽהֹוָ֗ה בְּחׇכְמָ֥ה יָסַד־אָ֑רֶץ כּוֹנֵ֥ן שָׁ֝מַ֗יִם בִּתְבוּנָֽה
verses.translation: GOD founded the earth by wisdomAnd established the heavens by understanding;
verses.rashi: The Lord founded the earth with wisdom According to the Torah. This is the Torah, which is discernment and which is knowledge.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּ֭דַעְתּוֹ תְּהוֹמ֣וֹת נִבְקָ֑עוּ וּ֝שְׁחָקִ֗ים יִרְעֲפוּ־טָֽל
verses.translation: By God’s knowledge the depths burst apart,And the skies distilled dew.
verses.rashi: drip Heb. ירעפו, like יטיפו. Now, since the entire world was created with them, therefore...

verses.hebrew_full: בְּ֭נִי אַל־יָלֻ֣זוּ מֵעֵינֶ֑יךָ נְצֹ֥ר תֻּ֝שִׁיָּ֗ה וּמְזִמָּֽה
verses.translation: My son, do not lose sight of them;Hold on to resourcefulness and foresight.
verses.rashi: let them not depart from your eyes Let them not be curved away from before your eyes, to remove them from before you.

verses.hebrew_full: וְיִֽהְי֣וּ חַיִּ֣ים לְנַפְשֶׁ֑ךָ וְ֝חֵ֗ן לְגַרְגְּרֹתֶֽיךָ
verses.translation: They will give life to your spiritAnd grace to your throat.
verses.rashi: and they shall be life for your soul for it is a tree of life. and grace for your neck For this is its praise that is mentioned above (1:9): “and a necklace for your neck.”

verses.hebrew_full: אָ֤ז תֵּלֵ֣ךְ לָבֶ֣טַח דַּרְכֶּ֑ךָ וְ֝רַגְלְךָ֗ לֹ֣א תִגּֽוֹף
verses.translation: Then you will go your way safelyAnd not injure your feet.
verses.rashi: shall not stumble Heb. לא תגוף. This is an expression of stumbling, (achopper in French, straucheln in German. And so in Jeremiah 13:16 Psalms 91.12.) And so (Ps. 91:12). “Lest your foot stumble (תגף) on a stone”; and so (Jer. 13:16): “And before your feet stumble (יתנגפו)” and so (Ex. 21:22): “and they dash (ונגפו) a pregnant woman.”

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־תִּשְׁכַּ֥ב לֹֽא־תִפְחָ֑ד וְ֝שָׁכַבְתָּ֗ וְֽעָרְבָ֥ה שְׁנָתֶֽךָ
verses.translation: When you lie down you will be unafraid;You will lie down and your sleep will be sweet.
verses.rashi: and when you lie down, your sleep shall be sweet Your sleep shall be sweet when you sleep, that you will not fear sudden terror.

verses.hebrew_full: אַל־תִּ֭ירָא מִפַּ֣חַד פִּתְאֹ֑ם וּמִשֹּׁאַ֥ת רְ֝שָׁעִ֗ים כִּ֣י תָבֹֽא
verses.translation: You will not fear sudden terrorOr the disaster that comes upon the wicked,
verses.rashi: or of the darkness when it comes upon the wicked.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־יְ֭הֹוָה יִֽהְיֶ֣ה בְכִסְלֶ֑ךָ וְשָׁמַ֖ר רַגְלְךָ֣ מִלָּֽכֶד
verses.translation: For GOD will be your trustAnd will keep your feet from being caught.
verses.rashi: For the Lord shall be your trust Heb. בכסלך, in your trust. Another explanation: in the things concerning which you are a fool (כסיל). This I found in Yerushalmi.

verses.hebrew_full: אַל־תִּמְנַע־ט֥וֹב מִבְּעָלָ֑יו בִּֽהְי֨וֹת לְאֵ֖ל (ידיך) יָדְךָ֣ לַעֲשֽׂוֹת
verses.translation: Do not withhold good from those who deserve itWhen you have the power to do it [for them].
verses.rashi: Do not withhold good from the one who needs it If you see that your friend wishes to benefit the poor, do not prevent him from doing so. when you have power in your hand to stop him. Another explanation: Do not withhold good from the one who needs it Do not withhold charity from the poor man. when you have the power in your hand to do charity; perhaps a day will come when you will not have the power in your hand, and similarly...

verses.hebrew_full: אַל־תֹּ֘אמַ֤ר (לרעיך) לְרֵעֲךָ֨ לֵ֣ךְ וָ֭שׁוּב וּמָחָ֥ר אֶתֵּ֗ן וְיֵ֣שׁ אִתָּֽךְ
verses.translation: Do not say to your fellow, “Come back again;I’ll give it to you tomorrow,” when you have it with you.
verses.rashi: Do not say to your fellow To the poor man. though you have it with you what to give to him. But our Sages explained: “Go and return” regarding the wages of a hireling.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object

verses.hebrew_full: אַל־תַּחֲרֹ֣שׁ עַל־רֵעֲךָ֣ רָעָ֑ה וְהֽוּא־יוֹשֵׁ֖ב לָבֶ֣טַח אִתָּֽךְ
verses.translation: Do not devise harm against your fellowWho lives trustfully with you.
verses.rashi: Devise no harm against your fellow Heb. אל תחרש, do not think—but it does not deviate from the meaning of plowing (חרישה) ; just as it is customary for the plower to prepare a place for the time of sowing, so does one who devises harm prepare a place for devices in his heart, how he will get up and execute it.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object

verses.hebrew_full: אַל־תָּרִ֣יב (תרוב) עִם־אָדָ֣ם חִנָּ֑ם אִם־לֹ֖א גְמָלְךָ֣ רָעָֽה
verses.translation: Do not quarrel with someone for no cause,When they have done you no harm.
verses.rashi: Do not quarrel with anyone to complain about him. if he did you no harm i.e., unless he transgressed a commandment written in the Torah. The Torah states (Lev. 19:18): “And you shall love your fellow as yourself,” but one who is wicked, you may hate.

verses.hebrew_full: אַל־תְּ֭קַנֵּא בְּאִ֣ישׁ חָמָ֑ס וְאַל־תִּ֝בְחַ֗ר בְּכׇל־דְּרָכָֽיו
verses.translation: Do not envy a lawless man,Or choose any of his ways;
verses.rashi: Do not envy a man of violence to do as his deeds if you see him prospering.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֤י תוֹעֲבַ֣ת יְהֹוָ֣ה נָל֑וֹז וְֽאֶת־יְשָׁרִ֥ים סוֹדֽוֹ
verses.translation: For the one who goes astray is an abomination to GOD,But the straightforward are intimates.
verses.rashi: for the perverse is an abomination to the Lord One who is crooked in his ways. but His counsel is with the upright Heb. ואת, and with, etc.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·straight

verses.hebrew_full: מְאֵרַ֣ת יְ֭הֹוָה בְּבֵ֣ית רָשָׁ֑ע וּנְוֵ֖ה צַדִּיקִ֣ים יְבָרֵֽךְ
verses.translation: GOD’s curse is on the house of the evildoer,But the abode of the righteous is blessed.

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־לַלֵּצִ֥ים הֽוּא־יָלִ֑יץ (ולעניים) וְ֝לַעֲנָוִ֗ים יִתֶּן־חֵֽן
verses.translation: At scoffers [God] scoffs,But the lowly are shown grace.
verses.rashi: If [one goes] to the scoffers If a person is attracted to them, he too will scoff with them. but [if he goes] to the humble, he evokes grace If he joins the humble, eventually his deeds will evoke grace in people’s eyes.

verses.hebrew_full: כָּ֭בוֹד חֲכָמִ֣ים יִנְחָ֑לוּ וּ֝כְסִילִ֗ים מֵרִ֥ים קָלֽוֹן
verses.translation: The wise shall obtain honor,But dullards get disgrace as their portion.
verses.rashi: The wise shall inherit honor, but the fools take disgrace as their portion For himself, he takes disgrace for his portion.

verses.hebrew_full: שִׁמְע֣וּ בָ֭נִים מ֣וּסַר אָ֑ב וְ֝הַקְשִׁ֗יבוּ לָדַ֥עַת בִּינָֽה
verses.translation: Sons, heed the discipline of a father;Listen and learn discernment,
verses.rashi: Children, hearken to the discipline of the Father The Holy One, blessed be He.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֤י לֶ֣קַח ט֭וֹב נָתַ֣תִּי לָכֶ֑ם תּ֝וֹרָתִ֗י אַֽל־תַּעֲזֹֽבוּ
verses.translation: For I give you good instruction;Do not forsake my teaching.
verses.rashi: For I gave you The prophet prophesies and speaks as an agent of the Holy One, blessed be He, and he is like His mouth.

words.eng: inflected m. pers. pron. meaning ‘to you’.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־בֵ֭ן הָיִ֣יתִי לְאָבִ֑י רַ֥ךְ וְ֝יָחִ֗יד לִפְנֵ֥י אִמִּֽי
verses.translation: Once I was a son to my father,The tender darling of my mother.
verses.rashi: For I was a son to my father Lest you say that Solomon hated people because he admonished them concerning robbery and immorality, something a person desires, he therefore states: I was a son, etc., a tender one and an only one, etc., that he loved me very much.

verses.hebrew_full: וַיֹּרֵ֗נִי וַיֹּ֥אמֶר לִ֗י יִֽתְמׇךְ־דְּבָרַ֥י לִבֶּ֑ךָ שְׁמֹ֖ר מִצְוֺתַ֣י וֶחְיֵֽה
verses.translation: He instructed me and said to me,“Let your mind hold on to my words;Keep my commandments and you will live.
verses.rashi: And he instructed me concerning that and he chastised me and said to me, “May your heart draw near to my words And since he chastened me with these words, therefore I admonish you concerning this (Rabbi Joseph Kara). Another explanation: For I was a son to my Father The prophet says, “I was a son to the Holy One, blessed be He, Who caused His spirit to rest upon me.” Now we find that the Holy One, blessed be He, called Solomon a son, for it is stated (II Sam. 7:14): “I will be to him a father, and he shall be to Me a son.” a tender one and an only one before my mother Heb. לפני אמי, before my nation (אמתי) I am chosen and beloved like a tender and only son. Therefore, my father instructed me.

verses.hebrew_full: קְנֵ֣ה חׇ֭כְמָה קְנֵ֣ה בִינָ֑ה אַל־תִּשְׁכַּ֥ח וְאַל־תֵּ֝֗ט מֵאִמְרֵי־פִֽי
verses.translation: Acquire wisdom, acquire discernment;Do not forget and do not swerve from my words.

verses.hebrew_full: אַל־תַּעַזְבֶ֥הָ וְתִשְׁמְרֶ֑ךָּ אֱהָבֶ֥הָ וְתִצְּרֶֽךָּ
verses.translation: Do not forsake her and she will guard you;Love her and she will protect you.
verses.rashi: Do not forsake her, and she will preserve you; love her Heb. אהבה, love her.

verses.hebrew_full: רֵאשִׁ֣ית חׇ֭כְמָה קְנֵ֣ה חׇכְמָ֑ה וּבְכׇל־קִ֝נְיָנְךָ֗ קְנֵ֣ה בִינָֽה
verses.translation: The beginning of wisdom is—acquire wisdom;With all your acquisitions, acquire discernment.
verses.rashi: The beginning of wisdom [is to] acquire wisdom At the beginning of your wisdom, learn from others and acquire for yourself the tradition from the mouth of the teacher, and afterwards with all your possession acquire understanding. Concentrate on it by yourself to understand the reasons, thereby deriving one thing from another.

verses.hebrew_full: סַלְסְלֶ֥הָ וּֽתְרוֹמְמֶ֑ךָּ תְּ֝כַבֵּ֗דְךָ כִּ֣י תְחַבְּקֶֽנָּה
verses.translation: Hug her to you and she will exalt you;She will bring you honor if you embrace her.
verses.rashi: Search for her Heb. סלסלה search for her. Review it to examine it minutely, as in (Jer. 6:9): “as a vintager over the searchings (סלסלות)” who goes repeatedly through the vineyard and brings back his hand to search for the single grapes. In the expression of the Sages (Rosh Hashanah 26b), curls (מסלסל) his hair.

verses.hebrew_full: תִּתֵּ֣ן לְ֭רֹאשְׁךָ לִוְיַת־חֵ֑ן עֲטֶ֖רֶת תִּפְאֶ֣רֶת תְּמַגְּנֶֽךָּ
verses.translation: She will adorn your head with a graceful wreath;Crown you with a glorious diadem.”
verses.rashi: She will give your head a wreath of grace Heb. לוית חן, a wreath of grace, as in (1:9): “They are a wreath of grace (לוית חן) for your head.”

verses.hebrew_full: שְׁמַ֣ע בְּ֭נִי וְקַ֣ח אֲמָרָ֑י וְיִרְבּ֥וּ לְ֝ךָ֗ שְׁנ֣וֹת חַיִּֽים
verses.translation: My son, heed and take in my words,And you will have many years of life.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּדֶ֣רֶךְ חׇ֭כְמָה הֹרֵיתִ֑יךָ הִ֝דְרַכְתִּ֗יךָ בְּמַעְגְּלֵי־יֹֽשֶׁר
verses.translation: I instruct you in the way of wisdom;I guide you in straight courses.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּֽ֭לֶכְתְּךָ לֹא־יֵצַ֣ר צַעֲדֶ֑ךָ וְאִם־תָּ֝ר֗וּץ לֹ֣א תִכָּשֵֽׁל
verses.translation: You will walk without breaking stride;When you run, you will not stumble.
verses.rashi: When you walk, your step will not be straightened One who does not spread out his legs is likely to fall.

verses.hebrew_full: הַחֲזֵ֣ק בַּמּוּסָ֣ר אַל־תֶּ֑רֶף נִ֝צְּרֶ֗הָ כִּי־הִ֥יא חַיֶּֽיךָ
verses.translation: Hold fast to discipline; do not let go;Keep it; it is your life.
verses.rashi: Take fast hold of discipline, do not let it loose Grasp the Torah, as in (Gen. 21:18) “and grasp (והחזיקי) your hand onto him”; (Ex. 4:4) “and he stretched out his hand and grasped it (ויחזק).”

verses.hebrew_full: בְּאֹ֣רַח רְ֭שָׁעִים אַל־תָּבֹ֑א וְאַל־תְּ֝אַשֵּׁ֗ר בְּדֶ֣רֶךְ רָעִֽים
verses.translation: Do not enter on the path of the wicked;Do not walk on the way of evildoers.
verses.rashi: You shall not come in the way of the wicked, and do not walk [as translated,] do not walk, as in (Job 23:11), “My foot has held to His path,” and as in (Proverbs 14:15), “but a cunning man understands his steps.”

verses.hebrew_full: פְּרָעֵ֥הוּ אַל־תַּעֲבׇר־בּ֑וֹ שְׂטֵ֖ה מֵעָלָ֣יו וַעֲבֹֽר
verses.translation: Avoid it; do not pass through it;Turn away from it; pass it by.
verses.rashi: Avoid it Heb. פרע, put it to naught. turn away from it Heb. שטה, turn away.

words.eng: ram·to pass over or by or through·inflected third pers. singular m. of בּֽ◌ ᴵ
to pass over or by or through

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֤י לֹ֣א יִֽ֭שְׁנוּ אִם־לֹ֣א יָרֵ֑עוּ וְֽנִגְזְלָ֥ה שְׁ֝נָתָ֗ם אִם־לֹ֥א (יכשולו) יַכְשִֽׁילוּ
verses.translation: For they cannot sleep unless they have done evil;Unless they make someone fall they are robbed of sleep.
verses.rashi: For they will not sleep They are unable to sleep.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֣י לָ֭חֲמוּ לֶ֣חֶם רֶ֑שַׁע וְיֵ֖ין חֲמָסִ֣ים יִשְׁתּֽוּ
verses.translation: They eat the bread of wickednessAnd drink the wine of lawlessness.

verses.hebrew_full: וְאֹ֣רַח צַ֭דִּיקִים כְּא֣וֹר נֹ֑גַהּ הוֹלֵ֥ךְ וָ֝א֗וֹר עַד־נְכ֥וֹן הַיּֽוֹם
verses.translation: The path of the righteous is like radiant sunlight,Ever brightening until noon.
verses.rashi: But the way of the righteous is like the light of dawn which shines and illuminates from the first ray of dawn. until the day is perfect Until midday, which is the brightest time of day.

verses.hebrew_full: דֶּ֣רֶךְ רְ֭שָׁעִים כָּאֲפֵלָ֑ה לֹ֥א יָ֝דְע֗וּ בַּמֶּ֥ה יִכָּשֵֽׁלוּ
verses.translation: The way of the wicked is all darkness;They do not know what will make them stumble.
verses.rashi: The way of the wicked is like pitch darkness; they do not know on what they stumble Suddenly, an obstacle will come to them, and they will not know how to beware of it.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּ֭נִי לִדְבָרַ֣י הַקְשִׁ֑יבָה לַ֝אֲמָרַ֗י הַט־אׇזְנֶֽךָ
verses.translation: My son, listen to my speech;Incline your ear to my words.

verses.hebrew_full: אַל־יַלִּ֥יזוּ מֵעֵינֶ֑יךָ שׇׁ֝מְרֵ֗ם בְּת֣וֹךְ לְבָבֶֽךָ
verses.translation: Do not lose sight of them;Keep them in your mind.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־חַיִּ֣ים הֵ֭ם לְמֹצְאֵיהֶ֑ם וּֽלְכׇל־בְּשָׂר֥וֹ מַרְפֵּֽא
verses.translation: They are life to those who find them,Healing for the whole body.
verses.rashi: for those who find them Heb. למצאיהם, an expression of finding.

verses.hebrew_full: מִֽכׇּל־מִ֭שְׁמָר נְצֹ֣ר לִבֶּ֑ךָ כִּֽי־מִ֝מֶּ֗נּוּ תּוֹצְא֥וֹת חַיִּֽים
verses.translation: More than all that you guard, guard your mind,For it is the source of life.
verses.rashi: From every interdict From whatever the Torah commanded to beware of, guard your heart (from transgressing), regardless whether it is a minor sin or a grave sin. for the issues of life [come] out of it For from even a minor one you shall have life and a great reward if you fulfill it—so did Rabbi Tanhuma expound.

verses.hebrew_full: הָסֵ֣ר מִ֭מְּךָ עִקְּשׁ֣וּת פֶּ֑ה וּלְז֥וּת שְׂ֝פָתַ֗יִם הַרְחֵ֥ק מִמֶּֽךָּ
verses.translation: Put crooked speech away from you;Keep devious talk far from you.
verses.rashi: Take crooked speech away from yourself Don’t do anything for which people will slander you and make their mouths crooked [when talking] about you. crooked speech Heb. עקשות. This is an expression of עקום, crooked, as in (Job 9:20): “Though I was innocent, He would prove me perverse (ויעקשני),” and in the Mishnah (Hullin 3:3) “its teeth are crooked and twisted (עקושות).” devious lips Crooked lips, that the people should not open their mouth wide [when talking] about you.

verses.hebrew_full: עֵ֭ינֶיךָ לְנֹ֣כַח יַבִּ֑יטוּ וְ֝עַפְעַפֶּ֗יךָ יַיְשִׁ֥רוּ נֶגְדֶּֽךָ
verses.translation: Let your eyes look forward,Your gaze be straight ahead.
verses.rashi: Let your eyes look forward Let them look to truth and uprightness. and let your eyelids look straight ahead of you Let them look at what is upright, to straighten your way before you.

verses.hebrew_full: פַּ֭לֵּס מַעְגַּ֣ל רַגְלֶ֑ךָ וְֽכׇל־דְּרָכֶ֥יךָ יִכֹּֽנוּ
verses.translation: Survey the course you take,And all your ways will prosper.
verses.rashi: Weigh the path of your feet Weigh your way: the loss of a commandment against its reward and the reward of a sin against its loss, and then all your ways will be established.

verses.hebrew_full: אַֽל־תֵּט־יָמִ֥ין וּשְׂמֹ֑אול הָסֵ֖ר רַגְלְךָ֣ מֵרָֽע
verses.translation: Do not swerve to the right or the left;Keep your feet from evil.
verses.rashi: Turn neither right nor left of the good weight.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּ֭נִי לְחׇכְמָתִ֣י הַקְשִׁ֑יבָה לִ֝תְבוּנָתִ֗י הַט־אׇזְנֶֽךָ
verses.translation: My son, listen to my wisdom;Incline your ear to my insight,

verses.hebrew_full: לִשְׁמֹ֥ר מְזִמּ֑וֹת וְ֝דַ֗עַת שְׂפָתֶ֥יךָ יִנְצֹֽרוּ
verses.translation: That you may have foresight,While your lips hold fast to knowledge.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֤י נֹ֣פֶת תִּ֭טֹּפְנָה שִׂפְתֵ֣י זָרָ֑ה וְחָלָ֖ק מִשֶּׁ֣מֶן חִכָּֽהּ
verses.translation: For the lips of a forbidden woman drip honey;Her mouth is smoother than oil;
verses.rashi: drip honey Heb. נפת, an expression of sweetness. the lips of a strange woman Apostasy. her palate Heb. חכה, an expression of חךּ, a palate.

verses.hebrew_full: וְֽ֭אַחֲרִיתָהּ מָרָ֣ה כַֽלַּעֲנָ֑ה חַ֝דָּ֗ה כְּחֶ֣רֶב פִּיּֽוֹת
verses.translation: But in the end she is as bitter as wormwood,Sharp as a two-edged sword.

verses.hebrew_full: רַ֭גְלֶיהָ יֹרְד֣וֹת מָ֑וֶת שְׁ֝א֗וֹל צְעָדֶ֥יהָ יִתְמֹֽכוּ
verses.translation: Her feet go down to Death;Her steps take hold of Sheol.
verses.rashi: come near Heb. יתמכו, an expression of nearness.

verses.hebrew_full: אֹ֣רַח חַ֭יִּים פֶּן־תְּפַלֵּ֑ס נָע֥וּ מַ֝עְגְּלֹתֶ֗יהָ לֹ֣א תֵדָֽע
verses.translation: She does not chart a path of life;Her course meanders for lack of knowledge.
verses.rashi: Lest you weigh the path of life So did the Sages (Tanhuma Ekev 2) expound it: The Holy One, blessed be He, moved away (טלטל) the reward for the commandments and did not explain it [the reward], so that a person should not see a commandment for which the reward is great and cling to it and leave over the other commandments. This is the interpretation: Lest you weigh the ways of the Torah, which one to take and which one to leave, therefore its paths wandered off—God caused them to wander off and He did not make them known. This is the Midrash Aggadah, but according to the sequence of the verses, since after it is written: “And now, children, hearken to me and do not turn away from the sayings of my mouth. Distance your way from her,” it appears that he has not yet ended the section dealing with the harlot, and it should be interpreted as follows: Lest you weigh the path of life Do not weigh the path of life in conjunction with that strange one, saying what to do, whether this one or this one [i.e., whether to follow the path of life or the path of the strange woman], for the paths of the harlot have wandered off to the grave, and you will not know how to beware until you wander off and fall.

verses.hebrew_full: וְעַתָּ֣ה בָ֭נִים שִׁמְעוּ־לִ֑י וְאַל־תָּ֝ס֗וּרוּ מֵאִמְרֵי־פִֽי
verses.translation: So now, sons, pay heed to me,And do not swerve from the words of my mouth.

verses.hebrew_full: הַרְחֵ֣ק מֵעָלֶ֣יהָ דַרְכֶּ֑ךָ וְאַל־תִּ֝קְרַ֗ב אֶל־פֶּ֥תַח בֵּיתָֽהּ
verses.translation: Keep yourself far away from her;Do not come near the doorway of her house

verses.hebrew_full: פֶּן־תִּתֵּ֣ן לַאֲחֵרִ֣ים הוֹדֶ֑ךָ וּ֝שְׁנֹתֶ֗יךָ לְאַכְזָרִֽי
verses.translation: Lest you give up your vigor to others,Your years to a ruthless one;
verses.rashi: lest you give others your glory Lest you turn your heart to other gods to give them the glory of your beauty and your praise. and your years to a cruel one To the prince of Gehinnom.

verses.hebrew_full: פֶּֽן־יִשְׂבְּע֣וּ זָרִ֣ים כֹּחֶ֑ךָ וַ֝עֲצָבֶ֗יךָ בְּבֵ֣ית נׇכְרִֽי
verses.translation: Lest strangers eat their fill of your strength,And your toil be for the house of another;
verses.rashi: lest strangers be sated with your strength The prophets of Baal, who collect money with their lies and their hastiness. and your labors And your toil, with which you were saddened and fatigued. in the house of an alien In the pagan temple of the pagans.

verses.hebrew_full: וְנָהַמְתָּ֥ בְאַחֲרִיתֶ֑ךָ בִּכְל֥וֹת בְּ֝שָׂרְךָ֗ וּשְׁאֵרֶֽךָ
verses.translation: And in the end you roar,When your flesh and body are consumed,
verses.rashi: And you shall moan when your end comes The end will be that you will moan when your end comes.

verses.hebrew_full: וְֽאָמַרְתָּ֗ אֵ֭יךְ שָׂנֵ֣אתִי מוּסָ֑ר וְ֝תוֹכַ֗חַת נָאַ֥ץ לִבִּֽי
verses.translation: And say,“O how I hated discipline,And heartily spurned rebuke.

verses.hebrew_full: וְֽלֹא־שָׁ֭מַעְתִּי בְּק֣וֹל מוֹרָ֑י וְ֝לִֽמְלַמְּדַ֗י לֹא־הִטִּ֥יתִי אׇזְנִֽי
verses.translation: I did not pay heed to my teachers,Or incline my ear to my instructors.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֭מְעַט הָיִ֣יתִי בְכׇל־רָ֑ע בְּת֖וֹךְ קָהָ֣ל וְעֵדָֽה
verses.translation: Soon I was in dire troubleAmidst the assembled congregation.”
verses.rashi: I was almost in all evil There is but a step between me and Gehinnom. (Because of a little thing, I was now “in all evil,” for I did not obey my instructors; had I obeyed them, this would not have happened to me. From Rabbi Joseph Kara.)

verses.hebrew_full: שְׁתֵה־מַ֥יִם מִבּוֹרֶ֑ךָ וְ֝נֹזְלִ֗ים מִתּ֥וֹךְ בְּאֵרֶֽךָ
verses.translation: Drink water from your own cistern,Running water from your own well.
verses.rashi: Drink water from your own cistern From the cistern that the Holy One, blessed be He, gave you for your share, viz. the Law of Moses. your own cistern This denotes gathered water. and running water Heb. ונזלים, spring water. In the beginning it is like gathered water, and at the end it gushes out more and more.

verses.hebrew_full: יָפ֣וּצוּ מַעְיְנֹתֶ֣יךָ ח֑וּצָה בָּ֝רְחֹב֗וֹת פַּלְגֵי־מָֽיִם
verses.translation: Your springs will gush forthIn streams in the public squares.
verses.rashi: May your springs spread out In the end you will acquire disciples, and you will promulgate decisions in public, and you will gain a reputation. in the squares of the city the rivulets of your water shall spread.

words.eng: broad or open place or plaza

verses.hebrew_full: יִֽהְיוּ־לְךָ֥ לְבַדֶּ֑ךָ וְאֵ֖ין לְזָרִ֣ים אִתָּֽךְ
verses.translation: They will be yours alone,Others having no part with you.
verses.rashi: You alone shall have them You alone will be honored by them, and no one else will share your honor with you. Since he says above, “Lest strangers be satisfied with your strength,” he says here, “You alone shall have them, and thereby...”

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object

verses.hebrew_full: יְהִֽי־מְקוֹרְךָ֥ בָר֑וּךְ וּ֝שְׂמַ֗ח מֵאֵ֥שֶׁת נְעוּרֶֽךָ
verses.translation: Let your fountain be blessed:Find joy in the wife of your youth—
verses.rashi: Your fountain shall be blessed, and you shall rejoice with the wife of your youth That is the Torah which you learned in your youth.

verses.hebrew_full: אַיֶּ֥לֶת אֲהָבִ֗ים וְֽיַ֫עֲלַת־חֵ֥ן דַּ֭דֶּיהָ יְרַוֻּ֣ךָ בְכׇל־עֵ֑ת בְּ֝אַהֲבָתָ֗הּ תִּשְׁגֶּ֥ה תָמִֽיד
verses.translation: A loving doe, a graceful mountain goat.Let her breasts satisfy you at all times;Be infatuated with love of her always.
verses.rashi: you shall always be intoxicated Heb. תשגה. I saw in the words of Rabbi Moshe Hadarshan that תשגה means “you shall always busy yourself,” and it is an Arabic expression. He cited as proof, “to seek shegiah,” meaning to seek business, but I do not know where it is stated. But our Sages explained it as an expression of intoxication (lit. inadvertence), as its apparent meaning. On account of her love, you shall be inadvertent in your other affairs, because she will guard your things. They said concerning Rabbi Eleazar the son of Pedath, that he was preaching in the lower market and his cloak was lying in the upper market. Once a man came and found a snake coiled on it.

words.eng: Elath or Eloth = "grove of lofty trees"

verses.hebrew_full: וְלָ֤מָּה תִשְׁגֶּ֣ה בְנִ֣י בְזָרָ֑ה וּ֝תְחַבֵּ֗ק חֵ֣ק נׇכְרִיָּֽה
verses.translation: Why be infatuated, my son, with a forbidden woman?Why clasp the bosom of an alien woman?

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֤י נֹ֨כַח עֵינֵ֣י יְ֭הֹוָה דַּרְכֵי־אִ֑ישׁ וְֽכׇל־מַעְגְּלֹתָ֥יו מְפַלֵּֽס
verses.translation: For everyone’s ways are before the eyes of God,Who surveys their entire course.
verses.rashi: For man’s ways are opposite the Lord’s eyes, and He weighs, etc. He weighs his ways and knows how many sins and how many merits he has.

verses.hebrew_full: עֲֽווֹנֹתָ֗יו יִלְכְּדֻנ֥וֹ אֶת־הָרָשָׁ֑ע וּבְחַבְלֵ֥י חַ֝טָּאת֗וֹ יִתָּמֵֽךְ
verses.translation: The wicked man will be trapped in his iniquities;He will be caught up in the ropes of his sin.
verses.rashi: His iniquities shall trap Heb. ילכדנו it. shall trap him, like ילכדוהו. and he shall be hanged with the ropes of his sin יתמך, lit. he shall be supported. He shall be hanged, for the one hanged is supported by the ropes with which he is hanged.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·wicked

verses.hebrew_full: ה֗וּא יָ֭מוּת בְּאֵ֣ין מוּסָ֑ר וּבְרֹ֖ב אִוַּלְתּ֣וֹ יִשְׁגֶּֽה
verses.translation: He will die for lack of discipline,Infatuated by his great folly.
verses.rashi: He shall die without discipline Because he did not accept discipline.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּ֭נִי אִם־עָרַ֣בְתָּ לְרֵעֶ֑ךָ תָּקַ֖עְתָּ לַזָּ֣ר כַּפֶּֽיךָ
verses.translation: My son, if you have stood surety for your fellow,Given your hand for another,
verses.rashi: My son, if you have stood surety Our Sages explained this as referring to surety in monetary matters, according to its apparent meaning.

verses.hebrew_full: נוֹקַ֥שְׁתָּ בְאִמְרֵי־פִ֑יךָ נִ֝לְכַּ֗דְתָּ בְּאִמְרֵי־פִֽיךָ
verses.translation: You have been trapped by the words of your mouth,Snared by the words of your mouth.

verses.hebrew_full: עֲשֵׂ֨ה זֹ֥את אֵפ֪וֹא בְּנִ֡י וְֽהִנָּצֵ֗ל כִּ֘י־בָ֤אתָ בְכַף־רֵעֶ֑ךָ לֵ֥ךְ הִ֝תְרַפֵּ֗ס וּרְהַ֥ב רֵעֶֽיךָ
verses.translation: Do this, then, my son, to extricate yourself,For you have come into the power of your fellow:Go grovel—and badger your fellow;
verses.rashi: go, humble yourself Heb. התרפס [a combination of two words, התר פס], open the palm of your hand for him to pay him his money. and give your fellow superiority Heb. ורהב. And if he has no money with you, only that you were trapped with the sayings of your mouth by speaking harshly to him, bring many friends to him to beg him to forgive you. Another explanation: [1] My son, if you stood surety for your Friend After you stood surety for the Holy One, blessed be He, Who is your “friend,” as it is written (Song 5:16): “This is my beloved and this is my friend”; you undertook at Sinai and in the plains of Moab with a curse and with an oath to observe His commandments. have given your hand to a stranger You have repented and turned from His ways and clung to the disbelievers to go in their ways. [2] you have been trapped by the sayings of your mouth You have given your hand to cling to strangers. [3] Do this, then, my son, and be saved Since you have come into the palm of your Friend at Sinai and you have accepted His Godliness over you, go, humble yourself Heb. התרפס. Humble yourself before Him like a threshold, which is trodden (נרפסת) and stepped on. and increase your friends Bring many friends who will pray for you before Him. In this manner it is expounded on in Midrash Psalms.

verses.hebrew_full: אַל־תִּתֵּ֣ן שֵׁנָ֣ה לְעֵינֶ֑יךָ וּ֝תְנוּמָ֗ה לְעַפְעַפֶּֽיךָ
verses.translation: Give your eyes no sleep,Your pupils no slumber.

verses.hebrew_full: הִ֭נָּצֵל כִּצְבִ֣י מִיָּ֑ד וּ֝כְצִפּ֗וֹר מִיַּ֥ד יָקֽוּשׁ
verses.translation: Save yourself like a deer out of the hand [of a hunter],Like a bird out of the hand of a fowler.
verses.rashi: Save yourself like a deer from the hand Hasten and extricate yourself from there like a deer that extricates itself from a man’s hand.

verses.hebrew_full: לֵֽךְ־אֶל־נְמָלָ֥ה עָצֵ֑ל רְאֵ֖ה דְרָכֶ֣יהָ וַחֲכָֽם
verses.translation: Lazybones, go to the ant;Study its ways and learn.
verses.rashi: Go to the ant, you sluggard...and become wise Heb. וחכם, and wisen yourself.

verses.hebrew_full: אֲשֶׁ֖ר אֵֽין־לָ֥הּ קָצִ֗ין שֹׁטֵ֥ר וּמֹשֵֽׁל
verses.translation: Without leaders, officers, or rulers,
verses.rashi: for she has no chief who would reprove her and alert her and take out of her hand if she steals anything from her companion, nevertheless...

verses.hebrew_full: תָּכִ֣ין בַּקַּ֣יִץ לַחְמָ֑הּ אָגְרָ֥ה בַ֝קָּצִ֗יר מַאֲכָלָֽהּ
verses.translation: It lays up its stores during the summer,Gathers in its food at the harvest.
verses.rashi: yet she prepares her bread in the summer, she gathers her food in the harvest She gathers her food, each one of them, and does not rob her companion.

verses.hebrew_full: עַד־מָתַ֖י עָצֵ֥ל תִּשְׁכָּ֑ב מָ֝תַ֗י תָּק֥וּם מִשְּׁנָתֶֽךָ
verses.translation: How long will you lie there, lazybones;When will you wake from your sleep?

verses.hebrew_full: מְעַ֣ט שֵׁ֭נוֹת מְעַ֣ט תְּנוּמ֑וֹת מְעַ֓ט חִבֻּ֖ק יָדַ֣יִם לִשְׁכָּֽב
verses.translation: A bit more sleep, a bit more slumber,A bit more hugging yourself in bed,
verses.rashi: a little folding of the hands The sleeper folds his hands, embrasser in French (abrasar in Provencal,) umfassen [in German], to embrace. In Rashi ms. plier in French [to fold] in German et was falten, to fold a little.

verses.hebrew_full: וּבָֽא־כִמְהַלֵּ֥ךְ רֵאשֶׁ֑ךָ וּ֝מַחְסֹרְךָ֗ כְּאִ֣ישׁ מָגֵֽן
verses.translation: And poverty will come calling upon you, And want, like a man with a shield.
verses.rashi: And your poverty shall come like a fast walker If you do this [keep sleeping], your loss and the thing from which you are impoverished shall come to you immediately, like a man walking fast, and your want will come and fill up like an armed man. who comes quickly to protect his master. These verses are mainly an allegory concerning those who are [too] lazy to engage in the Torah.

verses.hebrew_full: אָדָ֣ם בְּ֭לִיַּעַל אִ֣ישׁ אָ֑וֶן ה֝וֹלֵ֗ךְ עִקְּשׁ֥וּת פֶּֽה
verses.translation: A scoundrel, an evil manLives by crooked speech,
verses.rashi: walks with a crooked mouth He walks with crooked lips.

verses.hebrew_full: קֹרֵ֣ץ בְּ֭עֵינָו מֹלֵ֣ל בְּרַגְלָ֑ו מֹ֝רֶ֗ה בְּאֶצְבְּעֹתָֽיו
verses.translation: Winking his eyes,Shuffling his feet,Pointing his finger.
verses.rashi: he winks with his eyes winks of deceit. shuffles with his feet He rubs one on the other. points with his fingers They are all expressions of hinting: one applies to the eye, one applies to the foot, and one applies to the finger, but the main idea is that it is speaking of the wicked.

verses.hebrew_full: תַּ֥הְפֻּכ֨וֹת בְּלִבּ֗וֹ חֹרֵ֣שׁ רָ֣ע בְּכׇל־עֵ֑ת (מדנים) מִדְיָנִ֥ים יְשַׁלֵּֽחַ
verses.translation: Duplicity is in his heart;He plots evil all the time;He incites quarrels.
verses.rashi: he incites quarrels between man and his Creator.

verses.hebrew_full: עַל־כֵּ֗ן פִּ֭תְאֹם יָב֣וֹא אֵיד֑וֹ פֶּ֥תַע יִ֝שָּׁבֵ֗ר וְאֵ֣ין מַרְפֵּֽא
verses.translation: Therefore calamity will come upon him without warning;Suddenly he will be broken beyond repair.
verses.rashi: suddenly, etc. suddenly Heb. פתע פתאם. This is an expression of immediacy; he will not be aware of the downfall that is ready to descend upon him.

verses.hebrew_full: שֶׁשׁ־הֵ֭נָּה שָׂנֵ֣א יְהֹוָ֑ה וְ֝שֶׁ֗בַע (תועבות) תּוֹעֲבַ֥ת נַפְשֽׁוֹ
verses.translation: GOD hates six things—And abominates seven:
verses.rashi: There are six things that the Lord hates, and the seventh is an abomination of His soul The seventh, too, is [included] with them.

verses.hebrew_full: עֵינַ֣יִם רָ֭מוֹת לְשׁ֣וֹן שָׁ֑קֶר וְ֝יָדַ֗יִם שֹׁפְכ֥וֹת דָּם־נָקִֽי
verses.translation: A haughty bearing,A lying tongue,Hands that shed innocent blood,

verses.hebrew_full: לֵ֗ב חֹ֭רֵשׁ מַחְשְׁב֣וֹת אָ֑וֶן רַגְלַ֥יִם מְ֝מַהֲר֗וֹת לָר֥וּץ לָרָעָֽה
verses.translation: A mind that hatches evil plots,Feet quick to run to evil,

verses.hebrew_full: יָפִ֣יחַ כְּ֭זָבִים עֵ֣ד שָׁ֑קֶר וּמְשַׁלֵּ֥חַ מְ֝דָנִ֗ים בֵּ֣ין אַחִֽים
verses.translation: A false witness testifying lies,And one who incites siblings to quarrel.
verses.rashi: [one who] speaks Heb. יפיח, lit. he blows. This is an expression of speech since all speech is a product of the breath of the mouth.

verses.hebrew_full: נְצֹ֣ר בְּ֭נִי מִצְוַ֣ת אָבִ֑יךָ וְאַל־תִּ֝טֹּ֗שׁ תּוֹרַ֥ת אִמֶּֽךָ
verses.translation: My son, keep your father’s commandment;Do not forsake your mother’s teaching.

verses.hebrew_full: קׇשְׁרֵ֣ם עַל־לִבְּךָ֣ תָמִ֑יד עׇ֝נְדֵ֗ם עַל־גַּרְגְּרֹתֶֽךָ
verses.translation: Tie them over your heart always;Bind them around your throat.
verses.rashi: tie them Heb. ענדם, an expression of tying, as in (Job 31:36) “I will tie it (אענדנו) as crowns to me.”

verses.hebrew_full: בְּהִתְהַלֶּכְךָ֨ תַּנְחֶ֬ה אֹתָ֗ךְ בְּֽ֭שׇׁכְבְּךָ תִּשְׁמֹ֣ר עָלֶ֑יךָ וַ֝הֲקִיצ֗וֹתָ הִ֣יא תְשִׂיחֶֽךָ
verses.translation: When you walk it will lead you;When you lie down it will watch over you;And when you are awake it will talk with you.
verses.rashi: When you walk in your lifetime. it shall lead you Heb. תנחה אותך, like תנהיגך. when you lie down in the grave. and when you awaken for the resurrection of the dead, to stand in judgment. it shall speak for you It shall speak on your behalf.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֤י נֵ֣ר מִ֭צְוָה וְת֣וֹרָה א֑וֹר וְדֶ֥רֶךְ חַ֝יִּ֗ים תּוֹכְח֥וֹת מוּסָֽר
verses.translation: For the commandment is a lamp,The teaching is a light,And the way to life is the rebuke that disciplines.
verses.rashi: For a commandment is a candle, and the Torah is light Just as light always illuminates, so does the merit of the Torah stand for a person forever, but the merit of the commandment is only for a limited time, like the light of a candle. Another explanation: For a commandment is a candle, etc. The command of the father is a candle. Whoever fulfills the command of his father is as though he takes a candle in his hand to light a dark place, and if he loses anything there he finds it by its light; similarly, whoever fulfills his mother’s instruction—it is light to him, and so [Scripture] states (verse 20): “and forsake not the instruction of your mother.” It is certain that this verse speaks of the commands of one’s father and mother, for it is written (20:20): “If one curses his father or mother, his candle will be put out in the blackest darkness.” Now if a person’s candle goes out when he does not fulfill the commandments of his parents, conversely when he fulfills the command, his candle lights up (Rabbi Joseph Kara). and disciplining rebukes are the way of life Disciplining rebukes incline a person to life. It is found that they are the way of life.

verses.hebrew_full: לִ֭שְׁמׇרְךָ מֵאֵ֣שֶׁת רָ֑ע מֵ֝חֶלְקַ֗ת לָשׁ֥וֹן נׇכְרִיָּֽה
verses.translation: It will keep you from an evil woman,From the smooth tongue of a forbidden woman.
verses.rashi: to guard you from an evil woman The Torah shall guard you from a woman of evil behavior—perforce, Solomon did not speak of an evil woman, but concerning idolatry, [the prohibition of] which is equal in gravity to all [commandments], for if you say that he meant a real harlot, is that all the reward and the praise of the Torah that it guards one only from a harlot? Rather, perforce this is idolatry, [the prohibition of] which is as stringent as all other [commandments]. from the smoothness of the alien tongue From the sealing of the eyes by an alien tongue.

verses.hebrew_full: אַל־תַּחְמֹ֣ד יׇ֭פְיָהּ בִּלְבָבֶ֑ךָ וְאַל־תִּ֝קָּחֲךָ֗ בְּעַפְעַפֶּֽיהָ
verses.translation: Do not lust for her beautyOr let her captivate you with her eyes.
verses.rashi: do not let her captivate you And let her not take away your wisdom from you with her eyelids that she winks at you.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֤י בְעַד־אִשָּׁ֥ה זוֹנָ֗ה עַֽד־כִּכַּ֫ר־לָ֥חֶם וְאֵ֥שֶׁת אִ֑ישׁ נֶ֖פֶשׁ יְקָרָ֣ה תָצֽוּד
verses.translation: The last loaf of bread will go for a prostitute;A married woman will snare a person of honor.
verses.rashi: Because a man is brought to a loaf of bread for a harlot He is reduced to poverty and the lack of all good things.

verses.hebrew_full: הֲיַחְתֶּ֤ה אִ֓ישׁ אֵ֬שׁ בְּחֵיק֑וֹ וּ֝בְגָדָ֗יו לֹ֣א תִשָּׂרַֽפְנָה
verses.translation: Can anyone rake embers into their bosomWithout burning their clothes?
verses.rashi: Can a man rake Can a man pick up live coals with the skirt of his garments without burning them? Every expression of חתיה is an expression of raking according to its apparent meaning, that he fills the vessel in the fire.

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־יְהַלֵּ֣ךְ אִ֭ישׁ עַל־הַגֶּחָלִ֑ים וְ֝רַגְלָ֗יו לֹ֣א תִכָּוֶֽינָה
verses.translation: Can anyone walk on live coalsWithout scorching their feet?

verses.hebrew_full: כֵּ֗ן הַ֭בָּא אֶל־אֵ֣שֶׁת רֵעֵ֑הוּ לֹ֥א יִ֝נָּקֶ֗ה כׇּֽל־הַנֹּגֵ֥עַ בָּֽהּ
verses.translation: It is the same with the man who sleeps with his fellow’s wife;No one who touches her will go unpunished.
verses.rashi: to his neighbor’s wife According to its apparent meaning, and it can also be expounded upon as referring to the idolatry of the pagans.

verses.hebrew_full: לֹא־יָב֣וּזוּ לַ֭גַּנָּב כִּ֣י יִגְנ֑וֹב לְמַלֵּ֥א נַ֝פְשׁ֗וֹ כִּ֣י יִרְעָֽב
verses.translation: A thief is not held in contemptFor stealing to appease his hunger;
verses.rashi: They will not despise a thief, etc. And if he is found, he will pay sevenfold, etc. One who commits adultery with a woman The three of them are combined in one statement. If a thief commits a theft, he should not be despised as much as the adulterer. Why? Because he steals in order to sate his hunger, and perhaps he has nothing to eat. And, when he is found, he can rectify his sin with payment; at the most, he will pay sevenfold i.e., many times the double payment and even fifty times for one. Some explain “sevenfold” as referring to one who steals an ox and his trappings and slaughters it, who pays five cattle and double payment of the trappings (totalling seven). he must give all he owns lit. he must give all the property of his house. And, even if he must sell all he owns because of this, he can nevertheless rectify it, and from the beginning he did it because of hunger. But...

verses.hebrew_full: וְ֭נִמְצָא יְשַׁלֵּ֣ם שִׁבְעָתָ֑יִם אֶת־כׇּל־ה֖וֹן בֵּית֣וֹ יִתֵּֽן
verses.translation: Yet if caught he must pay sevenfold;He must give up all he owns.

words.eng: to be in a covenant of peace
sign of the definite direct object·all·n m

verses.hebrew_full: נֹאֵ֣ף אִשָּׁ֣ה חֲסַר־לֵ֑ב מַֽשְׁחִ֥ית נַ֝פְשׁ֗וֹ ה֣וּא יַעֲשֶֽׂנָּה
verses.translation: He who commits adultery is devoid of sense;Only one who would destroy himself does such a thing.
verses.rashi: One who commits adultery with a woman is devoid of sense since he does not do it because of hunger. one who would destroy his soul—he will do it The lewd act.

verses.hebrew_full: נֶגַע־וְקָל֥וֹן יִמְצָ֑א וְ֝חֶרְפָּת֗וֹ לֹ֣א תִמָּחֶֽה
verses.translation: He will meet with disease and disgrace;His reproach will never be expunged.
verses.rashi: He will find wounds and disgrace Lesions are sent for idolatry and immorality.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־קִנְאָ֥ה חֲמַת־גָּ֑בֶר וְלֹא־יַ֝חְמ֗וֹל בְּי֣וֹם נָקָֽם
verses.translation: The fury of the husband will be passionate;He will show no pity on his day of vengeance.
verses.rashi: for jealousy For the jealousy of the Holy One, blessed be He, Who is the Mighty One over all, will be aroused to mete out retribution upon him, and He will not have pity on the day of vengeance.

verses.hebrew_full: לֹֽא־יִ֭שָּׂא פְּנֵ֣י כׇל־כֹּ֑פֶר וְלֹא־יֹ֝אבֶ֗ה כִּ֣י תַרְבֶּה־שֹּֽׁחַד
verses.translation: He will not have regard for any ransom;He will refuse your bribe, however great.
verses.rashi: He will not have regard for any money to expiate for his denial of Him and his cleaving to idolatry. And our Rabbis expounded (Tosefta Baba Kamma 7:3): They will not despise a thief—This is one who steals away from his friend and goes to the study hall and engages in Torah. If he is found, he will pay sevenfold. [The intention is that] eventually, he will be appointed as a judge, and he will render legal decisions, for “sevenfold” refers only to the Torah, as it is said (Ps. 12:7): “refined sevenfold.”

verses.hebrew_full: בְּ֭נִי שְׁמֹ֣ר אֲמָרָ֑י וּ֝מִצְוֺתַ֗י תִּצְפֹּ֥ן אִתָּֽךְ
verses.translation: My son, heed my words;And store up my commandments with you.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object

verses.hebrew_full: שְׁמֹ֣ר מִצְוֺתַ֣י וֶחְיֵ֑ה וְ֝תוֹרָתִ֗י כְּאִישׁ֥וֹן עֵינֶֽיךָ
verses.translation: Keep my commandments and live,My teaching, as the apple of your eye—
verses.rashi: like the apple of your eyes The pupil of the eye, which is like darkness, like the darkness of night.

verses.hebrew_full: קׇשְׁרֵ֥ם עַל־אֶצְבְּעֹתֶ֑יךָ כׇּ֝תְבֵ֗ם עַל־ל֥וּחַ לִבֶּֽךָ
verses.translation: Bind them on your fingers,Write them on the tablet of your mind.

verses.hebrew_full: אֱמֹ֣ר לַ֭חׇכְמָה אֲחֹ֣תִי אָ֑תְּ וּ֝מֹדָ֗ע לַבִּינָ֥ה תִקְרָֽא
verses.translation: Say to Wisdom, “You are my sister,” And call Understanding a kinswoman.
verses.rashi: “You are my sister” (Draw her near to you.) a kinsman Heb. מדע, a kinsman, as in (Ruth 3: 2): “Boaz our kinsman (מדעתנו),” our close relative; i.e., draw her near to you always.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object

verses.hebrew_full: לִ֭שְׁמׇרְךָ מֵאִשָּׁ֣ה זָרָ֑ה מִ֝נׇּכְרִיָּ֗ה אֲמָרֶ֥יהָ הֶחֱלִֽיקָה
verses.translation: She will guard you from a forbidden woman;From an alien woman whose talk is smooth.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֭י בְּחַלּ֣וֹן בֵּיתִ֑י בְּעַ֖ד אֶשְׁנַבִּ֣י נִשְׁקָֽפְתִּי
verses.translation: From the window of my house,Through my lattice, I looked out

verses.hebrew_full: וָאֵ֤רֶא בַפְּתָאיִ֗ם אָ֘בִ֤ינָה בַבָּנִ֗ים נַ֣עַר חֲסַר־לֵֽב
verses.translation: And saw among the simple,Noticed among the youths,A young man devoid of sense.
verses.rashi: I discerned Heb. אבינה, I discerned and I saw.

verses.hebrew_full: עֹבֵ֣ר בַּ֭שּׁוּק אֵ֣צֶל פִּנָּ֑הּ וְדֶ֖רֶךְ בֵּיתָ֣הּ יִצְעָֽד
verses.translation: He was crossing the street near her corner,Walking toward her house
verses.rashi: next to her corner The corner of the harlot and of the pagan house of worship.

words.eng: to pass over or by or through

verses.hebrew_full: בְּנֶֽשֶׁף־בְּעֶ֥רֶב י֑וֹם בְּאִישׁ֥וֹן לַ֝֗יְלָה וַאֲפֵלָֽה
verses.translation: In the dusk of evening,In the dark hours of night.

verses.hebrew_full: וְהִנֵּ֣ה אִ֭שָּׁה לִקְרָאת֑וֹ שִׁ֥ית ז֝וֹנָ֗ה וּנְצֻ֥רַת לֵֽב
verses.translation: A woman comes toward himDressed like a streetwalker, with set purpose.
verses.rashi: And behold a woman As its apparent meaning. Another explanation: One of the enticers. the nakedness of a harlot Heb. שית, as in (II Sam. 10:4): “their buttocks (שתותיהם),” i.e., the nakedness of a harlot. with her heart besieged Heb. ונצרת לב. As a besieged city is surrounded by bulwarks, so is this one’s heart surrounded by lewdness and foolishness.

verses.hebrew_full: הֹמִיָּ֣ה הִ֣יא וְסֹרָ֑רֶת בְּ֝בֵיתָ֗הּ לֹא־יִשְׁכְּנ֥וּ רַגְלֶֽיהָ
verses.translation: She is bustling and restive;She is never at home.
verses.rashi: and rebellious Heb. וסררת, turning away from the road.

verses.hebrew_full: פַּ֤עַם בַּח֗וּץ פַּ֥עַם בָּרְחֹב֑וֹת וְאֵ֖צֶל כׇּל־פִּנָּ֣ה תֶאֱרֹֽב
verses.translation: Now in the street, now in the square,She lurks at every corner.

words.eng: broad or open place or plaza

verses.hebrew_full: וְהֶחֱזִ֣יקָה בּ֭וֹ וְנָ֣שְׁקָה לּ֑וֹ הֵעֵ֥זָה פָ֝נֶ֗יהָ וַתֹּ֣אמַר לֽוֹ
verses.translation: She lays hold of him and kisses him.Brazenly she says to him,

words.eng: inflected third pers. singular m. of בּֽ◌ ᴵ

verses.hebrew_full: זִבְחֵ֣י שְׁלָמִ֣ים עָלָ֑י הַ֝יּ֗וֹם שִׁלַּ֥מְתִּי נְדָרָֽי
verses.translation: “I had to make a sacrifice of well-being;Today I fulfilled my vows.
verses.rashi: I had to bring peace offerings I prepared a great feast, for today I sacrificed my vows and my peace offering.

words.eng: to be in a covenant of peace

verses.hebrew_full: עַל־כֵּ֭ן יָצָ֣אתִי לִקְרָאתֶ֑ךָ לְשַׁחֵ֥ר פָּ֝נֶ֗יךָ וָאֶמְצָאֶֽךָּ
verses.translation: Therefore I have come out to you,Seeking you, and have found you.
verses.rashi: and I have found you In order that I find you.

verses.hebrew_full: מַ֭רְבַדִּים רָבַ֣דְתִּי עַרְשִׂ֑י חֲ֝טֻב֗וֹת אֵט֥וּן מִצְרָֽיִם
verses.translation: I have decked my couch with coversOf dyed Egyptian linen;
verses.rashi: covers Heb. מרבדים. Garments of freedom and beauty; a similar term is found at the end of the book (31:22): “She made covers for herself.” I have bedecked my couch Heb. רבדתי, I have adorned. with superior braided work of Egypt Heb. חטבות אטון מצרים, praiseworthy, high quality linen garments coming from Egypt, where linen is common, as it is written in the Book of Isaiah (19: 9): “And those who work at flax... shall be ashamed.” braided work Heb. אטון. The Aramaic translation of מיתריהם, their ropes (Num. 4:32), is אטוניהון.

verses.hebrew_full: נַ֥פְתִּי מִשְׁכָּבִ֑י מֹ֥ר אֲ֝הָלִ֗ים וְקִנָּמֽוֹן
verses.translation: I have sprinkled my bedWith myrrh, aloes, and cinnamon.
verses.rashi: I fanned Heb. נפתי. I fanned the scent as one fans with a scarf in a perfumery to bring the scent from above down below. Dunash (Teshuvoth Dunash p. 22) defines it as an expression of smoking, which he states has no comparison.

verses.hebrew_full: לְכָ֤ה נִרְוֶ֣ה דֹ֭דִים עַד־הַבֹּ֑קֶר נִ֝תְעַלְּסָ֗ה בׇּאֳהָבִֽים
verses.translation: Let us drink our fill of love till morning;Let us delight in amorous embrace.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֤י אֵ֣ין הָאִ֣ישׁ בְּבֵית֑וֹ הָ֝לַ֗ךְ בְּדֶ֣רֶךְ מֵרָחֽוֹק
verses.translation: For the man of the house is away; He is off on a distant journey.
verses.rashi: For the man is not at home You have seen that the Holy One, blessed be He, has removed His Shechinah and has given all good to the pagans.

verses.hebrew_full: צְֽרוֹר־הַ֭כֶּסֶף לָקַ֣ח בְּיָד֑וֹ לְי֥וֹם הַ֝כֵּ֗סֶא יָבֹ֥א בֵיתֽוֹ
verses.translation: He took his bag of money with himAnd will return only at mid-month.”
verses.rashi: the bag of money He has slain the righteous among them. on the appointed day Heb. ליום הכסא. At the fixed appointed time, and similarly (Ps. 81:4), “At the appointed time for the day of our festival.”

verses.hebrew_full: הִ֭טַּתּוּ בְּרֹ֣ב לִקְחָ֑הּ בְּחֵ֥לֶק שְׂ֝פָתֶ֗יהָ תַּדִּיחֶֽנּוּ
verses.translation: She sways him with her eloquence,Turns him aside with her smooth talk.
verses.rashi: She swayed him the one devoid of sense, to her. with all her talk with which she is accustomed to familiarize men. she entices him from the road.

verses.hebrew_full: ה֤וֹלֵ֥ךְ אַחֲרֶ֗יהָ פִּ֫תְאֹ֥ם כְּ֭שׁוֹר אֶל־טֶ֣בַח יָבֹ֑א וּ֝כְעֶ֗כֶס אֶל־מוּסַ֥ר אֱוִֽיל
verses.translation: Thoughtlessly he follows her,Like an ox going to the slaughter,Like a fool to the stocks for punishment—
verses.rashi: and as a viper Heb. וכעכס. This is the venom of a snake. to the chastisement of a fool Like a snake that runs quickly as an agent of the Holy One, blessed be He, to chastise the fool who is condemned by the Omnipresent, blessed be He, so does this one run after her until he stumbles on her, and her arrow splits his liver.

verses.hebrew_full: עַ֤ד יְפַלַּ֪ח חֵ֡ץ כְּֽבֵד֗וֹ כְּמַהֵ֣ר צִפּ֣וֹר אֶל־פָּ֑ח וְלֹא־יָ֝דַ֗ע כִּֽי־בְנַפְשׁ֥וֹ הֽוּא
verses.translation: Until the arrow pierces his liver.He is like a bird rushing into a trap,Not knowing his life is at stake.
verses.rashi: as a bird hastens to run to a snare, and it does not know that the snare was spread out there for the life of the bird.

verses.hebrew_full: וְעַתָּ֣ה בָ֭נִים שִׁמְעוּ־לִ֑י וְ֝הַקְשִׁ֗יבוּ לְאִמְרֵי־פִֽי
verses.translation: Now, sons, listen to me;Pay attention to my words;

verses.hebrew_full: אַל־יֵ֣שְׂטְ אֶל־דְּרָכֶ֣יהָ לִבֶּ֑ךָ אַל־תֵּ֝֗תַע בִּנְתִיבוֹתֶֽיהָ
verses.translation: Let your mind not wander down her ways;Do not stray onto her paths.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־רַבִּ֣ים חֲלָלִ֣ים הִפִּ֑ילָה וַ֝עֲצֻמִ֗ים כׇּל־הֲרֻגֶֽיהָ
verses.translation: For many are those she has struck dead,And numerous are her victims.

verses.hebrew_full: דַּרְכֵ֣י שְׁא֣וֹל בֵּיתָ֑הּ יֹ֝רְד֗וֹת אֶל־חַדְרֵי־מָֽוֶת
verses.translation: Her house is a highway to SheolLeading down to Death’s inner chambers.

verses.hebrew_full: הֲלֹֽא־חׇכְמָ֥ה תִקְרָ֑א וּ֝תְבוּנָ֗ה תִּתֵּ֥ן קוֹלָֽהּ
verses.translation: It is Wisdom calling,Understanding raising her voice.
verses.rashi: Will not wisdom call out Does not the Torah announce for you the things mentioned below in this section?

verses.hebrew_full: בְּרֹאשׁ־מְרֹמִ֥ים עֲלֵי־דָ֑רֶךְ בֵּ֖ית נְתִיב֣וֹת נִצָּֽבָה
verses.translation: She takes her stand at the topmost heights,By the wayside, at the crossroads,

verses.hebrew_full: לְיַד־שְׁעָרִ֥ים לְפִי־קָ֑רֶת מְב֖וֹא פְתָחִ֣ים תָּרֹֽנָּה
verses.translation: Near the gates at the city entrance;At the entryways, she shouts,
verses.rashi: at the entrance of the roof Heb. קרת, the ceiling above the gate, where people sit. she cries Heb. תרנה, she cries, and what does she say? “To you, O men, I call.”

verses.hebrew_full: אֲלֵיכֶ֣ם אִישִׁ֣ים אֶקְרָ֑א וְ֝קוֹלִ֗י אֶל־בְּנֵ֥י אָדָֽם
verses.translation: “O human race, I call to you;My cry is to all mortals.

verses.hebrew_full: הָבִ֣ינוּ פְתָאיִ֣ם עׇרְמָ֑ה וּ֝כְסִילִ֗ים הָבִ֥ינוּ לֵֽב
verses.translation: O simple ones, learn shrewdness;O dullards, instruct your minds.

verses.hebrew_full: שִׁ֭מְעוּ כִּֽי־נְגִידִ֣ים אֲדַבֵּ֑ר וּמִפְתַּ֥ח שְׂ֝פָתַ֗י מֵישָׁרִֽים
verses.translation: Listen, for I speak noble things;Uprightness comes from my lips.
verses.rashi: Hearken for I will speak noble things Words of nobility and importance.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־אֱ֭מֶת יֶהְגֶּ֣ה חִכִּ֑י וְתוֹעֲבַ֖ת שְׂפָתַ֣י רֶֽשַׁע
verses.translation: My mouth utters truth;Wickedness is abhorrent to my lips.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּצֶ֥דֶק כׇּל־אִמְרֵי־פִ֑י אֵ֥ין בָּ֝הֶ֗ם נִפְתָּ֥ל וְעִקֵּֽשׁ
verses.translation: All my words are just,None of them perverse or crooked;
verses.rashi: there is nothing twisted or crooked in them There is no crookedness.

verses.hebrew_full: כֻּלָּ֣ם נְ֭כֹחִים לַמֵּבִ֑ין וִ֝ישָׁרִ֗ים לְמֹ֣צְאֵי דָֽעַת
verses.translation: All are straightforward to anyone with intelligence,And right to those who have attained knowledge.

words.eng: act or place of going out or forth

verses.hebrew_full: קְחֽוּ־מוּסָרִ֥י וְאַל־כָּ֑סֶף וְ֝דַ֗עַת מֵחָר֥וּץ נִבְחָֽר
verses.translation: Accept my discipline rather than silver,Knowledge rather than choice gold.
verses.rashi: gold Heb. חרוץ. A type of gold.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־טוֹבָ֣ה חׇ֭כְמָה מִפְּנִינִ֑ים וְכׇל־חֲ֝פָצִ֗ים לֹ֣א יִֽשְׁווּ־בָֽהּ
verses.translation: For wisdom is better than rubies;No goods can equal her.
verses.rashi: than pearls Heb. מפנינים, pearls. cannot be compared to it They will not equal its value.

verses.hebrew_full: אֲֽנִי־חׇ֭כְמָה שָׁכַ֣נְתִּי עׇרְמָ֑ה וְדַ֖עַת מְזִמּ֣וֹת אֶמְצָֽא
verses.translation: “I, Wisdom, live with Prudence;I attain knowledge and foresight.
verses.rashi: I dwelt [beside] cunning Beside cunning, for since a man has learned Torah, cunning about every matter enters into him.

words.eng: I (first pers. sing. -usually used for emphasis)·to be wise

verses.hebrew_full: יִ֥רְאַ֣ת יְהֹוָה֮ שְֽׂנֹ֫את־רָ֥ע גֵּ֘אָ֤ה וְגָא֨וֹן וְדֶ֣רֶךְ רָ֭ע וּפִ֨י תַהְפֻּכ֬וֹת שָׂנֵֽאתִי
verses.translation: To fear GOD is to hate evil;I hate pride, arrogance, the evil way,And duplicity in speech.
verses.rashi: Fear of the Lord is to hate evil This is the discipline that wisdom announces to the people.

verses.hebrew_full: לִֽי־עֵ֭צָה וְתוּשִׁיָּ֑ה אֲנִ֥י בִ֝ינָ֗ה לִ֣י גְבוּרָֽה
verses.translation: Mine are counsel and resourcefulness;I am understanding; courage is mine.

words.eng: I (first pers. sing. -usually used for emphasis)

verses.hebrew_full: בִּ֭י מְלָכִ֣ים יִמְלֹ֑כוּ וְ֝רֹזְנִ֗ים יְחֹ֣קְקוּ צֶֽדֶק
verses.translation: Through me kings reignAnd rulers decree just laws;
verses.rashi: Kings reign with me for the judges and the judgments I teach them.

words.eng: to be or become king or queen

verses.hebrew_full: בִּ֭י שָׂרִ֣ים יָשֹׂ֑רוּ וּ֝נְדִיבִ֗ים כׇּל־שֹׁ֥פְטֵי צֶֽדֶק
verses.translation: Through me princes rule,Great men and all the righteous judges.

verses.hebrew_full: אֲ֭נִי (אהביה) אֹהֲבַ֣י אֵהָ֑ב וּ֝מְשַׁחֲרַ֗י יִמְצָאֻֽנְנִי
verses.translation: Those who love me I love,And those who seek me will find me.
verses.rashi: I will love those who love me Heb. אהבי. [This is the reading.] The text is written: אוהביה, those who love her. Said the Holy One, blessed be He: “I will love those who love the Torah.” I heard this from Rabbi Aaron in the name of Rabbi Nathan. I will love Heb. אהב, like אאהב. will find me Heb. ימצאנני. There is an extra “nun,” denoting fifty. I will allow him to find the fifty gates of understanding.

words.eng: I (first pers. sing. -usually used for emphasis)

verses.hebrew_full: עֹֽשֶׁר־וְכָב֥וֹד אִתִּ֑י ה֥וֹן עָ֝תֵ֗ק וּצְדָקָֽה
verses.translation: Riches and honor belong to me,Enduring wealth and success.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object

verses.hebrew_full: ט֣וֹב פִּ֭רְיִי מֵחָר֣וּץ וּמִפָּ֑ז וּ֝תְבוּאָתִ֗י מִכֶּ֥סֶף נִבְחָֽר
verses.translation: My fruit is better than gold, fine gold,And my produce better than choice silver.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּאֹֽרַח־צְדָקָ֥ה אֲהַלֵּ֑ךְ בְּ֝ת֗וֹךְ נְתִיב֥וֹת מִשְׁפָּֽט
verses.translation: I walk on the way of righteousness,On the paths of justice.

verses.hebrew_full: לְהַנְחִ֖יל אֹהֲבַ֥י יֵ֑שׁ וְאֹצְרֹ֖תֵיהֶ֣ם אֲמַלֵּֽא
verses.translation: I endow those who love me with substance;I will fill their treasuries.
verses.rashi: There is substance to give inheritance Heb. יש, lit. there is. There is with me a great inheritance.

verses.hebrew_full: יְֽהֹוָ֗ה קָ֭נָנִי רֵאשִׁ֣ית דַּרְכּ֑וֹ קֶ֖דֶם מִפְעָלָ֣יו מֵאָֽז
verses.translation: “I was created at the beginning of GOD’s courseAs the first of the works of old.
verses.rashi: at the beginning of His way before the creation of the world.

verses.hebrew_full: מֵ֭עוֹלָם נִסַּ֥כְתִּי מֵרֹ֗אשׁ מִקַּדְמֵי־אָֽרֶץ
verses.translation: In the distant past I was fashioned,At the beginning, at the origin of earth.
