verses.rashi: I was enthroned, from the beginning Heb. נסכתי, an expression of (Ezek. 35): “Princes (נסיכי) of the sons of man.”

verses.hebrew_full: בְּאֵין־תְּהֹמ֥וֹת חוֹלָ֑לְתִּי בְּאֵ֥ין מַ֝עְיָנ֗וֹת נִכְבַּדֵּי־מָֽיִם
verses.translation: There was still no deep when I was brought forth,No springs rich in water;
verses.rashi: I was created Heb. חוללתי.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּטֶ֣רֶם הָרִ֣ים הׇטְבָּ֑עוּ לִפְנֵ֖י גְבָע֣וֹת חוֹלָֽלְתִּי
verses.translation: Before [the foundations of] the mountains were sunk,Before the hills I was born.
verses.rashi: before the mountains were sunk within the water.

verses.hebrew_full: עַד־לֹ֣א עָ֭שָׂה אֶ֣רֶץ וְחוּצ֑וֹת וְ֝רֹ֗אשׁ עַפְר֥וֹת תֵּבֵֽל
verses.translation: Earth and fields had not yet been made,Or the world’s first clumps of clay.
verses.rashi: the land and the outsides The land of Israel and other lands. and the beginning of the dust of the earth The first man.

verses.hebrew_full: בַּהֲכִינ֣וֹ שָׁ֭מַיִם שָׁ֣ם אָ֑נִי בְּחֻ֥קוֹ ח֝֗וּג עַל־פְּנֵ֥י תְהֽוֹם
verses.translation: I was there when the heavens were set into place;When the horizon was fixed upon the deep;
verses.rashi: when He drew a circle over the face of the deep When He drew the circle of the earth over the water, to draw a boundary that it may not pass. חוג is an expression of encircling, as in (Isa. 44: 13): “and with a compass (ובמחוגה) he rounds it.” (Compas in French, zirkel in German, as in Gen. 29: 17, Isa. 5:22, 44:13, and Job 22: 14.)

words.eng: I (first pers. sing. -usually used for emphasis)

verses.hebrew_full: בְּאַמְּצ֣וֹ שְׁחָקִ֣ים מִמָּ֑עַל בַּ֝עֲז֗וֹז עִינ֥וֹת תְּהֽוֹם
verses.translation: When the heavens above were made firm,And the fountains of the deep gushed forth;
verses.rashi: when He strengthened the fountains of the deep When He made mighty the fountains of the deep.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּשׂ֘וּמ֤וֹ לַיָּ֨ם חֻקּ֗וֹ וּ֭מַיִם לֹ֣א יַֽעַבְרוּ־פִ֑יו בְּ֝חוּק֗וֹ מ֣וֹסְדֵי אָֽרֶץ
verses.translation: When the sea was assigned its limits,So that its waters never transgress God’s command;When the foundations of the earth were fixed,
verses.rashi: when He gave the sea its boundary and decreed upon the Reed Sea when he created it, on the condition that it split before Moses. when He established the foundations of the earth Heb. בחקו from an expression of engraving (חקוי), as in (Isa. 49:16): “Behold on [My] hands I have engraved you (חקתיך),” and so (Ezek. 43:14): “And from the bottom (מחיק) upon the ground.”

words.eng: to pass over or by or through·peh

verses.hebrew_full: וָאֶהְיֶ֥ה אֶצְל֗וֹ אָ֫מ֥וֹן וָאֶהְיֶ֣ה שַׁ֭עֲשׁוּעִים י֤וֹם י֑וֹם מְשַׂחֶ֖קֶת לְפָנָ֣יו בְּכׇל־עֵֽת
verses.translation: I was with [God] as a confidant, A source of delight every day,Rejoicing before [God] at all times,
verses.rashi: a nursling Heb. אמון, one that was reared beside Him, an expression of (Lam. 4:5): “They that were reared (האמנים) amid crimson. every day יום יום, lit. a day a day, two thousand years.

verses.hebrew_full: מְ֭שַׂחֶקֶת בְּתֵבֵ֣ל אַרְצ֑וֹ וְ֝שַׁעֲשֻׁעַ֗י אֶת־בְּנֵ֥י אָדָֽם
verses.translation: Rejoicing in God’s inhabited world,Finding delight with mortals.
verses.rashi: playing in the habitable world of His earth All the generations of the wicked that were from Adam to Noah and from Noah to Abraham, I was laughing at them. and [having] my delights I waited until the generation of the desert came and accepted me.

verses.hebrew_full: וְעַתָּ֣ה בָ֭נִים שִׁמְעוּ־לִ֑י וְ֝אַשְׁרֵ֗י דְּרָכַ֥י יִשְׁמֹֽרוּ
verses.translation: Now, children, listen to me;Happy are they who keep my ways.

verses.hebrew_full: שִׁמְע֖וּ מוּסָ֥ר וַחֲכָ֗מוּ וְאַל־תִּפְרָֽעוּ
verses.translation: Heed discipline and become wise;Do not spurn it.
verses.rashi: and do not put it to naught Heb. תפרעו, and do not put my discipline to naught.

verses.hebrew_full: אַ֥שְֽׁרֵי־אָדָם֮ שֹׁמֵ֢עַֽ֫ לִ֥י לִשְׁקֹ֣ד עַל־דַּ֭לְתֹתַי י֤וֹם י֑וֹם לִ֝שְׁמֹ֗ר מְזוּזֹ֥ת פְּתָחָֽי
verses.translation: Happy is the one who listens to me,Coming early to my gates each day,Waiting outside my doors.
verses.rashi: to watch Heb. לשקד by my doors [to be the] first to enter into the study hall and the synagogue and [the] last to leave.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֣י מֹ֭צְאִי (מצאי) מָצָ֣א חַיִּ֑ים וַיָּ֥פֶק רָ֝צ֗וֹן מֵיְהֹוָֽה
verses.translation: For one who finds me finds lifeAnd obtains favor from GOD.

verses.hebrew_full: וְֽ֭חֹטְאִי חֹמֵ֣ס נַפְשׁ֑וֹ כׇּל־מְ֝שַׂנְאַ֗י אָ֣הֲבוּ מָֽוֶת
verses.translation: But one who misses me self-destructs;All who hate me love death.”

verses.hebrew_full: חׇ֭כְמוֹת בָּנְתָ֣ה בֵיתָ֑הּ חָצְבָ֖ה עַמּוּדֶ֣יהָ שִׁבְעָֽה
verses.translation: Wisdom has built her house,She has hewn her seven pillars.
verses.rashi: Wisdom has built her house With wisdom has the Holy One, blessed be He, built the world. she has hewn her seven pillars The seven days of Creation. Another explanation: This refers to the seven books of the Torah, since (Num. 10:35f.) “And it came about when the ark traveled...” is an individual book, as is stated in tractate Shabbath (116a).

verses.hebrew_full: טָבְחָ֣ה טִ֭בְחָהּ מָסְכָ֣ה יֵינָ֑הּ אַ֝֗ף עָרְכָ֥ה שֻׁלְחָנָֽהּ
verses.translation: She has prepared the feast,Mixed the wine,And also set the table.
verses.rashi: She has prepared her meat, she has mingled her wine She has mixed it with water, like strong wine, that is unfit to drink unmixed. she has even set her table All creations of liquid and solid.

verses.hebrew_full: שָׁלְחָ֣ה נַעֲרֹתֶ֣יהָ תִקְרָ֑א עַל־גַּ֝פֵּ֗י מְרֹ֣מֵי קָֽרֶת
verses.translation: She has sent out her maids to announceOn the heights of the town,
verses.rashi: She has sent her maidens Adam and Eve.

verses.hebrew_full: מִֽי־פֶ֭תִי יָסֻ֣ר הֵ֑נָּה חֲסַר־לֵ֝֗ב אָ֣מְרָה לּֽוֹ
verses.translation: “Let the simple enter here.”To those devoid of sense she says,
verses.rashi: “Whoever is simple, let him turn in here” and learn it and become wise.

verses.hebrew_full: לְ֭כוּ לַחֲמ֣וּ בְֽלַחֲמִ֑י וּ֝שְׁת֗וּ בְּיַ֣יִן מָסָֽכְתִּי
verses.translation: “Come, eat my foodAnd drink the wine that I have mixed;
verses.rashi: of the wine I have mingled Heb. מסכתי.

verses.hebrew_full: עִזְב֣וּ פְתָאיִ֣ם וִֽחְי֑וּ וְ֝אִשְׁר֗וּ בְּדֶ֣רֶךְ בִּינָֽה
verses.translation: Give up simpleness and live,Walk in the way of understanding.”
verses.rashi: Leave, you simpletons the way of simplicity and live. and step Heb. מסכתי, an expression of stepping, as in (Job 23:11): “To his steps (באשרו) my foot was held fast.”

verses.hebrew_full: יֹ֤סֵ֨ר לֵ֗ץ לֹקֵ֣חַֽ ל֣וֹ קָל֑וֹן וּמוֹכִ֖יחַ לְרָשָׁ֣ע מוּמֽוֹ
verses.translation: To correct a scoffer,Or rebuke an evildoer regarding a blemish,Is to call down abuse on oneself.
verses.rashi: and he who reproves a wicked man, that is his blemish It is a blemish to the one who reproves [him], for this one berates him and does not heed him. This is a warning that it is forbidden to talk with those who entice to worship idols, even to reprove them and to draw them near.

verses.hebrew_full: אַל־תּ֣וֹכַח לֵ֭ץ פֶּן־יִשְׂנָאֶ֑ךָּ הוֹכַ֥ח לְ֝חָכָ֗ם וְיֶאֱהָבֶֽךָּ
verses.translation: Do not rebuke scoffers, for they will hate you;Reprove the wise, and they will love you.

verses.hebrew_full: תֵּ֣ן לְ֭חָכָם וְיֶחְכַּם־ע֑וֹד הוֹדַ֥ע לְ֝צַדִּ֗יק וְי֣וֹסֶף לֶֽקַח
verses.translation: Instruct the wise, and they will grow wiser;Teach the righteous, and they will gain in learning.
verses.rashi: Give a wise man, and he will become yet wiser To a worthy pupil, and the Midrash Aggadah (Tanchuma, Vayakhel 6) states: It was said to Noah, “From every clean animal, etc.” (Gen. 7:2), and when he went out, “And he took from all clean animals, etc.” (ibid. 8:20). He said, “What did the Holy One, blessed be He, see to increase the number of these? It is only because He wished that I offer up some of them.” teach a righteous man wisdom, and he will increase in learning from his own knowledge in addition to what he heard.

verses.hebrew_full: תְּחִלַּ֣ת חׇ֭כְמָה יִרְאַ֣ת יְהֹוָ֑ה וְדַ֖עַת קְדֹשִׁ֣ים בִּינָֽה
verses.translation: The beginning of wisdom is fear of GOD,And knowledge of the Holy One is understanding.
verses.rashi: and the knowledge of the holy ones is the root of understanding.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּי־בִ֭י יִרְבּ֣וּ יָמֶ֑יךָ וְיוֹסִ֥יפוּ לְּ֝ךָ֗ שְׁנ֣וֹת חַיִּֽים
verses.translation: For through me your days will increase,And years be added to your life.
verses.rashi: and they will add to you years of life Years of life are those of sustenance and wealth.

verses.translation: If you are wise, you are wise for yourself;If you are a scoffer, you bear it alone.

verses.hebrew_full: אֵ֣שֶׁת כְּ֭סִילוּת הֹמִיָּ֑ה פְּ֝תַיּ֗וּת וּבַל־יָ֥דְעָה מָּֽה
verses.translation: The stupid woman bustles about;She is simple and knows nothing.

verses.hebrew_full: וְֽ֭יָשְׁבָה לְפֶ֣תַח בֵּיתָ֑הּ עַל־כִּ֝סֵּ֗א מְרֹ֣מֵי קָֽרֶת
verses.translation: She sits in the doorway of her house,Or on a chair at the heights of the town,

verses.hebrew_full: לִקְרֹ֥א לְעֹֽבְרֵי־דָ֑רֶךְ הַֽ֝מְיַשְּׁרִ֗ים אֹֽרְחוֹתָֽם
verses.translation: Calling to all the wayfarersWho go about their own affairs,

words.eng: to pass over or by or through·way

verses.hebrew_full: מִֽי־פֶ֭תִי יָסֻ֣ר הֵ֑נָּה וַחֲסַר־לֵ֝֗ב וְאָ֣מְרָה לּֽוֹ
verses.translation: “Let the simple enter here”;And to those devoid of sense she says,
verses.rashi: and whoever is devoid of sense (lit. and one devoid of sense.) and she says to him these words. What does she say to him?

verses.hebrew_full: מַֽיִם־גְּנוּבִ֥ים יִמְתָּ֑קוּ וְלֶ֖חֶם סְתָרִ֣ים יִנְעָֽם
verses.translation: “Stolen waters are sweet,And bread eaten furtively is tasty.”
verses.rashi: Stolen water is sweet The pleasure afforded by intimacy with a single woman does not equal that afforded by intimacy with a married woman. Also, regarding the commandments (other editions: sectarianism), stolen water is sweet, for they were afraid to do it in public, but did it in secret.

words.eng: to be or become sweet or pleasing

verses.hebrew_full: וְֽלֹא־יָ֭דַע כִּֽי־רְפָאִ֣ים שָׁ֑ם בְּעִמְקֵ֖י שְׁא֣וֹל קְרֻאֶֽיהָ
verses.translation: He does not know that the shades are there,That her guests are in the depths of Sheol.

verses.hebrew_full: מִשְׁלֵ֗י שְׁלֹ֫מֹ֥ה בֵּ֣ן חָ֭כָם יְשַׂמַּח־אָ֑ב וּבֵ֥ן כְּ֝סִ֗יל תּוּגַ֥ת אִמּֽוֹ
verses.translation: The proverbs of Solomon:A wise son brings joy to his father;A dull son is his mother’s sorrow.
verses.rashi: Proverbs of Solomon: A wise son makes his father happy This is the Holy One, blessed be He (Midrash); another explanation: his actual father. but a foolish son is the grief of his mother He is always with his mother at home, and she sees his foolishness and is troubled. And according to the allegory, a foolish son, like Jeroboam the son of Nebat, is the grief of his mother, the grief of his nation.

verses.hebrew_full: לֹֽא־י֭וֹעִילוּ אוֹצְר֣וֹת רֶ֑שַׁע וּ֝צְדָקָ֗ה תַּצִּ֥יל מִמָּֽוֶת
verses.translation: Ill-gotten wealth is of no avail,But righteousness saves from death.
verses.rashi: Treasures of wickedness will not avail For he was boasting with his riches, as it is stated (Hosea 12:9): “And Ephraim said: Surely I have become rich, etc.” but charity will save from death And if you ask, “A righteous man who squanders his property for charity—from where will he sustain himself?”

verses.hebrew_full: לֹא־יַרְעִ֣יב יְ֭הֹוָה נֶ֣פֶשׁ צַדִּ֑יק וְהַוַּ֖ת רְשָׁעִ֣ים יֶהְדֹּֽף
verses.translation: GOD will not let a righteous person go hungry,But denies the wicked what they crave.
verses.rashi: The Lord will not starve the soul of the righteous [This is the answer to the previous question.] but the destruction [wrought by] the wicked will cast [them] down It will thrust them down and cause them to fall.

verses.hebrew_full: רָ֗אשׁ עֹשֶׂ֥ה כַף־רְמִיָּ֑ה וְיַ֖ד חָרוּצִ֣ים תַּעֲשִֽׁיר
verses.translation: Negligent hands cause poverty,But diligent hands enrich.
verses.rashi: A poor man makes a deceitful scale Whoever is impoverished in [his knowledge of] Torah promulgates false decisions. deceitful scales Scales of deceit. According to its simple meaning, it refers to merchants. and the hand of those who make true decisions The upright, who decide a matter truly and justly without injustice.

words.eng: to be or become rich or wealthy

verses.hebrew_full: אֹגֵ֣ר בַּ֭קַּיִץ בֵּ֣ן מַשְׂכִּ֑יל נִרְדָּ֥ם בַּ֝קָּצִ֗יר בֵּ֣ן מֵבִֽישׁ
verses.translation: He who lays in stores during the summer is a capable son,But he who sleeps during the harvest is an incompetent.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּ֭רָכוֹת לְרֹ֣אשׁ צַדִּ֑יק וּפִ֥י רְ֝שָׁעִ֗ים יְכַסֶּ֥ה חָמָֽס
verses.translation: Blessings light upon the head of a righteous person,But lawlessness covers the mouths of the wicked.
verses.rashi: but violence shall cover the mouth of the wicked The violence shall cover their mouths and kill them.

verses.hebrew_full: זֵ֣כֶר צַ֭דִּיק לִבְרָכָ֑ה וְשֵׁ֖ם רְשָׁעִ֣ים יִרְקָֽב
verses.translation: The name of a righteous person is invoked in blessing,But the fame of the wicked rots.
verses.rashi: The mention of a righteous man is for a blessing Whoever mentions a righteous man blesses him. but the name of the wicked shall rot Decay develops in their name, for no one wishes to mention his [the wicked man’s] name, and it is automatically forgotten.

verses.hebrew_full: חֲכַם־לֵ֭ב יִקַּ֣ח מִצְוֺ֑ת וֶאֱוִ֥יל שְׂ֝פָתַ֗יִם יִלָּבֵֽט
verses.translation: Whoever has a wise heart accepts commands,But one whose speech is foolish comes to grief.
verses.rashi: The wise-hearted takes commandments This alludes to our teacher, Moses, for all Israel were busy with the plunder of Egypt, and he was busy with the commandments, as is it said (Exod. 13:19): “And Moses took Joseph’s bones, etc.” but he who talks foolishly will weary Heb. ילבט, an expression of weariness. It appears in Sifre, in the section commencing (Num. 11:1): “And the people were as complainers.” They said, “How much have we wearied (נתלבטנו) on the way!”

verses.hebrew_full: הוֹלֵ֣ךְ בַּ֭תֹּם יֵ֣לֶךְ בֶּ֑טַח וּמְעַקֵּ֥שׁ דְּ֝רָכָ֗יו יִוָּדֵֽעַ
verses.translation: Whoever lives blamelessly lives safely,But one who walks a crooked path will be found out.
verses.rashi: will be broken Heb. יודע, will be broken and chastised, as in (Jud. 8:16): “And with them he broke (ויודע) the men of Succoth.”

verses.hebrew_full: קֹ֣רֵֽץ עַ֭יִן יִתֵּ֣ן עַצָּ֑בֶת וֶאֱוִ֥יל שְׂ֝פָתַ֗יִם יִלָּבֵֽט
verses.translation: Whoever winks causes sorrow;One whose speech is foolish comes to grief.
verses.rashi: He who winks his eye causes grief That is the one who entices a person to evil with his winks.

verses.hebrew_full: מְק֣וֹר חַ֭יִּים פִּ֣י צַדִּ֑יק וּפִ֥י רְ֝שָׁעִ֗ים יְכַסֶּ֥ה חָמָֽס
verses.translation: The mouth of a righteous person is a fountain of life,But lawlessness covers the mouths of the wicked.
verses.rashi: The mouth of the righteous is a fountain of life, but the mouth of the wicked, etc. Their mouth conceals the violence that is in their heart, for they talk smoothly with their lips, but hatred is hidden in their heart. Now the interpretation of this verse is not the same as the above verse (8); the context is indicative of their meaning.

verses.hebrew_full: שִׂ֭נְאָה תְּעֹרֵ֣ר מְדָנִ֑ים וְעַ֥ל כׇּל־פְּ֝שָׁעִ֗ים תְּכַסֶּ֥ה אַהֲבָֽה
verses.translation: Hatred stirs up strife,But love covers up all faults.
verses.rashi: Hatred arouses quarrels Even a sin that was forgotten is remembered though additional iniquities. The final hatred comes and arouses them, for Ezekiel reproved Israel for the transgressions of Egypt (Ezek. 20:7), “And I said to them, ‘Every man shall cast away the abominations of his eyes, etc.’” How many (years was) this hatred hidden, that the Holy One, blessed be He, did not mention it to them until now, when they added transgressions to their sins. but love covers all transgressions When Israel improves their deeds, the Holy One, blessed be He, conceals their transgressions.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּשִׂפְתֵ֣י נָ֭בוֹן תִּמָּצֵ֣א חׇכְמָ֑ה וְ֝שֵׁ֗בֶט לְגֵ֣ו חֲסַר־לֵֽב
verses.translation: Wisdom is to be found on the lips of the intelligent,But a rod is ready for the back of the senseless.
verses.rashi: Wisdom is found in the lips of the understanding When a person reproves an understanding individual, he replies to him, “I have sinned,” e.g. David, who said to Nathan, “I have sinned” (11 Sam. 12:13). but a rod is for the body of one devoid of sense But one who is devoid of sense does not listen until he is smitten, like Pharaoh.

verses.hebrew_full: חֲכָמִ֥ים יִצְפְּנוּ־דָ֑עַת וּפִֽי־אֱ֝וִ֗יל מְחִתָּ֥ה קְרֹבָֽה
verses.translation: The wise store up knowledge;The mouth of a fool is an imminent ruin.
verses.rashi: Wise men store up knowledge They guard it in their heart so that they do not forget it.

verses.hebrew_full: ה֣וֹן עָ֭שִׁיר קִרְיַ֣ת עֻזּ֑וֹ מְחִתַּ֖ת דַּלִּ֣ים רֵישָֽׁם
verses.translation: For someone rich, wealth is a fortress;For the poor, poverty is a ruin.
verses.rashi: The wealth of the rich in Torah. is the city of his strength to him. the destruction of the poor is their poverty The poverty that they did not engage in the Torah is their destruction.
verses.ibn_ezra: The rich man's wealth is like a fortress that will save him from moments of troubles even though he is not wise; and “the poor” i.e., the heartless, their poverty will ruin them.

verses.hebrew_full: פְּעֻלַּ֣ת צַדִּ֣יק לְחַיִּ֑ים תְּבוּאַ֖ת רָשָׁ֣ע לְחַטָּֽאת
verses.translation: The labor of the righteous makes for life;The produce of the wicked makes for want.
verses.rashi: The act of the righteous is for life [This is to be understood] according to its apparent meaning, but the Midrash Aggadah states: Solomon performed the construction of the Temple for the life of Israel, for their atonement. what the wicked bring in What Manasseh brought in, for he brought in the image—that was for sin.

verses.hebrew_full: אֹ֣רַח לְ֭חַיִּים שׁוֹמֵ֣ר מוּסָ֑ר וְעֹזֵ֖ב תּוֹכַ֣חַת מַתְעֶֽה
verses.translation: One who follows discipline shows the way to life,But one who ignores reproof leads astray.
verses.rashi: The way to life He who keeps discipline—that is the way to life. but he who forsakes reproof misleads himself and others.

verses.hebrew_full: מְכַסֶּ֣ה שִׂ֭נְאָה שִׂפְתֵי־שָׁ֑קֶר וּמוֹצִ֥א דִ֝בָּ֗ה ה֣וּא כְסִֽיל
verses.translation: One who conceals hatred has lying lips,While one who speaks forth slander is a dullard.
verses.rashi: He who covers up hatred has false lips The flatterer has false lips, and he hides hatred in his heart. and he who spreads slander is a fool Heb. דבה, a rumor about his friend, about which people will converse (דובבין).

words.eng: act or place of going out or forth

verses.hebrew_full: בְּרֹ֣ב דְּ֭בָרִים לֹ֣א יֶחְדַּל־פָּ֑שַׁע וְחוֹשֵׂ֖ךְ שְׂפָתָ֣יו מַשְׂכִּֽיל
verses.translation: Where there is much talking, there is no lack of transgressing,But one who curbs the tongue shows sense.
verses.rashi: In a multitude of words, transgression will not be avoided He who talks too much brings on sin. and he who holds back his lips he is wise.

verses.hebrew_full: כֶּ֣סֶף נִ֭בְחָר לְשׁ֣וֹן צַדִּ֑יק לֵ֖ב רְשָׁעִ֣ים כִּמְעָֽט
verses.translation: The tongue of a righteous person is choice silver,But the mind of the wicked is of little worth.
verses.rashi: The tongue of the righteous is choice silver for he knows how to reprove. the heart of the wicked is worth little for he does not heed the reproof of the righteous man. Midrash Rabbi Tanhuma (Ki Thissa 6) states: This was stated concerning Iddo the prophet, who called [in prophecy] about the altar in Beth-El, and Jeroboam, although his hand had become stiff, did not heed the reproof, as it is said (I Kings 13:6): “Entreat now the Lord your God, etc.”; but not “my God, etc”; (ad loc.): “and the king’s hand was drawn back to him, and it was as before.” Just as before, he was standing and burning sacrifices to idols, so was it at the end.

verses.hebrew_full: שִׂפְתֵ֣י צַ֭דִּיק יִרְע֣וּ רַבִּ֑ים וֶ֝אֱוִילִ֗ים בַּחֲסַר־לֵ֥ב יָמֽוּתוּ
verses.translation: The lips of a righteous person sustain many,But fools die for lack of sense.
verses.rashi: The lips of the righteous will feed many, etc. Many eat in his merit and because of his prayer.

verses.hebrew_full: בִּרְכַּ֣ת יְ֭הֹוָה הִ֣יא תַעֲשִׁ֑יר וְלֹֽא־יוֹסִ֖ף עֶ֣צֶב עִמָּֽהּ
verses.translation: It is the blessing of GOD that enriches,And no toil can increase it.
verses.rashi: The blessing of the Lord will bring riches, etc. One need not toil to gain wealth, for it is enough with the blessing that He blesses him.

words.eng: to be or become rich or wealthy

verses.hebrew_full: כִּשְׂח֣וֹק לִ֭כְסִיל עֲשׂ֣וֹת זִמָּ֑ה וְ֝חׇכְמָ֗ה לְאִ֣ישׁ תְּבוּנָֽה
verses.translation: As mischief is sport for the dullard,So is wisdom for the man of understanding.
verses.rashi: As it is sport for a fool to carry out a sinful plot Heb. זמה, a plot of sins. [so is] wisdom like sport to a man of understanding; i.e., in his eyes, it is easy to do.

verses.hebrew_full: מְגוֹרַ֣ת רָ֭שָׁע הִ֣יא תְבוֹאֶ֑נּוּ וְתַאֲוַ֖ת צַדִּיקִ֣ים יִתֵּֽן
verses.translation: What an evildoer plots backfires;What the righteous desire is granted.
verses.rashi: The dread of a wicked man that will befall him What he fears will befall him. The generation of the Dispersion said, “Lest we scatter” (Gen. 11:4), and their end was that it is written (ibid. 8), “And the Lord scattered them from there.” but the desire of the righteous He will grant He Who has the power to grant it.

verses.hebrew_full: כַּעֲב֣וֹר ס֭וּפָה וְאֵ֣ין רָשָׁ֑ע וְ֝צַדִּ֗יק יְס֣וֹד עוֹלָֽם
verses.translation: When the storm passes a wicked person is gone,But the righteous person is an everlasting foundation.
verses.rashi: When the whirlwind passes, the wicked man is no more Suddenly the fury of a whirlwind comes, and the wicked man is cut off from his place.

words.eng: to pass over or by or through

verses.hebrew_full: כַּחֹ֤מֶץ לַשִּׁנַּ֗יִם וְכֶעָשָׁ֥ן לָעֵינָ֑יִם כֵּ֥ן הֶ֝עָצֵ֗ל לְשֹׁלְחָֽיו
verses.translation: Like vinegar to the teeth,Like smoke to the eyes,Are sluggards to those who send them on a mission.

verses.hebrew_full: יִרְאַ֣ת יְ֭הֹוָה תּוֹסִ֣יף יָמִ֑ים וּשְׁנ֖וֹת רְשָׁעִ֣ים תִּקְצֹֽרְנָה
verses.translation: The fear of GOD prolongs life,While the years of the wicked will be shortened.

verses.hebrew_full: תּוֹחֶ֣לֶת צַדִּיקִ֣ים שִׂמְחָ֑ה וְתִקְוַ֖ת רְשָׁעִ֣ים תֹּאבֵֽד
verses.translation: The righteous can look forward to joy,But the hopes of the wicked are doomed.
verses.rashi: The expectation of the righteous [will result in] joy Eventually, it will be realized, and they will rejoice. but the hope of the wicked shall be lost for it will not come.

verses.hebrew_full: מָע֣וֹז לַ֭תֹּם דֶּ֣רֶךְ יְהֹוָ֑ה וּ֝מְחִתָּ֗ה לְפֹ֣עֲלֵי אָֽוֶן
verses.translation: The way of GOD is a stronghold for a blameless person,But a ruin for evildoers.
verses.rashi: The way of the Lord is a stronghold for the innocent, but ruin for those who work iniquity For they do not follow it, and it exacts retribution from them.

verses.hebrew_full: צַ֭דִּיק לְעוֹלָ֣ם בַּל־יִמּ֑וֹט וּ֝רְשָׁעִ֗ים לֹ֣א יִשְׁכְּנוּ־אָֽרֶץ
verses.translation: The righteous person will never be shaken;The wicked will not inhabit the earth.
verses.rashi: The righteous will not collapse forever When he collapses, his collapse is not a permanent collapse, but he will fall and rise.

verses.hebrew_full: פִּֽי־צַ֭דִּיק יָנ֣וּב חׇכְמָ֑ה וּלְשׁ֥וֹן תַּ֝הְפֻּכ֗וֹת תִּכָּרֵֽת
verses.translation: The mouth of a righteous person produces wisdom,But the treacherous tongue shall be cut off.
verses.rashi: The mouth of the righteous man speaks wisdom Heb. ינוב, speaks, an expression of (Isa. 57: 9): “the speech (ניב) of the lips.”

verses.hebrew_full: שִׂפְתֵ֣י צַ֭דִּיק יֵדְע֣וּן רָצ֑וֹן וּפִ֥י רְ֝שָׁעִ֗ים תַּהְפֻּכֽוֹת
verses.translation: The lips of a righteous person know what is pleasing;The mouth of the wicked [knows] duplicity.
verses.rashi: the lips of a righteous man know how to please They know how to please and placate his Creator, and they know how to please the people and how to make peace among them.

verses.hebrew_full: מֹאזְנֵ֣י מִ֭רְמָה תּוֹעֲבַ֣ת יְהֹוָ֑ה וְאֶ֖בֶן שְׁלֵמָ֣ה רְצוֹנֽוֹ
verses.translation: False scales are an abomination to GOD,Who finds an honest weight pleasing.

verses.hebrew_full: בָּֽא־זָ֭דוֹן וַיָּבֹ֣א קָל֑וֹן וְֽאֶת־צְנוּעִ֥ים חׇכְמָֽה
verses.translation: When arrogance appears, disgrace follows,But wisdom is with those who are unassuming.
verses.rashi: When willful wickedness comes, etc. but with the modest is wisdom comes wisdom.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·to be humble

verses.hebrew_full: תֻּמַּ֣ת יְשָׁרִ֣ים תַּנְחֵ֑ם וְסֶ֖לֶף בֹּגְדִ֣ים (ושדם) יְשׇׁדֵּֽם
verses.translation: The integrity of the upright guides them;The deviousness of the treacherous leads them to ruin.
verses.rashi: The innocence of the upright leads them Heb. תנחם, will guide them. but the distortion of the treacherous robs them Heb. ישדם, like ישדדם

verses.hebrew_full: לֹא־יוֹעִ֣יל ה֭וֹן בְּי֣וֹם עֶבְרָ֑ה וּ֝צְדָקָ֗ה תַּצִּ֥יל מִמָּֽוֶת
verses.translation: Wealth is of no avail on the day of wrath,But righteousness saves from death.

words.eng: to pass over or by or through

verses.hebrew_full: צִדְקַ֣ת תָּ֭מִים תְּיַשֵּׁ֣ר דַּרְכּ֑וֹ וּ֝בְרִשְׁעָת֗וֹ יִפֹּ֥ל רָשָֽׁע
verses.translation: The righteousness of the blameless smooths their way,But the wicked fall by their wickedness.

verses.hebrew_full: צִדְקַ֣ת יְ֭שָׁרִים תַּצִּילֵ֑ם וּ֝בְהַוַּ֗ת בֹּגְדִ֥ים יִלָּכֵֽדוּ
verses.translation: The righteousness of the upright saves them,But the treacherous are trapped by their malice.
verses.rashi: but in the destruction In the destruction that they perpetrate, they are caught; the verse is transposed. [It means] “and the treacherous in the destruction will be caught.”

verses.hebrew_full: בְּמ֤וֹת אָדָ֣ם רָ֭שָׁע תֹּאבַ֣ד תִּקְוָ֑ה וְתוֹחֶ֖לֶת אוֹנִ֣ים אָבָֽדָה
verses.translation: At death the hopes of a wicked person are doomed,And the ambition of evildoers comes to nothing.
verses.rashi: When a wicked man dies, hope is lost The hope of all who trust in him. and the expectation of his children is lost Heb. אונים. The expectation of his children, who are the products of his strength, is lost, for no good will come to them in his merit. But when the righteous die, their children have trust in their righteousness.

verses.hebrew_full: צַ֭דִּיק מִצָּרָ֣ה נֶחֱלָ֑ץ וַיָּבֹ֖א רָשָׁ֣ע תַּחְתָּֽיו
verses.translation: A righteous person is rescued from troubleAnd it comes to the wicked instead.
verses.rashi: A righteous man is extricated from trouble when the wicked man dies, and this is connected to the preceding verse.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּפֶ֗ה חָ֭נֵף יַשְׁחִ֣ת רֵעֵ֑הוּ וּ֝בְדַ֗עַת צַדִּיקִ֥ים יֵחָלֵֽצוּ
verses.translation: An impious person destroys a neighbor through speech,But through knowledge the righteous are rescued.
verses.rashi: With his mouth, the flatterer The flatterer who entices his friend on an evil way, destroys him with his mouth. but with knowledge, righteous men are extricated But the righteous man is extricated from [the flatterer] with the knowledge of the Torah, which warned concerning him (Deut. 13: 9): “You shall not consent to him, etc.”

verses.hebrew_full: בְּט֣וּב צַ֭דִּיקִים תַּעֲלֹ֣ץ קִרְיָ֑ה וּבַאֲבֹ֖ד רְשָׁעִ֣ים רִנָּֽה
verses.translation: When the righteous prosper the city exults;When the wicked perish there are shouts of joy.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּבִרְכַּ֣ת יְ֭שָׁרִים תָּר֣וּם קָ֑רֶת וּבְפִ֥י רְ֝שָׁעִ֗ים תֵּהָרֵֽס
verses.translation: A city is built up by the blessing of the upright,But it is torn down by the speech of the wicked.
verses.rashi: the ceiling is raised The ceiling of the Temple will be preserved at its height from falling. As long as the kings of Judah were upright, their prayer preserved the Temple.

verses.hebrew_full: בׇּז־לְרֵעֵ֥הוּ חֲסַר־לֵ֑ב וְאִ֖ישׁ תְּבוּנ֣וֹת יַחֲרִֽישׁ
verses.translation: One who speaks contemptuously of neighbors is devoid of sense;A prudent man keeps his peace.
verses.rashi: but a man of understanding is silent when the one devoid of sense despises him, like Saul, as it is written (I Sam. 10:27): “And they despised him and brought him no gift; and he was as one who kept his peace.”

verses.hebrew_full: הוֹלֵ֣ךְ רָ֭כִיל מְגַלֶּה־סּ֑וֹד וְנֶאֱמַן־ר֝֗וּחַ מְכַסֶּ֥ה דָבָֽר
verses.translation: A base fellow gives away secrets,But a trustworthy soul keeps a confidence.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּאֵ֣ין תַּ֭חְבֻּלוֹת יִפׇּל־עָ֑ם וּ֝תְשׁוּעָ֗ה בְּרֹ֣ב יוֹעֵֽץ
verses.translation: For want of strategy an army falls,But victory comes with much planning.
verses.rashi: Without strategy When trouble befalls Israel, and they do not put their heart to understand, to fast and to repent, the people will fall.

verses.hebrew_full: רַע־יֵ֭רוֹעַ כִּי־עָ֣רַב זָ֑ר וְשֹׂנֵ֖א תֹקְעִ֣ים בּוֹטֵֽחַ
verses.translation: Harm awaits one who stands surety for another; One who spurns pledging shall be secure.
verses.rashi: will be broken by it The wicked man who gave his heart as surety to idolatry. but he who hates hand-claspers Those who clasp hands with the enticers to follow their counsel. But our Rabbis expounded this in regard to surety in monetary matters.

verses.hebrew_full: אֵֽשֶׁת־חֵ֭ן תִּתְמֹ֣ךְ כָּב֑וֹד וְ֝עָרִיצִ֗ים יִתְמְכוּ־עֹֽשֶׁר
verses.translation: A graceful woman obtains honor;Ruthless men obtain wealth.
verses.rashi: draws near to honor Heb. תתמך. The people of Israel constantly draw near to the honor of the Holy One, blessed be He, and His Torah. ( but strong men draw near to riches that it should not be lost to them.)

verses.hebrew_full: גֹּמֵ֣ל נַ֭פְשׁוֹ אִ֣ישׁ חָ֑סֶד וְעֹכֵ֥ר שְׁ֝אֵר֗וֹ אַכְזָרִֽי
verses.translation: A kindly man benefits himself;A cruel one makes trouble for himself.
verses.rashi: does himself good He does good to his kin. A kind man lit. a man of kindness, a man who is kind. but a cruel one troubles his own flesh But the cruel one troubles his kin.

verses.hebrew_full: רָשָׁ֗ע עֹשֶׂ֥ה פְעֻלַּת־שָׁ֑קֶר וְזֹרֵ֥עַ צְ֝דָקָ֗ה שֶׂ֣כֶר אֱמֶֽת
verses.translation: A wicked person earns illusory wages,But one who sows righteousness has a true reward.
verses.rashi: A wicked man earns illusory wages The wage of a wicked man lies to him. He thinks that his prosperity will remain, but all is lost. but he who sows charity [receives] a true reward But he who sows charity, it is a wage of truth, for he is surely confident that he will receive his wage at the end. שכר cornial in Old French. [This appears to mean a weir in several dialects; i.e., a fence placed in the water to catch fish.] Manuscripts of Rashi yield: eclusse or esklusa, which is e’cluse [in modern French]; in German wasserschleuse, a lock or a sluice gate). Like a man who locks a canal in order to gather fish, and he is confident that he will find many fish there. A similar instance is (Isa. 19:10): “all who make dams (שכר) for still ponds.”

verses.hebrew_full: כֵּן־צְדָקָ֥ה לְחַיִּ֑ים וּמְרַדֵּ֖ף רָעָ֣ה לְמוֹתֽוֹ
verses.translation: Righteousness is a prop of life,But to pursue evil leads to death.
verses.rashi: The truth of charity Heb. כן, the truth of charity is that its end is for life, as in (Num. 27:7): “The daughters of Zelofchad speak right (כן).”

verses.hebrew_full: תּוֹעֲבַ֣ת יְ֭הֹוָה עִקְּשֵׁי־לֵ֑ב וּ֝רְצוֹנ֗וֹ תְּמִ֣ימֵי דָֽרֶךְ
verses.translation: Crooked minds are an abomination to GOD,But those whose way is blameless are pleasing.

verses.hebrew_full: יָ֣ד לְ֭יָד לֹא־יִנָּ֣קֶה רָּ֑ע וְזֶ֖רַע צַדִּיקִ֣ים נִמְלָֽט
verses.translation: Assuredly, the evildoer will not escape,But the offspring of the righteous will be safe.
verses.rashi: From hand to hand From the hand of the Holy One, blessed be He, to his hand shall come to him his wage, and he will not be cleansed of the evil that he committed.

verses.hebrew_full: נֶ֣זֶם זָ֭הָב בְּאַ֣ף חֲזִ֑יר אִשָּׁ֥ה יָ֝פָ֗ה וְסָ֣רַת טָֽעַם
verses.translation: Like a gold ring in the snout of a pigIs a beautiful woman bereft of sense.
verses.rashi: [As] a gold ring in a swine’s snout that bemires it in the dungheap, so is a Torah scholar who turns away from the good way. from whom sense has departed Who has departed from the Torah.

verses.hebrew_full: תַּאֲוַ֣ת צַדִּיקִ֣ים אַךְ־ט֑וֹב תִּקְוַ֖ת רְשָׁעִ֣ים עֶבְרָֽה
verses.translation: What the righteous desire can only be good;What the wicked hope for [stirs] wrath.
verses.rashi: the hope of the wicked is wrath They are assured and hope for Gehinnom.

words.eng: to pass over or by or through

verses.hebrew_full: יֵ֣שׁ מְ֭פַזֵּר וְנוֹסָ֥ף ע֑וֹד וְחֹשֵׂ֥ךְ מִ֝יֹּ֗שֶׁר אַךְ־לְמַחְסֽוֹר
verses.translation: Some give generously and end with more;Others stint on doing the right thing and incur a loss.
verses.rashi: There is one who scatters his money for such things as charity, and he is given more. and one who withholds himself from [giving] what is proper, only for a loss it will be to him.

verses.hebrew_full: נֶֽפֶשׁ־בְּרָכָ֥ה תְדֻשָּׁ֑ן וּ֝מַרְוֶ֗ה גַּם־ה֥וּא יוֹרֶֽא
verses.translation: A generous person enjoys prosperity;One who satisfies others shall likewise be sated.
verses.rashi: A generous person [lit. a soul of blessing,] who is generous with his money, and every expression of blessing is fuison in Old French, excess. and he who sates who sates the poor. shall himself become sated as well He shall be sated with good.

verses.hebrew_full: מֹ֣נֵֽעַ בָּ֭ר יִקְּבֻ֣הוּ לְא֑וֹם וּ֝בְרָכָ֗ה לְרֹ֣אשׁ מַשְׁבִּֽיר
verses.translation: Whoever withholds grain earns the curses of the people,But blessings are on the head of the one who dispenses it.
verses.rashi: He who keeps back grain [who refrains] from teaching Torah.

verses.hebrew_full: שֹׁ֣חֵֽר ט֭וֹב יְבַקֵּ֣שׁ רָצ֑וֹן וְדֹרֵ֖שׁ רָעָ֣ה תְבוֹאֶֽנּוּ
verses.translation: One who earnestly seeks good pursues what is pleasing;Evil comes to someone who is bent on it.
verses.rashi: He who desires good Who wishes to lead the people in the good way and reproves and chastises them. seeks acceptance He desires that the Holy One, blessed be He, accept them and and be reconciled with them. Appaisement in Old French, besanftigen in German.

verses.hebrew_full: בּוֹטֵ֣חַ בְּ֭עׇשְׁרוֹ ה֣וּא יִפּ֑וֹל וְ֝כֶעָלֶ֗ה צַדִּיקִ֥ים יִפְרָֽחוּ
verses.translation: One who trusts in wealth shall fall,But the righteous shall flourish like foliage.

verses.hebrew_full: עֹכֵ֣ר בֵּ֭יתוֹ יִנְחַל־ר֑וּחַ וְעֶ֥בֶד אֱ֝וִ֗יל לַֽחֲכַם־לֵֽב
verses.translation: He who makes trouble for his household shall inherit the wind;A fool is a slave to the wise-hearted.
verses.rashi: He who inherits the wind will trouble his household A lazy man, who always inherits the wind and does not toil in Torah or in work, will ultimately trouble the members of his household, for they will have nothing to eat.

words.eng: to get as a possession·to smell

verses.hebrew_full: פְּֽרִי־צַ֭דִּיק עֵ֣ץ חַיִּ֑ים וְלֹקֵ֖חַ נְפָשׁ֣וֹת חָכָֽם
verses.translation: The fruit of the righteous is a tree of life;The wise captivate people.
verses.rashi: The fruit of a righteous man The reward of the fruit of the righteous man’s deeds is a tree of life for the world. and the wise man acquires souls Whoever is wise acquires souls for himself. he teaches them the good way, and they are his as though he had acquired them, as the matter is stated (Gen. 12:5): “And the souls that they acquired in Haran.”

verses.hebrew_full: הֵ֣ן צַ֭דִּיק בָּאָ֣רֶץ יְשֻׁלָּ֑ם אַ֝֗ף כִּֽי־רָשָׁ֥ע וְחוֹטֵֽא
verses.translation: If the righteous on earth get their deserts,How much more evildoers and sinners.
verses.rashi: Behold! The righteous man will be requited on earth Why should the wicked man feel confident when he prospers? Does he not see that the righteous man is requited the payment of the sins he has committed while he is still on earth, during his lifetime? surely a wicked man and a sinner Surely the wicked will ultimately be requited, either during his lifetime or after his death.

words.eng: to be in a covenant of peace

verses.hebrew_full: אֹהֵ֣ב מ֭וּסָר אֹ֣הֵֽב דָּ֑עַת וְשׂוֹנֵ֖א תוֹכַ֣חַת בָּֽעַר
verses.translation: One who loves discipline loves knowledge;Whoever spurns reproof is a brute.

verses.hebrew_full: ט֗וֹב יָפִ֣יק רָ֭צוֹן מֵיְהֹוָ֑ה וְאִ֖ישׁ מְזִמּ֣וֹת יַרְשִֽׁיעַ
verses.translation: A good person earns the favor of GOD,A man of intrigues, God’s condemnation.
verses.rashi: A good man will obtain Heb. יפיק. He will draw satisfaction from the Holy One, blessed be He, to bring goodness to the world. but a man of evil devices will condemn One who has evil devices will condemn. He will condemn the people to bring evil [upon them], and so he states (Ecc. 9:18): “but one sinner destroys much good.”

verses.hebrew_full: לֹא־יִכּ֣וֹן אָדָ֣ם בְּרֶ֑שַׁע וְשֹׁ֥רֶשׁ צַ֝דִּיקִ֗ים בַּל־יִמּֽוֹט
verses.translation: No one can be established in wickedness,But the root of the righteous will not be shaken loose.
verses.rashi: A man will not be established with wickedness Heb. יכון, will not be firmly established.

verses.hebrew_full: אֵֽשֶׁת־חַ֭יִל עֲטֶ֣רֶת בַּעְלָ֑הּ וּכְרָקָ֖ב בְּעַצְמוֹתָ֣יו מְבִישָֽׁה
verses.translation: A woman of substance is a crown for her husband,But an incompetent one is like rot in his bones.
verses.rashi: is like rot A worm that penetrates the bones and grinds them. So is an embarrassing, bad wife, whose deeds are shameful.

verses.hebrew_full: מַחְשְׁב֣וֹת צַדִּיקִ֣ים מִשְׁפָּ֑ט תַּחְבֻּל֖וֹת רְשָׁעִ֣ים מִרְמָֽה
verses.translation: The purposes of the righteous are justice,The schemes of the wicked are deceit.

verses.hebrew_full: דִּבְרֵ֣י רְשָׁעִ֣ים אֱרׇב־דָּ֑ם וּפִ֥י יְ֝שָׁרִ֗ים יַצִּילֵֽם
verses.translation: The words of the wicked are a deadly ambush,But the speech of the upright saves them.
verses.rashi: The words of the wicked are to lurk for blood They take counsel to murder directly or through false testimony. but the mouth of the upright when they hear [the wicked’s] counsel, will save the victims, for they will reveal [the wicked’s] plot, or if they are witnesses, [the upright] will prove them to be collusive.

verses.hebrew_full: הָפ֣וֹךְ רְשָׁעִ֣ים וְאֵינָ֑ם וּבֵ֖ית צַדִּיקִ֣ים יַעֲמֹֽד
verses.translation: Overturn the wicked and they are gone,But the house of the righteous will endure.
verses.rashi: The wicked will be overthrown Heb. הפוך, like להפוך, to overthrow. Like a sudden overthrow they are overthrown [lit. like an overthrow in a second]. and they are no more They are destroyed, e.g. Sodom. but the house of the righteous will stand Will endure.

verses.hebrew_full: לְֽפִי־שִׂ֭כְלוֹ יְהֻלַּל־אִ֑ישׁ וְנַעֲוֵה־לֵ֝֗ב יִהְיֶ֥ה לָבֽוּז
verses.translation: A man is commended according to his intelligence;A twisted mind is held up to contempt.
verses.rashi: According to his intelligence a man is praised Whether he possesses little or much intelligence, he will receive his reward according to his intelligence. but he whose heart has turned away He who moved his heart completely away from the Torah will be despised. Heb. נעוה, as in (Isa. 21:3): “I have become confused (נעויתי) from hearing,” and similarly (I Sam. 20:30): “the son of a straying (נעות) woman deserving of punishment.” And it is possible to say that נעוה means one whose heart has strayed, from the expression נע ונד, moving and wandering, and as one says זעוה, horror, from the root זע, stirred.

verses.hebrew_full: ט֣וֹב נִ֭קְלֶה וְעֶ֣בֶד ל֑וֹ מִ֝מִּתְכַּבֵּ֗ד וַחֲסַר־לָֽחֶם
verses.translation: Better to be lightly esteemed and have a servantThan to put on airs and have no food.
verses.rashi: Better is he who is lightly esteemed in his own eyes and became a slave to himself that one who is honored in his own eyes and says, “It is disgraceful for me to toil at labor, for I am one of the great men,”—he will ultimately lack bread.

verses.hebrew_full: יוֹדֵ֣עַ צַ֭דִּיק נֶ֣פֶשׁ בְּהֶמְתּ֑וֹ וְֽרַחֲמֵ֥י רְ֝שָׁעִ֗ים אַכְזָרִֽי
verses.translation: A righteous person knows the needs of their beast,But the compassion of the wicked is cruelty.
verses.rashi: A righteous man has regard for the desire of his beast What his beasts and his household members need.

verses.hebrew_full: עֹבֵ֣ד אַ֭דְמָתוֹ יִֽשְׂבַּֽע־לָ֑חֶם וּמְרַדֵּ֖ף רֵיקִ֣ים חֲסַר־לֵֽב
verses.translation: He who tills his land shall have food in plenty,But one who pursues vanities is devoid of sense.
verses.rashi: He who tills his soil will be sated with bread To be understood according to its apparent meaning, but according to its allegorical meaning, [this means] he who constantly reviews his studies lest they be forgotten.

verses.hebrew_full: חָמַ֣ד רָ֭שָׁע מְצ֣וֹד רָעִ֑ים וְשֹׁ֖רֶשׁ צַדִּיקִ֣ים יִתֵּֽן
verses.translation: A wicked man covets the catch of evildoers;The root of the righteous yields [fruit].
verses.rashi: The wicked man yearns to gain sustenance and maintenance from the prey of the wicked, who prey on people with robbery and violence. but the root of the righteous yields what it is fit to yield, and that is the fruit.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּפֶ֣שַׁע שְׂ֭פָתַיִם מוֹקֵ֣שׁ רָ֑ע וַיֵּצֵ֖א מִצָּרָ֣ה צַדִּֽיק
verses.translation: Sinful speech is a trap for the evildoer,But a righteous man escapes from trouble.
verses.rashi: Because of the transgression of the lips Because of the transgression of the lips of the generation of the Flood, who said (Job 21:15): “What is the Almighty that we should serve Him?” an evil snare came upon them, and Noah, the righteous man, came out of the trouble.

verses.hebrew_full: מִפְּרִ֣י פִי־אִ֭ישׁ יִשְׂבַּע־ט֑וֹב וּגְמ֥וּל יְדֵי־אָ֝דָ֗ם (ישוב) יָשִׁ֥יב לֽוֹ
verses.translation: A man gets his fill of good from the fruit of his speech;One is repaid in kind for one’s deeds.
verses.rashi: From the fruit of a man’s mouth From the reward of the mouth of those who engage in the Torah, they eat the good of this world, and the principal remains intact for them for the world to come.

verses.hebrew_full: דֶּ֣רֶךְ אֱ֭וִיל יָשָׁ֣ר בְּעֵינָ֑יו וְשֹׁמֵ֖עַ לְעֵצָ֣ה חָכָֽם
verses.translation: The way of fools is right in their own eyes;But those who are wise accept advice.

verses.hebrew_full: אֱוִ֗יל בַּ֭יּוֹם יִוָּדַ֣ע כַּעְס֑וֹ וְכֹסֶ֖ה קָל֣וֹן עָרֽוּם
verses.translation: Fools’ vexation is known at once,But the clever conceal their humiliation.
verses.rashi: A fool’s anger is known within the day On the day he is angry, on that very day he lets his anger be known, for he starts a quarrel and berates his fellow in public, and his anger has no restraint. But the clever man conceals the shame and does not hasten to quarrel. And the Midrash Aggadah states: On the day the first man was created, his sin was known, but the Holy One, blessed be He, Who is clever, did not wish to destroy His creatures. He concealed his shame and postponed His decree from Adam’s day to His day, which is one thousand years.

verses.hebrew_full: יָפִ֣יחַ אֱ֭מוּנָה יַגִּ֣יד צֶ֑דֶק וְעֵ֖ד שְׁקָרִ֣ים מִרְמָֽה
verses.translation: A faithful witness tells the truth,But a false witness, deceit.
verses.rashi: He who speaks the truth will testify justly He who speaks the truth will testify just testimony at a trial to justify the innocent.

verses.hebrew_full: יֵ֣שׁ בּ֭וֹטֶה כְּמַדְקְר֣וֹת חָ֑רֶב וּלְשׁ֖וֹן חֲכָמִ֣ים מַרְפֵּֽא
verses.translation: There is blunt talk like sword-thrusts,But the speech of the wise is healing.
verses.rashi: There is one who speaks Heb. בוטה, speaks, as in (Lev. 5:4): “to pronounce (לבטה) with the lips.” like the jabs of a sword For he stirs up the people and causes them to kill. but the tongue of the wise which brings peace between one man and his fellow. brings healing to that speech.

verses.hebrew_full: שְֽׂפַת־אֱ֭מֶת תִּכּ֣וֹן לָעַ֑ד וְעַד־אַ֝רְגִּ֗יעָה לְשׁ֣וֹן שָֽׁקֶר
verses.translation: Truthful speech abides forever,A lying tongue for but a moment.
verses.rashi: A true tongue will be established forever It will be established and will endure. just for a moment In a fleeting moment, it perishes and leaves, for falsehood has no feet.

verses.hebrew_full: מִ֭רְמָה בְּלֶב־חֹ֣רְשֵׁי רָ֑ע וּֽלְיֹעֲצֵ֖י שָׁל֣וֹם שִׂמְחָֽה
verses.translation: Deceit is in the minds of those who plot evil;For those who plan good there is joy.
verses.rashi: There is deceit in the heart of those who plot evil and since they are occupied with their thoughts of deceit, they have no joy—but for the counselors of peace, there is joy.

verses.hebrew_full: לֹא־יְאֻנֶּ֣ה לַצַּדִּ֣יק כׇּל־אָ֑וֶן וּ֝רְשָׁעִ֗ים מָ֣לְאוּ רָֽע
verses.translation: No harm befalls a righteous person,But the wicked have their fill of misfortune.
verses.rashi: No wrong shall be caused for the righteous It will not happen. No sin will chance before him inadvertently.

verses.hebrew_full: תּוֹעֲבַ֣ת יְ֭הֹוָה שִׂפְתֵי־שָׁ֑קֶר וְעֹשֵׂ֖י אֱמוּנָ֣ה רְצוֹנֽוֹ
verses.translation: Lying speech is an abomination to GOD,But those who act faithfully are pleasing.

verses.hebrew_full: אָדָ֣ם עָ֭רוּם כֹּ֣סֶה דָּ֑עַת וְלֵ֥ב כְּ֝סִילִ֗ים יִקְרָ֥א אִוֶּֽלֶת
verses.translation: A clever person conceals knowledge,But the mind of dullards cries out folly.
verses.rashi: A cunning man conceals knowledge Even in his wisdom, he is discreet—he surely conceals words of foolishness; but the heart of the fool announces foolishness aloud.

verses.hebrew_full: יַד־חָרוּצִ֥ים תִּמְשׁ֑וֹל וּ֝רְמִיָּ֗ה תִּהְיֶ֥ה לָמַֽס
verses.translation: The hand of the diligent wields authority;The negligent are held in subjection.
verses.rashi: The hand of the diligent The upright. will rule Will make wealthy.

words.eng: gang or body of forced labourers

verses.hebrew_full: דְּאָגָ֣ה בְלֶב־אִ֣ישׁ יַשְׁחֶ֑נָּה וְדָבָ֖ר ט֣וֹב יְשַׂמְּחֶֽנָּה
verses.translation: If there is anxiety in someone’s mind, let them quash it,And turn it into joy with a good word.
verses.rashi: If there is concern in a man’s heart, let him cast it down Let him divert his attention from it. and a good word will make it cheerful Let him engage in the Torah which will cause the concern in his heart to rejoice and save him therefrom. And according to the one who says, “he should tell it to others,” this is [the meaning of] the end of the verse: And a good word with which his friend consoles him will cause the concern to rejoice.

verses.hebrew_full: יָתֵ֣ר מֵרֵעֵ֣הוּ צַדִּ֑יק וְדֶ֖רֶךְ רְשָׁעִ֣ים תַּתְעֵֽם
verses.translation: A righteous person gives friends direction, But the way of the wicked leads astray.
verses.rashi: The righteous is more generous than his neighbor Heb. יתר. The righteous renounces his measures and passes over them. יתר large in French. That is freigebig in German, generous. the wicked who is accustomed to harming will lead them astray.

verses.hebrew_full: לֹא־יַחֲרֹ֣ךְ רְמִיָּ֣ה צֵיד֑וֹ וְהוֹן־אָדָ֖ם יָקָ֣ר חָרֽוּץ
verses.translation: A negligent person never has game to roast;A diligent one has precious wealth.
verses.rashi: He will not roast This is connected to the above verse; that is to say that the way of the wicked will mislead him, and he will not roast the prey of his deceit. He will not roast; he will not succeed with his deceit. As he [Solomon] spoke with a figure of prey, an expression of roasting is appropriate, for the successful hunter hunts the birds and roasts them in fire. but the wealth of an honest man is precious Heb. יקר חרוץ. This is an inverted verse. But the wealth of a man who is honest is precious. honest Heb. חרוץ, one who deals honestly, as in (10:4): “and the hand of those who make true decisions (חרוצים) will make [them] rich.”

verses.hebrew_full: בְּאֹֽרַח־צְדָקָ֥ה חַיִּ֑ים וְדֶ֖רֶךְ נְתִיבָ֣הֿ אַל־מָֽוֶת
verses.translation: The road of righteousness leads to life;By way of its path there is no death.
verses.rashi: In the road of charity is life, and [on] the way of its path of charity. there is no death He will not die.

verses.hebrew_full: בֵּ֣ן חָ֭כָם מ֣וּסַר אָ֑ב וְ֝לֵ֗ץ לֹא־שָׁמַ֥ע גְּעָרָֽה
verses.translation: A wise son—it is through the discipline of his father;A scoffer—he never heard reproof.
verses.rashi: A wise son... his father’s discipline This is an elliptical verse: a wise son asks for and loves his father’s discipline. Some say: Because of the father’s discipline, he is wise. but a scorner does not listen to rebuke For he does not accept reproof.

verses.hebrew_full: מִפְּרִ֣י פִי־אִ֭ישׁ יֹ֣אכַל ט֑וֹב וְנֶ֖פֶשׁ בֹּגְדִ֣ים חָמָֽס
verses.translation: A man enjoys good from the fruit of his speech;But out of the throat of the treacherous comes lawlessness.
verses.rashi: From the fruit of man’s mouth he will eat good From the reward of his Torah, he will eat the good in this world and the principal will exist in the world to come. but the desire of the treacherous is violence Heb. ונפש. The desire of the treacherous is violence, as in (Gen. 23:8): “If it is your desire (נפשכם)” and (Ps. 27:12): “Do not give me for the desire of (בנפש) my adversaries.”

verses.hebrew_full: נֹצֵ֣ר פִּ֭יו שֹׁמֵ֣ר נַפְשׁ֑וֹ פֹּשֵׂ֥ק שְׂ֝פָתָ֗יו מְחִתָּה־לֽוֹ
verses.translation: Those who guard their tongue preserve their life;Those who open wide their lips, it is their ruin.
verses.rashi: one who opens his lips wide Heb. פשק, opens his lips to speak constantly all his desire, from (Ezek. 16:25): “And you spread wide (ותפשקי) your legs,” an expression of wideness.

verses.hebrew_full: מִתְאַוָּ֣ה וָ֭אַיִן נַפְשׁ֣וֹ עָצֵ֑ל וְנֶ֖פֶשׁ חָרֻצִ֣ים תְּדֻשָּֽׁן
verses.translation: A sluggard craves, but has nothing;The diligent shall feast on rich fare.
verses.rashi: The soul of the lazy man desires but has nothing His soul desires all good, but has nothing. but the soul of the diligent shall be sated The upright who eat from the toil of their hands. This is its meaning according to its simple interpretation. According to its allegorical meaning, in the future he will see the glory of the Torah scholar and long for it, but he will not achieve it.

verses.hebrew_full: דְּבַר־שֶׁ֭קֶר יִשְׂנָ֣א צַדִּ֑יק וְ֝רָשָׁ֗ע יַבְאִ֥ישׁ וְיַחְפִּֽיר
verses.translation: The righteous hate lies;The wicked are vile and disgraceful.
verses.rashi: The righteous man hates a false word The righteous man hates a false word, but the wicked accepts it. disgraces and embarrasses the people therewith.

verses.hebrew_full: צְ֭דָקָה תִּצֹּ֣ר תׇּם־דָּ֑רֶךְ וְ֝רִשְׁעָ֗ה תְּסַלֵּ֥ף חַטָּֽאת
verses.translation: Righteousness protects the one whose way is blameless;Wickedness subverts the sinner.
verses.rashi: but wickedness brings ruin upon the sinner Heb. חטאת, lit. sin. Like חטאת, a sinner, but since he is completely wicked, he calls him חטאת, sin. The meaning is that his wickedness will bring ruin upon the sinner and humble him. Every expression of סלוף means ruin and stumbling, like (below 19:3) “A person’s foolishness ruins (תסלף) his way,” and brings evil upon him.

verses.hebrew_full: יֵ֣שׁ מִ֭תְעַשֵּׁר וְאֵ֣ין כֹּ֑ל מִ֝תְרוֹשֵׁ֗שׁ וְה֣וֹן רָֽב
verses.translation: Some pretend to be rich and have nothing;Others profess to be poor and have much wealth.
verses.rashi: There is one who feigns riches He shows himself to be rich. Another explanation: There is a man who becomes rich in the end but has nothing in the beginning, and there is one who comes to poverty from great riches. Another explanation: There is one who becomes wealthy by robbing the poor, but in the end he has nothing, and there is one who becomes poor by scattering his money and giving it to the poor, but great wealth is in store for him.

words.eng: to be or become rich or wealthy

verses.hebrew_full: כֹּ֣פֶר נֶפֶשׁ־אִ֣ישׁ עׇשְׁר֑וֹ וְ֝רָ֗שׁ לֹא־שָׁמַ֥ע גְּעָרָֽה
verses.translation: Riches are ransom for a person’s life,The poor never heard a reproof.
verses.rashi: The ransom of a man’s soul is his wealth A man’s wealth is the ransom of his soul, for he performed charity with it. as long as the poor man has not heard a rebuke As long as he does not let the poor man hear a rebuke, i.e., he gives to him and does not embarrass him. Another explanation: The ransom of a man’s soul is his wealth His Torah. but a poor man in the words of Torah, did not hear a rebuke He does not know to turn away from evil, since he is not cautious. The Midrash Aggadah interprets it as speaking of the half-shekel, which Scripture levied on all Israel and made the poor and the rich equal in that respect—so that the poor does not hear a rebuke from the rich, saying to him, “My share is greater than your share in the public sacrifices.”

verses.hebrew_full: אוֹר־צַדִּיקִ֥ים יִשְׂמָ֑ח וְנֵ֖ר רְשָׁעִ֣ים יִדְעָֽךְ
verses.translation: The light of the righteous is radiant;The lamp of the wicked is extinguished.
verses.rashi: will ebb away Heb. ידעך, an expression of springing, for the flame springs and is cut off.

verses.hebrew_full: רַק־בְּ֭זָדוֹן יִתֵּ֣ן מַצָּ֑ה וְאֶת־נ֖וֹעָצִ֣ים חׇכְמָֽה
verses.translation: Arrogance yields nothing but strife; Wisdom belongs to those who seek advice.
verses.rashi: Only with wickedness does one cause quarrels Heb. מצה, controversy. but there is wisdom with those who take counsel But wisdom lies with those who behave with counsel.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·to advise

verses.hebrew_full: ה֭וֹן מֵהֶ֣בֶל יִמְעָ֑ט וְקֹבֵ֖ץ עַל־יָ֣ד יַרְבֶּֽה
verses.translation: Wealth may dwindle to less than nothing,But one who gathers little by little increases it.
verses.rashi: Wealth gotten by vanity One who makes his study in bundles. shall be diminished For he will forget it little by little.

verses.hebrew_full: תּוֹחֶ֣לֶת מְ֭מֻשָּׁכָה מַחֲלָה־לֵ֑ב וְעֵ֥ץ חַ֝יִּ֗ים תַּאֲוָ֥ה בָאָֽה
verses.translation: Hope deferred sickens the heart,But desire realized is a tree of life.
verses.rashi: Hope deferred He relies on his friend and does nothing. makes the heart sick Heb. מחלה, brings sickness to the heart. This מחלה is not a noun like (Ex. 23:25): “And I will remove sickness (מחלה) from your midst,” but like (Lev. 11:6): “bringing up (מעלת) the cud.” but a desire fulfilled is a tree of life lit. but a tree of life is a desire fulfilled. This is inverted; i.e., a desire fulfilled is like a tree of life. The hope that the Holy One, blessed be He, had hoped for Israel and looked forward for them to repent—brought them ultimately to heartsickness when they did not repent. And when His desire is fulfilled—that they comply with His will—it is a tree of life to them.

words.eng: to be or become weak·inner man

verses.hebrew_full: בָּ֣ז לְ֭דָבָר יֵחָ֣בֶל ל֑וֹ וִירֵ֥א מִ֝צְוָ֗ה ה֣וּא יְשֻׁלָּֽם
verses.translation: One who disdains a precept will be injured thereby;One who respects a command will be rewarded.
verses.rashi: He who despises a thing will be pledged to it Heb. יחבל. He who despises one of the words of the Torah will ultimately be taken for it as a pledge. but he who reveres a commandment will be rewarded lit. will be paid. He will receive a reward. Midrash Tehillim expounds: “He who despises a thing will be pledged to it” concerning David, who said before the Holy One, blessed be He, “Of what use are the madmen that You created?” He replied, “By your life, you will ultimately need madness.” When he came before Achish to feign madness, “and he let his saliva run down upon his beard, etc.” (I Sam. 21:14).

words.eng: to be in a covenant of peace

verses.hebrew_full: תּוֹרַ֣ת חָ֭כָם מְק֣וֹר חַיִּ֑ים לָ֝ס֗וּר מִמֹּ֥קְשֵׁי מָֽוֶת
verses.translation: The instruction of the wise is a fountain of life,Enabling one to avoid deadly snares.
verses.rashi: The instruction of a wise man is a spring of life for he teaches him to turn away from the snares of death.

verses.hebrew_full: שֵֽׂכֶל־ט֭וֹב יִתֶּן־חֵ֑ן וְדֶ֖רֶךְ בֹּגְדִ֣ים אֵיתָֽן
verses.translation: Good sense wins favor;The way of treacherous men is unchanging.
verses.rashi: but the way of the treacherous is harsh It is harsh for him and for others.

verses.hebrew_full: כׇּל־עָ֭רוּם יַעֲשֶׂ֣ה בְדָ֑עַת וּ֝כְסִ֗יל יִפְרֹ֥שׂ אִוֶּֽלֶת
verses.translation: Those who are clever act knowledgeably,But dullards expose their stupidity.
verses.rashi: Every cunning man acts with forethought He performs his deeds with forethought, like David: “Let them seek for my lord, the king, a young girl, a virgin, etc.” (I Kings 1:2). but a fool exposes [his] stupidity This is Ahasuerus (Esther 2:3): “Let the king appoint, etc.” They knew that he would not marry them all. Therefore, whoever had a daughter concealed her.

verses.hebrew_full: מַלְאָ֣ךְ רָ֭שָׁע יִפֹּ֣ל בְּרָ֑ע וְצִ֖יר אֱמוּנִ֣ים מַרְפֵּֽא
verses.translation: Harm befalls a wicked messenger;A faithful courier brings healing.
verses.rashi: A wicked messenger falls into evil like Balaam, to whom the Holy One, blessed be He, said (Num. 22:35): “Go with the men,” but he dealt wickedly to entice Balak to follow his evil counsel; therefore, he fell by the sword. but a faithful emissary brings healing This is Moses, our teacher.

verses.hebrew_full: רֵ֣ישׁ וְ֭קָלוֹן פּוֹרֵ֣עַ מוּסָ֑ר וְשֹׁמֵ֖ר תּוֹכַ֣חַת יְכֻבָּֽד
verses.translation: Poverty and humiliation are for the one who spurns discipline;But one who takes reproof to heart gets honor.
verses.rashi: Poverty and disgrace Poverty and disgrace come upon him who spurns discipline.

verses.hebrew_full: תַּאֲוָ֣ה נִ֭הְיָה תֶּעֱרַ֣ב לְנָ֑פֶשׁ וְתוֹעֲבַ֥ת כְּ֝סִילִ֗ים ס֣וּר מֵרָֽע
verses.translation: Desire realized is sweet to the soul;To turn away from evil is abhorrent to the stupid.
verses.rashi: pleases the soul When a person’s desire comes to him it pleases his soul. Therefore, it is an abomination to a fool to turn away from evil because it pleases him to gratify his desire. fulfilled Heb. נהיה, lit. is. Another explanation: a desire fulfilled The Holy One, blessed be He, desires that Israel do His will, and when His desire is fulfilled, it pleases Him. And it is the abomination of the wicked that they turn away from their evil to gratify their desire.

verses.hebrew_full: (הלוך) הוֹלֵ֣ךְ אֶת־חֲכָמִ֣ים (וחכם) יֶחְכָּ֑ם וְרֹעֶ֖ה כְסִילִ֣ים יֵרֽוֹעַ
verses.translation: One who keeps company with the wise becomes wise,But one who consorts with dullards comes to grief.
verses.rashi: He who goes with the wise will become wise, but he who befriends the fools Who joins fools to be his friends. will be broken Heb. ירוע, will be shattered.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·wise

verses.hebrew_full: חַ֭טָּאִים תְּרַדֵּ֣ף רָעָ֑ה וְאֶת־צַ֝דִּיקִ֗ים יְשַׁלֶּם־טֽוֹב
verses.translation: Misfortune pursues sinners,But the righteous are well rewarded.
verses.rashi: Evil will pursue the sinners As for a wicked man, his wickedness pursues him to his destruction.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·just
to be in a covenant of peace·to be good

verses.hebrew_full: ט֗וֹב יַנְחִ֥יל בְּנֵֽי־בָנִ֑ים וְצָפ֥וּן לַ֝צַּדִּ֗יק חֵ֣יל חוֹטֵֽא
verses.translation: A good man has what to bequeath to his grandchildren,For the wealth of sinners is stored up for the righteous.
verses.rashi: A good man will leave an inheritance of his merit and his property to his sons’ sons, but a sinner does not leave over an inheritance to his sons, for his wealth and his property are laid away for the righteous, as it is written (Esther 8:2): “And Esther set Mordecai over the house of Haman.”

verses.hebrew_full: רׇב־אֹ֭כֶל נִ֣יר רָאשִׁ֑ים וְיֵ֥שׁ נִ֝סְפֶּ֗ה בְּלֹ֣א מִשְׁפָּֽט
verses.translation: The tillage of the poor yields much food;But substance is swept away for lack of moderation.
verses.rashi: An abundance of food is the result of the plowing of the poor I saw in the great Masorah [on the words רב אכל]: The word רב appears thirty-three times with a “kamatz,” but this one has a short “kamatz,” and it is joined to the word אכל, food, with a “makkaf” (hyphen); and I saw there that אכל is vowelized with a “pattah” (i.e., a “segol” which is a small “pattah”), and it is accented on the first syllable, as in (Gen. 41:35): “all the food (אכל) of the good years.” According to its vowelization, this is its interpretation: Much grain comes to the world through the plowing of the poor people; i.e., much Torah emanates from pupils whose teachers learn from them through their debate concerning the halachah. and some perish And many of them perish from the world because [they have] no propriety, for they do not behave properly; and concerning the grain, some grain suffers because of its owner, who does not separate its tithes and its gifts for the poor as is proper. Our Sages explained in tractate Hagigah (4b) “And some perish without justice,” concerning the messenger of the angel of death, who changes one name for another and causes the death of one whose time has not come. But, if it [this explanation] is so, the end of the verse has nothing to do with its beginning.

verses.hebrew_full: חוֹשֵׂ֣ךְ שִׁ֭בְטוֹ שׂוֹנֵ֣א בְנ֑וֹ וְ֝אֹהֲב֗וֹ שִׁחֲר֥וֹ מוּסָֽר
verses.translation: He who spares the rod hates his son,But he who loves him disciplines him early.
verses.rashi: He who holds back his rod His end will be that he will hate his son because he will see him getting into mischief. disciplines him early He always chastises him in the morning.

verses.hebrew_full: צַדִּ֗יק אֹ֭כֵֽל לְשֹׂ֣בַע נַפְשׁ֑וֹ וּבֶ֖טֶן רְשָׁעִ֣ים תֶּחְסָֽר
verses.translation: The righteous person eats to satiety,But the belly of the wicked is empty.
verses.rashi: to sate his appetite It always seems to him that he is satisfied. ( shall feel want It does not seem to them that they are satisfied.)

verses.hebrew_full: חַכְמ֣וֹת נָ֭שִׁים בָּנְתָ֣ה בֵיתָ֑הּ וְ֝אִוֶּ֗לֶת בְּיָדֶ֥יהָ תֶהֶרְסֶֽנּוּ
verses.translation: The wisest of women builds her house,But folly tears it down with its own hands.
verses.rashi: The wisest of women Heb. חכמות, vowelized with a “patach,” is not a noun, but [it means that] the wisest of women build their houses, for they are preserved by them, like the wife of On the son of Peleth, as is explained in the chapter entitled “Chelek.” but a foolish one Heb. ואולת. But a foolish woman. tears it down with her hands She tears down her house. This is Korah’s wife.]

verses.hebrew_full: הוֹלֵ֣ךְ בְּ֭יׇשְׁרוֹ יְרֵ֣א יְהֹוָ֑ה וּנְל֖וֹז דְּרָכָ֣יו בּוֹזֵֽהוּ
verses.translation: One who walks a straight path fears GOD;But one whose ways are devious shows scorn.
verses.rashi: He who fears the Lord goes in his uprightness Heb. ירא ה', he who fears the Lord, and he who is crooked in his ways... despises Him The Holy One, blessed be He.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּֽפִי־אֱ֭וִיל חֹ֣טֶר גַּאֲוָ֑ה וְשִׂפְתֵ֥י חֲ֝כָמִ֗ים תִּשְׁמוּרֵֽם
verses.translation: In the mouth of a fool is a rod of haughtiness,But the lips of the wise protect them.
verses.rashi: In a fool’s mouth is a staff of haughtiness A stick of haughtiness, like Pharaoh, who said (Ex. 5: 2): “Who is the Lord that I should hearken to His voice?” but the lips of the wise guard them Heb. תשמורם, like תשמרם. They guard the wise.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּאֵ֣ין אֲ֭לָפִים אֵב֣וּס בָּ֑ר וְרׇב־תְּ֝בוּא֗וֹת בְּכֹ֣חַ שֽׁוֹר
verses.translation: If there are no oxen the crib is clean,But a rich harvest comes through the strength of the ox.
verses.rashi: Without oxen the manger is empty Without oxen, the manger is empty, for not even straw is found in the house. empty Heb. בר, clean and empty. This means that without Torah scholars, there is no proper instruction.

verses.hebrew_full: עֵ֣ד אֱ֭מוּנִים לֹ֣א יְכַזֵּ֑ב וְיָפִ֥יחַ כְּ֝זָבִ֗ים עֵ֣ד שָֽׁקֶר
verses.translation: An honest witness will not lie;A false witness testifies lies.
verses.rashi: but he who speaks lies and always speaks lies, is a false witness.

verses.hebrew_full: בִּקֶּשׁ־לֵ֣ץ חׇכְמָ֣ה וָאָ֑יִן וְדַ֖עַת לְנָב֣וֹן נָקָֽל
verses.translation: Scoffers seek wisdom in vain,But knowledge comes easily to the intelligent.
verses.rashi: The scorner sought wisdom When he needs wisdom, he does not find it in his heart.

verses.hebrew_full: לֵ֣ךְ מִ֭נֶּגֶד לְאִ֣ישׁ כְּסִ֑יל וּבַל־יָ֝דַ֗עְתָּ שִׂפְתֵי־דָֽעַת
verses.translation: Keep your distance from a dull man,For you will not learn wise speech.
verses.rashi: Go far away from a foolish wisdom. Do not always associate with him. or you will know lips of knowledge You will eventually not know wisdom.

verses.hebrew_full: חׇכְמַ֣ת עָ֭רוּם הָבִ֣ין דַּרְכּ֑וֹ וְאִוֶּ֖לֶת כְּסִילִ֣ים מִרְמָֽה
verses.translation: It is the wisdom of a clever man to understand his course;But the stupidity of dullards is delusion.
verses.rashi: The wisdom of a cunning man Heb. ערום, he who is wise. is to understand his way To weigh his paths. but the folly of the fools That is the deceit in their hearts. It will ultimately bring them to foolishness.

verses.hebrew_full: אֱ֭וִלִים יָלִ֣יץ אָשָׁ֑ם וּבֵ֖ין יְשָׁרִ֣ים רָצֽוֹן
verses.translation: Reparations mediate between fools,Between the upright, good will.
verses.rashi: Amends for guilt plead for the fools For they sin and have to give money to the one against whom they sinned. And their amends, which they bring him with their money, will plead for them, like the Philistines, as it is stated (I Sam. 6:4f.): “five hemorrhoids of gold, etc. and you shall give honor to the God of Israel. Perhaps He will lighten His hand from upon you.” אשם (amende in French, geldstrafe in German) a fine. but there is good will among the upright apaisement in French (cf. above 11:27), appeasement. The Holy One, blessed be He, is pleased with them.

verses.hebrew_full: לֵ֗ב י֭וֹדֵעַ מׇרַּ֣ת נַפְשׁ֑וֹ וּ֝בְשִׂמְחָת֗וֹ לֹא־יִתְעָ֥רַב זָֽר
verses.translation: The heart alone knows its bitterness,And no outsider can share in its joy.
verses.rashi: One’s heart knows the bitterness of his soul His toil and labor that he toiled with the Torah. Therefore, in his joy a stranger shall not mingle. Another explanation: One’s heart knows the bitterness of his soul Israel, who are of embittered spirit in exile, for they are killed for the sanctification of the Name—in their joy no stranger shall mingle in the future.

verses.hebrew_full: בֵּ֣ית רְ֭שָׁעִים יִשָּׁמֵ֑ד וְאֹ֖הֶל יְשָׁרִ֣ים יַפְרִֽיחַ
verses.translation: The house of the wicked will be demolished,But the tent of the upright will flourish.

verses.hebrew_full: יֵ֤שׁ דֶּ֣רֶךְ יָ֭שָׁר לִפְנֵי־אִ֑ישׁ וְ֝אַחֲרִיתָ֗הּ דַּרְכֵי־מָֽוֶת
verses.translation: The road ahead may seem right,But in the end it can be a road to death.
verses.rashi: There is a way that seems right to a man He commits a transgression and says, “There is no transgression in it.” Another explanation: There is a way that seems right to a man The way of laziness seemed right to Esau, who was a man of the field, “but its end etc.“

verses.hebrew_full: גַּם־בִּשְׂחֹ֥ק יִכְאַב־לֵ֑ב וְאַחֲרִיתָ֖הּ שִׂמְחָ֣ה תוּגָֽה
verses.translation: The heart may ache even in laughter,And joy may end in grief.
verses.rashi: Even with laughter that the Holy One, blessed be He, laughs with the heathens in this world. Their heart will ache in the future, and so Scripture says (Isa. 65:14): “And you shall cry out from sorrow of heart.”

verses.hebrew_full: מִדְּרָכָ֣יו יִ֭שְׂבַּע ס֣וּג לֵ֑ב וּ֝מֵעָלָ֗יו אִ֣ישׁ טֽוֹב
verses.translation: Those who are unprincipled reap the fruits of their ways;The good man, of his deeds. as from ʻll; cf. Hos. 12.3.
verses.rashi: The one with an impure heart shall have his fill from his ways Esau. and above him is a good man Jacob.

verses.hebrew_full: פֶּ֭תִי יַאֲמִ֣ין לְכׇל־דָּבָ֑ר וְ֝עָר֗וּם יָבִ֥ין לַאֲשֻׁרֽוֹ
verses.translation: The simple believe anything;The clever ponders their course.
verses.rashi: A fool believes their words and is enticed after them. Another explanation: with laughter the heart aches It is sport for a fool to carry out a sinful plot (above 10:23), and ultimately, his heart will ache. The one with an impure heart is a wicked man, as in (below 26:23) “Impure (סיגים) silver.” סיג is the name of the dross. סוג is the silver mixed with the dross. and above him is a good man And above the wicked man will be the righteous man. A fool believes talebearers, but a cunning man understands his steps Heb. לאשרו, his steps. He waits to quarrel until he knows the matter correctly.

verses.hebrew_full: חָכָ֣ם יָ֭רֵא וְסָ֣ר מֵרָ֑ע וּ֝כְסִ֗יל מִתְעַבֵּ֥ר וּבוֹטֵֽחַ
verses.translation: A sage is diffident and shuns evil,But a dullard rushes in confidently.
verses.rashi: A wise man fears retribution. and turns away from evil From the evil. but a fool passes vigorously He strengthens himself to pass vigorously. and slips Heb. ובוטח, and slips and falls to the ground, as in (Jer. 12:5): “and in the peaceful you slip (בוטח),” and Jonathan renders: את מתבטח ונפיל, meaning “you slip and fall.” Others explain this as confidence. In his palace, he is confident, saying, “The evil will not befall me.”

words.eng: to pass over or by or through

verses.hebrew_full: קְֽצַר־אַ֭פַּיִם יַעֲשֶׂ֣ה אִוֶּ֑לֶת וְאִ֥ישׁ מְ֝זִמּ֗וֹת יִשָּׂנֵֽא
verses.translation: A hothead commits folly;A man of intrigues will be hated.
verses.rashi: A quick tempered man who hastens to avenge his anger. and a man of sinful plots Thoughts of counsels of evil.

verses.hebrew_full: נָחֲל֣וּ פְתָאיִ֣ם אִוֶּ֑לֶת וַ֝עֲרוּמִ֗ים יַכְתִּ֥רוּ דָֽעַת
verses.translation: Folly is the lot of the simple,But the clever glory in knowledge.
verses.rashi: make knowledge a crown Heb. יכתרו, they make it a crown for their head, and ultimately...

verses.hebrew_full: שַׁח֣וּ רָ֭עִים לִפְנֵ֣י טוֹבִ֑ים וּ֝רְשָׁעִ֗ים עַֽל־שַׁעֲרֵ֥י צַדִּֽיק
verses.translation: Evildoers are brought low before the good,So are the wicked at the gates of the righteous.
verses.rashi: The evil bend down before the good in the future.

verses.hebrew_full: גַּם־לְ֭רֵעֵהוּ יִשָּׂ֣נֵא רָ֑שׁ וְאֹהֲבֵ֖י עָשִׁ֣יר רַבִּֽים
verses.translation: The poor are despised even by their peers,But the rich have many friends.
verses.rashi: The poor man is hated even by his friend Even by his friends who accompany him to his wedding and by his close friends. even Heb. גם, an expression meaning even. The poor man is hated The ignoramus, who does not know how to behave properly.

verses.hebrew_full: בׇּז־לְרֵעֵ֥הוּ חוֹטֵ֑א וּמְחוֹנֵ֖ן (עניים) עֲנָוִ֣ים אַשְׁרָֽיו
verses.translation: Those who despise their fellows are wrong;Those who show pity for the lowly are happy.

verses.hebrew_full: הֲֽלוֹא־יִ֭תְעוּ חֹ֣רְשֵׁי רָ֑ע וְחֶ֥סֶד וֶ֝אֱמֶ֗ת חֹ֣רְשֵׁי טֽוֹב
verses.translation: Surely those who plan evil go astray,While those who plan good earn steadfast love.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּכׇל־עֶ֭צֶב יִהְיֶ֣ה מוֹתָ֑ר וּדְבַר־שְׂ֝פָתַ֗יִם אַךְ־לְמַחְסֽוֹר
verses.translation: From all toil there is some gain,But idle chatter is pure loss.
verses.rashi: In every toil In every toil of work, there is a gain, but in words of vanity, there is only loss.

verses.hebrew_full: עֲטֶ֣רֶת חֲכָמִ֣ים עׇשְׁרָ֑ם אִוֶּ֖לֶת כְּסִילִ֣ים אִוֶּֽלֶת
verses.translation: The ornament of the wise is their wealth;The stupidity of dullards is stupidity.
verses.rashi: The crown of the wise is their wealth that they are rich in Torah. the folly of the foolish is folly The ruin of the fools is the folly that they neglected to learn wisdom.

verses.hebrew_full: מַצִּ֣יל נְ֭פָשׁוֹת עֵ֣ד אֱמֶ֑ת וְיָפִ֖חַ כְּזָבִ֣ים מִרְמָֽה
verses.translation: A truthful witness saves lives;A lying witness [spreads] deceit.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּיִרְאַ֣ת יְ֭הֹוָה מִבְטַח־עֹ֑ז וּ֝לְבָנָ֗יו יִהְיֶ֥ה מַחְסֶֽה
verses.translation: Fear of GOD is a stronghold,A refuge for one’s children.
verses.rashi: In the fear of the Lord is a strong promise It is stated concerning Abraham (Gen. 22:12): “Now I know that you are a God-fearing man,” and He promised him: “For I will bless, you, etc.” and he will be... for his sons He. a shelter For they will take shelter and hide in the shadow of his merit.

verses.hebrew_full: יִרְאַ֣ת יְ֭הֹוָה מְק֣וֹר חַיִּ֑ים לָ֝ס֗וּר מִמֹּ֥קְשֵׁי מָֽוֶת
verses.translation: Fear of GOD is a fountain of life,Enabling one to avoid deadly snares.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּרׇב־עָ֥ם הַדְרַת־מֶ֑לֶךְ וּבְאֶ֥פֶס לְ֝אֹ֗ם מְחִתַּ֥ת רָזֽוֹן
verses.translation: A numerous people is the glory of a king;Without a nation a ruler is ruined.
verses.rashi: in a multitude of people that the people are righteous is the glory of the Holy One, blessed be He. but...in lack of people When they do not cleave to Him. the ruin of His Princedom It is the absence of His Princedom. So to speak, He gives of His honor to others.

verses.hebrew_full: אֶ֣רֶךְ אַ֭פַּיִם רַב־תְּבוּנָ֑ה וּקְצַר־ר֝֗וּחַ מֵרִ֥ים אִוֶּֽלֶת
verses.translation: Patience results in much understanding;Impatience gets folly as its portion.
verses.rashi: selects folly Heb. מרים, he separates it for his share.

verses.hebrew_full: חַיֵּ֣י בְ֭שָׂרִים לֵ֣ב מַרְפֵּ֑א וּרְקַ֖ב עֲצָמ֣וֹת קִנְאָֽה
verses.translation: A calm disposition gives bodily health;Passion is rot to the bones.
verses.rashi: A healing heart is the life of the flesh A heart of flesh, which heals the evil and passes over his retaliations, is the life of the creatures of the Holy One, blessed be He, who are flesh and blood. but anger is the rot of the bones A man who is often angry is the rot of everyone’s bones.

verses.hebrew_full: עֹ֣שֵֽׁק דָּ֭ל חֵרֵ֣ף עֹשֵׂ֑הוּ וּ֝מְכַבְּד֗וֹ חֹנֵ֥ן אֶבְיֽוֹן
verses.translation: One who withholds what is due to the poor affronts their Maker;One who shows pity for the needy shows honor.

words.eng: Elon Beth-hanan = "oak of the house of grace"

verses.hebrew_full: בְּֽ֭רָעָתוֹ יִדָּחֶ֣ה רָשָׁ֑ע וְחֹסֶ֖ה בְמוֹת֣וֹ צַדִּֽיק
verses.translation: The wicked fall by their own evil;The righteous find security in their death.
verses.rashi: but a righteous man is confident in his death When he dies, he is confident that he will come to Paradise.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּלֵ֣ב נָ֭בוֹן תָּנ֣וּחַ חׇכְמָ֑ה וּבְקֶ֥רֶב כְּ֝סִילִ֗ים תִּוָּדֵֽעַ
verses.translation: Wisdom rests quietly in the mind of a prudent person,But among dullards it makes itself known.
verses.rashi: Wisdom rests in the heart of an understanding man Heb. תנוח, it dwells and rests tranquilly; an expression of rest and tranquility. but in the midst of the fools it is known The little wisdom that is in his heart calls out. This parallels the Talmudic maxim (Baba Mezia 85b): “A coin in a bottle makes a sound of kish kish.”

verses.hebrew_full: צְדָקָ֥ה תְרוֹמֵֽם־גּ֑וֹי וְחֶ֖סֶד לְאֻמִּ֣ים חַטָּֽאת
verses.translation: Righteousness exalts a nation;Sin is a reproach to any people.
verses.rashi: Charity will elevate a nation Israel. but the kindness of the kingdoms is sin They are the heathens, who rob one to give another.

verses.hebrew_full: רְֽצוֹן־מֶ֭לֶךְ לְעֶ֣בֶד מַשְׂכִּ֑יל וְ֝עֶבְרָת֗וֹ תִּהְיֶ֥ה מֵבִֽישׁ
verses.translation: The king favors a capable servant;He rages at an incompetent one.
verses.rashi: but his wrath will be upon a shameful servant.
verses.ibn_ezra: רצון - (no actual comment is found in hard copy, only the opening word.)

verses.hebrew_full: מַֽעֲנֶה־רַּ֭ךְ יָשִׁ֣יב חֵמָ֑ה וּדְבַר־עֶ֝֗צֶב יַעֲלֶה־אָֽף
verses.translation: A gentle response allays wrath;A harsh word provokes anger.

verses.hebrew_full: לְשׁ֣וֹן חֲ֭כָמִים תֵּיטִ֣יב דָּ֑עַת וּפִ֥י כְ֝סִילִ֗ים יַבִּ֥יעַ אִוֶּֽלֶת
verses.translation: The tongue of the wise produces much knowledge,But the mouth of dullards pours out folly.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּֽכׇל־מָ֭קוֹם עֵינֵ֣י יְהֹוָ֑ה צֹ֝פ֗וֹת רָעִ֥ים וְטוֹבִֽים
verses.translation: GOD’s eyes are everywhere,Observing the bad and the good.

verses.hebrew_full: מַרְפֵּ֣א לָ֭שׁוֹן עֵ֣ץ חַיִּ֑ים וְסֶ֥לֶף בָּ֝֗הּ שֶׁ֣בֶר בְּרֽוּחַ
verses.translation: A healing tongue is a tree of life,But a devious one makes for a broken spirit.
verses.rashi: but if there is perverseness in it, it causes destruction by wind When one is perverse with his tongue, it will ultimately bring upon him destruction that comes from the east wind, which is prepared to mete out retribution upon the wicked, as it is stated (Ex. 14:21): “with a strong east wind”; (Jer. 18: 17): “Like an east wind I will scatter them”; and (Ps. 48:8): “With an east wind, You break the ships of Tarshish.”

verses.hebrew_full: אֱוִ֗יל יִ֭נְאַץ מוּסַ֣ר אָבִ֑יו וְשֹׁמֵ֖ר תּוֹכַ֣חַת יַעְרִֽים
verses.translation: A fool spurns a parent’s discipline,But one who heeds reproof becomes clever.

verses.hebrew_full: בֵּ֣ית צַ֭דִּיק חֹ֣סֶן רָ֑ב וּבִתְבוּאַ֖ת רָשָׁ֣ע נֶעְכָּֽרֶת
verses.translation: In the house of the righteous there is much treasure,But in the harvest of the wicked there is trouble.
verses.rashi: The house of a righteous man possesses great strength The Temple that David the Righteous built is a great strength and a mighty tower for Israel. but it becomes ruined with the bringing of the wicked man But with the bringing of the idols that Manasseh brought into it, it was ruined.

verses.hebrew_full: שִׂפְתֵ֣י חֲ֭כָמִים יְזָ֣רוּ דָ֑עַת וְלֵ֖ב כְּסִילִ֣ים לֹא־כֵֽן
verses.translation: The lips of the wise disseminate knowledge;Not so the minds of dullards.
verses.rashi: The lips of the wise crown knowledge יזרו, they crown knowledge, as in (Ex. 25: “a gold crown (זר).” is not so It is not true gold.

verses.hebrew_full: זֶ֣בַח רְ֭שָׁעִים תּוֹעֲבַ֣ת יְהֹוָ֑ה וּתְפִלַּ֖ת יְשָׁרִ֣ים רְצוֹנֽוֹ
verses.translation: The sacrifice of the wicked is an abomination to GOD,But the prayer of the upright is pleasing.
verses.rashi: The sacrifice of the wicked is an abomination of the Lord Balak and Balaam. but the prayer of the upright This is Moses.

verses.hebrew_full: תּוֹעֲבַ֣ת יְ֭הֹוָה דֶּ֣רֶךְ רָשָׁ֑ע וּמְרַדֵּ֖ף צְדָקָ֣ה יֶאֱהָֽב
verses.translation: The way of the wicked is an abomination to GOD,But those who pursue righteousness are loved.

verses.hebrew_full: מוּסָ֣ר רָ֭ע לְעֹזֵ֣ב אֹ֑רַח שׂוֹנֵ֖א תוֹכַ֣חַת יָמֽוּת
verses.translation: Discipline seems bad to whoever forsakes the way;One who spurns reproof will die.
verses.rashi: Harsh discipline will come to him who forsakes the way Harsh suffering is ready for him who transgresses the way of the Holy One, blessed be He.

verses.hebrew_full: שְׁא֣וֹל וַ֭אֲבַדּוֹן נֶ֣גֶד יְהֹוָ֑ה אַ֝֗ף כִּֽי־לִבּ֥וֹת בְּֽנֵי־אָדָֽם
verses.translation: Sheol and Abaddon lie exposed to GOD,How much more the minds of mortals!
verses.rashi: The grave and Destruction are opposite the Lord It is revealed to Him all that are therein. surely people’s hearts This is an inference from a major to a minor.

verses.hebrew_full: לֹ֣א יֶאֱהַב־לֵ֭ץ הוֹכֵ֣חַֽ ל֑וֹ אֶל־חֲ֝כָמִ֗ים לֹ֣א יֵלֵֽךְ
verses.translation: The scoffer, who dislikes being reproved,Will not resort to the wise.

verses.hebrew_full: לֵ֣ב שָׂ֭מֵחַ יֵיטִ֣ב פָּנִ֑ים וּבְעַצְּבַת־לֵ֝֗ב ר֣וּחַ נְכֵאָֽה
verses.translation: A joyful heart makes a cheerful face;A sad heart makes a despondent mood.
verses.rashi: A merry heart makes a cheerful face If you cheer up the heart of the Holy One, blessed be He, by following His ways, He will show you a cheerful face to do your will, but if you sadden Him, He will show you a breaking spirit, as it is stated (Gen. 6:6f): “And He became saddened to His heart. And the Lord said, ‘I will erase man, etc.’“ a breaking spirit (Talant in Old French, explained in Ecc. 1:6) a spirit of fury.

verses.hebrew_full: לֵ֣ב נָ֭בוֹן יְבַקֶּשׁ־דָּ֑עַת (ופני) וּפִ֥י כְ֝סִילִ֗ים יִרְעֶ֥ה אִוֶּֽלֶת
verses.translation: The mind of a prudent person seeks knowledge;The mouth of dullards pursues folly.

verses.hebrew_full: כׇּל־יְמֵ֣י עָנִ֣י רָעִ֑ים וְטֽוֹב־לֵ֝֗ב מִשְׁתֶּ֥ה תָמִֽיד
verses.translation: All the days of a pauper are wretched,But contentment is a feast without end.
verses.rashi: All the days of a poor man are wretched Even Sabbaths and festivals, as Samuel said. A change of diet is the beginning of intestinal disorders. but he who has a cheerful heart One whose heart is cheerful with his wealth. always has a feast All his years seem to him like days of feasting to teach you that a person should be happy with his lot (Avoth 4:1). And our Sages expounded [on this] in the chapter entitled Chelek (Sanh. 100b, 101a).

verses.hebrew_full: טוֹב־מְ֭עַט בְּיִרְאַ֣ת יְהֹוָ֑ה מֵאוֹצָ֥ר רָ֝֗ב וּמְה֥וּמָה בֽוֹ
verses.translation: Better a little with fear of GOD Than great wealth with confusion.
verses.rashi: Better a little with the fear of the Lord, than a great treasury and turmoil with it The voice of people shouting that the treasury was made from robbery and violence, as in (Amos 3:9): “And see great confusions within it and people being oppressed in its midst.”

words.eng: inflected third pers. singular m. of בּֽ◌ ᴵ
