verses.hebrew_full: ט֤וֹב אֲרֻחַ֣ת יָ֭רָק וְאַהֲבָה־שָׁ֑ם מִשּׁ֥וֹר אָ֝ב֗וּס וְשִׂנְאָה־בֽוֹ
verses.translation: Better a meal of vegetables where there is loveThan a fattened ox where there is hate.
verses.rashi: than a fattened ox To feed him fat meat and to show him an angry countenance. than a fattened ox אבוס. An ox into whose mouth they stuff the food against his will, in order to fatten him. Likewise (I Kings 5:3): “fatted (אבוסים) fowl.” And it can be explained allegorically as referring to the handful—the little bit offered up by the poor—[being better] than an ox for a sin-offering offered up by a wicked man. Another explanation: Better a repast of herbs To give a poor man. where there is love To show him a friendly countenance.

words.eng: to hate·inflected third pers. singular m. of בּֽ◌ ᴵ

verses.hebrew_full: אִ֣ישׁ חֵ֭מָה יְגָרֶ֣ה מָד֑וֹן וְאֶ֥רֶךְ אַ֝פַּ֗יִם יַשְׁקִ֥יט רִֽיב
verses.translation: A hot-tempered man provokes a quarrel;A patient person calms strife.
verses.rashi: A man of wrath stirs up quarrel A man who has no control over his anger, to be slow to anger, stirs up quarrels. but he who is slow to anger Who does not hasten to take revenge and to quarrel. abates strife It ends and abates by itself.

verses.hebrew_full: דֶּ֣רֶךְ עָ֭צֵל כִּמְשֻׂ֣כַת חָ֑דֶק וְאֹ֖רַח יְשָׁרִ֣ים סְלֻלָֽה
verses.translation: The way of a sluggard is like a hedge of thorns,But the path of the upright is paved.
verses.rashi: The way of a lazy man is like a hedge of thorns In his eyes, it appears as though the roads are fenced before him with a fence of thorns. like a hedge of Heb. כמשכת. A fence, as in (Isa. 5:5): “I will remove its hedge (משוכתו).” thorns Heb. חדק, thorns. but the path of the upright is even סללה, trodden and cleared. The Midrash Aggadah (Pesikta d’Rav Kahana 11a) states: The way of Esau is like a thorn entangled in a fleece of wool. If you extract it from here, it catches on here. Likewise, no one can extricate himself from his false accusations without money.

verses.hebrew_full: בֵּ֣ן חָ֭כָם יְשַׂמַּח־אָ֑ב וּכְסִ֥יל אָ֝דָ֗ם בּוֹזֶ֥ה אִמּֽוֹ
verses.translation: A wise son makes his father happy;A fool of a man humiliates his mother.
verses.rashi: A wise son makes his father happy, but a foolish person despises his mother He causes people to despise his mother.

verses.hebrew_full: אִ֭וֶּלֶת שִׂמְחָ֣ה לַחֲסַר־לֵ֑ב וְאִ֥ישׁ תְּ֝בוּנָ֗ה יְיַשֶּׁר־לָֽכֶת
verses.translation: Folly is joy to one devoid of sense;A prudent man walks a straight path.
verses.rashi: Folly is joy It is joy for the one devoid of sense.

verses.hebrew_full: הָפֵ֣ר מַ֭חֲשָׁבוֹת בְּאֵ֣ין ס֑וֹד וּבְרֹ֖ב יוֹעֲצִ֣ים תָּקֽוּם
verses.translation: Plans are foiled for want of counsel,But they succeed through many advisers.
verses.rashi: Plans are foiled for lack of counsel Without counsel, the plan will not be established.

verses.hebrew_full: שִׂמְחָ֣ה לָ֭אִישׁ בְּמַעֲנֵה־פִ֑יו וְדָבָ֖ר בְּעִתּ֣וֹ מַה־טּֽוֹב
verses.translation: Having a ready response feels satisfying,And how good is a word rightly timed!
verses.rashi: A man has joy with the response of his mouth According to its apparent meaning: Through a soft answer and gentle speech, people love him. Our Sages expounded it as referring to those who memorize the Torah; if they utter an answer with their mouth, they will remember it and rejoice with it. and how good is a word in time! They ask concerning the laws of Passover and the laws of Sukkoth in their season.

verses.hebrew_full: אֹ֣רַח חַ֭יִּים לְמַ֣עְלָה לְמַשְׂכִּ֑יל לְמַ֥עַן ס֝֗וּר מִשְּׁא֥וֹל מָֽטָּה
verses.translation: For the intelligent the path of life leads upward,In order to avoid Sheol below.
verses.rashi: The path of life is above the intelligent person As in (Isa. 6:2): “Seraphim stood above for Him.” Before the wise man, the path of life is arrayed and prepared.

verses.hebrew_full: בֵּ֣ית גֵּ֭אִים יִסַּ֥ח יְהֹוָ֑ה וְ֝יַצֵּ֗ב גְּב֣וּל אַלְמָנָֽה
verses.translation: GOD will tear down the house of the proud—But will establish the homestead of the widow.

verses.hebrew_full: תּוֹעֲבַ֣ת יְ֭הֹוָה מַחְשְׁב֣וֹת רָ֑ע וּ֝טְהֹרִ֗ים אִמְרֵי־נֹֽעַם
verses.translation: Evil thoughts are an abomination to GOD,But pleasant words are pure.

verses.hebrew_full: עֹכֵ֣ר בֵּ֭יתוֹ בּוֹצֵ֣עַ בָּ֑צַע וְשׂוֹנֵ֖א מַתָּנֹ֣ת יִחְיֶֽה
verses.translation: Whoever pursues ill-gotten gain makes trouble for the household;One who spurns gifts will live long.

verses.hebrew_full: לֵ֣ב צַ֭דִּיק יֶהְגֶּ֣ה לַעֲנ֑וֹת וּפִ֥י רְ֝שָׁעִ֗ים יַבִּ֥יעַ רָעֽוֹת
verses.translation: A righteous person’s heart as a source of speech, see note to Eccl. 5.1. rehearses the answer,But the mouth of the wicked blurts out evil things.
verses.rashi: The heart of a righteous man thinks to answer He will think and understand what to answer before replying.

verses.hebrew_full: רָח֣וֹק יְ֭הֹוָה מֵרְשָׁעִ֑ים וּתְפִלַּ֖ת צַדִּיקִ֣ים יִשְׁמָֽע
verses.translation: GOD is far from the wicked—But hears the prayer of the righteous.

verses.hebrew_full: מְֽאוֹר־עֵ֭ינַיִם יְשַׂמַּֽח־לֵ֑ב שְׁמוּעָ֥ה ט֝וֹבָ֗ה תְּדַשֶּׁן־עָֽצֶם
verses.translation: What brightens the eye gladdens the heart;Good news puts fat on the bones.
verses.rashi: The light of the eyes in Torah. makes the heart happy when they ask him something, and he knows what to answer. According to its simple meaning, it is to be interpreted according to its apparent meaning: A thing that is a delight to the sight of the eyes makes the heart happy and cleanses the sadness of the heart, such as a vegetable garden and flowing rivers.

verses.hebrew_full: אֹ֗זֶן שֹׁ֭מַעַת תּוֹכַ֣חַת חַיִּ֑ים בְּקֶ֖רֶב חֲכָמִ֣ים תָּלִֽין
verses.translation: One whose ear heeds the discipline of lifeLodges among the wise.

verses.hebrew_full: פּוֹרֵ֣עַ מ֭וּסָר מוֹאֵ֣ס נַפְשׁ֑וֹ וְשׁוֹמֵ֥עַ תּ֝וֹכַ֗חַת ק֣וֹנֶה לֵּֽב
verses.translation: Those who spurn discipline hate themselves;But those who heed reproof gain understanding.

verses.hebrew_full: יִרְאַ֣ת יְ֭הֹוָה מוּסַ֣ר חׇכְמָ֑ה וְלִפְנֵ֖י כָב֣וֹד עֲנָוָֽה
verses.translation: The fear of GOD is the discipline of wisdom;Humility precedes honor.
verses.rashi: and before honor there is humility Humility causes honor to come.

verses.hebrew_full: לְאָדָ֥ם מַעַרְכֵי־לֵ֑ב וּ֝מֵיְהֹוָ֗ה מַעֲנֵ֥ה לָשֽׁוֹן
verses.translation: Mortals may arrange their thoughts,But what they say depends on GOD.
verses.rashi: The preparations of the heart are man’s He arranges his counsel and his words in his heart. but the answer of the tongue is from the Lord When he comes to answer, the Holy One, blessed be He, causes him to stumble with his words—or if he merits, He prepares a good reply for him.

verses.hebrew_full: כׇּֽל־דַּרְכֵי־אִ֭ישׁ זַ֣ךְ בְּעֵינָ֑יו וְתֹכֵ֖ן רוּח֣וֹת יְהֹוָֽה
verses.translation: People look approvingly on their own ways,But GOD probes motives.
verses.rashi: but the Lord counts the spirits and counts the hearts; who is the good one, and who is the bad one.

verses.hebrew_full: גֹּ֣ל אֶל־יְהֹוָ֣ה מַעֲשֶׂ֑יךָ וְ֝יִכֹּ֗נוּ מַחְשְׁבֹתֶֽיךָ
verses.translation: Entrust your affairs to GOD,And your plans will succeed.
verses.rashi: Commit your affairs to the Lord גל. Roll and cast your necessities upon Him, and your plans will be established. Another explanation: Commit your affairs to the Lord pray to Him for all your necessities. will be established Will be founded and will endure.

verses.hebrew_full: כֹּ֤ל פָּעַ֣ל יְ֭הֹוָה לַֽמַּעֲנֵ֑הוּ וְגַם־רָ֝שָׁ֗ע לְי֣וֹם רָעָֽה
verses.translation: GOD made everything for a purpose,Even the wicked for an evil day.
verses.rashi: The Lord made everything for His praise Heb. למענהו. He made everything for His praise, as in (Ps. 147:7): “Praise (ענו) the Lord with thanksgiving.” Another explanation: To testify concerning Him. That is to say that His work testifies concerning Him, about His mighty acts. Both are in the Aggadah of Psalms (Midrash Tehillim 19:1). even the wicked man He made, to leave him for the day of evil. All this is for His praise.

verses.hebrew_full: תּוֹעֲבַ֣ת יְ֭הֹוָה כׇּל־גְּבַהּ־לֵ֑ב יָ֥ד לְ֝יָ֗ד לֹ֣א יִנָּקֶֽה
verses.translation: Everyone haughty is an abomination to GOD;Assuredly, they will not go unpunished.
verses.rashi: hand to hand he will not go unpunished From hand to hand the punishment for his haughtiness will come to him.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּחֶ֣סֶד וֶ֭אֱמֶת יְכֻפַּ֣ר עָוֺ֑ן וּבְיִרְאַ֥ת יְ֝הֹוָ֗ה ס֣וּר מֵרָֽע
verses.translation: Iniquity is expiated by loyalty and faithfulness,And evil is avoided through fear of GOD.
verses.rashi: and through fear of the Lord turn away from evil Through the fear of the Lord turn away from evil.

verses.hebrew_full: בִּרְצ֣וֹת יְ֭הֹוָה דַּרְכֵי־אִ֑ישׁ גַּם־א֝וֹיְבָ֗יו יַשְׁלִ֥ם אִתּֽוֹ
verses.translation: GOD, when pleased with someone’s conduct,May turn even their enemies into allies.
verses.rashi: When the Lord accepts a person’s ways, He will cause even his enemies to make peace with him He will appease [them] for him that they should make peace with him.

words.eng: to be in a covenant of peace
sign of the definite direct object

verses.hebrew_full: טוֹב־מְ֭עַט בִּצְדָקָ֑ה מֵרֹ֥ב תְּ֝בוּא֗וֹת בְּלֹ֣א מִשְׁפָּֽט
verses.translation: Better a little with righteousnessThan a large income with injustice.

verses.hebrew_full: לֵ֣ב אָ֭דָם יְחַשֵּׁ֣ב דַּרְכּ֑וֹ וַ֝יהֹוָ֗ה יָכִ֥ין צַעֲדֽוֹ
verses.translation: Mortals may plot out their course,But it is GOD who directs their steps.
verses.rashi: A man’s heart plans his way to walk on the straight way. but the Lord prepares his step as we learned: He who comes to purify himself is granted assistance.

verses.hebrew_full: קֶ֤סֶם עַֽל־שִׂפְתֵי־מֶ֑לֶךְ בְּ֝מִשְׁפָּ֗ט לֹ֣א יִמְעַל־פִּֽיו
verses.translation: There is magic on the lips of the king;He cannot err in judgment.
verses.rashi: There is magic on a king’s lips On the lips of a wise man sitting in judgment.

verses.hebrew_full: פֶּ֤לֶס וּמֹאזְנֵ֣י מִ֭שְׁפָּט לַיהֹוָ֑ה מַ֝עֲשֵׂ֗הוּ כׇּל־אַבְנֵי־כִֽיס
verses.translation: Honest scales and balances are GOD’s,Whose work includes all the weights in the bag.
verses.rashi: The balance and scales of justice are the Lord’s etc. to requite a person according to his deed. משפט is (justice in French, in German Gerechtigkeit, and so in Isaiah 7,) the retribution for a person’s sins with a balance and scales. all the weights in the bag Just as among the weights in the bag, there are large weights and small ones, so is everything according to a person’s deeds.

verses.hebrew_full: תּוֹעֲבַ֣ת מְ֭לָכִים עֲשׂ֣וֹת רֶ֑שַׁע כִּ֥י בִ֝צְדָקָ֗ה יִכּ֥וֹן כִּסֵּֽא
verses.translation: Wicked deeds are an abomination to kings,For the throne is established by righteousness.
verses.rashi: It is an abomination to kings to commit wickedness It is an abominable thing for judges, and it is not proper for them to commit wickedness.

verses.hebrew_full: רְצ֣וֹן מְ֭לָכִים שִׂפְתֵי־צֶ֑דֶק וְדֹבֵ֖ר יְשָׁרִ֣ים יֶאֱהָֽב
verses.translation: Truthful speech wins the favor of kings;They love those who speak honestly.

verses.hebrew_full: חֲמַת־מֶ֥לֶךְ מַלְאֲכֵי־מָ֑וֶת וְאִ֖ישׁ חָכָ֣ם יְכַפְּרֶֽנָּה
verses.translation: The king’s wrath is a messenger of death,But a man who is wise can appease it.
verses.rashi: The king’s wrath is like messengers of death, like messengers of death [but not angels of death]. will pacify it Will placate it.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּאוֹר־פְּנֵי־מֶ֥לֶךְ חַיִּ֑ים וּ֝רְצוֹנ֗וֹ כְּעָ֣ב מַלְקֽוֹשׁ
verses.translation: The king’s smile means life;His favor is like a rain cloud in spring.
verses.rashi: In the light of the King’s countenance is life The one to whom the Holy One, blessed be He, shows a bright countenance—it is life to him. Therefore, a person must straighten his way before Him. and His delight is like a cloud of the late rain Whoever is accepted by Him—His delight is beneficial to him like the cloud that brings the rain.

verses.hebrew_full: קְֽנֹה־חׇכְמָ֗ה מַה־טּ֥וֹב מֵחָר֑וּץ וּקְנ֥וֹת בִּ֝ינָ֗ה נִבְחָ֥ר מִכָּֽסֶף
verses.translation: How much better to acquire wisdom than gold;To acquire understanding is preferable to silver.

verses.hebrew_full: מְסִלַּ֣ת יְ֭שָׁרִים ס֣וּר מֵרָ֑ע שֹׁמֵ֥ר נַ֝פְשׁ֗וֹ נֹצֵ֥ר דַּרְכּֽוֹ
verses.translation: The highway of the upright avoids evil;Those who would preserve their lives watch their way.

verses.hebrew_full: לִפְנֵי־שֶׁ֥בֶר גָּא֑וֹן וְלִפְנֵ֥י כִ֝שָּׁל֗וֹן גֹּ֣בַהּ רֽוּחַ
verses.translation: Pride goes before ruin,Arrogance, before failure.

verses.hebrew_full: ט֣וֹב שְׁפַל־ר֭וּחַ אֶת־עֲנָוִ֑ים (עניים) מֵחַלֵּ֥ק שָׁ֝לָ֗ל אֶת־גֵּאִֽים
verses.translation: Better to be humble and among the lowlyThan to share spoils with the proud.
verses.rashi: It is better to be of humble spirit It is better to join the lowly and to be of humble spirit with them than to divide spoils with the haughty.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·poor
sign of the definite direct object·proud

verses.hebrew_full: מַשְׂכִּ֣יל עַל־דָּ֭בָר יִמְצָא־ט֑וֹב וּבוֹטֵ֖חַ בַּיהֹוָ֣ה אַשְׁרָֽיו
verses.translation: One who is adept in a matter will attain success;Happy is the one who trusts in GOD.
verses.rashi: He who considers a matter One who sets his heart to consider his matters, to weigh his ways, will find good. and he who trusts in the Lord is fortunate When he weighs his ways and sees that a commandment involves danger or monetary loss, but he trusts in the Holy One, blessed be He, and [still] does that good deed. is fortunate They are his fortunes.

verses.hebrew_full: לַֽחֲכַם־לֵ֭ב יִקָּרֵ֣א נָב֑וֹן וּמֶ֥תֶק שְׂ֝פָתַ֗יִם יֹסִ֥יף לֶֽקַח
verses.translation: The wise-hearted are called discerning;Those whose speech is pleasing gain wisdom.
verses.rashi: The wise-hearted shall be called understanding He who learned wisdom from his teacher. shall be called understanding. Ultimately, he will be understanding in matters and keen in his wisdom, and they will call him understanding. and the sweetness of speech increases learning When a person explains his words to a pupil and makes his words sweet with reasons, he increases learning.

verses.hebrew_full: מְק֣וֹר חַ֭יִּים שֵׂ֣כֶל בְּעָלָ֑יו וּמוּסַ֖ר אֱוִלִ֣ים אִוֶּֽלֶת
verses.translation: Good sense is a fountain of life to those who have it,And folly is the punishment of fools.
verses.rashi: Intelligence is a fountain of life to one who has it lit. a fountain of life is the intelligence of its possessor. And this is the interpretation of the verse: The intelligence of the one who has it is a fountain of life to him. but the chastisement of the fools is folly But the chastisement of the fools comes to them because of folly. And this is the apparent meaning: Folly is chastisement to the fools.

verses.hebrew_full: לֵ֣ב חָ֭כָם יַשְׂכִּ֣יל פִּ֑יהוּ וְעַל־שְׂ֝פָתָ֗יו יֹסִ֥יף לֶֽקַח
verses.translation: The mind of the wise makes their speech effectiveAnd increases the wisdom on their lips.
verses.rashi: The heart of a wise man gives his mouth intelligence His heart teaches his mouth to speak lucidly.

verses.hebrew_full: צוּף־דְּ֭בַשׁ אִמְרֵי־נֹ֑עַם מָת֥וֹק לַ֝נֶּ֗פֶשׁ וּמַרְפֵּ֥א לָעָֽצֶם
verses.translation: Pleasant words are like a honeycomb,Sweet to the palate and a cure for the body.
verses.rashi: a honeycomb Heb. צוף דבש, sweet as honey (bresche in O.F.). Pleasant words Words of Torah.

verses.hebrew_full: יֵ֤שׁ דֶּ֣רֶךְ יָ֭שָׁר לִפְנֵי־אִ֑ישׁ וְ֝אַחֲרִיתָ֗הּ דַּרְכֵי־מָֽוֶת
verses.translation: The road ahead may seem right,But in the end it can be a road to death.
verses.rashi: There is a way that is straight in a man’s view.

verses.hebrew_full: נֶ֣פֶשׁ עָ֭מֵל עָ֣מְלָה לּ֑וֹ כִּֽי־אָכַ֖ף עָלָ֣יו פִּֽיהוּ
verses.translation: The appetite of laborers labors for them,Because their hunger forces them on.
verses.rashi: labors for him It labors for his needs. when his mouth forces him When his mouth forces him and demands food, then his labor stands up for him, for he eats what he has already worked for.

verses.hebrew_full: אִ֣ישׁ בְּ֭לִיַּעַל כֹּרֶ֣ה רָעָ֑ה וְעַל־שְׂ֝פָת֗וֹ (שפתיו) כְּאֵ֣שׁ צָרָֽבֶת
verses.translation: A worthless man plots evil;What is on his lips is like a scorching fire.
verses.rashi: digs up evil In his heart, he plots evil. and on his lips is a kind of searing fire The evil burns on his lips until he expresses it with his mouth and executes it. a kind of searing fire Heb. צרבת burning, as in (Ezek. 21:3): “And all faces shall be scorched (ונצרבו) by it.”

verses.hebrew_full: אִ֣ישׁ תַּ֭הְפֻּכוֹת יְשַׁלַּ֣ח מָד֑וֹן וְ֝נִרְגָּ֗ן מַפְרִ֥יד אַלּֽוּף
verses.translation: A shifty man stirs up strife,And a querulous one alienates his friend.
verses.rashi: and a grumbler alienates the Lord And through his grumbling, he alienates the Lord of the world from himself.

verses.hebrew_full: אִ֣ישׁ חָ֭מָס יְפַתֶּ֣ה רֵעֵ֑הוּ וְ֝הוֹלִיכ֗וֹ בְּדֶ֣רֶךְ לֹא־טֽוֹב
verses.translation: A lawless man misleads his friend,Making him take the wrong way.

verses.hebrew_full: עֹצֶ֣ה עֵ֭ינָיו לַחְשֹׁ֣ב תַּהְפֻּכ֑וֹת קֹרֵ֥ץ שְׂ֝פָתָ֗יו כִּלָּ֥ה רָעָֽה
verses.translation: He closes his eyes while meditating deception;He purses his lips while deciding upon evil.
verses.rashi: He winks his eyes Heb. עצה, an expression of winking, and so (Ps. 32:8): “I will wink (איעצה) to you my eye.” bringing evil to pass כלה, as in (Esther 7:7) “that the evil was determined (כלתה) against him by the king.” He causes evil to come to the world.

verses.hebrew_full: עֲטֶ֣רֶת תִּפְאֶ֣רֶת שֵׂיבָ֑ה בְּדֶ֥רֶךְ צְ֝דָקָ֗ה תִּמָּצֵֽא
verses.translation: Gray hair is a crown of glory;It is attained by the way of righteousness.
verses.rashi: it will be found in the way of righteousness Through righteousness, they live long.

verses.hebrew_full: ט֤וֹב אֶ֣רֶךְ אַ֭פַּיִם מִגִּבּ֑וֹר וּמֹשֵׁ֥ל בְּ֝רוּח֗וֹ מִלֹּכֵ֥ד עִֽיר
verses.translation: Better to be forbearing than mighty,To have self-control than to conquer a city.
verses.rashi: and one who rules over his spirit He conquers his evil inclination.

verses.hebrew_full: בַּ֭חֵיק יוּטַ֣ל אֶת־הַגּוֹרָ֑ל וּ֝מֵיְהֹוָ֗ה כׇּל־מִשְׁפָּטֽוֹ
verses.translation: Lots are cast into the lap;The decision depends on GOD.
verses.rashi: The lot is cast in the lap By himself, a person cast lots. but all his judgment to choose for each one his share.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·lot

verses.hebrew_full: ט֤וֹב פַּ֣ת חֲ֭רֵבָה וְשַׁלְוָה־בָ֑הּ מִ֝בַּ֗יִת מָלֵ֥א זִבְחֵי־רִֽיב
verses.translation: Better a dry crust with peaceThan a house full of feasting with strife.
verses.rashi: Better a piece of dry bread and tranquility with it It would have been better for the Holy One, blessed be He, to destroy His Temple and His city, for He would enjoy tranquility from the sins of Israel. than a house full of sacrifices of strife For they were sacrificing sacrifices of strife in his Temple.

verses.hebrew_full: עֶֽבֶד־מַשְׂכִּ֗יל יִ֭מְשֹׁל בְּבֵ֣ן מֵבִ֑ישׁ וּבְת֥וֹךְ אַ֝חִ֗ים יַחֲלֹ֥ק נַחֲלָֽה
verses.translation: A capable servant will dominate an incompetent sonAnd share the inheritance with the brothers.
verses.rashi: An intelligent slave, etc. Because Nebuchadnezzar was wise enough to take three steps in honor of the Holy One, blessed be He, he achieved greatness and ruled over Israel, who performed disgraceful deeds, and he divided their inheritance before their eyes. Another explanation: A righteous proselyte is better than a wicked person who was home born, and in the future, he will share the spoils and the inheritance among the children of Israel, as it is said (Ezek. 47:23): “And it shall be, in whatever tribe the stranger will live, etc.”

verses.hebrew_full: מַצְרֵ֣ף לַ֭כֶּסֶף וְכ֣וּר לַזָּהָ֑ב וּבֹחֵ֖ן לִבּ֣וֹת יְהֹוָֽה
verses.translation: For silver—the crucible,For gold—the furnace,And GOD tests the mind.
verses.rashi: A refining pot is for silver A vessel in which they refine silver and which is made from burnt ash on earthenware is called מצרף, a refining pot. and a furnace is for gold A vessel in which gold is refined, a vessel in which gold is purified, like a broken pot, is called כור, a furnace. A refining pot is for silver, and a furnace is for gold The refining pot is made to refine silver for its work, and so is the furnace to purify the gold, but [peoples’] hearts—the Holy One, blessed be He, purifies them and tests them and knows their thoughts.

verses.hebrew_full: מֵ֭רַע מַקְשִׁ֣יב עַל־שְׂפַת־אָ֑וֶן שֶׁ֥קֶר מֵ֝זִ֗ין עַל־לְשׁ֥וֹן הַוֺּֽת
verses.translation: An evildoer listens to mischievous talk;A liar gives ear to malicious words.
verses.rashi: An evildoer A wicked man. hearkens to a language of violence and lies He accepts slander and false testimony. lends an ear Heb. מזין like מאזין He who lies lends an ear to destructive speech.

verses.hebrew_full: לֹעֵ֣ג לָ֭רָשׁ חֵרֵ֣ף עֹשֵׂ֑הוּ שָׂמֵ֥חַ לְ֝אֵ֗יד לֹ֣א יִנָּקֶֽה
verses.translation: One who mocks the poor affronts their Maker;One who rejoices over another’s misfortune will not go unpunished.

verses.hebrew_full: עֲטֶ֣רֶת זְ֭קֵנִים בְּנֵ֣י בָנִ֑ים וְתִפְאֶ֖רֶת בָּנִ֣ים אֲבוֹתָֽם
verses.translation: Grandchildren are the crown of their elders,And the glory of children is their parents.
verses.rashi: the crown of the aged when they see their children’s children going on the good way. and the glory of the children is their fathers When their fathers are righteous, it is glory for the children.

verses.hebrew_full: לֹא־נָאוָ֣ה לְנָבָ֣ל שְׂפַת־יֶ֑תֶר אַ֝֗ף כִּֽי־לְנָדִ֥יב שְׂפַת־שָֽׁקֶר
verses.translation: Lofty words are not fitting for a villain,Much less lying words for a noble.
verses.rashi: Proud words Heb. שפת יתר, words of pride. surely not lying speech a generous one And surely, lying speech does not befit a generous one.

verses.hebrew_full: אֶבֶן־חֵ֣ן הַ֭שֹּׁחַד בְּעֵינֵ֣י בְעָלָ֑יו אֶֽל־כׇּל־אֲשֶׁ֖ר יִפְנֶ֣ה יַשְׂכִּֽיל
verses.translation: A bribe seems like a charm to its user;It brings success at every turn.
verses.rashi: a precious stone When a person comes before the Holy One, blessed be He, and placates Him with words and returns to Him, it is as a precious stone and as pearls in His eyes. wherever he turns, he prospers In whatever he asks of Him, He makes him prosper.

verses.hebrew_full: מְֽכַסֶּה־פֶּ֭שַׁע מְבַקֵּ֣שׁ אַהֲבָ֑ה וְשֹׁנֶ֥ה בְ֝דָבָ֗ר מַפְרִ֥יד אַלּֽוּף
verses.translation: One who seeks love overlooks faults,But one who harps on a matter alienates friends.
verses.rashi: He who conceals transgression seeks love If one man sins against another, and the latter conceals it for him, does not remind him of his sin, and does not show him an angry face, he causes him to love him. but he who harps on a matter Who bears a grudge and reminds him, “You did such and such a thing to me.” Immediately... alienates the Lord Then he alienates the Lord of the world.

verses.hebrew_full: תֵּ֣חַת גְּעָרָ֣ה בְמֵבִ֑ין מֵהַכּ֖וֹת כְּסִ֣יל מֵאָֽה
verses.translation: A rebuke works on an intelligent personMore than one hundred blows on a fool.
verses.rashi: The humility caused by the rebuke of an understanding person Heb. תחת. The accent in the word תחת is above on the “tav,” unlike the rest of the Scriptures. Therefore, I say that it is a noun, like תחת and its explanation is as follows. The humility caused by a rebuke is recognizable in an understanding person more than one hundred blows that they strike the fool.

verses.hebrew_full: אַךְ־מְרִ֥י יְבַקֶּשׁ־רָ֑ע וּמַלְאָ֥ךְ אַ֝כְזָרִ֗י יְשֻׁלַּח־בּֽוֹ
verses.translation: Evildoers seek only to rebel;A ruthless messenger will be sent against them.
verses.rashi: He who is only rebellious seeks evil One whose every word is rebelliousness and stubbornness constantly seeks evil.

words.eng: to send·inflected third pers. singular m. of בּֽ◌ ᴵ

verses.hebrew_full: פָּג֬וֹשׁ דֹּ֣ב שַׁכּ֣וּל בְּאִ֑ישׁ וְאַל־כְּ֝סִ֗יל בְּאִוַּלְתּֽוֹ
verses.translation: Sooner meet a bereaved she-bearThan a fool immersed in nonsense.
verses.rashi: May a bereaving bear encounter a person It is better for a person that a bereaving bear encounter him rather than one of the foolish heathens, who entice him to idolatry.

verses.hebrew_full: מֵשִׁ֣יב רָ֭עָה תַּ֣חַת טוֹבָ֑ה לֹא־תָמ֥וּשׁ (תמיש) רָ֝עָ֗ה מִבֵּיתֽוֹ
verses.translation: Evil will never depart from the houseOf one who repays good with evil.

verses.hebrew_full: פּ֣וֹטֵֽר מַ֭יִם רֵאשִׁ֣ית מָד֑וֹן וְלִפְנֵ֥י הִ֝תְגַּלַּ֗ע הָרִ֥יב נְטֽוֹשׁ
verses.translation: To start a quarrel is to open a sluice;Before a dispute flares up, drop it.
verses.rashi: The beginning of strife is like letting out water Whoever commences a quarrel is like one who opens a hole in the stone walls of a water canal, and the water goes out through it, and the hole becomes progressively wider; so does the quarrel constantly escalate. and before you are exposed Before your shame is exposed, abandon the quarrel.

verses.hebrew_full: מַצְדִּ֣יק רָ֭שָׁע וּמַרְשִׁ֣יעַ צַדִּ֑יק תּוֹעֲבַ֥ת יְ֝הֹוָ֗ה גַּם־שְׁנֵיהֶֽם
verses.translation: To acquit the guilty and convict the innocent—Both are an abomination to GOD.

verses.hebrew_full: לָמָּה־זֶּ֣ה מְחִ֣יר בְּיַד־כְּסִ֑יל לִקְנ֖וֹת חׇכְמָ֣ה וְלֶב־אָֽיִן
verses.translation: What good is money in the hand of a foolTo purchase wisdom, when that person has no mind?
verses.rashi: Why is there a price in the fool’s hand to buy wisdom To learn the Torah. when the heart is not here In his heart, he has no intention of fulfilling it, and he learns only to gain a reputation.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּכׇל־עֵ֭ת אֹהֵ֣ב הָרֵ֑עַ וְאָ֥ח לְ֝צָרָ֗ה יִוָּלֵֽד
verses.translation: A friend is devoted at all times;A sibling is born to share adversity.
verses.rashi: At all times, love a friend You should always love friends, i.e., to acquire people who love you. for he is born a brother for adversity At the time of adversity, the friend will be born to you as a brother, to help you and to participate in your adversity.

verses.hebrew_full: אָדָ֣ם חֲסַר־לֵ֭ב תּוֹקֵ֣עַ כָּ֑ף עֹרֵ֥ב עֲ֝רֻבָּ֗ה לִפְנֵ֥י רֵעֵֽהוּ
verses.translation: Devoid of sense are the people who give their handTo stand surety for their fellows.
verses.rashi: A person without sense clasps hands [This refers to] surety of money. Another explanation: He clasps with the wicked to go in their ways, while he is already a guarantor for his friend. He already accepted surety for the Holy One, blessed be He, to keep His commandments.

verses.hebrew_full: אֹ֣הֵֽב פֶּ֭שַׁע אֹהֵ֣ב מַצָּ֑ה מַגְבִּ֥יהַּ פִּ֝תְח֗וֹ מְבַקֶּשׁ־שָֽׁבֶר
verses.translation: One who loves transgression loves strife;One who builds a high threshold invites broken bones.
verses.rashi: He who delights in transgression to sin against his friend. delights in quarrels To quarrel and strive. he who speaks haughtily lit. he who raises his opening, speaks with haughtiness, as in (Micah 7:5) “guard the openings of your mouth.”

verses.hebrew_full: עִקֶּשׁ־לֵ֭ב לֹ֣א יִמְצָא־ט֑וֹב וְנֶהְפָּ֥ךְ בִּ֝לְשׁוֹנ֗וֹ יִפּ֥וֹל בְּרָעָֽה
verses.translation: Crooked minds come to no good,And the double-tongued fall into trouble.

verses.hebrew_full: יֹלֵ֣ד כְּ֭סִיל לְת֣וּגָה ל֑וֹ וְלֹא־יִ֝שְׂמַ֗ח אֲבִ֣י נָבָֽל
verses.translation: One begets a dullard to one’s own grief;The father of a villain has no joy.

verses.hebrew_full: לֵ֣ב שָׂ֭מֵחַ יֵיטִ֣יב גֵּהָ֑ה וְר֥וּחַ נְ֝כֵאָ֗ה תְּיַבֶּשׁ־גָּֽרֶם
verses.translation: A joyful heart makes for good health; Despondency dries up the bones.
verses.rashi: A happy heart enhances one’s brilliance Heb. גהה. When a person is satisfied with his lot, his countenance shines.

verses.hebrew_full: שֹׁ֣חַד מֵ֭חֵק רָשָׁ֣ע יִקָּ֑ח לְ֝הַטּ֗וֹת אׇרְח֥וֹת מִשְׁפָּֽט
verses.translation: The wicked draw bribes out of their bosomTo pervert the course of justice.
verses.rashi: He will take a bribe from a wicked man’s bosom The Holy One, blessed be He, accepts words of humility and appeasement from the bosom of the wicked; i.e., in secret, between Him and them. to pervert the roads of justice To overturn his verdict from evil to good.

verses.hebrew_full: אֶת־פְּנֵ֣י מֵבִ֣ין חׇכְמָ֑ה וְעֵינֵ֥י כְ֝סִ֗יל בִּקְצֵה־אָֽרֶץ
verses.translation: Wisdom lies before the intelligent;The eyes of dullards range to the ends of the earth.
verses.rashi: Wisdom is directly in front of an understanding man The wisdom is directly in front of an understanding man. but the eyes of a fool are at the end of the earth saying, “Wisdom is inaccessible to me [lit. The wisdom is not found before me] because it is far from me. How will I be able to learn the Order Nezikin, which consists of thirty chapters; Tractate Kelim, consisting of thirty chapters; Tractate Shabbath, consisting of twenty-four chapters?” But, for the wise man, it is an easy matter: “Today I learn two chapters and tomorrow two chapters.” And he says, “This is what those who preceded me from time immemorial did.”

words.eng: to turn to or from; to go. Targ. Y. Gen. XXIV

verses.hebrew_full: כַּ֣עַס לְ֭אָבִיו בֵּ֣ן כְּסִ֑יל וּ֝מֶ֗מֶר לְיֽוֹלַדְתּֽוֹ
verses.translation: A stupid son is vexation for his fatherAnd a heartache for the woman who bore him.
verses.rashi: A foolish son causes anger to his father [A foolish son] like Jeroboam causes anger to the Holy One, blessed be He. and bitterness to her who bore him To the people of Israel, whom he caused to sin.

verses.hebrew_full: גַּ֤ם עֲנ֣וֹשׁ לַצַּדִּ֣יק לֹא־ט֑וֹב לְהַכּ֖וֹת נְדִיבִ֣ים עַל־יֹֽשֶׁר
verses.translation: To punish an innocent is surely not right,Or to flog the great for their uprightness.
verses.rashi: To punish also the righteous man is not good The Holy One, blessed be He, did not decide to obliterate the name of Israel from upon the earth, as it was not good in his sight to punish all of them. to strike the generous who possess uprightness To strike the good people among them, who were generous and possessing uprightness, as it is said (I Kings 19:18): “And I will leave over in Israel seven thousand—all the knees that did not kneel to the Baal.” the generous who possess uprightness Heb. נדיבים עלי ישר, an analogy of what is stated elsewhere (Isa. 32:8): “But the generous person plans generous deeds, and he, because of generous deeds, shall stand.” We find “a generous man because of generous deeds.”

verses.hebrew_full: חוֹשֵׂ֣ךְ אֲ֭מָרָיו יוֹדֵ֣עַ דָּ֑עַת (וקר) יְקַר־ר֝֗וּחַ אִ֣ישׁ תְּבוּנָֽה
verses.translation: One who is knowledgeable is sparing with words;A man of understanding is reticent.
verses.rashi: keeps back his words He who has knowledge does not talk excessively. he whose breath is dear (Whose words are dear; Salonica ed.) He who keeps back his words is a man of understanding.

verses.hebrew_full: גַּ֤ם אֱוִ֣יל מַ֭חֲרִישׁ חָכָ֣ם יֵחָשֵׁ֑ב אֹטֵ֖ם שְׂפָתָ֣יו נָבֽוֹן
verses.translation: Even fools who keep silent are deemed wise;Intelligent, while their mouth is shut.

verses.hebrew_full: לְֽ֭תַאֲוָה יְבַקֵּ֣שׁ נִפְרָ֑ד בְּכׇל־תּ֝וּשִׁיָּ֗ה יִתְגַּלָּֽע
verses.translation: Those who isolate themselves pursue their desires;They disdain all competence.
verses.rashi: He who is separated seeks lust He who is separated from the Holy One, blessed be He, not keeping His precepts, pursues the lust of his heart and his evil inclination, and finally... in all sound wisdom, he is exposed Among the wise, his disgrace will be revealed. Our Sages expounded this as referring to Lot, who separated from Abraham because of the lust of his heart, as it is stated (Gen. 13:11): “And Lot chose for himself the entire plain of the Jordan, etc.” This whole verse is stated concerning adultery. His end was that his shame was exposed in the synagogues and in the study halls, [when people read] (Deut. 23:4): “Neither an Ammonite nor a Moabite may enter [the congregation of the Lord].”

verses.hebrew_full: לֹא־יַחְפֹּ֣ץ כְּ֭סִיל בִּתְבוּנָ֑ה כִּ֝֗י אִֽם־בְּהִתְגַּלּ֥וֹת לִבּֽוֹ
verses.translation: Fools do not desire understanding,But only to air their thoughts.
verses.rashi: but in revealing his heart But in the revelation of his heart. He wishes to reveal what is in his heart.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּֽבוֹא־רָ֭שָׁע בָּ֣א גַם־בּ֑וּז וְֽעִם־קָל֥וֹן חֶרְפָּֽה
verses.translation: Along with the wicked comes derision,And with rogues, contempt.
verses.rashi: and with disdain disgrace comes. He who chooses disdain and adultery—it is a disgrace for him.

verses.hebrew_full: מַ֣יִם עֲ֭מֻקִּים דִּבְרֵ֣י פִי־אִ֑ישׁ נַ֥חַל נֹ֝בֵ֗עַ מְק֣וֹר חׇכְמָֽה
verses.translation: The words a person speaks are deep waters,A flowing stream, a fountain of wisdom.
verses.rashi: the wellspring of wisdom is like a flowing stream and like deep water. The words of a man’s mouth Heb. איש. Every instance of איש in Scripture is an expression of a mighty man, one who is great in might.

verses.hebrew_full: שְׂאֵ֣ת פְּנֵי־רָשָׁ֣ע לֹא־ט֑וֹב לְהַטּ֥וֹת צַ֝דִּ֗יק בַּמִּשְׁפָּֽט
verses.translation: It is not right to be partial to the guiltyAnd subvert the innocent in judgment.
verses.rashi: It is not good to be partial to the wicked As our Sages explained: It is not good for the wicked, that they are favored in this world and are requited in the next world. to subvert the righteous in judgment It is good for the righteous that the scale is weighed down for them to make them guilty in this world, where they are requited during their lifetime, and they merit the world to come. Its simple meaning, however, is according to its apparent meaning.

verses.hebrew_full: שִׂפְתֵ֣י כְ֭סִיל יָבֹ֣אוּ בְרִ֑יב וּ֝פִ֗יו לְֽמַהֲלֻמ֥וֹת יִקְרָֽא
verses.translation: The words of fools lead to strife;Their speech invites blows.
verses.rashi: The lips of a fool will enter a quarrel All his matters come about with an expression of quarreling. and his mouth calls out for blows He calls pains to come upon himself.

verses.hebrew_full: פִּֽי־כְ֭סִיל מְחִתָּה־ל֑וֹ וּ֝שְׂפָתָ֗יו מוֹקֵ֥שׁ נַפְשֽׁוֹ
verses.translation: The fools’ speech is their ruin;Their words are a trap for them.

verses.hebrew_full: דִּבְרֵ֣י נִ֭רְגָּן כְּמִֽתְלַהֲמִ֑ים וְ֝הֵ֗ם יָרְד֥וּ חַדְרֵי־בָֽטֶן
verses.translation: The words of a grumbler are bruising; They penetrate one’s inmost parts.
verses.rashi: The words of a grumbler are like blows Heb. כמתלהמים.

verses.hebrew_full: גַּ֭ם מִתְרַפֶּ֣ה בִמְלַאכְתּ֑וֹ אָ֥ח ה֝֗וּא לְבַ֣עַל מַשְׁחִֽית
verses.translation: One who is slack at workIs akin to a vandal.
verses.rashi: Even one who is slack in his work If he is a Torah scholar who separated himself from the Torah. to the destroyer To Satan.

verses.hebrew_full: מִגְדַּל־עֹ֭ז שֵׁ֣ם יְהֹוָ֑ה בּֽוֹ־יָר֖וּץ צַדִּ֣יק וְנִשְׂגָּֽב
verses.translation: GOD’s name is a tower of strengthTo which the righteous run and are safe.
verses.rashi: The name of the Lord is a tower of strength; the righteous runs into it and is strengthened Heb. ונשגב.

words.eng: inflected third pers. singular m. of בּֽ◌ ᴵ·to run

verses.hebrew_full: ה֣וֹן עָ֭שִׁיר קִרְיַ֣ת עֻזּ֑וֹ וּכְחוֹמָ֥ה נִ֝שְׂגָּבָ֗ה בְּמַשְׂכִּתֽוֹ
verses.translation: The wealth of the rich is their fortress;In their fancy it is a protective wall.
verses.rashi: and like a strong wall his wealth is to him in his chambers. Since the house is paved with a floor of stones that cover the earth, it is called אבן משכית, a covering stone.

verses.hebrew_full: לִפְנֵי־שֶׁ֭בֶר יִגְבַּ֣הּ לֶב־אִ֑ישׁ וְלִפְנֵ֖י כָב֣וֹד עֲנָוָֽה
verses.translation: Before ruin a person’s heart is proud;Humility goes before honor.

verses.hebrew_full: מֵשִׁ֣יב דָּ֭בָר בְּטֶ֣רֶם יִשְׁמָ֑ע אִוֶּ֥לֶת הִיא־ל֝֗וֹ וּכְלִמָּֽה
verses.translation: To answer before listening—This is foolish and disgraceful.

verses.hebrew_full: רֽוּחַ־אִ֭ישׁ יְכַלְכֵּ֣ל מַחֲלֵ֑הוּ וְר֥וּחַ נְ֝כֵאָ֗ה מִ֣י יִשָּׂאֶֽנָּה
verses.translation: A person’s spirit can endure an illness;But low spirits—who can bear them?
verses.rashi: A man’s spirit The spirit of a גבר, who is a mighty man, does not take worry to heart, but accepts with joy and love whatever befalls him. will sustain his illness He does not lose his strength.

verses.hebrew_full: לֵ֣ב נָ֭בוֹן יִקְנֶה־דָּ֑עַת וְאֹ֥זֶן חֲ֝כָמִ֗ים תְּבַקֶּשׁ־דָּֽעַת
verses.translation: The mind of an intelligent person acquires knowledge;The ears of the wise seek out knowledge.

verses.hebrew_full: מַתָּ֣ן אָ֭דָם יַרְחִ֣יב ל֑וֹ וְלִפְנֵ֖י גְדֹלִ֣ים יַנְחֶֽנּוּ
verses.translation: A gift eases a person’s wayAnd gives access to the great.
verses.rashi: A man’s gift will make room [This is to be understood] according to its simple meaning, but, according to its midrashic interpretation, it deals with those who give (sic) charity, which widens his share in the future world, as well as during his lifetime, and it will lead him before the great, who say that he is esteemed.

verses.hebrew_full: צַדִּ֣יק הָרִאשׁ֣וֹן בְּרִיב֑וֹ (יבא) וּבָֽא־רֵ֝עֵ֗הוּ וַחֲקָרֽוֹ
verses.translation: The first to speak in court seems rightTill the other party cross-examines.

verses.hebrew_full: מִ֭דְיָנִים יַשְׁבִּ֣ית הַגּוֹרָ֑ל וּבֵ֖ין עֲצוּמִ֣ים יַפְרִֽיד
verses.translation: The lot puts an end to strifeAnd separates those locked in dispute.
verses.rashi: and it separates contentious people Heb. עצומים, these are the litigants, as in (Isa. 41: 21): “‘present your strong points (עצמותיכם).’” As their pleas are strong, they are called עצומים, either an expression of strength or an expression of closing, as in (ibid. 33:15) “and closes (עצם) his eyes.”

verses.hebrew_full: אָ֗ח נִפְשָׁ֥ע מִקִּרְיַת־עֹ֑ז (ומדונים) וּ֝מִדְיָנִ֗ים כִּבְרִ֥יחַ אַרְמֽוֹן
verses.translation: A sibling offended is more formidable than a stronghold;Such strife is like the bars of a fortress.
verses.rashi: A rebellious brother...of a strong city There is a brother who rebels against his brother and loses a strong city, e.g. Lot against Abraham and Esau against Jacob. and the quarrels are like the bolt of a castle The quarrel between them separates them forever, like a bolt with which they lock the gates of a castle, so that no one should enter it.

verses.hebrew_full: מִפְּרִ֣י פִי־אִ֭ישׁ תִּשְׂבַּ֣ע בִּטְנ֑וֹ תְּבוּאַ֖ת שְׂפָתָ֣יו יִשְׂבָּֽע
verses.translation: A man’s belly is filled by the fruit of his mouth;He will be filled by the produce of his lips.

verses.hebrew_full: מָ֣וֶת וְ֭חַיִּים בְּיַד־לָשׁ֑וֹן וְ֝אֹהֲבֶ֗יהָ יֹאכַ֥ל פִּרְיָֽהּ
verses.translation: Death and life are in the power of the tongue;Those who love it will eat its fruit.
verses.rashi: and those who love it will eat its produce He who loves his tongue and accustoms it to [speaking words of] Torah, partakes of its reward in this world.

verses.hebrew_full: מָצָ֣א אִ֭שָּׁה מָ֣צָא ט֑וֹב וַיָּ֥פֶק רָ֝צ֗וֹן מֵיְהֹוָֽה
verses.translation: He who finds a wife has found happinessAnd has won GOD’s favor.
verses.rashi: He who has found a wife has found good He who has found the Torah; and according to its apparent meaning, a good wife. and has obtained favor And has obtained. This is its simple meaning. Another explanation: A man who found a wife and found good, has obtained favor. That man obtains favor from the Holy One, blessed be He. [This is in the name of] Rabbi Joseph Kara.

verses.hebrew_full: תַּחֲנוּנִ֥ים יְדַבֶּר־רָ֑שׁ וְ֝עָשִׁ֗יר יַעֲנֶ֥ה עַזּֽוֹת
verses.translation: The pauper speaks in terms of mercy;The rich person’s answer is harsh.
verses.rashi: A poor man speaks with supplications This one is accustomed to [speak] in this manner, and that one is accustomed to [speak] in that manner. He teaches you a rule of conduct; that although the rich man answers with impudence, the poor man should speak with supplications, and so is the matter of a teacher to a pupil.

verses.hebrew_full: אִ֣ישׁ רֵ֭עִים לְהִתְרֹעֵ֑עַ וְיֵ֥שׁ אֹ֝הֵ֗ב דָּבֵ֥ק מֵאָֽח
verses.translation: There are companions to keep one company,And there is a friend more devoted than a sibling.
verses.rashi: A man acquires friends with whom to associate A man who acquires friends for himself [will find] that the day will arrive when he will need them, and they will befriend him. Now, if you ask, “What of it?” there is a friend who sticks closer than a brother, for he will befriend him more than his kin and his brothers.

verses.hebrew_full: טֽוֹב־רָ֭שׁ הוֹלֵ֣ךְ בְּתֻמּ֑וֹ מֵעִקֵּ֥שׁ שְׂ֝פָתָ֗יו וְה֣וּא כְסִֽיל
verses.translation: Better a pauper who lives blamelesslyThan one who speaks perversely and is a dullard.

verses.hebrew_full: גַּ֤ם בְּלֹא־דַ֣עַת נֶ֣פֶשׁ לֹא־ט֑וֹב וְאָ֖ץ בְּרַגְלַ֣יִם חוֹטֵֽא
verses.translation: A person without knowledge is surely not good;One who moves hurriedly blunders.
verses.rashi: It is also not good that a soul be without knowledge It is not good for a person to be without Torah. and he who hastens with his feet sins The sinner treads and tramples the sins with his heels and says, “This is of minor import; I will transgress it.” And our Sages explained: It is also...without knowledge, as referring to one who compels his wife to copulate with him against her will. and he who hastens with his feet sins This refers to one who performs the sex act and repeats it. Another explanation: One who walks on grass on the Sabbath.

verses.hebrew_full: אִוֶּ֣לֶת אָ֭דָם תְּסַלֵּ֣ף דַּרְכּ֑וֹ וְעַל־יְ֝הֹוָ֗ה יִזְעַ֥ף לִבּֽוֹ
verses.translation: Mortals’ folly subverts their way,And their hearts rage against GOD.
verses.rashi: A man’s folly perverts his way Harm comes upon him because of his sin, because his folly perverts his way and he commits sins for which he is punished; and when the trouble befalls him, his heart is wroth with the Holy One, blessed be He, and he questions the Divine standard of justice, e.g. Joseph’s brothers, who said (Gen. 42:28): “What is this that God has done to us?”

verses.hebrew_full: ה֗וֹן יֹ֭סִיף רֵעִ֣ים רַבִּ֑ים וְ֝דָ֗ל מֵרֵעֵ֥הוּ יִפָּרֵֽד
verses.translation: Wealth makes many friends,But a pauper is left friendless.
verses.rashi: Wealth adds many friends Heb. רעים רבים, many lovers. And it can be interpreted as concerning one who learned Torah.

verses.hebrew_full: עֵ֣ד שְׁ֭קָרִים לֹ֣א יִנָּקֶ֑ה וְיָפִ֥יחַ כְּ֝זָבִ֗ים לֹ֣א יִמָּלֵֽט
verses.translation: A false witness will not go unpunished;One who testifies lies will not escape.

verses.hebrew_full: רַ֭בִּים יְחַלּ֣וּ פְנֵֽי־נָדִ֑יב וְכׇל־הָ֝רֵ֗עַ לְאִ֣ישׁ מַתָּֽן
verses.translation: Many court the favor of a noble,And all are the friends of a man who dispenses gifts.
verses.rashi: The great will beg the favor of a generous man, and everyone is a friend All become friends to a man who gives gifts. This may be interpreted as referring to those who give charity, and it may also be interpreted as referring to those who disseminate the Torah.

verses.hebrew_full: כׇּ֥ל־אֲחֵי־רָ֨שׁ שְֽׂנֵאֻ֗הוּ אַ֤ף כִּ֣י מְ֭רֵעֵהוּ רָחֲק֣וּ מִמֶּ֑נּוּ מְרַדֵּ֖ף אֲמָרִ֣ים (לא) לוֹ־הֵֽמָּה
verses.translation: The poor are despised even by their kin;How much more are they shunned by their friends!One who pursues words—they are of no avail.
verses.rashi: All the kinsmen of a poor man lit. the brothers, the kinsmen. surely his friends distance themselves Surely his friends, his companions, and his lovers. he pursues statements that are fit for him He says, “So-and-so and so-and-so are my relatives. So-and-so and so-and-so are my friends.” And all are words of futility. This can be interpreted as concerning one who is poor in Torah and in good deeds. he pursues statements which are futile He seeks to promulgate the halachah, but it is not in his possession. The midrash interprets it as concerning Joseph, who spread slander about his brothers. He would say to his father that they were suspected of [eating] limbs from a living animal. Therefore, they hated him, and he was called a poor man because he would spread false rumors about them and look for statements that were not true (לא המה). We read לו, “to him,” for he would pursue statements for his own benefit, and in Joseph’s matter, they were his, because his slander returned upon him. He would say that they ate limbs from a living animal, but Scripture testifies about them that even in the time of sin, they would slaughter a kid, as it is said: (Gen. 37:31) “and they slaughtered a kid.” He would say that they degraded the sons of the maidservants. Therefore, Joseph was sold as a slave. He would say that they gazed upon the women of the land. Said the Holy One, blessed be He, “By your life, I will incite a bear upon you.” Therefore, (ibid. 39:7) “his master’s wife lifted, etc.” [This appears] in Genesis Rabbah [see 84:7].

verses.hebrew_full: קֹֽנֶה־לֵּ֭ב אֹהֵ֣ב נַפְשׁ֑וֹ שֹׁמֵ֥ר תְּ֝בוּנָ֗ה לִמְצֹא־טֽוֹב
verses.translation: Those who acquire wisdom are their own best friend;They preserve understanding and attain happiness.

verses.hebrew_full: עֵ֣ד שְׁ֭קָרִים לֹ֣א יִנָּקֶ֑ה וְיָפִ֖יחַ כְּזָבִ֣ים יֹאבֵֽד
verses.translation: A false witness will not go unpunished;One who testifies falsely is doomed.

verses.hebrew_full: לֹא־נָאוֶ֣ה לִכְסִ֣יל תַּעֲנ֑וּג אַ֝֗ף כִּֽי־לְעֶ֤בֶד מְשֹׁ֬ל בְּשָׂרִֽים
verses.translation: Luxury is not fitting for a dullard,Much less that a servant rule over princes.
verses.rashi: Pleasure does not befit a fool, much less a slave It is derived by an a fortiori conclusion that it does not befit a slave to rule over princes.

verses.hebrew_full: שֵׂ֣כֶל אָ֭דָם הֶאֱרִ֣יךְ אַפּ֑וֹ וְ֝תִפְאַרְתּ֗וֹ עֲבֹ֣ר עַל־פָּֽשַׁע
verses.translation: People show intelligence by their forbearance;It is their glory when they overlook an offense.
verses.rashi: It is good sense for a man to be slow to anger Heb. האריך, like להאריך, to be slow. If he did so, it is good sense for him. and it is his glory that he pass over his retaliations.

words.eng: to pass over or by or through

verses.hebrew_full: נַ֣הַם כַּ֭כְּפִיר זַ֣עַף מֶ֑לֶךְ וּכְטַ֖ל עַל־עֵ֣שֶׂב רְצוֹנֽוֹ
verses.translation: The rage of a king is like the roar of a lion;His favor is like dew upon the grass.
verses.rashi: The King’s fury is like a lion’s roar [The fury of] the Holy One, blessed be He.

verses.hebrew_full: הַוֺּ֣ת לְ֭אָבִיו בֵּ֣ן כְּסִ֑יל וְדֶ֥לֶף טֹ֝רֵ֗ד מִדְיְנֵ֥י אִשָּֽׁה
verses.translation: A stupid son is a calamity to his father;The nagging of a wife is like the endless dripping of water.
verses.rashi: a constant dripping lit. a driving drip. [As] the dripping of the rain that falls in the house disturbs and drives out the inhabitants of the house, so are the quarrels of a bad wife.

verses.hebrew_full: בַּ֣יִת וָ֭הוֹן נַחֲלַ֣ת אָב֑וֹת וּ֝מֵיְהֹוָ֗ה אִשָּׁ֥ה מַשְׂכָּֽלֶת
verses.translation: Property and riches are bequeathed by fathers,But an efficient wife comes from GOD.

verses.hebrew_full: עַ֭צְלָה תַּפִּ֣יל תַּרְדֵּמָ֑ה וְנֶ֖פֶשׁ רְמִיָּ֣ה תִרְעָֽב
verses.translation: Laziness induces sleep,And a negligent person will go hungry.
verses.rashi: Laziness causes one to fall into a deep sleep Laziness causes one to fall asleep. This is the figure, but the allegory is that it concerns one who neglects his studies; it will ultimately come about that people will ask him words of wisdom, and he will be sound asleep. and a deceitful soul shall suffer hunger He will ultimately become poor. Similarly, one who is deceitful in his studies, making his Torah into “bundles,” will ultimately forget.

verses.hebrew_full: שֹׁמֵ֣ר מִ֭צְוָה שֹׁמֵ֣ר נַפְשׁ֑וֹ בּוֹזֵ֖ה דְרָכָ֣יו (יומת) יָמֽוּת
verses.translation: Those who have regard for their lives pay regard to commandments;Those who are heedless of their ways will die.
verses.rashi: He who keeps a commandment keeps his soul; he who despises his ways will die He does not take heed to weigh them.

verses.hebrew_full: מַלְוֵ֣ה יְ֭הֹוָה ח֣וֹנֵֽן דָּ֑ל וּ֝גְמֻל֗וֹ יְשַׁלֶּם־לֽוֹ
verses.translation: Those who are generous to the poor make a loan to GOD—They will receive their due.
verses.rashi: He who is gracious to a poor man lends to the Lord, and He will repay him his reward When he becomes ill and is close to death, his charity defends him before the Divine standard of justice, saying, “The poor man’s soul was struggling to leave him because of hunger, and this one sustained it. I, too, will return his soul to him.”

words.eng: to be in a covenant of peace·unto him; v. ל־.

verses.hebrew_full: יַסֵּ֣ר בִּ֭נְךָ כִּֽי־יֵ֣שׁ תִּקְוָ֑ה וְאֶל־הֲ֝מִית֗וֹ אַל־תִּשָּׂ֥א נַפְשֶֽׁךָ
verses.translation: Discipline your son while there is still hope,And do not set your heart on his destruction.
verses.rashi: Chastise your son for there is hope, but do not set your heart on his destruction Do not deal him a lethal blow.

verses.hebrew_full: (גרל) גְּֽדׇל־חֵ֭מָה נֹ֣שֵׂא עֹ֑נֶשׁ כִּ֥י אִם־תַּ֝צִּ֗יל וְע֣וֹד תּוֹסִֽף
verses.translation: A hot-tempered man incurs punishment;If you try to save him you will only make it worse.
verses.rashi: A hot-tempered man incurs punishment, etc. But if you pass over your anger and save your enemy if you see harm coming to him, you will yet increase your days and have much goodness.

verses.hebrew_full: שְׁמַ֣ע עֵ֭צָה וְקַבֵּ֣ל מוּסָ֑ר לְ֝מַ֗עַן תֶּחְכַּ֥ם בְּאַחֲרִיתֶֽךָ
verses.translation: Listen to advice and accept disciplineIn order that you may be wise in the end.

verses.hebrew_full: רַבּ֣וֹת מַחֲשָׁב֣וֹת בְּלֶב־אִ֑ישׁ וַעֲצַ֥ת יְ֝הֹוָ֗ה הִ֣יא תָקֽוּם
verses.translation: Many designs are in a person’s mind,But it is GOD’s plan that is accomplished.

verses.hebrew_full: תַּאֲוַ֣ת אָדָ֣ם חַסְדּ֑וֹ וְטֽוֹב־רָ֝֗שׁ מֵאִ֥ישׁ כָּזָֽב
verses.translation: Greed is a reproach to a man; Better be poor than a deceitful man.
verses.rashi: The attraction to a man is his kindness The main reason why people are attracted to a person is because of his kindness. and a poor man is better than a liar And if he is a liar, who promises but does not perform, a poor man is better than he.

verses.hebrew_full: יִרְאַ֣ת יְהֹוָ֣ה לְחַיִּ֑ים וְשָׂבֵ֥עַ יָ֝לִ֗ין בַּל־יִפָּ֥קֶד רָֽע
verses.translation: One who fears GOD earns life,And shall abide in contentment, Free from misfortune.
verses.rashi: and he will rest satisfied He who fears the Lord will rest, satisfied that he will not be visited by any evil.

verses.hebrew_full: טָ֘מַ֤ן עָצֵ֣ל יָ֭דוֹ בַּצַּלָּ֑חַת גַּם־אֶל־פִּ֝֗יהוּ לֹ֣א יְשִׁיבֶֽנָּה
verses.translation: A sluggard buries a hand in the bowl,And will not even bring it to the mouth.
verses.rashi: A lazy man hid his hand in a kettle Heb. בצלחת, in a hot kettle, in which, when removed from the fire and emptied, one may warm his hand. Similar to it in Chronicles (II 35:13): “they cooked in pots, in cauldrons, and in kettles (בצלחות),” In the name of Rabbi Isaac HaLevi I heard that בצלחת is derived from the expression of the Aramaic translation of ויבקע “and he split,” which is וצלח. At the time of the cold and the frost, since the cold and the frost crack and split the hands. [Hence, we render: A lazy man hid his hand in winter.] But I heard that is a rend of a cloak called fenditura [in Italian, in German Riss, Spalt, Schlitz], i.e., he hides it in his bosom.

verses.hebrew_full: לֵ֣ץ תַּ֭כֶּה וּפֶ֣תִי יַעְרִ֑ם וְהוֹכִ֥יחַ לְ֝נָב֗וֹן יָבִ֥ין דָּֽעַת
verses.translation: Beat scoffers and the simple will become clever;Reprove the intelligent and they gain knowledge.
verses.rashi: Beat a scorner, and a simple man will gain cunning Through the plagues visited upon Pharaoh and the war with Amalek, Jethro gained cunning and converted.

verses.hebrew_full: מְֽשַׁדֶּד־אָ֭ב יַבְרִ֣יחַ אֵ֑ם בֵּ֝֗ן מֵבִ֥ישׁ וּמַחְפִּֽיר
verses.translation: A son who causes shame and disgracePlunders his father, puts his mother to flight.
verses.rashi: A son who causes shame and disgrace robs his father, puts his mother to flight Heb. יבריח, puts his mother to flight. A son who causes shame and disgrace Whose ways are disgraceful. Since Sarah saw the son of the Egyptian maidservant making sport with idolatry and adultery, he caused his mother to be sent away, “and the matter displeased Abraham” (Gen. 21:11).

words.eng: to deal violently with·father of an individual

verses.hebrew_full: חֲֽדַל־בְּ֭נִי לִשְׁמֹ֣עַ מוּסָ֑ר לִ֝שְׁג֗וֹת מֵאִמְרֵי־דָֽעַת
verses.translation: My son, cease to stray from words of knowledgeAnd receive discipline.
verses.rashi: My son, cease to stray, etc. This is an elliptical verse, and this is its explanation: My son, cease to stray from words of knowledge in order to hear discipline. cease to stray Heb. לשגות, like מלשגות, from straying. Likewise, (Num. 9:13): “and refrains (וחדל) from performing (לעשות) the Passover service.” Also, (Gen. 41:49): “they stopped counting (לספר).”

verses.hebrew_full: עֵ֣ד בְּ֭לִיַּעַל יָלִ֣יץ מִשְׁפָּ֑ט וּפִ֥י רְ֝שָׁעִ֗ים יְבַלַּע־אָֽוֶן
verses.translation: A malicious witness scoffs at justice,And the speech of the wicked conceals mischief.
verses.rashi: A godless witness testifies judgment He testifies against a man [with] the rhetoric of the judgments of tortures and death. but violence will devour the mouth of the wicked The violence will swallow him up.

verses.hebrew_full: נָכ֣וֹנוּ לַלֵּצִ֣ים שְׁפָטִ֑ים וּ֝מַהֲלֻמ֗וֹת לְגֵ֣ו כְּסִילִֽים
verses.translation: Punishments are in store for scoffersAnd blows for the backs of dullards.
verses.rashi: are prepared for the scorners The Holy One, blessed be He, prepared punishments of zaraath for this scorner who slanders.

verses.hebrew_full: לֵ֣ץ הַ֭יַּיִן הֹמֶ֣ה שֵׁכָ֑ר וְכׇל־שֹׁ֥גֶה בּ֝֗וֹ לֹ֣א יֶחְכָּֽם
verses.translation: Wine is a scoffer, strong drink a roisterer;No one who is muddled by them will ever grow wise.

words.eng: inflected third pers. singular m. of בּֽ◌ ᴵ

verses.hebrew_full: נַ֣הַם כַּ֭כְּפִיר אֵ֣ימַת מֶ֑לֶךְ מִ֝תְעַבְּר֗וֹ חוֹטֵ֥א נַפְשֽׁוֹ
verses.translation: The terror of a king is like the roar of a lion;Whoever provokes it forfeits life.
verses.rashi: Fear of a king is like a lion’s roar; he who provokes him Who angers him.

words.eng: to pass over or by or through

verses.hebrew_full: כָּב֣וֹד לָ֭אִישׁ שֶׁ֣בֶת מֵרִ֑יב וְכׇל־אֱ֝וִ֗יל יִתְגַּלָּֽע
verses.translation: It is honorable for a man to desist from strife,But every fool becomes embroiled.
verses.rashi: It is honor for a man to refrain from quarreling To rest from quarrel. and every fool who does not refrain from quarreling—his disgrace will be exposed.

verses.hebrew_full: מֵ֭חֹרֶף עָצֵ֣ל לֹא־יַחֲרֹ֑שׁ (ישאל) וְשָׁאַ֖ל בַּקָּצִ֣יר וָאָֽיִן
verses.translation: In winter sluggards do not plow;At harvesttime they seek, and find nothing.
verses.rashi: Because of the winter, a lazy man does not plow Because of the cold, a lazy man sits and does no work, neither does he engage in Torah.

verses.hebrew_full: מַ֣יִם עֲ֭מֻקִּים עֵצָ֣ה בְלֶב־אִ֑ישׁ וְאִ֖ישׁ תְּבוּנָ֣ה יִדְלֶֽנָּה
verses.translation: The designs in a person’s mind are deep waters,But a man of understanding can draw them out.
verses.rashi: Counsel in man’s heart is like deep water A halachah in a wise man’s heart is sealed. but a man of understanding will draw it out An understanding pupil comes and draws it out of his innards.

verses.hebrew_full: רׇב־אָדָ֗ם יִ֭קְרָא אִ֣ישׁ חַסְדּ֑וֹ וְאִ֥ישׁ אֱ֝מוּנִ֗ים מִ֣י יִמְצָֽא
verses.translation: He calls many a man his loyal friend,But who can find a faithful man?
verses.rashi: Many people call upon the man who promises them kindness There are many people who rely on their friends who promise them kindness, and they call them at the time of their straits. but who can find a trustworthy man? who promises and keeps his promise. who promises them kindness Heb. איש חסדו, lit. a man of his kindness, who promises him to do kindness.

verses.hebrew_full: מִתְהַלֵּ֣ךְ בְּתֻמּ֣וֹ צַדִּ֑יק אַשְׁרֵ֖י בָנָ֣יו אַחֲרָֽיו
verses.translation: The righteous live blamelessly;Happy are their children who come after them.

verses.hebrew_full: מֶ֗לֶךְ יוֹשֵׁ֥ב עַל־כִּסֵּא־דִ֑ין מְזָרֶ֖ה בְעֵינָ֣יו כׇּל־רָֽע
verses.translation: The king seated on the throne of judgmentCan winnow out all evil by his glance.
verses.rashi: A king sits upon a throne of judgment This may be explained as referring to the Holy One, blessed be He, or it may be explained as referring to the true judges.

verses.hebrew_full: מִֽי־יֹ֭אמַר זִכִּ֣יתִי לִבִּ֑י טָ֝הַ֗רְתִּי מֵחַטָּאתִֽי
verses.translation: Who can say, “I have cleansed my heart,I am purged of my sin”?

verses.hebrew_full: אֶ֣בֶן וָ֭אֶבֶן אֵיפָ֣ה וְאֵיפָ֑ה תּוֹעֲבַ֥ת יְ֝הֹוָ֗ה גַּם־שְׁנֵיהֶֽם
verses.translation: False weights and false measures—Both are an abomination to GOD.

verses.hebrew_full: גַּ֣ם בְּ֭מַעֲלָלָיו יִתְנַכֶּר־נָ֑עַר אִם־זַ֖ךְ וְאִם־יָשָׁ֣ר פׇּעֳלֽוֹ
verses.translation: Children may be dissembling in their behaviorEven though their actions are blameless and proper.

verses.hebrew_full: אֹ֣זֶן שֹׁ֭מַעַת וְעַ֣יִן רֹאָ֑ה יְ֝הֹוָ֗ה עָשָׂ֥ה גַם־שְׁנֵיהֶֽם
verses.translation: The ear that hears, the eye that sees— GOD made them both.
verses.rashi: The hearing ear and the seeing eye, etc. They are His handiwork, and He desires an ear that hears reproof and an eye that sees what will develop.

verses.hebrew_full: אַל־תֶּאֱהַ֣ב שֵׁ֭נָה פֶּן־תִּוָּרֵ֑שׁ פְּקַ֖ח עֵינֶ֣יךָ שְֽׂבַֽע־לָֽחֶם
verses.translation: Do not love sleep lest you be impoverished;Keep your eyes open and you will have plenty of food.
verses.rashi: lest you become poor Heb. תורש you become poor (רש).

verses.hebrew_full: רַ֣ע רַ֭ע יֹאמַ֣ר הַקּוֹנֶ֑ה וְאֹזֵ֥ל ל֝֗וֹ אָ֣ז יִתְהַלָּֽל
verses.translation: “Bad, bad,” says the buyer,Then goes away in self-congratulation.
verses.rashi: “It is bad, it is bad,” says the buyer If one acquires Torah through poverty and the pains of hunger, he says, “Woe is to me for this evil and for this trouble,” but when he goes away full of wisdom... then he boasts about the pain he suffered. but when he goes away Heb. ואזל, but when he goes away.

verses.hebrew_full: יֵ֣שׁ זָ֭הָב וְרׇב־פְּנִינִ֑ים וּכְלִ֥י יְ֝קָ֗ר שִׂפְתֵי־דָֽעַת
verses.translation: Gold is plentiful, jewels abundant,But wise speech is a precious object.

verses.hebrew_full: לְֽקַח־בִּ֭גְדוֹ כִּי־עָ֣רַב זָ֑ר וּבְעַ֖ד (נכרים) נׇכְרִיָּ֣ה חַבְלֵֽהוּ
verses.translation: Seize his garment, for he stood surety for another; Take it as a pledge, [for he stood surety] for an unfamiliar woman.

verses.hebrew_full: עָרֵ֣ב לָ֭אִישׁ לֶ֣חֶם שָׁ֑קֶר וְ֝אַחַ֗ר יִמָּֽלֵא־פִ֥יהוּ חָצָֽץ
verses.translation: Bread gained by fraud may be tasty to a man,But later his mouth will be filled with gravel.
verses.rashi: Bread of falsehood is sweet to a man Adultery with a married woman. but afterwards his mouth will be filled with gravel Heb. חצץ, fine pebbles. Similarly, (Lam. 3:16): “And He has made my teeth grind on gravel (בחצץ).”

verses.hebrew_full: מַ֭חֲשָׁבוֹת בְּעֵצָ֣ה תִכּ֑וֹן וּ֝בְתַחְבֻּל֗וֹת עֲשֵׂ֣ה מִלְחָמָֽה
verses.translation: Plans laid in council will succeed;Wage war with stratagems.
verses.rashi: Plans with counsel will be established, and with strategies wage war If you come to wage war against Satan, come with strategies of repentance, prayer and fasting.

verses.hebrew_full: גּֽוֹלֶה־סּ֭וֹד הוֹלֵ֣ךְ רָכִ֑יל וּלְפֹתֶ֥ה שְׂ֝פָתָ֗יו לֹ֣א תִתְעָרָֽב
verses.translation: Whoever gives away secrets is base;Do not take up with a big-mouth.
verses.rashi: with one who divulges secrets, one who gossips, or with one who entices with his lips who speaks smoothly to seduce you and entice you.

verses.hebrew_full: מְ֭קַלֵּל אָבִ֣יו וְאִמּ֑וֹ יִֽדְעַ֥ךְ נֵ֝ר֗וֹ (באישון) בֶּאֱשׁ֥וּן חֹֽשֶׁךְ
verses.translation: Whoever reviles their father or mother,Will be without lamplight when darkness comes.
verses.rashi: If one curses...his lamp will flicker in the blackest darkness When the darkness becomes blacker and blacker—when evil befalls him.

verses.hebrew_full: נַ֭חֲלָה (מבחלת) מְבֹהֶ֣לֶת בָּרִאשׁוֹנָ֑ה וְ֝אַחֲרִיתָ֗הּ לֹ֣א תְבֹרָֽךְ
verses.translation: An estate acquired in haste at the outsetWill not be blessed in the end.
verses.rashi: An inheritance may be acquired hastily in the beginning Which one hastened to take hurriedly, e.g. the sons of Gad and the sons of Reuben, who hastened to take their share on the other side of the Jordan, and they spoke hastily, as it is said (Num. 32:16): “We want to build sheepfolds for our cattle here and cities for our children.” They made the main thing of secondary import, for they placed their flocks before their children. but its end will not be blessed For they were exiled many years before the rest of the tribes, as is explained in Seder Olam (ch. 22), and in the Midrash of Rabbi Tanhuma (see Mattoth 7): “In the second year of Ahaz, the Lord aroused the desire of the king of Assyria, etc. (I Chron. 5:26),” but the rest of the tribes were exiled in the sixth year of Hezekiah, which is the ninth year of Hoshea the son of Elah.

verses.hebrew_full: אַל־תֹּאמַ֥ר אֲשַׁלְּמָה־רָ֑ע קַוֵּ֥ה לַ֝יהֹוָ֗ה וְיֹ֣שַֽׁע לָֽךְ
verses.translation: Do not say, “I will requite evil”;Put your hope in GOD and you will be delivered.

words.eng: to be in a covenant of peace·adj

verses.hebrew_full: תּוֹעֲבַ֣ת יְ֭הֹוָה אֶ֣בֶן וָאָ֑בֶן וּמֹאזְנֵ֖י מִרְמָ֣ה לֹא־טֽוֹב
verses.translation: False weights are an abomination to GOD;Dishonest scales are not right.

verses.hebrew_full: מֵיְהֹוָ֥ה מִצְעֲדֵי־גָ֑בֶר וְ֝אָדָ֗ם מַה־יָּבִ֥ין דַּרְכּֽוֹ
verses.translation: People’s steps are decided by GOD;What do mortals know about their own way?

verses.hebrew_full: מוֹקֵ֣שׁ אָ֭דָם יָ֣לַע קֹ֑דֶשׁ וְאַחַ֖ר נְדָרִ֣ים לְבַקֵּֽר
verses.translation: It is a snare for mortals to pledge a sacred gift rashly And after the vows to reconsider.
verses.rashi: When a man is trapped, he impairs his sanctity Heb. ילע. When a man stumbles and is trapped in sins, he impairs his sanctity, as in (Obadiah verse 16) “and they shall drink and be stunned (ולעו).” and he must seek vows Man must seek sacrifices to vow and to bring and to beg for his life.

verses.hebrew_full: מְזָרֶ֣ה רְ֭שָׁעִים מֶ֣לֶךְ חָכָ֑ם וַיָּ֖שֶׁב עֲלֵיהֶ֣ם אוֹפָֽן
verses.translation: A wise king winnows out the wicked,And turns the wheel upon them.
verses.rashi: scatters the wicked Pharaoh and his host. The Wise King The Holy One, blessed be He. and turns the wheel over them The wheel of their traits He turned over them. “And drove it heavily (Ex. 14:25),” corresponding to (ibid. 8: 11) “and he made his heart heavy.”

verses.hebrew_full: נֵ֣ר יְ֭הֹוָה נִשְׁמַ֣ת אָדָ֑ם חֹ֝פֵ֗שׂ כׇּל־חַדְרֵי־בָֽטֶן
verses.translation: A mortal’s lifebreath is GOD’s lampRevealing all their inmost parts.
verses.rashi: Man’s soul is the Lord’s lamp The soul in his midst testifies concerning him in judgment.

verses.hebrew_full: חֶ֣סֶד וֶ֭אֱמֶת יִצְּרוּ־מֶ֑לֶךְ וְסָעַ֖ד בַּחֶ֣סֶד כִּסְאֽוֹ
verses.translation: Faithfulness and loyalty protect the king;He maintains his throne by faithfulness.

verses.hebrew_full: תִּפְאֶ֣רֶת בַּחוּרִ֣ים כֹּחָ֑ם וַהֲדַ֖ר זְקֵנִ֣ים שֵׂיבָֽה
verses.translation: The glory of young men is their strength;The majesty of elders is their gray hair.
verses.rashi: The glory of young men is their strength Just as the glory of young men is their strength, so is the beauty of elders their hoary head.

verses.hebrew_full: חַבֻּר֣וֹת פֶּ֭צַע (תמריק) תַּמְר֣וּק בְּרָ֑ע וּ֝מַכּ֗וֹת חַדְרֵי־בָֽטֶן
verses.translation: Bruises and wounds are repayment for evil,Striking at one’s inmost parts.
verses.rashi: Contusions and wounds come for outpouring in evil Our Sages expounded: Whoever “pours himself” out into sinful things, will ultimately come to wounds and contusions. and plagues in the innermost parts This is dropsy.

verses.hebrew_full: פַּלְגֵי־מַ֣יִם לֶב־מֶ֭לֶךְ בְּיַד־יְהֹוָ֑ה עַֽל־כׇּל־אֲשֶׁ֖ר יַחְפֹּ֣ץ יַטֶּֽנּוּ
verses.translation: Like channeled water is the mind of the king in GOD’s hand—For it is directed according to God’s wishes.

verses.hebrew_full: כׇּֽל־דֶּרֶךְ־אִ֭ישׁ יָשָׁ֣ר בְּעֵינָ֑יו וְתֹכֵ֖ן לִבּ֣וֹת יְהֹוָֽה
verses.translation: People look approvingly on their own ways,But GOD probes the mind.
verses.rashi: Man’s every way is straight in his own eyes, but the Lord counts Heb. ותכן.

verses.hebrew_full: עֲ֭שֹׂה צְדָקָ֣ה וּמִשְׁפָּ֑ט נִבְחָ֖ר לַיהֹוָ֣ה מִזָּֽבַח
verses.translation: To do what is right and justIs more desired by GOD than sacrifice.

verses.hebrew_full: רוּם־עֵ֭ינַיִם וּרְחַב־לֵ֑ב נִ֖ר רְשָׁעִ֣ים חַטָּֽאת
verses.translation: Haughty looks, a proud heart—The tillage of the wicked is sinful.
verses.rashi: Haughty eyes A haughty spirit. and unlimited lust The desire to satisfy the lusts of his heart; that is the tillage of the wicked. Their “plowing” and their thoughts is their sin.

verses.hebrew_full: מַחְשְׁב֣וֹת חָ֭רוּץ אַךְ־לְמוֹתָ֑ר וְכׇל־אָ֝֗ץ אַךְ־לְמַחְסֽוֹר
verses.translation: The plans of the diligent make only for gain;All rash haste makes only for loss.
verses.rashi: The plans of a diligent man Heb. חרוץ, an honest man who walks in truth and according to the established judgment. lead only to advantage They come to prosperity and profit. whereas every hasty one One who forces the time.

verses.hebrew_full: פֹּ֣עַל אֹ֭צָרוֹת בִּלְשׁ֣וֹן שָׁ֑קֶר הֶ֥בֶל נִ֝דָּ֗ף מְבַקְשֵׁי־מָֽוֶת
verses.translation: Treasures acquired by a lying tongueAre like driven vapor, heading for extinction.
verses.rashi: Gathering treasures to increase wealth. with a lying tongue is like a driven vapor, etc. They are snares of death (seekers of death) for him.

verses.hebrew_full: שֹׁד־רְשָׁעִ֥ים יְגוֹרֵ֑ם כִּ֥י מֵ֝אֲנ֗וּ לַעֲשׂ֥וֹת מִשְׁפָּֽט
verses.translation: The violence of the wicked sweeps them away,For they refuse to act justly.
verses.rashi: The plunder of the wicked frightens them Heb. יגורם, [like] יגור אותם.

verses.hebrew_full: הֲפַכְפַּ֬ךְ דֶּ֣רֶךְ אִ֣ישׁ וָזָ֑ר וְ֝זַ֗ךְ יָשָׁ֥ר פׇּעֳלֽוֹ
verses.translation: A person’s way may be tortuous and strange,Though their actions are blameless and proper.
verses.rashi: A man’s way is changeable and he is strange A man who is strange, turning away from the commandment—his way is changeable.

verses.hebrew_full: ט֗וֹב לָשֶׁ֥בֶת עַל־פִּנַּת־גָּ֑ג מֵאֵ֥שֶׁת מִ֝דְיָנִ֗ים וּבֵ֥ית חָֽבֶר
verses.translation: Dwelling in the corner of a roof is betterThan a contentious wife in a spacious house.
verses.rashi: and the house of a friend That he enters his friend’s house to converse with his wife. And the Midrash Aggadah states: He prophesied that the Shechinah would ultimately leave Israel, who is like a quarrelsome wife. and the house of a friend A house in which they associate friends with the Holy One, blessed be He; for example, the image that Manasseh erected in the Temple of the Lord.

verses.hebrew_full: נֶ֣פֶשׁ רָ֭שָׁע אִוְּתָה־רָ֑ע לֹֽא־יֻחַ֖ן בְּעֵינָ֣יו רֵעֵֽהוּ
verses.translation: The desire of the wicked is set upon evil;Their neighbors find no favor in their eyes.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·adj

verses.hebrew_full: בַּֽעֲנׇשׁ־לֵ֭ץ יֶחְכַּם־פֶּ֑תִי וּבְהַשְׂכִּ֥יל לְ֝חָכָ֗ם יִקַּח־דָּֽעַת
verses.translation: When scoffers are punished, the simple are edified;When sages are taught, they gain insight.
verses.rashi: When a scorner is punished, a fool gains wisdom Through the chastisements of scorners, fools gain wisdom and return [to God] in repentance.

verses.hebrew_full: מַשְׂכִּ֣יל צַ֭דִּיק לְבֵ֣ית רָשָׁ֑ע מְסַלֵּ֖ף רְשָׁעִ֣ים לָרָֽע
verses.translation: The Righteous One observes the house of a wicked person—And subverts the wicked to their ruin.
verses.rashi: The Righteous One considers The Righteous One of the world sets His heart to destroy the house of the wicked, e.g. the remembrance of Amalek.

verses.hebrew_full: אֹטֵ֣ם אׇ֭זְנוֹ מִזַּעֲקַת־דָּ֑ל גַּֽם־ה֥וּא יִ֝קְרָ֗א וְלֹ֣א יֵעָנֶֽה
verses.translation: One who turns a deaf ear to the cry of the wretchedWill likewise call and not be answered.

verses.hebrew_full: מַתָּ֣ן בַּ֭סֵּתֶר יִכְפֶּה־אָ֑ף וְשֹׁ֥חַד בַּ֝חֵ֗ק חֵמָ֥ה עַזָּֽה
verses.translation: A gift in secret subdues anger,A present in private, fierce rage.
verses.rashi: A gift in secret Charity. and a bribe in the pocket This too refers to charity. It will appease even strong wrath.

verses.hebrew_full: שִׂמְחָ֣ה לַ֭צַּדִּיק עֲשׂ֣וֹת מִשְׁפָּ֑ט וּ֝מְחִתָּ֗ה לְפֹ֣עֲלֵי אָֽוֶן
verses.translation: Justice done is a joy to the righteous,To evildoers, ruination.
verses.rashi: It is a joy for a righteous man to do judgment It is a joy for the Holy One, blessed be He, to inflict judgment upon the righteous in order to bestow upon them the life of the world to come, for the Holy One, blessed be He, brings pains upon them in order that they merit the world to come. In this manner, it is expounded in Midrash Psalms (3:1). and a ruin It is [a ruin] for those who commit violence Because they do not put their hearts to repent and the pains do not avail them, but they suffer to no avail.

verses.hebrew_full: אָדָ֗ם תּ֭וֹעֶה מִדֶּ֣רֶךְ הַשְׂכֵּ֑ל בִּקְהַ֖ל רְפָאִ֣ים יָנֽוּחַ
verses.translation: One who strays from the path of prudenceWill rest in the company of ghosts.
verses.rashi: A man who strays from the way of understanding From the Torah. in the congregation of the shades in the congregation of Gehinnom.

verses.hebrew_full: אִ֣ישׁ מַ֭חְסוֹר אֹהֵ֣ב שִׂמְחָ֑ה אֹהֵ֥ב יַֽיִן־וָ֝שֶׁ֗מֶן לֹ֣א יַעֲשִֽׁיר
verses.translation: A man who loves pleasure comes to want;He who loves wine and oil does not grow rich.
verses.rashi: shall be a poor man He who loves the joy of always banqueting.

words.eng: to be or become rich or wealthy

verses.hebrew_full: כֹּ֣פֶר לַצַּדִּ֣יק רָשָׁ֑ע וְתַ֖חַת יְשָׁרִ֣ים בּוֹגֵֽד
verses.translation: The wicked are the ransom of the righteous;The traitor comes in place of the upright.
verses.rashi: A wicked man shall be ransom for a righteous man, and a treacherous one instead of the upright A righteous man is rescued, and a wicked man comes in his stead (above 11:8), such as Mordecai and Haman.

verses.hebrew_full: ט֗וֹב שֶׁ֥בֶת בְּאֶֽרֶץ־מִדְבָּ֑ר מֵאֵ֖שֶׁת (מדונים) מִדְיָנִ֣ים וָכָֽעַס
verses.translation: It is better to live in the desertThan with a contentious, vexatious wife.
verses.rashi: It is better to dwell in a desert land This too was stated regarding the withdrawal of the Shechinah.

verses.hebrew_full: אוֹצָ֤ר נֶחְמָ֣ד וָ֭שֶׁמֶן בִּנְוֵ֣ה חָכָ֑ם וּכְסִ֖יל אָדָ֣ם יְבַלְּעֶֽנּוּ
verses.translation: Precious treasure and oil are in the house of a wise man,And a fool of a man will run through them.
verses.rashi: but man’s foolishness Heb. וכסיל אדם foolishness of a man.

verses.hebrew_full: רֹ֭דֵף צְדָקָ֣ה וָחָ֑סֶד יִמְצָ֥א חַ֝יִּ֗ים צְדָקָ֥ה וְכָבֽוֹד
verses.translation: One who strives to do good and kind deedsAttains life, success, and honor.

verses.hebrew_full: עִ֣יר גִּ֭בֹּרִים עָלָ֣ה חָכָ֑ם וַ֝יֹּ֗רֶד עֹ֣ז מִבְטֶחָֽהֿ
verses.translation: One wise man prevailed over a city of warriorsAnd brought down its mighty stronghold.
verses.rashi: A wise man ascended to a city of mighty men This refers to Moses, our teacher, who went up among the angels, who are mighty in strength. (and brought down, etc. The Torah.)

verses.hebrew_full: שֹׁמֵ֣ר פִּ֭יו וּלְשׁוֹנ֑וֹ שֹׁמֵ֖ר מִצָּר֣וֹת נַפְשֽׁוֹ
verses.translation: One who watches over mouth and tongueKeeps out of trouble.

verses.hebrew_full: זֵ֣ד יָ֭הִיר לֵ֣ץ שְׁמ֑וֹ ע֝וֹשֶׂ֗ה בְּעֶבְרַ֥ת זָדֽוֹן
verses.translation: The proud, insolent person, named scoffer,Acts in a frenzy of insolence.
verses.rashi: haughty Heb. יהיר, one who is haughty; he will ultimately become a scorner, for he does not care to hear reproof.

verses.hebrew_full: תַּאֲוַ֣ת עָצֵ֣ל תְּמִיתֶ֑נּוּ כִּֽי־מֵאֲנ֖וּ יָדָ֣יו לַעֲשֽׂוֹת
verses.translation: The cravings of sluggards are fatal,For their hands refuse to work.

verses.hebrew_full: כׇּל־הַ֭יּוֹם הִתְאַוָּ֣ה תַאֲוָ֑ה וְצַדִּ֥יק יִ֝תֵּ֗ן וְלֹ֣א יַחְשֹֽׂךְ
verses.translation: All day long they are seized with cravingWhile the righteous give without stint.
verses.rashi: but the righteous gives all the necessities of his house, for the Holy One, blessed be He, prepares them for him.

verses.hebrew_full: זֶ֣בַח רְ֭שָׁעִים תּוֹעֵבָ֑ה אַ֝֗ף כִּֽי־בְזִמָּ֥ה יְבִיאֶֽנּוּ
verses.translation: The sacrifice of the wicked is an abomination,The more so when offered in depravity.
verses.rashi: how much more if he brings it with plans of wickedness! How much more if he brings it to be wicked and with evil counsel, such as the sacrifices of Balaam and Balak, who brought them only to curse Israel.

verses.hebrew_full: עֵד־כְּזָבִ֥ים יֹאבֵ֑ד וְאִ֥ישׁ שׁ֝וֹמֵ֗עַ לָנֶ֥צַח יְדַבֵּֽר
verses.translation: A false witness is doomed,But a man who listens will testify with success.
verses.rashi: but an obedient man who obeys what is written in the Torah: “You shall not bear false witness against your neighbor.” forever Always.

verses.hebrew_full: הֵעֵ֬ז אִ֣ישׁ רָשָׁ֣ע בְּפָנָ֑יו וְ֝יָשָׁ֗ר ה֤וּא (יכין דרכיו) יָבִ֬ין דַּרְכּֽוֹ
verses.translation: The wicked man is brazen-faced;The upright man discerns his course.
verses.rashi: A wicked man shows his brazenness in his anger A wicked man shows his brazenness at the time of his anger. in his anger Heb. בפניו, an expression of anger.

verses.hebrew_full: אֵ֣ין חׇ֭כְמָה וְאֵ֣ין תְּבוּנָ֑ה וְאֵ֥ין עֵ֝צָ֗ה לְנֶ֣גֶד יְהֹוָֽה
verses.translation: No wisdom, no prudence, and no counselCan prevail against GOD.
verses.rashi: There is neither wisdom, etc. No wise or understanding man is of any importance against the Lord. Wherever there is a desecration of the Name, we do not pay regard to a teacher.

verses.hebrew_full: ס֗וּס מ֭וּכָן לְי֣וֹם מִלְחָמָ֑ה וְ֝לַיהֹוָ֗ה הַתְּשׁוּעָֽה
verses.translation: The horse is readied for the day of battle,But victory comes from GOD.

verses.hebrew_full: נִבְחָ֣ר שֵׁ֭ם מֵעֹ֣שֶׁר רָ֑ב מִכֶּ֥סֶף וּ֝מִזָּהָ֗ב חֵ֣ן טֽוֹב
verses.translation: Repute is preferable to great wealth,Grace is better than silver and gold.
verses.rashi: A name is chosen A good name above great wealth. good favor over silver and gold Good favor is chosen.

verses.hebrew_full: עָשִׁ֣יר וָרָ֣שׁ נִפְגָּ֑שׁוּ עֹשֵׂ֖ה כֻלָּ֣ם יְהֹוָֽה
verses.translation: The rich and the poor meet; GOD made them both.
verses.rashi: the Lord is the Maker of them all When the poor man says to the rich man, “Sustain me,” and he answers him harshly, the Holy One, blessed be He, makes them new: this one poor and this one rich.

verses.hebrew_full: עָר֤וּם רָאָ֣ה רָעָ֣ה (ויסתר) וְנִסְתָּ֑ר וּ֝פְתָיִ֗ים עָבְר֥וּ וְֽנֶעֱנָֽשׁוּ
verses.translation: One who was shrewd saw trouble and took cover;The simple kept going and paid the penalty.
verses.rashi: A cunning man saw harm The punishment for a sin, and hid, not committing the sin.

words.eng: to pass over or by or through

verses.hebrew_full: עֵ֣קֶב עֲ֭נָוָה יִרְאַ֣ת יְהֹוָ֑ה עֹ֖שֶׁר וְכָב֣וֹד וְחַיִּֽים
verses.translation: The effect of humility is fear of GOD,Wealth, honor, and life.
verses.rashi: In the wake of humility Because of humility, fear of the Lord comes. Another explanation: Humility is the main attribute, and fear is secondary to it and a “heel” to it, a mat for its feet.

verses.hebrew_full: צִנִּ֣ים פַּ֭חִים בְּדֶ֣רֶךְ עִקֵּ֑שׁ שׁוֹמֵ֥ר נַ֝פְשׁ֗וֹ יִרְחַ֥ק מֵהֶֽם
verses.translation: Thorns and snares are in the path of the crooked;Those who value their life will keep far from them.
verses.rashi: Troops [and] snares Heb. צנים, as in (Num. 33:55) “troops (לצנינים) in your sides”; (Ezek. 23: 24) “And they will come upon you, a band (הצן),” an expression of bands and brigands. Troops and snares are hidden on the ways of the one who perverts his ways; i.e., torments are prepared for him. he who preserves his soul will distance himself from them He who is upright in his deeds will be saved from them.

verses.hebrew_full: חֲנֹ֣ךְ לַ֭נַּעַר עַל־פִּ֣י דַרְכּ֑וֹ גַּ֥ם כִּי־יַ֝זְקִ֗ין לֹא־יָס֥וּר מִמֶּֽנָּה
verses.translation: Train children in the way they each ought to go;They will not swerve from it even in old age.
verses.rashi: Train a child According to what you teach a child and train him in matters, either for good or bad, even when he grows old, he will not turn away from it.

verses.hebrew_full: עָ֭שִׁיר בְּרָשִׁ֣ים יִמְשׁ֑וֹל וְעֶ֥בֶד לֹ֝וֶ֗ה לְאִ֣ישׁ מַלְוֶֽה
verses.translation: A rich person rules the poor,And the borrower is a slave to the man who lends.
verses.rashi: A rich man will rule over the poor An ignoramus always needs a Torah scholar.

verses.hebrew_full: זוֹרֵ֣עַ עַ֭וְלָה (יקצור) יִקְצׇר־אָ֑וֶן וְשֵׁ֖בֶט עֶבְרָת֣וֹ יִכְלֶֽה
verses.translation: One who sows injustice shall reap misfortune;The rod of wrath shall fail.
verses.rashi: He who sows injustice will reap violence According to his sowing will be his reaping; according to his deed will be the reception of his reward. and the rod of his wrath will fail The rod of his ear of grain will fail more and more [interpreting עברתו as עבור, grain]. Some say: The rod of his wrath with which he rules over people will become progressively lessened because he impairs his ability.

verses.hebrew_full: טֽוֹב־עַ֭יִן ה֣וּא יְבֹרָ֑ךְ כִּֽי־נָתַ֖ן מִלַּחְמ֣וֹ לַדָּֽל
verses.translation: One who is generous is blessed,While giving bread to the poor.

verses.hebrew_full: גָּ֣רֵֽשׁ לֵ֭ץ וְיֵצֵ֣א מָד֑וֹן וְ֝יִשְׁבֹּ֗ת דִּ֣ין וְקָלֽוֹן
verses.translation: Expel the scoffer and contention departs,Quarrel and contumely cease.
verses.rashi: Banish a scorner The evil inclination.

verses.hebrew_full: אֹהֵ֥ב (טהור) טְהׇר־לֵ֑ב חֵ֥ן שְׂ֝פָתָ֗יו רֵעֵ֥הוּ מֶֽלֶךְ
verses.translation: A pure-hearted comrade—His speech is gracious;He has the king for his companion.
verses.rashi: He who loves one pure of heart with charm on his lips One whose lips have grace. the King is his friend The Holy One, blessed be He, loves him and endears him.

verses.hebrew_full: עֵינֵ֣י יְ֭הֹוָה נָ֣צְרוּ דָ֑עַת וַ֝יְסַלֵּ֗ף דִּבְרֵ֥י בֹגֵֽד
verses.translation: The eyes of GOD watch the person who is wise; [God] subverts the words of the treacherous.

verses.hebrew_full: אָמַ֣ר עָ֭צֵל אֲרִ֣י בַח֑וּץ בְּת֥וֹךְ רְ֝חֹב֗וֹת אֵרָצֵֽחַ
verses.translation: The sluggard says, “There’s a lion in the street;I shall be killed if I step outside.”
verses.rashi: The lazy man says, “There is a lion outside...” How will I go out to learn Torah?

words.eng: broad or open place or plaza

verses.hebrew_full: שׁוּחָ֣ה עֲ֭מֻקָּה פִּ֣י זָר֑וֹת זְע֥וּם יְ֝הֹוָ֗ה (יפול) יִפׇּל־שָֽׁם
verses.translation: The mouth of a forbidden woman is a deep pit;He who is doomed by GOD falls into it.
verses.rashi: The mouth of strange women is [like] a deep pit An idolatress. the one abhorred by the Lord Heb. זעום. He whom the Holy One, blessed be He, hates, falls and stumbles upon her. the one abhorred rebuked from before Him.

verses.hebrew_full: אִ֭וֶּלֶת קְשׁוּרָ֣ה בְלֶב־נָ֑עַר שֵׁ֥בֶט מ֝וּסָ֗ר יַרְחִיקֶ֥נָּה מִמֶּֽנּוּ
verses.translation: If folly settles in the heart of a youth,The rod of discipline will remove it.

verses.hebrew_full: עֹ֣שֵֽׁק דָּ֭ל לְהַרְבּ֣וֹת ל֑וֹ נֹתֵ֥ן לְ֝עָשִׁ֗יר אַךְ־לְמַחְסֽוֹר
verses.translation: To profit by withholding what is due to the poorIs like making gifts to the rich—pure loss.
verses.rashi: He who exploits a poor man to increase for himself money. will give to a rich man He will ultimately give his money to the rich pagan kings, and it will be only to want.

verses.hebrew_full: הַ֥ט אׇזְנְךָ֗ וּ֭שְׁמַע דִּבְרֵ֣י חֲכָמִ֑ים וְ֝לִבְּךָ֗ תָּשִׁ֥ית לְדַעְתִּֽי
verses.translation: Incline your ear and listen to the words of the sages;Pay attention to my wisdom.
verses.rashi: Incline your ear and hearken to the words of the wise to learn the Torah from a sage of any stature. and put your heart to my knowledge But if your teacher is wicked, do not learn from his deeds.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־נָ֭עִים כִּֽי־תִשְׁמְרֵ֣ם בְּבִטְנֶ֑ךָ יִכֹּ֥נוּ יַ֝חְדָּ֗ו עַל־שְׂפָתֶֽיךָ
verses.translation: It is good that you store them inside you,And that all of them be constantly on your lips,
verses.rashi: for it is pleasant It will be [pleasant] for you at a later time if you preserve them and hide them in your innards lest you forget them. Now when will they be preserved in your heart? When they are established on your lips, when you utter them with your mouth.

verses.hebrew_full: לִֽהְי֣וֹת בַּ֭יהֹוָה מִבְטַחֶ֑ךָ הוֹדַעְתִּ֖יךָ הַיּ֣וֹם אַף־אָֽתָּה
verses.translation: That you may put your trust in GOD.I let you know today—yes, you—
verses.rashi: That your trust shall be in the Lord, I have made known to you I make known to you that you should trust in the Lord and engage in the Torah, and you shall not say, “How will I be idle from my work, and how will I earn a livelihood?”

verses.hebrew_full: הֲלֹ֤א כָתַ֣בְתִּֽי לְ֭ךָ (שלשום) שָׁלִישִׁ֑ים בְּמֹ֖עֵצ֣וֹת וָדָֽעַת
verses.translation: Indeed, I wrote down for you a threefold lore, Wise counsel,
verses.rashi: Have I not written to you thirds The Torah, the Prophets, and the Hagiographa.
