verses.hebrew_full: לְהֽוֹדִיעֲךָ֗ קֹ֭שְׁטְ אִמְרֵ֣י אֱמֶ֑ת לְהָשִׁ֥יב אֲמָרִ֥ים אֱ֝מֶ֗ת לְשֹׁלְחֶֽיךָ
verses.translation: To let you know truly reliable words,That you may give a faithful reply to those who sent you.
verses.rashi: to make known to you the certainty I wrote thirds for you in order that you should understand from them the certainty of the true words. to respond with words of truth to those who send you To those who ask you for instruction.

verses.hebrew_full: אַֽל־תִּגְזׇל־דָּ֭ל כִּ֣י דַל־ה֑וּא וְאַל־תְּדַכֵּ֖א עָנִ֣י בַשָּֽׁעַר
verses.translation: Do not rob the wretched because they are wretched;Do not crush the poor in the gate;
verses.rashi: Do not rob a poor man Do not rob him because you see that he is poor and has no strength to resist you.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־יְ֭הֹוָה יָרִ֣יב רִיבָ֑ם וְקָבַ֖ע אֶת־קֹבְעֵיהֶ֣ם נָֽפֶשׁ
verses.translation: For GOD will take up their causeAnd despoil those who despoil them of life.
verses.rashi: For the Lord will plead their cause and rob Heb. וקבע. And will rob the life. those who rob them Heb. קבעים, those who rob them; in Aramaic קביעה means robbery. In tractate Rosh Hashanah (26b): A certain man came before him and said to him, “So-and-so robbed me (קבען)” etc.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·to rob

verses.hebrew_full: אַל־תִּ֭תְרַע אֶת־בַּ֣עַל אָ֑ף וְאֶת־אִ֥ישׁ חֵ֝מ֗וֹת לֹ֣א תָבֽוֹא
verses.translation: Do not associate with a hothead,Or go about with a hot-tempered man,
verses.rashi: Do not befriend Heb. תתרע, do not befriend, an expression of friendship (רעות)

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·man

verses.hebrew_full: פֶּן־תֶּאֱלַ֥ף אֹרְחֹתָ֑ו וְלָקַחְתָּ֖ מוֹקֵ֣שׁ לְנַפְשֶֽׁךָ
verses.translation: Lest you learn their waysAnd find yourself ensnared.
verses.rashi: lest you learn Heb. תאלף.

verses.hebrew_full: אַל־תְּהִ֥י בְתֹֽקְעֵי־כָ֑ף בַּ֝עֹרְבִ֗ים מַשָּׁאֽוֹת
verses.translation: Do not be one of those who give their hand,Who stand surety for debts,
verses.rashi: Do not be one of those who give their hands, who stand surety for debts Loans, as in (Deut. 24: 10), “When you lend you fellow [Jew] any item.”

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־אֵֽין־לְךָ֥ לְשַׁלֵּ֑ם לָ֥מָּה יִקַּ֥ח מִ֝שְׁכָּבְךָ֗ מִתַּחְתֶּֽיךָ
verses.translation: Lest your bed be taken from under youWhen you have no money to pay.

verses.hebrew_full: אַל־תַּ֭סֵּג גְּב֣וּל עוֹלָ֑ם אֲשֶׁ֖ר עָשׂ֣וּ אֲבוֹתֶֽיךָ
verses.translation: Do not remove the ancient boundary stoneThat your ancestors set up.
verses.rashi: Do not remove an ancient boundary Do not turn away from the custom of your forefathers. Our Sages of blessed memory said: If one places a basket under a vine at the time of vintage so that the fallen grapes should fall into it, concerning this was stated: “Do not remove an ancient boundary, etc.” remove Heb. תסג, as in (Ps. 129:5) “and shall draw backwards (ויסגו).”

verses.hebrew_full: חָזִ֡יתָ אִ֤ישׁ מָ֘הִ֤יר בִּמְלַאכְתּ֗וֹ לִֽפְנֵי־מְלָכִ֥ים יִתְיַצָּ֑ב בַּל־יִ֝תְיַצֵּ֗ב לִפְנֵ֥י חֲשֻׁכִּֽים
verses.translation: See a man skilled at his work—He shall attend upon kings;He shall not attend upon the lowly.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־תֵ֭שֵׁב לִלְח֣וֹם אֶת־מוֹשֵׁ֑ל בִּ֥ין תָּ֝בִ֗ין אֶת־אֲשֶׁ֥ר לְפָנֶֽיךָ
verses.translation: When you sit down to dine with a ruler,Consider well who is before you.
verses.rashi: If you sit down to dine Heb. ללחום, to eat. you should understand well Give thought to know who he is, whether he is stingy or generous.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·to rule
sign of the definite direct object·(relative part.)

verses.hebrew_full: וְשַׂמְתָּ֣ שַׂכִּ֣ין בְּלֹעֶ֑ךָ אִם־בַּ֖עַל נֶ֣פֶשׁ אָֽתָּה
verses.translation: Thrust a knife into your gulletIf you have a large appetite.
verses.rashi: and you shall put a knife into your jaw Heb. בלועך, into your jaw. If you see that he is stingy, do not eat of his [food]. if you are a man with a hearty appetite If you are a glutton and long to eat, it is better that you thrust a knife between your teeth. Our Sages expounded this as referring to a disciple sitting before his teacher. If he knows that his teacher will give him an answer for everything he asks, let him investigate the subtle points and ask concerning his tradition, but if not, let him keep his peace.

verses.hebrew_full: אַל־תִּ֭תְאָו לְמַטְעַמּוֹתָ֑יו וְ֝ה֗וּא לֶ֣חֶם כְּזָבִֽים
verses.translation: Do not crave the dainties,For they are counterfeit food.
verses.rashi: Do not desire his delicacies but leave him and go [to study] before a competent teacher, and do not embarrass him with questions, since he does not know what to answer.

verses.hebrew_full: אַל־תִּיגַ֥ע לְֽהַעֲשִׁ֑יר מִֽבִּינָתְךָ֥ חֲדָֽל
verses.translation: Do not toil to gain wealth;Have the sense to desist.
verses.rashi: Do not weary yourself to grow rich To make your learning into bundles because you will ultimately forget it.

words.eng: to be or become rich or wealthy

verses.hebrew_full: (התעוף) הֲתָ֤עִיף־עֵינֶ֥יךָ בּ֗וֹ וְֽאֵ֫ינֶ֥נּוּ כִּ֤י עָשֹׂ֣ה יַעֲשֶׂה־לּ֣וֹ כְנָפַ֑יִם כְּ֝נֶ֗שֶׁר (ועיף) יָע֥וּף הַשָּׁמָֽיִם
verses.translation: You see it, then it is gone;It grows wings and flies away,Like an eagle, heavenward.
verses.rashi: Should you blink your eyes at it The moment you blink your eyes to close them, that study will no longer be found with you. התעיף, like “and you shall fold” (Ex. 26:9), which Onkelos renders: ותעיף.

words.eng: inflected third pers. singular m. of בּֽ◌ ᴵ

verses.hebrew_full: אַל־תִּלְחַ֗ם אֶת־לֶ֭חֶם רַ֣ע עָ֑יִן וְאַל־תִּ֝תְאָ֗ו לְמַטְעַמֹּתָֽיו
verses.translation: Do not eat of a miser’s food;Do not crave for his dainties;

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·bread

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֤י כְּמ֥וֹ שָׁעַ֥ר בְּנַפְשׁ֗וֹ כֶּ֫ן־ה֥וּא אֱכ֣וֹל וּ֭שְׁתֵה יֹ֣אמַר לָ֑ךְ וְ֝לִבּ֗וֹ בַּל־עִמָּֽךְ
verses.translation: He is like one keeping accounts;“Eat and drink,” he says to you,But he does not really mean it.
verses.rashi: for it is as though it poured gall שער. This שער is vowelized half with a “patach” and half with a “kamatz,” and the accent is at the end of the word; therefore, it is a verb and not a noun; for were it a noun, it would be [vowelized completely with] a “pattah” and the accent would be at the beginning of the word, like every שער in Scriptures. Its meaning is that it as though this food poured gall into the soul of this stingy man, so it is. gall Heb. שער, from the expression of (Jer. 29:17): “the loathsome (השערים) figs,” the bitter ones.

verses.hebrew_full: פִּֽתְּךָ־אָכַ֥לְתָּ תְקִיאֶ֑נָּה וְ֝שִׁחַ֗תָּ דְּבָרֶ֥יךָ הַנְּעִימִֽים
verses.translation: The morsel you eat you will vomit;You will waste your courteous words.
verses.rashi: your morsel that you ate in his house, you will ultimately vomit because of embarrassment. and you will lose your sweet words The gratitude that you extended and spoke to him tender words—you lost everything.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּאׇזְנֵ֣י כְ֭סִיל אַל־תְּדַבֵּ֑ר כִּי־יָ֝ב֗וּז לְשֵׂ֣כֶל מִלֶּֽיךָ
verses.translation: Do not speak to a dullard—Who will disdain your sensible words.

verses.hebrew_full: אַל־תַּ֭סֵּג גְּב֣וּל עוֹלָ֑ם וּבִשְׂדֵ֥י יְ֝תוֹמִ֗ים אַל־תָּבֹֽא
verses.translation: Do not remove ancient boundary stones;Do not encroach upon the field of orphans,
verses.rashi: and...the fields of the orphans The gleanings, the forgotten sheaves, and the corner that is due them.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־גֹאֲלָ֥ם חָזָ֑ק הֽוּא־יָרִ֖יב אֶת־רִיבָ֣ם אִתָּֽךְ
verses.translation: For they have a mighty Redeemer,Who will surely take up their cause with you.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·to strive
sign of the definite direct object

verses.hebrew_full: הָבִ֣יאָה לַמּוּסָ֣ר לִבֶּ֑ךָ וְ֝אׇזְנֶ֗ךָ לְאִמְרֵי־דָֽעַת
verses.translation: Apply your mind to disciplineAnd your ears to wise sayings.

verses.hebrew_full: אַל־תִּמְנַ֣ע מִנַּ֣עַר מוּסָ֑ר כִּֽי־תַכֶּ֥נּוּ בַ֝שֵּׁ֗בֶט לֹ֣א יָמֽוּת
verses.translation: Do not withhold discipline from children;If you beat them with a rod they will not die.

verses.hebrew_full: אַ֭תָּה בַּשֵּׁ֣בֶט תַּכֶּ֑נּוּ וְ֝נַפְשׁ֗וֹ מִשְּׁא֥וֹל תַּצִּֽיל
verses.translation: Beat them with a rodAnd you will save them from the grave.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּ֭נִי אִם־חָכַ֣ם לִבֶּ֑ךָ יִשְׂמַ֖ח לִבִּ֣י גַם־אָֽנִי
verses.translation: My son, if your mind gets wisdom,My mind, too, will be gladdened.

words.eng: also·I (first pers. sing. -usually used for emphasis)

verses.hebrew_full: וְתַעְלֹ֥זְנָה כִלְיוֹתָ֑י בְּדַבֵּ֥ר שְׂ֝פָתֶ֗יךָ מֵישָׁרִֽים
verses.translation: I shall rejoice with all my heart When your lips speak right things.
verses.rashi: when your lips speak right things since your heart has grown wise.

verses.hebrew_full: אַל־יְקַנֵּ֣א לִ֭בְּךָ בַּחַטָּאִ֑ים כִּ֥י אִם־בְּיִרְאַת־יְ֝הֹוָ֗ה כׇּל־הַיּֽוֹם
verses.translation: Do not envy sinners in your heart,But only those who fear GOD, at all times,
verses.rashi: Let your heart not envy the sinners their success, to be wicked like them.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֭י אִם־יֵ֣שׁ אַחֲרִ֑ית וְ֝תִקְוָתְךָ֗ לֹ֣א תִכָּרֵֽת
verses.translation: For then you will have a future,And your hope will never fail.
verses.rashi: for because of this, there is a future Heb. כי אם. This [word] אם is used as an expression of “that”; i.e., for because of this there is a future and hope for you.

verses.hebrew_full: שְׁמַע־אַתָּ֣ה בְנִ֣י וַחֲכָ֑ם וְאַשֵּׁ֖ר בַּדֶּ֣רֶךְ לִבֶּֽךָ
verses.translation: Listen, my son, and get wisdom;Lead your mind in a [proper] path.
verses.rashi: Hear you, my son, and grow wise, and walk in the way of your heart Since you will grow wise, you will be able to walk in the ways of your heart, for the heart of a wise man will not entice you to sin.

verses.hebrew_full: אַל־תְּהִ֥י בְסֹֽבְאֵי־יָ֑יִן בְּזֹלְלֵ֖י בָשָׂ֣ר לָֽמוֹ
verses.translation: Do not be of those who guzzle wine,Or glut themselves on meat,

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־סֹבֵ֣א וְ֭זוֹלֵל יִוָּרֵ֑שׁ וּ֝קְרָעִ֗ים תַּלְבִּ֥ישׁ נוּמָֽה
verses.translation: For guzzlers and gluttons will be impoverished,And drowsing will clothe you in tatters.
verses.rashi: for the guzzler and the glutton will become impoverished Heb. יורש, will become impoverished. and slumber will clothe [you] with tatters Laziness and slumber will clothe you with tattered clothing.

verses.hebrew_full: שְׁמַ֣ע לְ֭אָבִיךָ זֶ֣ה יְלָדֶ֑ךָ וְאַל־תָּ֝ב֗וּז כִּֽי־זָקְנָ֥ה אִמֶּֽךָ
verses.translation: Listen to your father who begot you;Do not disdain your mother when she is old.

verses.hebrew_full: אֱמֶ֣ת קְ֭נֵה וְאַל־תִּמְכֹּ֑ר חׇכְמָ֖ה וּמוּסָ֣ר וּבִינָֽה
verses.translation: Buy truth and never sell it,And wisdom, discipline, and understanding.
verses.rashi: Buy truth If you do not find to learn for nothing, learn for pay [pay to learn], but do not say, “Just as I learned for pay, so will I teach it for pay.”
verses.ibn_ezra: “Truth – the word is missing: books of truth: acquire (them) and do not sell (them).”

verses.hebrew_full: (גול) גִּ֣יל יָ֭גִיל אֲבִ֣י צַדִּ֑יק (יולד) וְיוֹלֵ֥ד חָ֝כָ֗ם (וישמח) יִשְׂמַח־בּֽוֹ
verses.translation: The father of a righteous person will exult;He who begets a wise child will rejoice in them.

words.eng: to rejoice·inflected third pers. singular m. of בּֽ◌ ᴵ

verses.hebrew_full: יִֽשְׂמַח־אָבִ֥יךָ וְאִמֶּ֑ךָ וְ֝תָגֵ֗ל יוֹלַדְתֶּֽךָ
verses.translation: Your father and mother will rejoice;She who bore you will exult.

verses.hebrew_full: תְּנָה־בְנִ֣י לִבְּךָ֣ לִ֑י וְ֝עֵינֶ֗יךָ דְּרָכַ֥י (תרצנה) תִּצֹּֽרְנָה
verses.translation: Give your mind to me, my son;Let your eyes watch my ways.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־שׁוּחָ֣ה עֲמֻקָּ֣ה זוֹנָ֑ה וּבְאֵ֥ר צָ֝רָ֗ה נׇכְרִיָּֽה
verses.translation: A prostitute is a deep pit;A forbidden woman is a narrow well.
verses.rashi: a narrow well (etroitte in French, in German, eng, schmal.)

verses.hebrew_full: אַף־הִ֭יא כְּחֶ֣תֶף תֶּאֱרֹ֑ב וּ֝בוֹגְדִ֗ים בְּאָדָ֥ם תּוֹסִֽף
verses.translation: She too lies in wait as if for prey,And destroys the unfaithful among men.
verses.rashi: suddenly Heb. כחתף, suddenly. and she will increase the faithless among men She increases in Israel those who are faithless to God. He is referring to apostasy.

verses.hebrew_full: לְמִ֨י א֥וֹי לְמִ֪י אֲב֡וֹי לְמִ֤י (מדונים) מִדְיָנִ֨ים לְמִ֥י שִׂ֗יחַ לְ֭מִי פְּצָעִ֣ים חִנָּ֑ם לְ֝מִ֗י חַכְלִל֥וּת עֵינָֽיִם
verses.translation: Who cries, “Woe!” who, “Alas!”;Who has quarrels, who complaints;Who has wounds without cause;Who has bleary eyes?
verses.rashi: Who cries, “Woe!” Who, “Alas!” An expression of crying and yelling. He who has quarrels. He who talks too much To him who has quarrels with the people and talks too much, woe and alas, for transgression does not stop with much speech. Who has bloodshot eyes? From drinking much wine, the eyes become red, and that is embarrassing.

verses.hebrew_full: לַֽמְאַחֲרִ֥ים עַל־הַיָּ֑יִן לַ֝בָּאִ֗ים לַחְקֹ֥ר מִמְסָֽךְ
verses.translation: Those whom wine keeps till the small hours,Those who gather to drain the cups.
verses.rashi: for mixed wine [as translated,] for mixed wine; this word is a noun, like ממכר, merchandise. to search for mixed wine Who search and hunt where good wine is sold.

verses.hebrew_full: אַל־תֵּ֥רֶא יַּיִן֮ כִּ֤י יִתְאַ֫דָּ֥ם כִּֽי־יִתֵּ֣ן (בכיס) בַּכּ֣וֹס עֵינ֑וֹ יִ֝תְהַלֵּ֗ךְ בְּמֵֽישָׁרִֽים
verses.translation: Do not ogle that red wineAs it lends its color to the cup,As it flows on smoothly;
verses.rashi: Do not look at wine when it is red Do not put your eye on it. when he puts his eye on the cup, it goes smoothly Whoever is habitually drunk—all transgressions appear to him to be straight; all ways appear straight. on the cup Heb. בכוס. The masoretic text reads: בכיס, on the pocket; i.e., the drinker puts his eye on the cup, and the storekeeper puts his eye on this one’s pocket.

verses.hebrew_full: אַ֭חֲרִיתוֹ כְּנָחָ֣שׁ יִשָּׁ֑ךְ וּֽכְצִפְעֹנִ֥י יַפְרִֽשׁ
verses.translation: In the end, it bites like a snake;It spits like a basilisk.
verses.rashi: and sting Heb. יפרש, an expression of stinging (pointe in French, in German stechend). (Jud. 3: 31): “With an ox-goad” is translated into Aramaic as בפרש תורי, and in the Gemara (Baba Mezia 80a): “The one who holds the colter (פרשא),” aiguillon in Old French, in German stachel, Gewohneisen, and so in Jud. 3:31, II Sam. 8:1, and Jer. 31:18. Others interpret the word to mean that it separates him from life.

verses.hebrew_full: עֵ֭ינֶיךָ יִרְא֣וּ זָר֑וֹת וְ֝לִבְּךָ֗ יְדַבֵּ֥ר תַּהְפֻּכֽוֹת
verses.translation: Your eyes will see strange sights;Your heart will speak distorted things.
verses.rashi: Your eyes will see strange women When you will become drunk, they will burn in you and entice you to ogle harlots.

verses.hebrew_full: וְ֭הָיִיתָ כְּשֹׁכֵ֣ב בְּלֶב־יָ֑ם וּ֝כְשֹׁכֵ֗ב בְּרֹ֣אשׁ חִבֵּֽל
verses.translation: You will be like one lying in bed on high seas,Like one lying on top of the rigging.
verses.rashi: And you shall be mad like one lying in the midst of the sea. and like one lying at the top of a mast The mast of a ship sways to fall from it.

verses.hebrew_full: הִכּ֥וּנִי בַל־חָלִיתִי֮ הֲלָמ֗וּנִי בַּל־יָ֫דָ֥עְתִּי מָתַ֥י אָקִ֑יץ א֝וֹסִ֗יף אֲבַקְשֶׁ֥נּוּ עֽוֹד
verses.translation: “They struck me, but I felt no hurt;They beat me, but I was unaware;As often as I wake,I go after it again.”
verses.rashi: “They struck me but I did not become ill, etc.” When he sobers himself up from his wine, he does not feel any of the evils that passed over him, and he drinks again.

verses.hebrew_full: אַל־תְּ֭קַנֵּא בְּאַנְשֵׁ֣י רָעָ֑ה וְאַל־תִּ֝תְאָ֗ו לִֽהְי֥וֹת אִתָּֽם
verses.translation: Do not envy the wicked;Do not desire to be with them;

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object

verses.hebrew_full: כִּי־שֹׁ֭ד יֶהְגֶּ֣ה לִבָּ֑ם וְ֝עָמָ֗ל שִׂפְתֵיהֶ֥ם תְּדַבֵּֽרְנָה
verses.translation: For their hearts talk violence,And their lips speak mischief.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּ֭חׇכְמָה יִבָּ֣נֶה בָּ֑יִת וּ֝בִתְבוּנָ֗ה יִתְכּוֹנָֽן
verses.translation: A house is built by wisdom,And is established by understanding;

verses.hebrew_full: וּ֭בְדַעַת חֲדָרִ֣ים יִמָּלְא֑וּ כׇּל־ה֖וֹן יָקָ֣ר וְנָעִֽים
verses.translation: By knowledge are its rooms filledWith all precious and beautiful things.

verses.hebrew_full: גֶּבֶר־חָכָ֥ם בַּע֑וֹז וְאִֽישׁ־דַּ֝֗עַת מְאַמֶּץ־כֹּֽחַ
verses.translation: A wise man is strength;A knowledgeable man exerts power;
verses.rashi: A wise man is with strength A wise man is always with strength.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֣י בְ֭תַחְבֻּלוֹת תַּעֲשֶׂה־לְּךָ֣ מִלְחָמָ֑ה וּ֝תְשׁוּעָ֗ה בְּרֹ֣ב יוֹעֵֽץ
verses.translation: For by stratagems you wage war,And victory comes with much planning.

verses.hebrew_full: רָאמ֣וֹת לֶאֱוִ֣יל חׇכְמ֑וֹת בַּ֝שַּׁ֗עַר לֹ֣א יִפְתַּח־פִּֽיהוּ
verses.translation: Wisdom is too lofty for an ignoramus;He does not open his mouth in the gate.
verses.rashi: Wisdom is as pearls to the fool Heb. ראמות, a type of rare precious stone, as it is stated (Job 28: 18): “Pearls (ראמות) and beryls.” All wisdom appears to the fool as unattainable, [as difficult] to purchase as precious stones and pearls, saying, ‘’How will I learn Torah? When will I attain it?” But the wise man studies a little today and a little tomorrow.

verses.hebrew_full: מְחַשֵּׁ֥ב לְהָרֵ֑עַ ל֝֗וֹ בַּעַל־מְזִמּ֥וֹת יִקְרָֽאוּ
verses.translation: One who lays plans to do harmIs called a schemer.
verses.rashi: He who plots to do evil He plans strategies of wickedness. is called a man of wicked designs He brings harm to himself, for people call him a man of wicked designs, a counsel of the wicked.

verses.hebrew_full: זִמַּ֣ת אִוֶּ֣לֶת חַטָּ֑את וְתוֹעֲבַ֖ת לְאָדָ֣ם לֵֽץ
verses.translation: The schemes of folly are sin,And a scoffer is an abomination to all.
verses.rashi: The counsel of folly Heb. זמת אולת, the counsel of folly is sin for those who perpetrate it. and what makes man an abomination is scorn Scorn is what makes man an abomination to the Holy One blessed be He, and to people.

verses.hebrew_full: הִ֭תְרַפִּיתָ בְּי֥וֹם צָרָ֗ה צַ֣ר כֹּחֶֽכָה
verses.translation: If you showed yourself slack in time of trouble,Wanting in power,
verses.rashi: If you have become lax in the Torah. on a day of trouble your strength will be weak indeed The ministering angels will not maintain your strength. This is the midrash of the Sages (Midrash Mishle ad loc., Berachoth 63a). But according to its simple meaning, you have become lax on the day of your friend’s trouble, and stand from afar. your strength will be weak indeed on the day of your punishment, as it is written (Esther 4:14): “For if you remain silent, etc.”

verses.hebrew_full: הַ֭צֵּל לְקֻחִ֣ים לַמָּ֑וֶת וּמָטִ֥ים לַ֝הֶ֗רֶג אִם־תַּחְשֽׂוֹךְ
verses.translation: If you refrained from rescuing those taken off to death,Those condemned to slaughter—
verses.rashi: rescuing those taken, etc. To rescue those taken to death. If you refrain from rescuing them, concerning this I said, “Your strength will be weak indeed.”

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־תֹאמַ֗ר הֵן֮ לֹא־יָדַ֢עְנ֫וּ זֶ֥ה הֲֽלֹא־תֹ֘כֵ֤ן לִבּ֨וֹת הֽוּא־יָבִ֗ין וְנֹצֵ֣ר נַ֭פְשְׁךָ ה֣וּא יֵדָ֑ע וְהֵשִׁ֖יב לְאָדָ֣ם כְּפׇעֳלֽוֹ
verses.translation: If you say, “We knew nothing of it,”Surely the One who fathoms hearts will discern [the truth],The One who watches over your life will know it,And [God] will pay each person as they deserve.
verses.rashi: will you say Heb. כי תאמר. And this instance of כי is used as an expression of “perhaps.”

verses.hebrew_full: אֱכׇל־בְּנִ֣י דְבַ֣שׁ כִּי־ט֑וֹב וְנֹ֥פֶת מָ֝ת֗וֹק עַל־חִכֶּֽךָ
verses.translation: My son, eat honey, for it is good;Let its sweet drops be on your palate.
verses.rashi: Eat honey, my son It is customary for people to eat honey because it is good; so too know wisdom for your soul, and just as you run to eat honey, so should you run to know wisdom.

verses.hebrew_full: כֵּ֤ן דְּעֶ֥ה חׇכְמָ֗ה לְנַ֫פְשֶׁ֥ךָ אִם־מָ֭צָאתָ וְיֵ֣שׁ אַחֲרִ֑ית וְ֝תִקְוָתְךָ֗ לֹ֣א תִכָּרֵֽת
verses.translation: Know: such is wisdom for your soul;If you attain it, there is a future;Your hope will not be cut off.
verses.rashi: if you have found it, then you will have a future.

verses.hebrew_full: אַל־תֶּאֱרֹ֣ב רָ֭שָׁע לִנְוֵ֣ה צַדִּ֑יק אַֽל־תְּשַׁדֵּ֥ד רִבְצֽוֹ
verses.translation: Wicked ones! Do not lurk by the home of the righteous;Do no violence to their dwellings.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֤י שֶׁ֨בַע יִפּ֣וֹל צַדִּ֣יק וָקָ֑ם וּ֝רְשָׁעִ֗ים יִכָּשְׁל֥וּ בְרָעָֽה
verses.translation: Seven times a righteous person falls and gets up,While the wicked are tripped by one misfortune.

verses.hebrew_full: בִּנְפֹ֣ל (אויביך) א֭וֹיִבְךָ אַל־תִּשְׂמָ֑ח וּ֝בִכָּשְׁל֗וֹ אַל־יָגֵ֥ל לִבֶּֽךָ
verses.translation: If your enemies fall, do not exult;If they trip, let your heart not rejoice,

verses.hebrew_full: פֶּן־יִרְאֶ֣ה יְ֭הֹוָה וְרַ֣ע בְּעֵינָ֑יו וְהֵשִׁ֖יב מֵעָלָ֣יו אַפּֽוֹ
verses.translation: Lest GOD see it and be displeased,And avert God’s wrath from them.

verses.hebrew_full: אַל־תִּתְחַ֥ר בַּמְּרֵעִ֑ים אַל־תְּ֝קַנֵּ֗א בָּֽרְשָׁעִֽים
verses.translation: Do not be vexed by evildoers;Do not be incensed by the wicked;
verses.rashi: Do not compete to be like them, as in (Jer. 12:5): “How will you compete (תתחרה)?” Also, (ibid. 22:15): “for you compete (מתחרה) with the cedar.”

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֤י לֹא־תִהְיֶ֣ה אַחֲרִ֣ית לָרָ֑ע נֵ֖ר רְשָׁעִ֣ים יִדְעָֽךְ
verses.translation: For there is no future for an evildoer;The lamp of the wicked goes out.

verses.hebrew_full: יְרָא־אֶת־יְהֹוָ֣ה בְּנִ֣י וָמֶ֑לֶךְ עִם־שׁ֝וֹנִ֗ים אַל־תִּתְעָרָֽב
verses.translation: Fear GOD, my son, and the king,And do not mix with dissenters,
verses.rashi: My son, fear the Lord and the king Fear the Lord and fear the mortal king, but only if he does not turn you away from the fear of the Lord, for the fear of the Lord is always first. do not mingle with dualists Heb. שונים, who say that there are two powers [governing the world].

words.eng: to fear·sign of the definite direct object·Commonly transcribed YHWH

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־פִ֭תְאֹם יָק֣וּם אֵידָ֑ם וּפִ֥יד שְׁ֝נֵיהֶ֗ם מִ֣י יוֹדֵֽעַ
verses.translation: For disaster comes from them suddenly;The doom both decree who can foreknow?
verses.rashi: and the ruin of both of them The ruin of the idol and the ruin of its worshippers.

verses.hebrew_full: גַּם־אֵ֥לֶּה לַחֲכָמִ֑ים הַֽכֵּר־פָּנִ֖ים בְּמִשְׁפָּ֣ט בַּל־טֽוֹב
verses.translation: These also are by the sages:It is not right to be partial in judgment.
verses.rashi: These too are for the wise All the statements in the following section are addressed to the wise who sit in judgment, that they should not show respect to persons in judgment, for it is not good.

verses.hebrew_full: אֹ֤מֵ֨ר לְרָשָׁע֮ צַדִּ֢יק אָ֥֫תָּה יִקְּבֻ֥הוּ עַמִּ֑ים יִזְעָמ֥וּהוּ לְאֻמִּֽים
verses.translation: One who says to the guilty, “You are innocent,”Shall be cursed by peoples,Damned by nations;

verses.hebrew_full: וְלַמּוֹכִיחִ֥ים יִנְעָ֑ם וַ֝עֲלֵיהֶ֗ם תָּב֥וֹא בִרְכַּת־טֽוֹב
verses.translation: But it shall go well with them who decide justly;Blessings of good things will light upon them.

verses.hebrew_full: שְׂפָתַ֥יִם יִשָּׁ֑ק מֵ֝שִׁ֗יב דְּבָרִ֥ים נְכֹחִֽים
verses.translation: Giving a straightforward replyIs like giving a kiss.
verses.rashi: Lips should kiss It is fitting that all lips kiss him.

verses.hebrew_full: הָ֘כֵ֤ן בַּח֨וּץ מְלַאכְתֶּ֗ךָ וְעַתְּדָ֣הּ בַּשָּׂדֶ֣ה לָ֑ךְ אַ֝חַ֗ר וּבָנִ֥יתָ בֵיתֶֽךָ
verses.translation: Put your external affairs in order,Get ready what you have in the field,Then build yourself a home.
verses.rashi: Prepare...outside This refers to Scripture. and make it fit in the field This is Mishnah. afterwards you shall build your house This is Talmud (Sotah 44a). Another explanation according to its apparent meaning: In the beginning, buy yourself fields and vineyards, and afterwards make it fit, ועתדה, an expression concerning goats (עתודים). Stock your field with livestock, and afterwards you shall build a house, meaning that you shall take a wife.

verses.hebrew_full: אַל־תְּהִ֣י עֵד־חִנָּ֣ם בְּרֵעֶ֑ךָ וַ֝הֲפִתִּ֗יתָ בִּשְׂפָתֶֽיךָ
verses.translation: Do not be a witness against your fellow without good cause;Would you mislead with your speech?
verses.rashi: that you should be enticed with your lips You should be enticed by your friend with your speech.

verses.hebrew_full: אַל־תֹּאמַ֗ר כַּאֲשֶׁ֣ר עָֽשָׂה־לִ֭י כֵּ֤ן אֶֽעֱשֶׂה־לּ֑וֹ אָשִׁ֖יב לָאִ֣ישׁ כְּפׇעֳלֽוֹ
verses.translation: Do not say, “I will do to him what he did to me;I will pay the man what he deserves.”

words.eng: to do·inflected pers. pron. meaning ‘to me’.

verses.hebrew_full: עַל־שְׂדֵ֣ה אִישׁ־עָצֵ֣ל עָבַ֑רְתִּי וְעַל־כֶּ֝֗רֶם אָדָ֥ם חֲסַר־לֵֽב
verses.translation: I passed by the field of a lazy man,By the vineyard of someone lacking sense.
verses.rashi: a lazy man who does not review the memorization of his studies.

words.eng: to pass over or by or through

verses.hebrew_full: וְהִנֵּ֨ה עָ֘לָ֤ה כֻלּ֨וֹ קִמְּשֹׂנִ֗ים כׇּסּ֣וּ פָנָ֣יו חֲרֻלִּ֑ים וְגֶ֖דֶר אֲבָנָ֣יו נֶהֱרָֽסָה
verses.translation: It was all overgrown with thorns;Its surface was covered with chickweed,And its stone fence lay in ruins.
verses.rashi: thistles Heb. קמשונים, thistles. nettles חרלים. They are larger than thistles, and they are as sharp as thorns. and its stone fence had been torn down Thus, one who does not review his studies first forgets the beginnings of the chapters and at the end confuses the names of the Sages from this one to that one, thereby saying “unclean” for something that is clean and “clean” for something that is unclean, and he destroys the world.

verses.hebrew_full: וָאֶחֱזֶ֣ה אָ֭נֹכִי אָשִׁ֣ית לִבִּ֑י רָ֝אִ֗יתִי לָקַ֥חְתִּי מוּסָֽר
verses.translation: I observed and took it to heart;I saw it and learned a lesson.

verses.hebrew_full: מְעַ֣ט שֵׁ֭נוֹת מְעַ֣ט תְּנוּמ֑וֹת מְעַ֓ט חִבֻּ֖ק יָדַ֣יִם לִשְׁכָּֽב
verses.translation: A bit more sleep, a bit more slumber,A bit more hugging yourself in bed,

verses.hebrew_full: וּבָֽא־מִתְהַלֵּ֥ךְ רֵישֶׁ֑ךָ וּ֝מַחְסֹרֶ֗יךָ כְּאִ֣ישׁ מָגֵֽן
verses.translation: And poverty will come calling upon you, And want, like a man with a shield.
verses.rashi: your poverty will come strolling The things that impoverish you will come to you quickly.

verses.hebrew_full: גַּם־אֵ֭לֶּה מִשְׁלֵ֣י שְׁלֹמֹ֑ה אֲשֶׁ֥ר הֶ֝עְתִּ֗יקוּ אַנְשֵׁ֤י חִזְקִיָּ֬ה מֶלֶךְ־יְהוּדָֽה
verses.translation: These too are proverbs of Solomon, which the officials of King Hezekiah of Judah copied:
verses.rashi: These too are Solomon’s proverbs, which...maintained When Hezekiah was appointed king, he returned to expound upon them [Solomon’s proverbs]. Now he placed disciples in every city, as it is stated in chapter “Chelek” (Sanh. 94b): “They searched from Dan to Beersheba and could not find an ignoramus.” maintained Heb. העתיקו they strengthened.

words.eng: Hezekiah or Hizkiah or Hizkijah = "Jehovah is my strength"

verses.hebrew_full: כְּבֹ֣ד אֱ֭לֹהִים הַסְתֵּ֣ר דָּבָ֑ר וּכְבֹ֥ד מְ֝לָכִ֗ים חֲקֹ֣ר דָּבָֽר
verses.translation: It is the glory of God to conceal a matter,And the glory of a king to plumb a matter.
verses.rashi: The honor of God is to conceal a matter For instance, the account of the Merkavah and the account of the Creation. whereas the honor of kings is to search out a matter When you expound on the honor of the Sages and on the safeguards that they enacted to the Torah, and on the decrees that they decreed upon them, you should search, seek, and ask the reason for the matter. When you expound on the account of the Merkavah or on the account of the Creation, or on the statutes written in the Torah—like the statutes and things that Satan denounces and refutes, such as eating pork, mingled species in a vineyard, and shaatnes—you should not search, but only conceal [the reason] and say, “It is the King’s decree.”

verses.hebrew_full: שָׁמַ֣יִם לָ֭רוּם וָאָ֣רֶץ לָעֹ֑מֶק וְלֵ֥ב מְ֝לָכִ֗ים אֵ֣ין חֵֽקֶר
verses.translation: Like the heavens in their height, like the earth in its depth,Is the mind of kings—unfathomable.
verses.rashi: are unsearchable The height of the heaven and the depth of the earth and the heart of kings are unsearchable, for many judgments come before them, as well as many wars, and they must apply their heart to all of them. And if all tongues would speak, and all hands would write, they would not be able to inscribe the intricacy of political government.

verses.hebrew_full: הָג֣וֹ סִיגִ֣ים מִכָּ֑סֶף וַיֵּצֵ֖א לַצֹּרֵ֣ף כֶּֽלִי
verses.translation: The dross having been separated from the silver,A vessel emerged for the smith.
verses.rashi: Remove dross from silver Heb. הגו, draw away, as in (II Sam. 20:13): “When he was removed (הגה) from the highway.” Just as the silver vessel does not emerge for the work of the refiner until the copper dross is removed from it, similarly, the community cannot be freed from punishment until they remove the wicked from their midst and execute judgment upon them.

verses.hebrew_full: הָג֣וֹ רָ֭שָׁע לִפְנֵי־מֶ֑לֶךְ וְיִכּ֖וֹן בַּצֶּ֣דֶק כִּסְאֽוֹ
verses.translation: Remove the wicked from the king’s presence,And his throne will be established in justice.

verses.hebrew_full: אַל־תִּתְהַדַּ֥ר לִפְנֵי־מֶ֑לֶךְ וּבִמְק֥וֹם גְּ֝דֹלִ֗ים אַֽל־תַּעֲמֹֽד
verses.translation: Do not exalt yourself in the king’s presence;Do not stand in the place of nobles.
verses.rashi: Do not glorify yourself before a king to show your honor and to be proud before one who is greater than you, because it is better that they say to you, “Come up here,” than that you should go without his permission, and they should tell you, “Go down, get out.”

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֤י ט֥וֹב אֲמׇר־לְךָ֗ עֲֽלֵ֫ה־הֵ֥נָּה מֵ֭הַשְׁפִּ֣ילְךָ לִפְנֵ֣י נָדִ֑יב אֲשֶׁ֖ר רָא֣וּ עֵינֶֽיךָ
verses.translation: For it is better to be told, “Step up here,”Than to be degraded in the presence of the great.Do not let what your eyes have seen
verses.rashi: as your eyes have seen that this thing is true.

verses.hebrew_full: אַל־תֵּצֵ֥א לָרִ֗ב מַ֫הֵ֥ר פֶּ֣ן מַה־תַּ֭עֲשֶׂה בְּאַחֲרִיתָ֑הּ בְּהַכְלִ֖ים אֹתְךָ֣ רֵעֶֽךָ
verses.translation: Be vented rashly in a quarrel;Think of what it will effect in the end,When your fellow puts you to shame.
verses.rashi: lest [you will not know] what you will do at its end Lest you come to a situation that you will not know what to do at its end.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object

verses.hebrew_full: רִ֭יבְךָ רִ֣יב אֶת־רֵעֶ֑ךָ וְס֖וֹד אַחֵ֣ר אַל־תְּגָֽל
verses.translation: Defend your right against your fellow,But do not give away the secrets of another,
verses.rashi: Have your quarrel with your friend But if, perforce, you must quarrel and debate with your friend, in any event, do not divulge another’s secret Do not mention to him the disgrace of his ancestors, which not everyone knows, and you are revealing it.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·shouting

verses.hebrew_full: פֶּֽן־יְחַסֶּדְךָ֥ שֹׁמֵ֑עַ וְ֝דִבָּתְךָ֗ לֹ֣א תָשֽׁוּב
verses.translation: Lest someone who hears it reproach you,And your bad repute never end.
verses.rashi: lest one who hears embarrass you Heb. יחסדך (Josh. 5:9) “The reproach of the Egyptians” is rendered: חסודא דמצראי. Lest one who hears embarrass you, and they will call you a slanderer.

verses.hebrew_full: תַּפּוּחֵ֣י זָ֭הָב בְּמַשְׂכִּיּ֣וֹת כָּ֑סֶף דָּ֝בָ֗ר דָּבֻ֥ר עַל־אׇפְנָֽיו
verses.translation: Like golden apples in silver showpieces Is a phrase well turned.
verses.rashi: golden apples Like knobs depicted on silverplated vessels. silverplated vessels Heb. משכיות כסף, vessels plated with silver, as in (Ex. 33:22): “and I will cover (ושכתי) you with My hand.” a word spoken with proper basis Heb. אפניו, on its basis. An example of it is (Ps. 88:16): “I bear your terrors, it is settled (אפונה) [in my heart],” based and settled within me. This is not from the root of (Isa. 28: 27f): אפן and גלגל, wheel, for, were it so, it would be vowelized with a “pattach” under the “fe” as in (Ezek. 1:16) “האופנים the wheels.” The vowelization of the short kamatz is inappropriate for it.

verses.hebrew_full: נֶ֣זֶם זָ֭הָב וַחֲלִי־כָ֑תֶם מוֹכִ֥יחַ חָ֝כָ֗ם עַל־אֹ֥זֶן שֹׁמָֽעַת
verses.translation: Like a ring of gold, a golden ornament,Is a wise person’s reproof in a receptive ear.
verses.rashi: Like a golden earring and jewelry of finest gold A collection of golden jewelry, as in (Hos. 2: 15): “and adorned herself with her earrings and her jewelry (וחליתה),” and like (Song 7:3): “jewels (חלאים).” finest gold Heb. כתם an expression of a golden ornament, and likewise (Song 5:11): “His head is as a jewel (כתם) of the finest gold.”

verses.hebrew_full: כְּצִנַּת־שֶׁ֨לֶג בְּי֬וֹם קָצִ֗יר צִ֣יר נֶ֭אֱמָן לְשֹׁלְחָ֑יו וְנֶ֖פֶשׁ אֲדֹנָ֣יו יָשִֽׁיב
verses.translation: Like the coldness of snow at harvesttimeIs a trustworthy messenger to those who send him;He lifts his master’s spirits.
verses.rashi: Like the cold of snow Like the cold of the days of snow (winter), for which a person longs at harvest, but not actual snow, because snow is not beneficial during harvest.

verses.hebrew_full: נְשִׂיאִ֣ים וְ֭רוּחַ וְגֶ֣שֶׁם אָ֑יִן אִ֥ישׁ מִ֝תְהַלֵּ֗ל בְּמַתַּת־שָֽׁקֶר
verses.translation: Like clouds, wind—but no rain—Is a man who boasts of gifts not given.
verses.rashi: Clouds and wind, etc. When there is a false hope—when the heavens thicken with clouds and the wind blows—people hope that it will rain. Now if it does not rain, they are troubled and their eyes languish. So is a man who boasts, saying, “So much and so much charity I will give to the collector,” but he lies, and the eyes of the poor languish for his gift, but it does not come.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּאֹ֣רֶךְ אַ֭פַּיִם יְפֻתֶּ֣ה קָצִ֑ין וְלָשׁ֥וֹן רַ֝כָּ֗ה תִּשְׁבׇּר־גָּֽרֶם
verses.translation: Through forbearance a ruler may be won over;A gentle tongue can break bones.
verses.rashi: The Ruler is won over with slowness to anger As long as the Holy One, blessed be He, holds back His anger and does not requite, the sinners will apply their hearts to win Him over with repentance and prayer. and a gentle tongue with prayer and supplications. will break a bone Heb. גרם a bone, the severity of the decree.

verses.hebrew_full: דְּבַ֣שׁ מָ֭צָאתָ אֱכֹ֣ל דַּיֶּ֑ךָּ פֶּן־תִּ֝שְׂבָּעֶ֗נּוּ וַהֲקֵאתֽוֹ
verses.translation: If you find honey, eat only what you need,Lest, surfeiting yourself, you throw it up.
verses.rashi: If you have found honey, etc....

verses.hebrew_full: הֹקַ֣ר רַ֭גְלְךָ מִבֵּ֣ית רֵעֶ֑ךָ פֶּן־יִ֝שְׂבָּעֲךָ֗ וּשְׂנֵאֶֽךָ
verses.translation: Visit your neighbor sparingly,Lest he have his surfeit of you and loathe you.
verses.rashi: Visit your neighbor sparingly Just as, if you find honey, and it is sweet to your palate, you must not eat [too much] of it lest you become sated with it and vomit it, so should you visit your neighbor sparingly, although he is from your midst [of your kin], refrain from going there daily lest he become sated with you and hate you. [Bracketed words appear in Salonica edition only.] According to its homiletic interpretation, do not accustom yourself to sin inadvertently and to constantly bring sin- offerings and guilt-offerings to the house of the Lord, Who is called a friend to Israel, as it is written (Song 5:16): “This is my beloved and this is my friend.”

verses.hebrew_full: מֵפִ֣יץ וְ֭חֶרֶב וְחֵ֣ץ שָׁנ֑וּן אִ֥ישׁ עֹנֶ֥ה בְ֝רֵעֵ֗הוּ עֵ֣ד שָֽׁקֶר
verses.translation: Like a club, a sword, a sharpened arrow,Is a man who testifies falsely against his fellow.
verses.rashi: like a club, a sword Names of weapons.

verses.hebrew_full: שֵׁ֣ן רֹ֭עָה וְרֶ֣גֶל מוּעָ֑דֶת מִבְטָ֥ח בּ֝וֹגֵ֗ד בְּי֣וֹם צָרָֽה
verses.translation: Like a loose tooth and an unsteady leg,Is a treacherous support in time of trouble.
verses.rashi: a broken tooth Heb. רעה, broken. unsteady Heb. מועדת, as in (Ps. 18:37): “and my ankles have not slipped (מעדו).” trust in a traitor He disappoints him on the day of his trouble like a broken tooth and an unsteady [foot].

verses.hebrew_full: מַ֥עֲדֶה־בֶּ֨גֶד בְּי֣וֹם קָ֭רָה חֹ֣מֶץ עַל־נָ֑תֶר וְשָׁ֥ר בַּ֝שִּׁרִ֗ים עַ֣ל לֶב־רָֽע
verses.translation: Disrobing on a chilly day,Like vinegar on natron,Is one who sings songs to a sorrowful soul.
verses.rashi: A worn-out garment on a cold day Heb. מעדה בגד, synonymous with (Isa. 64:5): “בגד עדים, a discarded garment,” a worn-out garment that is fit to be taken off since it is worn out, as we translate into Aramaic “ויסר” (Ex. 7:24) as ויעדי, and He shall remove. Now this is its interpretation: A worn-out garment on a cold day is like vinegar on chalk. chalk Heb. נתר, a kind of soft earth, like our earth that is called creide, which they would hew and make vessels therefrom, and if vinegar falls on it, [the earth] is dissolved and ruined. So... is one who sings songs to a broken heart [lit. on a bad heart. This one] resembles them both. Now what is one who sings songs? This is one who teaches the Torah to a wicked student, who has no intention in his heart to fulfill it.

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־רָעֵ֣ב שֹׂ֭נַאֲךָ הַאֲכִלֵ֣הוּ לָ֑חֶם וְאִם־צָ֝מֵ֗א הַשְׁקֵ֥הוּ מָֽיִם
verses.translation: If your enemy is hungry, give him bread to eat;If he is thirsty, give him water to drink.
verses.rashi: If your enemy is hungry [to be explained] according to its apparent meaning. Our Rabbis, however, explained it as referring to the evil inclination. If he is hungry and tells you to sate him with sins, draw yourself into the study hall and feed him the bread of Torah, and likewise, give him the water of Torah to drink.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֤י גֶחָלִ֗ים אַ֭תָּה חֹתֶ֣ה עַל־רֹאשׁ֑וֹ וַ֝יהֹוָ֗ה יְשַׁלֶּם־לָֽךְ
verses.translation: You will be heaping live coals on his head,And GOD will reward you.
verses.rashi: for...coals they are to him, that you are scooping from the fire to heap upon his head. scooping Heb. חותה. Any scooping of coals from a fire is called חותה, as it is stated (Isa. 30:14): “to scoop (לחתות) fire from a hearth.” will reward you Heb. ישלם לך, will cause him to make peace with you, that he should not overpower you.

words.eng: to be in a covenant of peace·unto thee

verses.hebrew_full: ר֣וּחַ צָ֭פוֹן תְּח֣וֹלֵֽל גָּ֑שֶׁם וּפָנִ֥ים נִ֝זְעָמִ֗ים לְשׁ֣וֹן סָֽתֶר
verses.translation: A north wind produces rain,And whispered words, a glowering face.
verses.rashi: As the north wind begets rain It begets and creates the rain. an angry countenance A backbiting tongue begets [an angry countenance]. Slander causes the countenance of the Holy One, blessed be He, to be angry, and this is the comparison [of the interpretation] of the verse. [The bracketed words do not appear in the Salonica edition, which seems more correct than our edition.] The north wind is accustomed to beget rain and a backbiting tongue is accustomed to [beget] an angry countenance.

verses.hebrew_full: ט֗וֹב שֶׁ֥בֶת עַל־פִּנַּת־גָּ֑ג מֵאֵ֥שֶׁת (מדונים) מִ֝דְיָנִ֗ים וּבֵ֥ית חָֽבֶר
verses.translation: Dwelling in the corner of a roof is betterThan a contentious woman in a spacious house.
verses.rashi: It is better to sit on the corner of a roof This is stated concerning the withdrawal of the Shechinah. than a quarrelsome wife The nation of lsrael, who dealt wickedly and provoked the Holy One, blessed be He. and a house of companionship A house in which they associated idolatry with the Shechinah.

verses.hebrew_full: מַ֣יִם קָ֭רִים עַל־נֶ֣פֶשׁ עֲיֵפָ֑ה וּשְׁמוּעָ֥ה ט֝וֹבָ֗ה מֵאֶ֥רֶץ מֶרְחָֽק
verses.translation: Like cold water to a parched throatIs good news from a distant land.
verses.rashi: to a faint soul It [cold water] is accustomed to revive a faint soul. so is good news That too is equal to it, and so was it with Jacob (Gen. 45:27): “And the spirit of Jacob their father was revived.”

verses.hebrew_full: מַעְיָ֣ן נִ֭רְפָּשׂ וּמָק֣וֹר מׇשְׁחָ֑ת צַ֝דִּ֗יק מָ֣ט לִפְנֵֽי־רָשָֽׁע
verses.translation: Like a muddied spring, a ruined fountain,Are the righteous fallen before the wicked.
verses.rashi: a muddied spring Muddied by the feet. A righteous man slipping before a wicked man When a righteous man slips before a wicked man and is afraid to reprove him for his way to his face, the matter is hateful like a muddied spring and a ruined fountain.

verses.hebrew_full: אָ֘כֹ֤ל דְּבַ֣שׁ הַרְבּ֣וֹת לֹא־ט֑וֹב וְחֵ֖קֶר כְּבֹדָ֣ם כָּבֽוֹד
verses.translation: It is not good to eat much honey,Nor is it honorable to search for honor.
verses.rashi: Eating honey To eat honey to excess. The topic symbolizes one who expounds on the account of the Merkavah and the account of the Creation to the public; the ignoramuses will ridicule the words and ask what is above and what is below. but fathoming their honor is honor But when is fathoming appropriate? In the words of the Sages, whose honor is honor: We may ask why they decreed such and such a decree, and why they enacted a safeguard in each decree.

verses.hebrew_full: עִ֣יר פְּ֭רוּצָה אֵ֣ין חוֹמָ֑ה אִ֝֗ישׁ אֲשֶׁ֤ר אֵ֖ין מַעְצָ֣ר לְרוּחֽוֹ
verses.translation: Like an open city without wallsIs a man whose temper is uncurbed.

words.eng: to break through or down or over

verses.hebrew_full: כַּשֶּׁ֤לֶג בַּקַּ֗יִץ וְכַמָּטָ֥ר בַּקָּצִ֑יר כֵּ֤ן לֹֽא־נָאוֶ֖ה לִכְסִ֣יל כָּבֽוֹד
verses.translation: Like snow in summer and rain at harvesttime,So honor is not fitting for a dullard.
verses.rashi: Like snow in the summer when they spread the figs in the sun to dry them and to make fig cakes, as is stated (II Sam. 16:2): “the bread and the dried figs for the young men to eat.”

verses.hebrew_full: כַּצִּפּ֣וֹר לָ֭נוּד כַּדְּר֣וֹר לָע֑וּף כֵּ֥ן קִֽלְלַ֥ת חִ֝נָּ֗ם (לא) ל֣וֹ תָבֹֽא
verses.translation: As a sparrow must flit and a swallow fly,So a gratuitous curse must backfire., “fail.”
verses.rashi: Like a wandering sparrow that wanders and like a swallow that returns to its nest, so... will a vain curse come home to the one who uttered it with his mouth. swallow Heb. דרור. This is the bird known as arondele in Old French, [hirondelle in modern French, schwalbe in German,] which is called דרור, because it lives (דר) in a house as in a field.

verses.hebrew_full: שׁ֣וֹט לַ֭סּוּס מֶ֣תֶג לַחֲמ֑וֹר וְ֝שֵׁ֗בֶט לְגֵ֣ו כְּסִילִֽים
verses.translation: A whip for a horse and a bridle for a donkey,And a rod for the back of dullards.
verses.rashi: A whip is for the horse [It] is made for the horse, and also a rod is prepared for the body of fools. Tortures are prepared for the wicked.

verses.hebrew_full: אַל־תַּ֣עַן כְּ֭סִיל כְּאִוַּלְתּ֑וֹ פֶּֽן־תִּשְׁוֶה־לּ֥וֹ גַם־אָֽתָּה
verses.translation: Do not answer a dullard in accord with his folly,Else you will become like him.
verses.rashi: Do not answer a fool with words of quarrel and contention lest you become like him.

words.eng: corner·to agree with·unto him; v. ל־.

verses.hebrew_full: עֲנֵ֣ה כְ֭סִיל כְּאִוַּלְתּ֑וֹ פֶּן־יִהְיֶ֖ה חָכָ֣ם בְּעֵינָֽיו
verses.translation: Answer a dullard in accord with his folly,Else he will think himself wise.
verses.rashi: Answer a fool who comes to win you over to idolatry; let him know his folly. lest he be wise in his sight The meaning of these two verses is explained in [the verses] themselves: Do not answer in a matter in which you will become like him if you answer him. Answer a fool in a matter in which he will be wise in his sight if you do not answer him.

verses.hebrew_full: מְקַצֶּ֣ה רַ֭גְלַיִם חָמָ֣ס שֹׁתֶ֑ה שֹׁלֵ֖חַ דְּבָרִ֣ים בְּיַד־כְּסִֽיל
verses.translation: One who sends a message by a dullardWill wear out legs and must put up with lawlessness.
verses.rashi: wears out legs and “drinks violence” He who sends a message by the hand of a fool wears out the legs of many messengers by repetitively sending [them] to rectify what the first one, whom he sent at the beginning, distorted. And he “drinks violence,” for his fellow is wroth with him for sending a fool.

verses.hebrew_full: דַּלְי֣וּ שֹׁ֭קַיִם מִפִּסֵּ֑חַ וּ֝מָשָׁ֗ל בְּפִ֣י כְסִילִֽים
verses.translation: As legs hang limp on a cripple,So is a proverb in the mouth of dullards.
verses.rashi: thighs seem raised to a lame man Heb. דליו, as in (Isa. 38:14): “My eyes were lifted (דלו).” Every man’s thighs appear to the lame to be higher than he. This phrase is a parable in the mouth of the fool, who says it in reference to the study of wisdom, “How can we come to study wisdom? It is hidden and raised up higher than I am. The parable states, ‘Thighs seem raised to a lame man.’”

verses.hebrew_full: כִּצְר֣וֹר אֶ֭בֶן בְּמַרְגֵּמָ֑ה כֵּן־נוֹתֵ֖ן לִכְסִ֣יל כָּבֽוֹד
verses.translation: Like a pebble in a sling,So is paying honor to a dullard.
verses.rashi: in a slingshot Heb. במרגמה fronde in French, Schleuder in German, in which the stone tied will not remain, for it is destined to be thrown, so is it with one who gives honor to a fool—it is not permanent. And our Sages (Hullin 133a) explained it homiletically as referring to one who teaches Torah to an unfitting student, that he is tantamount to casting a stone into a merculis.

verses.hebrew_full: ח֭וֹחַ עָלָ֣ה בְיַד־שִׁכּ֑וֹר וּ֝מָשָׁ֗ל בְּפִ֣י כְסִילִֽים
verses.translation: As a thorn comes to the hand of a drunkard,So a proverb to the mouth of a dullard.
verses.rashi: A thorn came up in a drunkard’s hand Just like a thorn that is stuck to a drunkard’s hand, so is the parable stated below stuck in the mouth of a fool to be for him as a thorn—yea, a painful thorn. Now what is the parable?...

verses.hebrew_full: רַ֥ב מְחוֹלֵֽל־כֹּ֑ל וְשֹׂכֵ֥ר כְּ֝סִ֗יל וְשֹׂכֵ֥ר עֹבְרִֽים
verses.translation: A master can produce anything, But anyone who hires a dullard is as one who hires transients.
verses.rashi: The Master created all The Holy One, blessed be He, created all and sustains all, the fool like the wise man. We do not need any wisdom. and He hires a fool Unlike a mortal, who hires only expert workers, and who hires only those engaged in his work, the Holy One, blessed be He, creates all and hires the fools and hires wayfarers, who are idle from all work. [According to] Midrash Aggadah, ושכר כסיל is from an expression of (Gen. 8:2) “And the fountains of the deep were closed (ויסכרו),” and an expression of (Isa. 19:10) “who make dams (שכר).” The Holy One, blessed be He, closes up the constellation of Orion (כסיל), which serves in the summer from Tishri on, and from then on, He closes off all who traverse the sea from going into it until Passover. And in the words of Rabbi Moses, I saw: רב מחולל כל, A wealthy man has many activities, and if he hires a fool, it is as though he is hiring all wayfarers who witness the deterioration of the work, to instruct how to rectify it and how he should work. This is, however, an empty thing and has no connection here.

verses.hebrew_full: כְּ֭כֶלֶב שָׁ֣ב עַל־קֵא֑וֹ כְּ֝סִ֗יל שׁוֹנֶ֥ה בְאִוַּלְתּֽוֹ
verses.translation: As a dog returns to its vomit,So a dullard repeats his folly.

verses.hebrew_full: רָאִ֗יתָ אִ֭ישׁ חָכָ֣ם בְּעֵינָ֑יו תִּקְוָ֖ה לִכְסִ֣יל מִמֶּֽנּוּ
verses.translation: If you see a man who thinks himself wise,There is more hope for a dullard than for him.

verses.hebrew_full: אָמַ֣ר עָ֭צֵל שַׁ֣חַל בַּדָּ֑רֶךְ אֲ֝רִ֗י בֵּ֣ין הָרְחֹבֽוֹת
verses.translation: The sluggard says,“There’s a cub on the road, a lion in the squares.”

words.eng: broad or open place or plaza

verses.hebrew_full: הַ֭דֶּלֶת תִּסּ֣וֹב עַל־צִירָ֑הּ וְ֝עָצֵ֗ל עַל־מִטָּתֽוֹ
verses.translation: The door turns upon the hinge,And the sluggard upon the bed.

verses.hebrew_full: טָ֘מַ֤ן עָצֵ֣ל יָ֭דוֹ בַּצַּלָּ֑חַת נִ֝לְאָ֗ה לַהֲשִׁיבָ֥הּ אֶל־פִּֽיו
verses.translation: The sluggard buries a hand in the bowl,And will not even bring it to the mouth.
verses.rashi: in the cauldron He buries his hand in a hot cauldron because of the cold.

verses.hebrew_full: חָכָ֣ם עָצֵ֣ל בְּעֵינָ֑יו מִ֝שִּׁבְעָ֗ה מְשִׁ֣יבֵי טָֽעַם
verses.translation: In self-regard, the sluggard is wiserThan seven who give good advice.
verses.rashi: who give advice wise men.

verses.hebrew_full: מַחֲזִ֥יק בְּאׇזְנֵי־כָ֑לֶב עֹבֵ֥ר מִ֝תְעַבֵּ֗ר עַל־רִ֥יב לֹּא־לֽוֹ
verses.translation: A passerby who gets embroiled in someone else’s quarrelIs like one who seizes a dog by its ears.
verses.rashi: one who grabs a dog by its ears One who passes by and becomes embroiled in a quarrel that is not his is like one who grabs a dog by his ears, who causes him to bite him for no reason.

words.eng: to pass over or by or through
to pass over or by or through

verses.hebrew_full: כְּֽ֭מִתְלַהְלֵהַּ הַיֹּרֶ֥ה זִקִּ֗ים חִצִּ֥ים וָמָֽוֶת
verses.translation: Like a lunatic scattering deadly firebrands, arrows,
verses.rashi: Like one who wearies himself Like one who wearies himself shooting sparks of fire, as in (Isa. 50: 11): “and in the flames (ובזיקות) you have kindled”; from the expression זיקוקים דינור (Ber. 58b), flames of fire. Another explanation. זקים, frondeles in Old French, slings, from an expression of slingstones (Zech. 9:15), and so in the Talmud (Baba Mezia 94a) זיקתא פסוק לן, “slingers are assigned to us.” He shoots arrows and death.

verses.hebrew_full: כֵּֽן־אִ֭ישׁ רִמָּ֣ה אֶת־רֵעֵ֑הוּ וְ֝אָמַ֗ר הֲֽלֹא־מְשַׂחֵ֥ק אָֽנִי
verses.translation: Is the man who cheats his fellow and says, “I was only joking.”
verses.rashi: so is a man who deceives He wins over and entices his friend from ways of life to ways of death, and when his friend realizes that he is misleading him, he says, “I am joking.”

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·shouting
I (first pers. sing. -usually used for emphasis)

verses.hebrew_full: בְּאֶ֣פֶס עֵ֭צִים תִּכְבֶּה־אֵ֑שׁ וּבְאֵ֥ין נִ֝רְגָּ֗ן יִשְׁתֹּ֥ק מָדֽוֹן
verses.translation: For lack of wood a fire goes out,And without a grumbler contention is stilled.
verses.rashi: without wood That is to say: These two things are analogous; just as without wood the fire goes out, so without a grumbler, who slanders and incites quarrelers, strife quiets down.

verses.hebrew_full: פֶּחָ֣ם לְ֭גֶחָלִים וְעֵצִ֣ים לְאֵ֑שׁ וְאִ֥ישׁ (מדונים) מִ֝דְיָנִ֗ים לְחַרְחַר־רִֽיב
verses.translation: Charcoal for embers and wood for a fireAnd a contentious man for kindling strife.
verses.rashi: As charcoal is for coals and wood is for fire Charcoal on fire is capable of igniting dying embers, and wood is capable of kindling fire, and a quarrelsome man of kindling strife.

verses.hebrew_full: דִּבְרֵ֣י נִ֭רְגָּן כְּמִֽתְלַהֲמִ֑ים וְ֝הֵ֗ם יָרְד֥וּ חַדְרֵי־בָֽטֶן
verses.translation: The words of a grumbler are bruising; They penetrate one’s inmost parts.
verses.rashi: as though waging battle Heb. כמתלהמים, an expression of מתלחמים. Our Sages however, explained כמתלהמים, כמתלהם, like death to them. The words of the spies became their death. the innermost parts of the body lit. they go down into the chambers of the stomach. This is the death of dropsy.

verses.hebrew_full: כֶּ֣סֶף סִ֭יגִים מְצֻפֶּ֣ה עַל־חָ֑רֶשׂ שְׂפָתַ֖יִם דֹּלְקִ֣ים וְלֶב־רָֽע
verses.translation: Base silver laid over earthenwareAre ardent lips with an evil mind.
verses.rashi: overlaid on earthenware Like silver dross attached to the earthenware in which it is refined, making the utensil shine as though it were silver although it has no use, so are burning lips and wicked heart, which pursue people to entice them with smooth and hypocritical talk. and a wicked heart They appear to be friends, but they are enemies. burning Heb. דולקים, as in “you pursued (דלקת) me so hotly” (Gen. 31:36).

verses.hebrew_full: בִּ֭שְׂפָתָו יִנָּכֵ֣ר שׂוֹנֵ֑א וּ֝בְקִרְבּ֗וֹ יָשִׁ֥ית מִרְמָֽה
verses.translation: An enemy dissembles with his speech,Inwardly he harbors deceit.
verses.rashi: An enemy dissembles With his speech, an enemy dissembles, so that it is not recognizable that he is an enemy. ינכר, deconnoitre in French, [verkennen in German, and so did Redak write, that it is an expression of denial and making oneself strange, the opposite of recognition (הכרה)].

verses.hebrew_full: כִּֽי־יְחַנֵּ֣ן ק֭וֹלוֹ אַל־תַּאֲמֶן־בּ֑וֹ כִּ֤י שֶׁ֖בַע תּוֹעֵב֣וֹת בְּלִבּֽוֹ
verses.translation: Though he be fair-spoken do not trust him,For seven abominations are in his mind.

words.eng: ram·to support·inflected third pers. singular m. of בּֽ◌ ᴵ

verses.hebrew_full: תִּכַּסֶּ֣ה שִׂ֭נְאָה בְּמַשָּׁא֑וֹן תִּגָּלֶ֖ה רָעָת֣וֹ בְקָהָֽל
verses.translation: His hatred may be concealed by dissimulation,But his evil will be exposed to public view.
verses.rashi: When the hatred is covered with darkness If one performs his deed in the darkness and conceals with משאון, which is pitch darkness (שואת חשך) the thing that is hated by the Holy One, blessed be He, [then] ultimately the Holy One, blessed be He, will reveal his evil in public, so that they should recognize that he is wicked.

verses.hebrew_full: כֹּֽרֶה־שַּׁ֭חַת בָּ֣הּ יִפּ֑וֹל וְג֥וֹלֵֽל אֶ֝֗בֶן אֵלָ֥יו תָּשֽׁוּב
verses.translation: One who digs a pit will fall in it,And a stone will roll back upon the one who is rolling it.
verses.rashi: He who digs a pit For example, Balaam, who persuaded Balak to cause Israel to sin and caused twenty-four thousand of them to fall. He came to Midian to demand his pay and was slain by Israel. and he who rolls That is to say that whoever places a stumbling block will ultimately stumble on it. and he who rolls a stone He rolls it from place to place so that people will stumble on it. The Aggadah (Tanhuma Buber, Vayera 28, see footnote 157) interprets it in reference to Abimelech, who slew his seventy brothers on one stone and his end was that he died by a stone, as it is said (Jud. 9:53): “And a certain woman cast a piece of an upper millstone upon Abimelech’s head and crushed his skull.”

verses.hebrew_full: לְֽשׁוֹן־שֶׁ֭קֶר יִשְׂנָ֣א דַכָּ֑יו וּפֶ֥ה חָ֝לָ֗ק יַעֲשֶׂ֥ה מִדְחֶֽה
verses.translation: A lying tongue hates those crushed by it; Smooth speech throws one down.
verses.rashi: hates those crushed under him He who accepts slander hates those crushed under him, for Saul pursued David because of slander and slew Nob. and a smooth mouth A mouth of flattery. effects rejection It makes him rejected, for it rejects the one who accepts it from upon the Holy One, blessed be He.

verses.hebrew_full: אַֽל־תִּ֭תְהַלֵּל בְּי֣וֹם מָחָ֑ר כִּ֤י לֹֽא־תֵ֝דַ֗ע מַה־יֵּ֥לֶד יֽוֹם
verses.translation: Do not boast of tomorrow,For you do not know what the day will bring.
verses.rashi: Do not boast Do not boast now concerning a thing that is destined to be tomorrow. for you do not know what the day will bear Perhaps the day will bear some evil that will nullify it.

verses.hebrew_full: יְהַלֶּלְךָ֣ זָ֣ר וְלֹא־פִ֑יךָ נׇ֝כְרִ֗י וְאַל־שְׂפָתֶֽיךָ
verses.translation: Let the mouth of another praise you, not yours,The lips of a stranger, not your own.

verses.hebrew_full: כֹּֽבֶד־אֶ֭בֶן וְנֵ֣טֶל הַח֑וֹל וְכַ֥עַס אֱ֝וִ֗יל כָּבֵ֥ד מִשְּׁנֵיהֶֽם
verses.translation: A stone has weight, sand is heavy,But a fool’s vexation outweighs them both.
verses.rashi: and the burden Heb. ונטל, a burden. the anger of a fool that a fool angers the Holy One, blessed be He, causes [Him] to bring anger to the world.

verses.hebrew_full: אַכְזְרִיּ֣וּת חֵ֭מָה וְשֶׁ֣טֶף אָ֑ף וּמִ֥י יַ֝עֲמֹ֗ד לִפְנֵ֥י קִנְאָֽה
verses.translation: There is the cruelty of fury, the overflowing of anger,But who can withstand jealousy?
verses.rashi: but who can stand up before jealousy Who can stand up before the jealousy of He Who is jealous and vengeful?

verses.hebrew_full: ט֭וֹבָה תּוֹכַ֣חַת מְגֻלָּ֑ה מֵאַהֲבָ֥ה מְסֻתָּֽרֶת
verses.translation: Open reproof is better than concealed love.

verses.hebrew_full: נֶ֭אֱמָנִים פִּצְעֵ֣י אוֹהֵ֑ב וְ֝נַעְתָּר֗וֹת נְשִׁיק֥וֹת שׂוֹנֵֽא
verses.translation: Wounds by a loved one are long lasting;The kisses of an enemy are profuse.
verses.rashi: are burdensome Heb. נעתרות, an expression of largeness, as in (Ezek. 35: 13): “You have multiplied (נעתרות) against Me.”

verses.hebrew_full: נֶ֣פֶשׁ שְׂ֭בֵעָה תָּב֣וּס נֹ֑פֶת וְנֶ֥פֶשׁ רְ֝עֵבָ֗ה כׇּל־מַ֥ר מָתֽוֹק
verses.translation: A sated appetite disdains honey,But to a ravenous appetite, even the bitter is sweet.
verses.rashi: tramples honeycomb Heb. תבוס, tramples by foot, as in (Ps. 44:6): “we will trample (נבוס) those who rise up against us.” all bitter is sweet Every sweet thing is bitter to it. This can be interpreted in regard to the study of the Torah. If one shows himself to be sated, that he has no desire for words of the Torah as a desire of his soul, he tramples honeycomb. [That is,] even the reasons that are acceptable to the heart are not important to him, but he who yearns for it, even the things that come to him with bitterness and toil are sweet to him.

verses.hebrew_full: כְּ֭צִפּוֹר נוֹדֶ֣דֶת מִן־קִנָּ֑הּ כֵּֽן־אִ֝֗ישׁ נוֹדֵ֥ד מִמְּקוֹמֽוֹ
verses.translation: Like a sparrow wandering from its nestIs a man who wanders from his home.
verses.rashi: wandering from its nest that goes and wanders, so is a man who wanders from his place, i.e., a Torah scholar who wanders away from his studies, from reviewing what he learned.

verses.hebrew_full: שֶׁ֣מֶן וּ֭קְטֹרֶת יְשַׂמַּֽח־לֵ֑ב וּמֶ֥תֶק רֵ֝עֵ֗הוּ מֵעֲצַת־נָֽפֶשׁ
verses.translation: Oil and incense gladden the heart,And the sweetness of a friend is better than one’s own counsel.
verses.rashi: Oil and incense The scent of balsamum oil and the scent of incense cause the heart to rejoice. and the sweet words of his friend more than one’s own counsel He whose friend draws him near with words is better than what his [own] soul advises him. Another explanation: and the sweetness of his friend who improves his deeds, that they should be sweet to the Holy One, blessed be He, is better for him than gratifying the desires of his heart.

verses.hebrew_full: רֵ֥עֲךָ֨ (ורעה) וְרֵ֪עַ אָבִ֡יךָ אַֽל־תַּעֲזֹ֗ב וּבֵ֥ית אָחִ֗יךָ אַל־תָּ֭בוֹא בְּי֣וֹם אֵידֶ֑ךָ ט֥וֹב שָׁכֵ֥ן קָ֝ר֗וֹב מֵאָ֥ח רָחֽוֹק
verses.translation: Do not desert your friend and your father’s friend;Do not enter your brother’s house in your time of misfortune;A close neighbor is better than a distant brother.
verses.rashi: your friend and your father’s friend The Holy One, blessed be He, Who is called a friend to Israel, and your father’s friend, for He endeared your forefathers. Do not forsake And, if you do forsake Him, retribution will befall you. and...your brother’s house Do not rely on the children of Esau and Ishmael that they should befriend you. We find that when Israel was exiled to Babylon, they would say to those who led them in neck irons, “We beg of you, lead us on the way of our brethren, the sons of Esau and Ishmael,” and the sons of Esau went out toward them and welcomed them with various kinds of salty foods and blown up flasks. than a distant brother It is better that the One Who is near to those who call Him dwell among you than that you should come to your brother who distanced himself by saying (Gen. 27:41): “When the days of mourning for my father are at hand, etc.”

verses.hebrew_full: חֲכַ֣ם בְּ֭נִי וְשַׂמַּ֣ח לִבִּ֑י וְאָשִׁ֖יבָה חֹרְפִ֣י דָבָֽר
verses.translation: Get wisdom, my son, and gladden my heart,That I may have what to answer those who taunt me.
verses.rashi: Be wise, my son Heb. חכם, become wise, my son. and cause my heart to rejoice and let my heart be happy with you.

verses.hebrew_full: עָר֤וּם רָאָ֣ה רָעָ֣ה נִסְתָּ֑ר פְּ֝תָאיִ֗ם עָבְר֥וּ נֶעֱנָֽשׁוּ
verses.translation: The shrewd man saw trouble and took cover;The simple kept going and paid the penalty.
verses.rashi: A cunning man saw harm He sees the retribution coming upon the earth and hides from it by withdrawing from sin, but the fools did not hide and passed on the way of harm. and were punished They suffered loss.

words.eng: to pass over or by or through

verses.hebrew_full: קַח־בִּ֭גְדוֹ כִּי־עָ֣רַב זָ֑ר וּבְעַ֖ד נׇכְרִיָּ֣ה חַבְלֵֽהוּ
verses.translation: Seize his garment, for he stood surety for another; Take it as a pledge, [for he stood surety] for an unfamiliar woman.
verses.rashi: because he stood surety for a stranger A man who stood surety brings about that the judge should say to the creditor, “Take his garment.”

verses.hebrew_full: מְבָ֘רֵ֤ךְ רֵעֵ֨הוּ בְּק֣וֹל גָּ֭דוֹל בַּבֹּ֣קֶר הַשְׁכֵּ֑ים קְ֝לָלָ֗ה תֵּחָ֥שֶׁב לֽוֹ
verses.translation: He who greets his fellow loudly early in the morningShall have it reckoned to him as a curse.
verses.rashi: He who blesses his friend Someone blesses his friend daily, and the blessing is converted into a curse, for people say about him that he is generous with his money and wealthy, and everyone comes and borrows from him, and the government provokes him to collect his money. In this manner, it is interpreted in tractate Arachin (16a). Besides this, it appears in the midrash of Rabbi Tanhuma (Balak 15) as regards Balaam, who blessed Israel with a loud voice, as it is stated (Num. 23:7): “And he took up his discourse, etc.,” an expression of raising the voice, and he later counseled to cause them to sin.

verses.hebrew_full: דֶּ֣לֶף ט֭וֹרֵד בְּי֣וֹם סַגְרִ֑יר וְאֵ֥שֶׁת (מדונים) מִ֝דְיָנִ֗ים נִשְׁתָּוָֽה
verses.translation: An endless dripping on a rainy dayAnd a contentious wife are alike;
verses.rashi: A constant dripping Rain that drips from the roof into the house and drives out the inhabitants of the house. on a rainstormy day Heb. סגריר, a rainy day, when all are shut in their houses. and a quarrelsome woman, are alike They are both the same...

verses.hebrew_full: צֹפְנֶ֥יהָ צָפַן־ר֑וּחַ וְשֶׁ֖מֶן יְמִינ֣וֹ יִקְרָֽא
verses.translation: As soon repress her as repress the wind,Or declare one’s right hand to be oil.
verses.rashi: He who guards her guards the wind He who thinks to watch her from her lewdness guards the wind; just as it is impossible to guard the wind, so will she not be guarded. and he calls “the oil of his right hand” He calls “tzaraath” to come upon him until he drives her out like a “metzora,” who is purified with oil on the thumb of his right hand [as in Lev. 14:17].

verses.hebrew_full: בַּרְזֶ֣ל בְּבַרְזֶ֣ל יָ֑חַד וְ֝אִ֗ישׁ יַ֣חַד פְּנֵֽי־רֵעֵֽהוּ
verses.translation: As iron sharpens ironSo a man sharpens the wit of his friend.
verses.rashi: sharpens the countenance of his friend Heb. יחד, sharpens. Torah scholars sharpen each other in halachah.

verses.hebrew_full: נֹצֵ֣ר תְּ֭אֵנָה יֹאכַ֣ל פִּרְיָ֑הּ וְשֹׁמֵ֖ר אֲדֹנָ֣יו יְכֻבָּֽד
verses.translation: One who tends a fig tree will enjoy its fruit,And one who cares for a master will be honored.
verses.rashi: shall eat its fruit and so will one who guards his master be honored and eat the fruit of his deeds.

verses.hebrew_full: כַּ֭מַּיִם הַפָּנִ֣ים לַפָּנִ֑ים כֵּ֤ן לֵֽב־הָ֝אָדָ֗ם לָאָדָֽם
verses.translation: As face answers to face in water,So does one’s heart to another’s.
verses.rashi: As in water the face that you show it, it shows you. is the heart of a man to a man his friend. According to how much a man knows that his friend loves him, so he will show him his face.

verses.hebrew_full: שְׁא֣וֹל וַ֭אֲבַדֹּה לֹ֣א תִשְׂבַּ֑עְנָה וְעֵינֵ֥י הָ֝אָדָ֗ם לֹ֣א תִשְׂבַּֽעְנָה
verses.translation: Sheol and Abaddon cannot be satisfied,Nor can the human eye be satisfied.
verses.rashi: The grave and Gehinnom will not be sated from receiving the wicked therein, just as the eyes of a wicked man will not be sated from wandering after temptation and gratifying his lust.

verses.hebrew_full: מַצְרֵ֣ף לַ֭כֶּסֶף וְכ֣וּר לַזָּהָ֑ב וְ֝אִ֗ישׁ לְפִ֣י מַהֲלָלֽוֹ
verses.translation: For silver—the crucible, for gold—the furnace,And a person is tested by praise.
verses.rashi: The refining pot A vessel made to refine silver. The furnace is made to refine gold. A man is refined and considered according to his praise; according to how people praise him for his good deeds, he is tested by the public, whether good or bad.

verses.hebrew_full: אִ֥ם־תִּכְתּֽוֹשׁ־אֶת־הָאֱוִ֨יל בַּ֥מַּכְתֵּ֡שׁ בְּת֣וֹךְ הָ֭רִיפוֹת בַּעֱלִ֑י לֹא־תָס֥וּר מֵ֝עָלָ֗יו אִוַּלְתּֽוֹ
verses.translation: Even if you pound the fool in a mortarWith a pestle along with grain,His folly will not leave him.
verses.rashi: grain Heb. הריפות, wheat crushed in a mortar. in a mortar Heb. במכתש, a mortar, in French mortier; Morser in German. with a pestle Heb. בעלי, a pestle, pilon in French, Stossel in German. Since they lift it up (מעלין) and beat with it, it is called עלי.

words.eng: mother·to pound·sign of the definite direct object·be foolish

verses.hebrew_full: יָדֹ֣עַ תֵּ֭דַע פְּנֵ֣י צֹאנֶ֑ךָ שִׁ֥ית לִ֝בְּךָ֗ לַעֲדָרִֽים
verses.translation: Mind well the looks of your flock;Pay attention to your herds;
verses.rashi: the condition of your flocks lit. the face of your flocks. Do not think lightly of always paying attention to them to determine what they require.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֤י לֹ֣א לְעוֹלָ֣ם חֹ֑סֶן וְאִם־נֵ֝֗זֶר לְד֣וֹר (דור) וָדֽוֹר
verses.translation: For property does not last forever,Or a crown for all generations.
verses.rashi: For riches are not forever For, if you are rich with silver and gold, perhaps it will not last forever. Therefore, do not despise your small things.

verses.hebrew_full: גָּלָ֣ה חָ֭צִיר וְנִרְאָה־דֶ֑שֶׁא וְ֝נֶאֶסְפ֗וּ עִשְּׂב֥וֹת הָרִֽים
verses.translation: Grass vanishes, new grass appears,And the herbage of the hills is gathered in.
verses.rashi: When the hay is carried away When the hay is carried away in the days of Nissan, the grass becomes visible, and the grasses are gathered to grow (?), then it will be beneficial to you, for you will have the fleece of the lambs for your clothing.

verses.hebrew_full: כְּבָשִׂ֥ים לִלְבוּשֶׁ֑ךָ וּמְחִ֥יר שָׂ֝דֶ֗ה עַתּוּדִֽים
verses.translation: The lambs will provide you with clothing,The he-goats, the price of a field.
verses.rashi: and... the worth of the field The he-goats will be worth as much as a field because you will eat the flesh and sell the hides.

verses.hebrew_full: וְדֵ֤י חֲלֵ֬ב עִזִּ֗ים לְֽ֭לַחְמְךָ לְלֶ֣חֶם בֵּיתֶ֑ךָ וְ֝חַיִּ֗ים לְנַעֲרוֹתֶֽיךָ
verses.translation: The goats’ milk will suffice for your food,The food of your household,And the maintenance of your maids.
verses.rashi: and enough for your sustenance. For your food and the food of your household; you will have sufficient goat milk. This is the figure. The allegory is: the condition of your flocks The teacher who is appointed over the congregation is required to carry them in his bosom and to lead them slowly. For riches are not forever and thereby, he eats the fruit, and the principal remains. When the hay is carried away When his laws are disseminated and the Torah is magnified through him, then these lambs will be his clothing, and the disciples shall be to him for a name and for raiment of glory and beauty.

verses.hebrew_full: נָ֣סוּ וְאֵין־רֹדֵ֣ף רָשָׁ֑ע וְ֝צַדִּיקִ֗ים כִּכְפִ֥יר יִבְטָֽח
verses.translation: A wicked person flees though no one gives chase,But the righteous are as confident as a lion.
verses.rashi: The wicked flee without a pursuer The wicked flee when their misfortune comes, and they fall on an insignificant thing without a pursuer. but the righteous are courageous [through their trust] in the Holy One, blessed be He, as a young lion which trusts its might.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּפֶ֣שַֽׁע אֶ֭רֶץ רַבִּ֣ים שָׂרֶ֑יהָ וּבְאָדָ֥ם מֵבִ֥ין יֹ֝דֵ֗עַ כֵּ֣ן יַאֲרִֽיךְ
verses.translation: When there is rebellion in the land, many are its rulers;But with someone who has understanding and knowledge, stability will last.
verses.rashi: Because of the sin of a land, its princes are many This is the punishment of a land, viz. that it has many princes, and they do not respond to any man. but because of an understanding... man But because of an understanding man, the retribution will be delayed from coming.

verses.hebrew_full: גֶּֽבֶר־רָ֭שׁ וְעֹשֵׁ֣ק דַּלִּ֑ים מָטָ֥ר סֹ֝חֵ֗ף וְאֵ֣ין לָֽחֶם
verses.translation: A poor man who withholds what is due to the wretchedIs like a destructive rain that leaves no food.
verses.rashi: A poor man An ignorant judge. who oppresses the poor in judgment because he does not take his time in arriving at a verdict. He is like rain that floods a field, and it does not produce fruit. flooding Heb. סחף and the language of the Mishnah (Keth. 1:6): “his field was ruined (נסתחפה)” and similarly (Jer. 46: 15): “Have your mighty men been swept away (נחסף)?” They were flooded, they slipped and stumbled.

verses.hebrew_full: עֹזְבֵ֣י ת֭וֹרָה יְהַלְל֣וּ רָשָׁ֑ע וְשֹׁמְרֵ֥י ת֝וֹרָ֗ה יִתְגָּ֥רוּ בָֽם
verses.translation: Those who forsake instruction praise the wicked,But those who heed instruction fight them.

verses.hebrew_full: אַנְשֵׁי־רָ֭ע לֹא־יָבִ֣ינוּ מִשְׁפָּ֑ט וּמְבַקְשֵׁ֥י יְ֝הֹוָ֗ה יָבִ֥ינוּ כֹֽל
verses.translation: The wicked cannot discern judgment,But those who seek GOD discern all things.
verses.rashi: Men of evil do not understand judgment They do not give attention to the ultimate retribution, that they should think about it and repent of their way and be saved. but those who seek the Lord understand everything Good and evil. משפט is justise in Old French. In this manner, Rabbi Tanhuma explained it as referring to the generation of the Flood.

verses.hebrew_full: טֽוֹב־רָ֭שׁ הוֹלֵ֣ךְ בְּתֻמּ֑וֹ מֵעִקֵּ֥שׁ דְּ֝רָכַ֗יִם וְה֣וּא עָשִֽׁיר
verses.translation: Better is a poor man who lives blamelesslyThan a rich man whose ways are crooked.
verses.rashi: A poor man...is better Even one who is poor in Torah. who walks innocently with good deeds.

verses.hebrew_full: נוֹצֵ֣ר תּ֭וֹרָה בֵּ֣ן מֵבִ֑ין וְרֹעֶ֥ה ז֝וֹלְלִ֗ים יַכְלִ֥ים אָבִֽיו
verses.translation: An intelligent son heeds instruction,But he who keeps company with gluttons disgraces his father.
verses.rashi: but he who befriends gluttons Heb. ורעה. He associates with gluttons. This is an expression of friends (רעים).

verses.hebrew_full: מַרְבֶּ֣ה ה֭וֹנוֹ בְּנֶ֣שֶׁךְ (ובתרבית) וְתַרְבִּ֑ית לְחוֹנֵ֖ן דַּלִּ֣ים יִקְבְּצֶֽנּוּ
verses.translation: He who increases his wealth by loans at discount or interestAmasses it for one who is generous to the poor.
verses.rashi: He who increases his riches with usury and interest...for him who is gracious to the poor The government hears about him, that he is becoming wealthy by illegal means, and they confiscate his money, with which they build bridges and repair highways. This is being gracious to the poor. In this manner, Rabbi Tanhuma expounded on it.

verses.hebrew_full: מֵסִ֣יר אׇ֭זְנוֹ מִשְּׁמֹ֣עַ תּוֹרָ֑ה גַּ֥ם תְּ֝פִלָּת֗וֹ תּוֹעֵבָֽה
verses.translation: He who turns a deaf ear to instruction—His prayer is an abomination.

verses.hebrew_full: מַשְׁגֶּ֤ה יְשָׁרִ֨ים בְּדֶ֥רֶךְ רָ֗ע בִּשְׁחוּת֥וֹ הֽוּא־יִפּ֑וֹל וּ֝תְמִימִ֗ים יִנְחֲלוּ־טֽוֹב
verses.translation: He who misleads the upright into an evil courseWill fall into his own pit,But the blameless will prosper.
verses.rashi: into his own pit Heb. בשחותו, from an expression of שחת, a pit.

words.eng: to get as a possession·to be good

verses.hebrew_full: חָכָ֣ם בְּ֭עֵינָיו אִ֣ישׁ עָשִׁ֑יר וְדַ֖ל מֵבִ֣ין יַחְקְרֶֽנּוּ
verses.translation: A rich man is clever in his own eyes,But a perceptive pauper can see through him.
verses.rashi: A rich man...but a discerning poor man Scripture speaks about a teacher and a pupil, that the pupil searches out [the understanding of the subject matter,] and through him, the teacher gains wisdom.

verses.hebrew_full: בַּעֲלֹ֣ץ צַ֭דִּיקִים רַבָּ֣ה תִפְאָ֑רֶת וּבְק֥וּם רְ֝שָׁעִ֗ים יְחֻפַּ֥שׂ אָדָֽם
verses.translation: When the righteous exult there is great glory,But when the wicked rise up, everyone makes themselves scarce.
verses.rashi: person will be searched out He will be searched out with all kinds of false accusations.

verses.hebrew_full: מְכַסֶּ֣ה פְ֭שָׁעָיו לֹ֣א יַצְלִ֑יחַ וּמוֹדֶ֖ה וְעֹזֵ֣ב יְרֻחָֽם
verses.translation: No one who covers up faults will succeed;One who confesses and gives them up will find mercy.
verses.rashi: but he who confesses and abandons He confesses his sins and abandons them, never to return to them.

verses.hebrew_full: אַשְׁרֵ֣י אָ֭דָם מְפַחֵ֣ד תָּמִ֑יד וּמַקְשֶׁ֥ה לִ֝בּ֗וֹ יִפּ֥וֹל בְּרָעָֽה
verses.translation: Happy is the person who is anxious always,But one who is hard-hearted falls into misfortune.
verses.rashi: who is always afraid He is concerned with punishment, and therefore distances himself from sin.

verses.hebrew_full: אֲֽרִי־נֹ֭הֵם וְדֹ֣ב שׁוֹקֵ֑ק מוֹשֵׁ֥ל רָ֝שָׁ֗ע עַ֣ל עַם־דָּֽל
verses.translation: A roaring lion and a prowling bearIs a wicked man ruling a helpless people.
verses.rashi: like a roaring lion he is to them. and a growling bear נהימה, roaring, to a lion, and שקיקה, growling, to a bear, are both expressions of a cry.

verses.hebrew_full: נָגִ֗יד חֲסַ֣ר תְּ֭בוּנוֹת וְרַ֥ב מַעֲשַׁקּ֑וֹת (שנאי) שֹׂ֥נֵא בֶ֝֗צַע יַאֲרִ֥יךְ יָמִֽים
verses.translation: A prince who lacks understanding is very oppressive;One who spurns ill-gotten gains will live long.
verses.rashi: who lacks understanding As he lacks understanding, he is a great oppressor because he does not give any thought to his life. [Therefore,] he oppresses excessively, for one who hates unlawful gain will live long. [Consequently, he who oppresses others will die young.]

verses.hebrew_full: אָ֭דָם עָשֻׁ֣ק בְּדַם־נָ֑פֶשׁ עַד־בּ֥וֹר יָ֝נ֗וּס אַל־יִתְמְכוּ־בֽוֹ
verses.translation: Anyone oppressed by bloodguilt will flee to a pit;Let none offer support.
verses.rashi: A man who is guilty of robbing one’s lifeblood (Upon whom the crime of robbing blood lies.) He is one who causes his fellow to sin, and his soul is lost because of him. will flee to the pit Until his dying day, he will flee for aid, that it should be expiated for him. but no one will support him from Heaven to give him the possibility to repent, so that he will not be in Paradise while his pupil is in Gehinnom. In this manner, it is expounded upon in Tractate Yoma (87a).

words.eng: ram·to grasp·inflected third pers. singular m. of בּֽ◌ ᴵ

verses.hebrew_full: הוֹלֵ֣ךְ תָּ֭מִים יִוָּשֵׁ֑עַ וְנֶעְקַ֥שׁ דְּ֝רָכַ֗יִם יִפּ֥וֹל בְּאֶחָֽת
verses.translation: One who lives blamelessly will be delivered,But one whose ways are crooked will fall all at once.
verses.rashi: will fall in one In one evil, and there will be no rising up after his downfall.

verses.hebrew_full: עֹבֵ֣ד אַ֭דְמָתוֹ יִֽשְׂבַּֽע־לָ֑חֶם וּמְרַדֵּ֥ף רֵ֝יקִ֗ים יִֽשְׂבַּֽע־רִֽישׁ
verses.translation: Those who till their land will have food in plenty,But those who pursue vanities will have poverty in plenty.
verses.rashi: will be sated with poverty Heb. ריש, poverty.

verses.hebrew_full: אִ֣ישׁ אֱ֭מוּנוֹת רַב־בְּרָכ֑וֹת וְאָ֥ץ לְ֝הַעֲשִׁ֗יר לֹ֣א יִנָּקֶֽה
verses.translation: A dependable man will receive many blessings,But one in a hurry to get rich will not go unpunished.
verses.rashi: A trustworthy man who gives his tithes faithfully, for there is no witness to the matter, but the Holy One, blessed be He, sees and lavishes blessings upon him. but he who hastens to become rich He hastens the time to become rich from the belongings of the poor.

words.eng: to be or become rich or wealthy

verses.hebrew_full: הַֽכֵּר־פָּנִ֥ים לֹא־ט֑וֹב וְעַל־פַּת־לֶ֝֗חֶם יִפְשַׁע־גָּֽבֶר
verses.translation: To be partial is not right;Even one who is valiant may do wrong for a piece of bread.
verses.rashi: a man will sin one who perverts justice.

verses.hebrew_full: נִ֥בְהָֽל־לַה֗וֹן אִ֭ישׁ רַ֣ע עָ֑יִן וְלֹא־יֵ֝דַ֗ע כִּי־חֶ֥סֶר יְבֹאֶֽנּוּ
verses.translation: A miserly man runs after wealth;He does not realize that loss will overtake it.
verses.rashi: He who hastens to [acquire] wealth He who hastens to increase wealth and steals his heave- offerings and tithes. that want will come upon him Want will come to him, for a curse will be sent upon the work of his hands.

verses.hebrew_full: מ֘וֹכִ֤יחַ אָדָ֣ם אַ֭חֲרַי חֵ֣ן יִמְצָ֑א מִֽמַּחֲלִ֥יק לָשֽׁוֹן
verses.translation: One who reproves a person will in the endFind more favor than a flatterer.
verses.rashi: He who admonishes man for the sins he has committed and separates him from them. afterwards will find more favor After a time, he will find more favor in his eyes than one who speaks with flattery. We find in Gen. Rabbah (44:5): “Wherever it says אחרי, it means only long after; אחר, it is soon after. In Tanhuma, in the portion entitled “These are the words”: “He who admonishes man after Me”—this refers to Moses, who admonished Israel after the Holy One, blessed be He, who were called “man,” as it is stated (Ezek. 34:31): “You are men,” and it stated concerning him (Ex. 33: 17): “For you have found favor in My sight.” more favor than he who speaks with flattery This refers to Balaam, who said fine things to Israel (Num. 24:5): “How goodly are your tents, O Jacob!” Moses announces (Deut. 11:16): “Beware lest your heart be deceived,” and Balaam announces and flatters, “Do what your heart desires; He does not care: (Num. 23:19) ‘God is not a man that He should lie,’ concerning the nations, but concerning you, ‘He says but He does not do.’” And Solomon cries about both of them, (above 27:6): “Wounds of a lover are faithful, whereas kisses of an enemy are burdensome.”

verses.hebrew_full: גּוֹזֵ֤ל אָ֘בִ֤יו וְאִמּ֗וֹ וְאֹמֵ֥ר אֵֽין־פָּ֑שַׁע חָבֵ֥ר ה֝֗וּא לְאִ֣ישׁ מַשְׁחִֽית
verses.translation: Anyone who robs their father and mother and says, “It is no offense,”Is the companion to a vandal.
verses.rashi: He who robs his father The Holy One, blessed be He. or his mother The people of Israel. He who causes the people to sin by robbing the Holy One, blessed be He, estranges His children from Him and robs them of goodness. destroyer Jeroboam.

verses.hebrew_full: רְחַב־נֶ֭פֶשׁ יְגָרֶ֣ה מָד֑וֹן וּבֹטֵ֖חַ עַל־יְהֹוָ֣ה יְדֻשָּֽׁן
verses.translation: A gaping maw provokes quarrels,But one who trusts GOD shall enjoy prosperity.
verses.rashi: A greedy man to attain all his desire. stirs up quarrels He incites the Divine standard of justice upon himself

verses.hebrew_full: בּוֹטֵ֣חַ בְּ֭לִבּוֹ ה֣וּא כְסִ֑יל וְהוֹלֵ֥ךְ בְּ֝חׇכְמָ֗ה ה֣וּא יִמָּלֵֽט
verses.translation: Those who trust their own instincts are dullards,But those who live by wisdom shall escape.

verses.hebrew_full: נוֹתֵ֣ן לָ֭רָשׁ אֵ֣ין מַחְס֑וֹר וּמַעְלִ֥ים עֵ֝ינָ֗יו רַב־מְאֵרֽוֹת
verses.translation: Those who give to the poor will not be in want,But those who turn a blind eye will be roundly cursed.
verses.rashi: He who gives to a poor man charity. will suffer no want (to give him much charity.) And so it is with the teacher who does not withhold Torah from his pupil.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּק֣וּם רְ֭שָׁעִים יִסָּתֵ֣ר אָדָ֑ם וּ֝בְאׇבְדָ֗ם יִרְבּ֥וּ צַדִּיקִֽים
verses.translation: When the wicked rise up, everyone goes into hiding,But when they perish the righteous increase.

verses.hebrew_full: אִ֣ישׁ תּ֭וֹכָחוֹת מַקְשֶׁה־עֹ֑רֶף פֶּ֥תַע יִ֝שָּׁבֵ֗ר וְאֵ֣ין מַרְפֵּֽא
verses.translation: One oft reproved may become stiffnecked—But then will be suddenly broken beyond repair.
verses.rashi: but stiffens his nape not to listen. will suddenly be broken He will quickly be broken.

verses.hebrew_full: בִּרְב֣וֹת צַ֭דִּיקִים יִשְׂמַ֣ח הָעָ֑ם וּבִמְשֹׁ֥ל רָ֝שָׁ֗ע יֵאָ֥נַֽח עָֽם
verses.translation: When the righteous become great the people rejoice,But when the wicked dominate the people groan.

verses.hebrew_full: אִישׁ־אֹהֵ֣ב חׇ֭כְמָה יְשַׂמַּ֣ח אָבִ֑יו וְרֹעֶ֥ה ז֝וֹנ֗וֹת יְאַבֶּד־הֽוֹן
verses.translation: A man who loves wisdom brings joy to his father,But he who keeps company with prostitutes will lose his wealth.
verses.rashi: but one who keeps company with harlots Heb. ורעה. He joins harlots to himself, as its apparent meaning. Our Sages, however, said: “He who says, ‘This tradition is acceptable, I will learn it, but this one is not acceptable, I will not learn it.’” The support [for this view] from the verse is that זונות throughout the Scriptures is not spelled fully [with two “vavim”] except this once only.

verses.hebrew_full: מֶ֗לֶךְ בְּ֭מִשְׁפָּט יַעֲמִ֣יד אָ֑רֶץ וְאִ֖ישׁ תְּרוּמ֣וֹת יֶהֶרְסֶֽנָּה
verses.translation: By justice a king sustains the land,But a fraudulent man tears it down.
verses.rashi: establishes the country If he is an honest judge, he establishes the country. but a haughty man Heb. תרומות, a haughty man, who does not care to take time in judgment. Our Rabbis (Keth. 105b) stated: “If the judge is like a king, who does not have to acquire friends and accept bribes, he establishes the country. But if he is like a priest, who asks for terumoth in the threshing floors, he tears it down.”

verses.hebrew_full: גֶּ֭בֶר מַחֲלִ֣יק עַל־רֵעֵ֑הוּ רֶ֝֗שֶׁת פּוֹרֵ֥שׂ עַל־פְּעָמָֽיו
verses.translation: A man who flatters his fellowSpreads a net for his feet.
verses.rashi: flatters Heb. מחליק, talks smoothly.
