verses.hebrew_full: בְּפֶ֤שַֽׁע אִ֣ישׁ רָ֣ע מוֹקֵ֑שׁ וְ֝צַדִּ֗יק יָר֥וּן וְשָׂמֵֽחַ
verses.translation: An evil man’s offenses are a trap for himself,But a righteous person sings out joyously.
verses.rashi: When a wicked man sins a snare will come. and a righteous man who did not go in his ways, sings and rejoices.

verses.hebrew_full: יֹדֵ֣עַ צַ֭דִּיק דִּ֣ין דַּלִּ֑ים רָ֝שָׁ֗ע לֹא־יָבִ֥ין דָּֽעַת
verses.translation: The righteous are concerned with the cause of the wretched;The wicked cannot understand such concern.
verses.rashi: the judgment of the poor The torments of the poor and what they require, and puts his mind to them.

verses.hebrew_full: אַנְשֵׁ֣י לָ֭צוֹן יָפִ֣יחוּ קִרְיָ֑ה וַ֝חֲכָמִ֗ים יָשִׁ֥יבוּ אָֽף
verses.translation: Scoffers inflame a city,But the wise allay anger.
verses.rashi: inflame Heb. יפיחו, lit. they blow. They inflame it like a flame of fire that is fanned by the wind.

verses.hebrew_full: אִֽישׁ־חָכָ֗ם נִ֭שְׁפָּט אֶת־אִ֣ישׁ אֱוִ֑יל וְרָגַ֥ז וְ֝שָׂחַ֗ק וְאֵ֣ין נָֽחַת
verses.translation: When someone who is wise enters into litigation with a foolThere is ranting and ridicule, but no satisfaction.
verses.rashi: a wise man contends Heb. נשפט, debates with the fool. he will have no contentment Whether he shows him an angry countenance or he shows him a laughing countenance, there is no contentment either in this or in that. He finds no satisfaction. We find in the case of Amaziah that the Holy One, blessed be He, showed him a laughing countenance and delivered Edom into his hands, and when he returned from defeating them, (11 Chron 25:14) “he brought their (sic) gods, etc. and prostrated himself before them.” To Ahaz, the Holy One, blessed be He, showed an angry countenance and delivered him into the hands of the kings of Aram (ibid. 28:23): “And he sacrificed to the gods of Damascus, etc. for he said, ‘The gods of Aram are helping them.’”

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·man

verses.hebrew_full: אַנְשֵׁ֣י דָ֭מִים יִשְׂנְאוּ־תָ֑ם וִ֝ישָׁרִ֗ים יְבַקְשׁ֥וּ נַפְשֽׁוֹ
verses.translation: Those who are bloodthirsty detest the blameless,But the upright seek them out.
verses.rashi: seek his soul [This is] an expression of love, and it appears that it is so, as David said to Abiathar (I Sam. 22:23): “for he who seeks my soul seeks your soul.” He who will deal kindly with me will deal kindly with you. The exegetes, however, do not agree with me.

verses.hebrew_full: כׇּל־ר֭וּחוֹ יוֹצִ֣יא כְסִ֑יל וְ֝חָכָ֗ם בְּאָח֥וֹר יְשַׁבְּחֶֽנָּה
verses.translation: A dullard gives full vent to rage,But a wise person calms it down.
verses.rashi: will quiet it When the fool has let out all his wind, the wise man comes and humbles it with the reply of his mouth. Similar to this is (Ps. 65:8): “Who stills (משביח) the noise of the seas”; (ibid. 89: 10): “when its waves rise up, You still (תשבחם) them.”

verses.hebrew_full: מֹ֭שֵׁל מַקְשִׁ֣יב עַל־דְּבַר־שָׁ֑קֶר כׇּֽל־מְשָׁרְתָ֥יו רְשָׁעִֽים
verses.translation: A ruler who listens to lies—All his ministers will be wicked.

verses.hebrew_full: רָ֤שׁ וְאִ֣ישׁ תְּכָכִ֣ים נִפְגָּ֑שׁוּ מֵ֤אִיר־עֵינֵ֖י שְׁנֵיהֶ֣ם יְהֹוָֽה
verses.translation: The poor and the fraudulent meet; GOD gives luster to the eyes of both.
verses.rashi: and a man of deep thoughts A man of thoughts, a Torah scholar. And our Sages explained it concerning a disciple who said to a teacher, “Teach me.”

verses.hebrew_full: מֶ֤לֶךְ שׁוֹפֵ֣ט בֶּאֱמֶ֣ת דַּלִּ֑ים כִּ֝סְא֗וֹ לָעַ֥ד יִכּֽוֹן
verses.translation: A king who judges the wretched honestly—His throne will be established forever.

verses.hebrew_full: שֵׁ֣בֶט וְ֭תוֹכַחַת יִתֵּ֣ן חׇכְמָ֑ה וְנַ֥עַר מְ֝שֻׁלָּ֗ח מֵבִ֥ישׁ אִמּֽוֹ
verses.translation: Rod and reproof produce wisdom,But a youth out of control is a disgrace to his mother.
verses.rashi: but a child left free Whom his father set free to follow the dictates of his heart, ultimately... brings shame to his mother This is Ishmael; since he was wicked, he brought about that Sarah should say to Abraham (Gen. 21:10): “Expel this bondwoman and her son.”

verses.hebrew_full: בִּרְב֣וֹת רְ֭שָׁעִים יִרְבֶּה־פָּ֑שַׁע וְ֝צַדִּיקִ֗ים בְּֽמַפַּלְתָּ֥ם יִרְאֽוּ
verses.translation: When the wicked increase, offenses increase,But the righteous will see their downfall.

verses.hebrew_full: יַסֵּ֣ר בִּ֭נְךָ וִינִיחֶ֑ךָ וְיִתֵּ֖ן מַעֲדַנִּ֣ים לְנַפְשֶֽׁךָ
verses.translation: Discipline your son and he will give you peace;He will gratify you with dainties.

verses.hebrew_full: בְּאֵ֣ין חָ֭זוֹן יִפָּ֣רַֽע עָ֑ם וְשֹׁמֵ֖ר תּוֹרָ֣ה אַשְׁרֵֽהוּ
verses.translation: For lack of vision a people lose restraint,But happy is he who heeds instruction.
verses.rashi: Without vision the people become unrestrained When Israel causes prophecy to withdraw from them by mocking the prophets, they will become unrestrained and will degenerate.

verses.hebrew_full: בִּ֭דְבָרִים לֹא־יִוָּ֣סֶר עָ֑בֶד כִּי־יָ֝בִ֗ין וְאֵ֣ין מַעֲנֶֽה
verses.translation: A slave cannot be disciplined by words;Though he may comprehend, he does not respond.
verses.rashi: because he will understand, but without response As soon as he sees him (sic) that the one who was reproving him is silent, he reverts to his sins; therefore, one must chastise him with blows and punishments. He does not speak of an actual slave, but about all who disobey the words of the judges.

verses.hebrew_full: חָזִ֗יתָ אִ֭ישׁ אָ֣ץ בִּדְבָרָ֑יו תִּקְוָ֖ה לִכְסִ֣יל מִמֶּֽנּוּ
verses.translation: If you see a man hasty in speech,There is more hope for a fool than for him.
verses.rashi: hasty with his words Who hurries and rushes to reply. there is more hope for a fool than for him A fool has more hope than he.

verses.hebrew_full: מְפַנֵּ֣ק מִנֹּ֣עַר עַבְדּ֑וֹ וְ֝אַחֲרִית֗וֹ יִהְיֶ֥ה מָנֽוֹן
verses.translation: A slave pampered from youthWill come to a bad end.
verses.rashi: If one pampers his slave from childhood [This refers to] the evil inclination. a ruler Heb. מנון, a ruler, and so is (Ps.72:17): “May His name be magnified (ינון) as long as the sun exists.” Similarly, every instance of נין in the Bible, since the son rises in his father’s stead to rule over his property.

verses.hebrew_full: אִֽישׁ־אַ֭ף יְגָרֶ֣ה מָד֑וֹן וּבַ֖עַל חֵמָ֣ה רַב־פָּֽשַׁע
verses.translation: An angry man provokes a quarrel;A hothead commits many offenses.
verses.rashi: stirs up strife According to its apparent meaning, מדון is the Divine standard of justice.

verses.hebrew_full: גַּאֲוַ֣ת אָ֭דָם תַּשְׁפִּילֶ֑נּוּ וּשְׁפַל־ר֝֗וּחַ יִתְמֹ֥ךְ כָּבֽוֹד
verses.translation: Pride of mortals will bring humiliation,But one who is humble will obtain honor.
verses.rashi: will grasp honor Heb. יתמך, will come near the honor and constantly grasp it.

words.eng: to be or become low·to smell

verses.hebrew_full: חוֹלֵ֣ק עִם־גַּ֭נָּב שׂוֹנֵ֣א נַפְשׁ֑וֹ אָלָ֥ה יִ֝שְׁמַ֗ע וְלֹ֣א יַגִּֽיד
verses.translation: He who shares with a thief is his own enemy;He hears the imprecation and does not tell.
verses.rashi: he hears an oath that they adjure him, “Did you see so and so who stole so much and so much from me?” And since he shares with him, he does not testify.

verses.hebrew_full: חֶרְדַּ֣ת אָ֭דָם יִתֵּ֣ן מוֹקֵ֑שׁ וּבוֹטֵ֖חַ בַּיהֹוָ֣ה יְשֻׂגָּֽב
verses.translation: A person’s fears become a trap,But one who trusts in GOD shall be safeguarded.
verses.rashi: A snare brings terror to a person It is transposed. A snare of sin brings terror to a person. Another explanation: According to its apparent meaning, if a person is stingy and fears that if he gives charity he will require help from people, this fear will be a snare for him. I prefer the former [interpretation], however.

verses.hebrew_full: רַ֭בִּים מְבַקְשִׁ֣ים פְּנֵֽי־מוֹשֵׁ֑ל וּ֝מֵיְהֹוָ֗ה מִשְׁפַּט־אִֽישׁ
verses.translation: Many seek audience with a ruler,But it is from GOD that a [wronged] party gets justice.
verses.rashi: the countenance of a ruler to litigate before him. but the judgment of a person Any [judgment], whether he will be adjudged innocent or guilty. Scripture [here] speaks of the gentile kings.

verses.hebrew_full: תּוֹעֲבַ֣ת צַ֭דִּיקִים אִ֣ישׁ עָ֑וֶל וְתוֹעֲבַ֖ת רָשָׁ֣ע יְשַׁר־דָּֽרֶךְ
verses.translation: The unjust man is an abomination to the righteous,And one whose way is straight is an abomination to the wicked.
verses.rashi: whose way is straight A man who is straight in his ways.

verses.hebrew_full: דִּבְרֵ֤י אָג֥וּר בִּן־יָקֶ֗ה הַמַּ֫שָּׂ֥א נְאֻ֣ם הַ֭גֶּבֶר לְאִיתִיאֵ֑ל לְאִ֖יתִיאֵ֣ל וְאֻכָֽל
verses.translation: The words of Agur son of Jakeh, [man of] Massa; the speech of the man to Ithiel, to Ithiel and Ucal:
verses.rashi: The words of Agur, the son of Jakeh אגור בן יקה, the words of Solomon, who gathered (אגר) understanding (בינה) and vomited it (והקיאה). The Sages interpreted it in this manner. the prophecy He said this prophecy on that matter. the words of the man concerning, “God is with me; said the man that is Solomon this prophecy concerning himself because of איתיאל because he relied on his wisdom to increase gold, horses and wives, which he was forbidden to increase, and so he said, “God is with me, and I will be able. I will increase wives, and they will not turn my heart away; I will increase gold, and I will not turn away; I will increase horses, and I will not take the people back to Egypt.” yea, God is with me, and I will be able” Since he said, “God is with me, and I will be able to do it, and I will not stumble.” לאיתיאל, because of “God is with me,” as in (Ex. 14:3): “For Pharaoh will say of the children of Israel (לבני),” meaning of the children of Israel.
verses.ibn_ezra: The words of Agur — he was in the days of Solomon, walking in righteousness, knowing knowledge, and honored in his generation, so the king Solomon gathered the words of his wisdom into his book. son of — bin with a ḥiriq is equivalent to ben with a tsere. See also rish (Prov. 28:19) and resh (Prov. 13:18), bin Nun (Ex. 33:11 et al.), bin layla (Jon. 4:10), bin hakot (Deut. 25:2). the burden — the prophecy, as a matter the Name bears upon a human. the saying of —ne'um is in the same noun form as zevul. the man — (ha-gever), that is, Agur, since his wisdom was great (gavra). Itiel and Uchal — were sages, fellows or students of Agur. Another interpretation: son of Yaqeh — a son of peoples' obedience (yiqhat 'amim). For regarding David it was said of his son, Shlomo, son of Yaqeh, and Qohelet, and Agur — that is, the saying of the man to Itiel — about the matter of women and horses (Deuteronomy 17:16-17), as he said: "I will increase, but my heart will not stray, and I am capable,"1Punning on the names Itiel = God is with me, and Ukhal = I am capable which was foolish. Itiel repented after that and said, "I acted as one who had not learned wisdom nor known heavenly knowledge." And Qohelet sought knowledge in judgment to acquit and convict without witnesses, but the Torah says only with witnesses. And about this it is said Qohelet sought (Eccl. 12:10) — i.e. that he be answered from heaven, but it was not so. Or the verse says upright words of truth (ibid.) for the Torah: every word of God is forged, do not add etc. (Prov. 30:5-6). "But I did not ask the right thing, for I asked what was not mine to ask." What was he compared to Moses who went up to heaven and went down (Prov. 30:4), or Aaron who gathered the winds (ibid.), as it is said, and he stood between the living and the dead (Num. 17:13), or Elijah who bound the water with a cloak, or Joshua who established all the earth's ceasings — Sun be still in Gibeon (Joshua 10:12)!?

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֤י בַ֣עַר אָנֹכִ֣י מֵאִ֑ישׁ וְלֹֽא־בִינַ֖ת אָדָ֣ם לִֽי
verses.translation: I am more brutish than anyone else; I lack common sense.
verses.rashi: For I am more boorish Because I relied on my wisdom in a matter that the Holy One, blessed be He, is concerned lest one come to sin.

verses.hebrew_full: וְלֹֽא־לָמַ֥דְתִּי חׇכְמָ֑ה וְדַ֖עַת קְדֹשִׁ֣ים אֵדָֽע
verses.translation: I have not learned wisdom,Nor do I possess knowledge of the Holy One.
verses.rashi: Neither have I learned wisdom nor do I know the knowledge of the holy ones, for I subtracted or added to the words of Moses.

verses.hebrew_full: מִ֤י עָלָֽה־שָׁמַ֨יִם וַיֵּרַ֡ד מִ֤י אָֽסַף־ר֨וּחַ בְּחׇפְנָ֡יו מִ֤י צָֽרַר־מַ֨יִם בַּשִּׂמְלָ֗ה מִ֭י הֵקִ֣ים כׇּל־אַפְסֵי־אָ֑רֶץ מַה־שְּׁמ֥וֹ וּמַֽה־שֶּׁם־בְּ֝נ֗וֹ כִּ֣י תֵדָֽע
verses.translation: Who has ascended heaven and come down?Who has gathered up the wind in the hollow of his hand?Who has wrapped the waters in his garment?Who has established all the extremities of the earth?What is his name or his son’s name, if you know it?
verses.rashi: Who ascended to heaven like Moses? Who gathered wind The soot of the furnace. Who wrapped the waters (Ex. 15:8): “The depths were congealed”; (ad loc.): “The floods stood upright like a heap,” through Moses’s prayer. Who established the Tabernacle, through whose establishment all the ends of the earth were firmly established. In this way, it is expounded in the Pesikta. What is his name and what is the name of his son If you say that there already was one like him, tell me what his son’s name is; i.e., what family is descended from him, and we will know who he is. if you know if you know who he is. Now how did you not fear to transgress His words?
verses.ibn_ezra: Who is it who ascended to heaven and descended? — that is to say, that he has power to go up to the high heavens and descend to earth which is of a depth I do not know with all my wisdom. With his palms — that the listener understand that his palms and his hands are his will or his cunning, that is to say that he would gather the wind that it not blow out. Who bundled the waters — as if he bundled them in clouds, which are like a cloak, to rain down on the land. All the earth's ceasings — that they not fail, and the ceasings are the ends which stand upon the void. What is his name — for their sons did not reach their name and their knowledge, even I, as I am one of them! and now I ask two things of you. What is his name and what is his son's name — that he know it in his wisdom. For you know — you, Itiel, a man or his son, tell me one name of theirs that you know. And the meaning: he cannot know it, and no creations know His wisdom.

verses.hebrew_full: כׇּל־אִמְרַ֣ת אֱל֣וֹהַּ צְרוּפָ֑ה מָגֵ֥ן ה֝֗וּא לַחֹסִ֥ים בּֽוֹ
verses.translation: Every word of God is pure,A shield to those who seek refuge.
verses.rashi: Every word of God is refined Heb. צרופה, refined, and He did not write anything unnecessary. [Therefore,] I should have been careful.
verses.ibn_ezra: Every word of God is forged — meaning, the words of the LORD are pure words, and they are the commandments forged like pure gold, and among them are wisdom of the fear of the Name, and it is not worth it to engage in anything else. Thus do not add to its words (Prov. 30:6), you, Itiel, i.e. do not add to it nor remove from it (Deut. 13:1). A shield — the initial mem in magen is not part of the root, as in menagen (musician), mishberei el (divine waves). God, do not, two — all three verses are connected, in the manner of prayer.

words.eng: inflected third pers. singular m. of בּֽ◌ ᴵ

verses.hebrew_full: אַל־תּ֥וֹסְףְּ עַל־דְּבָרָ֑יו פֶּן־יוֹכִ֖יחַ בְּךָ֣ וְנִכְזָֽבְתָּ
verses.translation: Do not add to God’s words,Lest you be indicted and proved to be a liar.
verses.rashi: lest He prove to you, and you be found a liar He will prove to your face that through your addition you have come to sin, and certainly if you subtract.

verses.hebrew_full: שְׁ֭תַּיִם שָׁאַ֣לְתִּי מֵאִתָּ֑ךְ אַל־תִּמְנַ֥ע מִ֝מֶּ֗נִּי בְּטֶ֣רֶם אָמֽוּת
verses.translation: Two things I ask of You; do not deny them to me before I die:
verses.rashi: I ask two [things] of You Now he addresses the Holy One, blessed be He.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object

verses.hebrew_full: שָׁ֤וְא וּֽדְבַר־כָּזָ֡ב הַרְחֵ֬ק מִמֶּ֗נִּי רֵ֣אשׁ וָ֭עֹשֶׁר אַל־תִּֽתֶּן־לִ֑י הַ֝טְרִיפֵ֗נִי לֶ֣חֶם חֻקִּֽי
verses.translation: Keep lies and false words far from me;Give me neither poverty nor riches,But provide me with my daily bread,
verses.rashi: poverty Heb. ראש, poverty. provide me Heb. הטריפני, an expression of food, and similarly (Ps. 111: 5): “He has given food (טרף) to those who fear Him.”

words.eng: ram·to give·inflected pers. pron. meaning ‘to me’.

verses.hebrew_full: פֶּ֥ן־אֶשְׂבַּ֨ע וְכִחַשְׁתִּי֮ וְאָמַ֗רְתִּי מִ֥י־יְ֫הֹוָ֥ה וּפֶֽן־אִוָּרֵ֥שׁ וְגָנַ֑בְתִּי וְ֝תָפַ֗שְׂתִּי שֵׁ֣ם אֱלֹהָֽי
verses.translation: Lest, being sated, I renounce, saying,“Who is GOD?”Or, being impoverished, I take to theftAnd profane the name of my God.
verses.rashi: lest I become sated from wealth. and deny the existence of the Holy One, blessed be He, out of my extreme haughtiness. Now what is the denial? And I say, “Who is the Lord?” Meaning, there is no God. and take hold of the name of my God to become accustomed to swearing by it falsely.

verses.hebrew_full: אַל־תַּלְשֵׁ֣ן עֶ֭בֶד אֶל־אֲדֹנָ֑ו פֶּֽן־יְקַלֶּלְךָ֥ וְאָשָֽׁמְתָּ
verses.translation: Do not inform on a slave to his master,Lest he curse you and you incur guilt.
verses.rashi: Do not inform Do not deliver your case against a person to complain about him to the Holy One, blessed be He, even if he is wicked, who curses his father and possesses all the abominations mentioned here, and the proof of the matter is from Hosea son of Beeri, as is stated in Pesachim (87b) in the chapter entitled “The Woman,” that he informed on lsrael and said, “Exchange them for another nation.” Replied the Holy One, blessed be He, “Go, take yourself a wife of harlotry” (Hos. 1:2).

verses.hebrew_full: דּ֭וֹר אָבִ֣יו יְקַלֵּ֑ל וְאֶת־אִ֝מּ֗וֹ לֹ֣א יְבָרֵֽךְ
verses.translation: There is a breed of people that brings a curse on its fathersAnd brings no blessing to its mothers,

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·mother

verses.hebrew_full: דּ֭וֹר טָה֣וֹר בְּעֵינָ֑יו וּ֝מִצֹּאָת֗וֹ לֹ֣א רֻחָֽץ
verses.translation: A breed that thinks itself pure,Though it is not washed of its filth;

verses.hebrew_full: דּ֭וֹר מָה־רָמ֣וּ עֵינָ֑יו וְ֝עַפְעַפָּ֗יו יִנָּשֵֽׂאוּ
verses.translation: A breed so haughty of bearing, so supercilious;
verses.rashi: A generation—how lofty etc. This refers to haughtiness.

verses.hebrew_full: דּ֤וֹר חֲרָב֣וֹת שִׁנָּיו֮ וּֽמַאֲכָל֢וֹת מְֽתַלְּעֹ֫תָ֥יו לֶאֱכֹ֣ל עֲנִיִּ֣ים מֵאֶ֑רֶץ וְ֝אֶבְיוֹנִ֗ים מֵאָדָֽם
verses.translation: A breed whose teeth are swords,Whose jaws are knives,Ready to devour the poor of the land,The needy among us.
verses.rashi: A generation whose teeth are [like] swords [These are] the outer [teeth]. and its molars Its inner teeth.

verses.hebrew_full: לַ֥עֲלוּקָ֨ה שְׁתֵּ֥י בָנוֹת֮ הַ֤ב הַ֥֫ב שָׁל֣וֹשׁ הֵ֭נָּה לֹ֣א תִשְׂבַּ֑עְנָה אַ֝רְבַּ֗ע לֹא־אָ֥מְרוּ הֽוֹן
verses.translation: The leech has two daughters, “Give!” and “Give!”Three things are insatiable;Four never say, “Enough!”:
verses.rashi: The leech has Menachem (Machbereth p. 134) interprets עלוקה according to its apparent meaning. We learn that it is Arabic, but the commentators say that it is an expression of the grave and the descent. Indeed, we learn this in Midrash Psalms (31:9), which interprets the “two daughters” as Paradise and Gehinnom. This one says, “Give me righteous people!” and this one says, “Give me wicked people!” four that do not say, “Enough!” Heb. הון, lit. wealth. We have much [wealth].

verses.hebrew_full: שְׁאוֹל֮ וְעֹ֢צֶ֫ר רָ֥חַם אֶ֭רֶץ לֹא־שָׂ֣בְעָה מַּ֑יִם וְ֝אֵ֗שׁ לֹא־אָ֥מְרָה הֽוֹן
verses.translation: Sheol, an infertile womb,Earth that cannot get enough water,And fire that never says, “Enough!”
verses.rashi: the confined womb Sexual intercourse.

verses.hebrew_full: עַ֤יִן תִּ֥לְעַ֣ג לְאָב֮ וְתָבֻ֢ז לִֽיקְּהַ֫ת־אֵ֥ם יִקְּר֥וּהָ עֹֽרְבֵי־נַ֑חַל וְֽיֹאכְל֥וּהָ בְנֵי־נָֽשֶׁר
verses.translation: The eye that mocks a fatherAnd disdains the homage due a mother—The ravens of the brook will gouge it out,Young eagles will devour it.
verses.rashi: the mother’s wrinkles Heb. ליקהת, the wrinkles that gather (נקהין) in his mother’s face, from the expression of (Gen. 49:10): “a gathering (יקהת) of peoples.” The “yud” is a radical, like יפעת, splendor; (יעלת חן) (Prov. 5:19), a graceful mountain goat. pick it out Heb. יקרוה, an expression of (Num. 16: 14): “will you pick (תנקר)” (Ex. 33:22) “In the cleft (בנקרת) of the rock.” Forer in French, to bore through. Let the raven, which is cruel to its young, come and pick it and not eat it and not derive benefit from it, and let the eagle, which is compassionate with its young, come and eat it and derive benefit from it. The raven is cruel, as it is stated (Ps. 147:9): “to the young ravens which cry,” and the eagle is compassionate, as it is stated (Deut. 32: 11): “It bears its young on its wing.”

verses.hebrew_full: שְׁלֹשָׁ֣ה הֵ֭מָּה נִפְלְא֣וּ מִמֶּ֑נִּי (וארבע) וְ֝אַרְבָּעָ֗ה לֹ֣א יְדַעְתִּֽים
verses.translation: Three things are beyond me;Four I cannot fathom:
verses.rashi: are concealed from me They are covered after they passed from my eyes, and I do not know where they went, because they hasten to hide from the eye.

verses.hebrew_full: דֶּ֤רֶךְ הַנֶּ֨שֶׁר בַּשָּׁמַיִם֮ דֶּ֥רֶךְ נָחָ֗שׁ עֲלֵ֫י־צ֥וּר דֶּרֶךְ־אֳנִיָּ֥ה בְלֶב־יָ֑ם וְדֶ֖רֶךְ גֶּ֣בֶר בְּעַלְמָֽה
verses.translation: How an eagle makes its way over the sky;How a snake makes its way over a rock;How a ship makes its way through the high seas;How a man has his way with a maiden.

verses.hebrew_full: כֵּ֤ן דֶּ֥רֶךְ אִשָּׁ֗ה מְנָ֫אָ֥פֶת אָ֭כְלָה וּמָ֣חֲתָה פִ֑יהָ וְ֝אָמְרָ֗ה לֹא־פָעַ֥לְתִּי אָֽוֶן
verses.translation: Such is the way of an adulteress:She eats, wipes her mouth,And says, “I have done no wrong.”
verses.rashi: and [she] wipes Heb. ומחתה she turns over her lower orifice, as it is written (II Kings 21:13): “as one wipes (ימחה) a dish, he wipes (מחה) and turns it upside down.” she eats Scripture speaks euphemistically.

verses.hebrew_full: תַּ֣חַת שָׁ֭לוֹשׁ רָ֣גְזָה אֶ֑רֶץ וְתַ֥חַת אַ֝רְבַּ֗ע לֹא־תוּכַ֥ל שְׂאֵֽת
verses.translation: The earth shudders at three things,At four that it cannot bear:

verses.hebrew_full: תַּֽחַת־עֶ֭בֶד כִּ֣י יִמְל֑וֹךְ וְ֝נָבָ֗ל כִּ֣י יִֽשְׂבַּֽע־לָֽחֶם
verses.translation: A slave who becomes king;A scoundrel sated with food;

words.eng: to be or become king or queen

verses.hebrew_full: תַּ֣חַת שְׂ֭נוּאָה כִּ֣י תִבָּעֵ֑ל וְ֝שִׁפְחָ֗ה כִּֽי־תִירַ֥שׁ גְּבִרְתָּֽהּ
verses.translation: A loathsome woman who gets married;A maidservant who supplants her mistress.

verses.hebrew_full: אַרְבָּ֣עָה הֵ֭ם קְטַנֵּי־אָ֑רֶץ וְ֝הֵ֗מָּה חֲכָמִ֥ים מְחֻכָּמִֽים
verses.translation: Four are among the tiniest on earth,Yet they are the wisest of the wise:

verses.hebrew_full: הַ֭נְּמָלִים עַ֣ם לֹא־עָ֑ז וַיָּכִ֖ינוּ בַקַּ֣יִץ לַחְמָֽם
verses.translation: Ants are a folk without power,Yet they prepare food for themselves in summer;

verses.hebrew_full: שְׁ֭פַנִּים עַ֣ם לֹא־עָצ֑וּם וַיָּשִׂ֖ימוּ בַסֶּ֣לַע בֵּיתָֽם
verses.translation: The badger is a folk without strength,Yet it makes its home in the rock;

verses.hebrew_full: מֶ֭לֶךְ אֵ֣ין לָאַרְבֶּ֑ה וַיֵּצֵ֖א חֹצֵ֣ץ כֻּלּֽוֹ
verses.translation: The locusts have no king,Yet they all march forth in formation;

verses.hebrew_full: שְׂ֭מָמִית בְּיָדַ֣יִם תְּתַפֵּ֑שׂ וְ֝הִ֗יא בְּהֵ֣יכְלֵי מֶֽלֶךְ
verses.translation: You can catch the lizard in your hand,Yet it is found in royal palaces.
verses.rashi: The spider Erinee in Old French. [Araignee in modern French.] grasps with [her] hands With her hands she grasps and clings to the walls.

verses.hebrew_full: שְׁלֹשָׁ֣ה הֵ֭מָּה מֵיטִ֣יבֵי צָ֑עַד וְ֝אַרְבָּעָ֗ה מֵיטִ֥בֵי לָֽכֶת
verses.translation: There are three that are stately of stride,Four that carry themselves well:
verses.rashi: outstanding in their step They walk and succeed with their might.

verses.hebrew_full: לַ֭יִשׁ גִּבּ֣וֹר בַּבְּהֵמָ֑ה וְלֹא־יָ֝שׁ֗וּב מִפְּנֵי־כֹֽל
verses.translation: The lion is mightiest among the beasts,And recoils before none;

verses.hebrew_full: זַרְזִ֣יר מׇתְנַ֣יִם אוֹ־תָ֑יִשׁ וּ֝מֶ֗לֶךְ אַלְק֥וּם עִמּֽוֹ
verses.translation: The greyhound, the he-goat,The king whom none dares resist.
verses.rashi: The greyhound Heb. זרזיר מתנים. I do not know what it is, but from the context appears to be an animal with weak loins. and the king against whom no one dares to rise up Heb. ומלך אלקום I do not know what it is according to its simple meaning, but the Aggadic midrashim interpret these five sections of four as corresponding to the four kingdoms. Since their rule over Israel was strengthened because of the iniquity of having transgressed the five Books of the Pentateuch, Scripture mentions them five times. [16] The grave and the barren womb The grave represents the kingdom of Babylon, for it is stated regarding Nebuchadnezzar (Hab. 2:5): “who widened his desire like the nether- world.” And the barren womb, that is Media, in whose time mercy (רחמים) was held back from Israel, as it is said (Esther 3:13): to destroy, kill and cause to perish.” the earth, which is not sated with water This represents Greece, which was not sated with issuing decrees on Israel. and fire, which does not say, “Enough!” Corresponding to Esau, who acted with burning wrath against Israel, for he said to destroy children and women in one day. And likewise... [19] The way of the eagle This is Babylon, the great eagle, with the long wings (Ezek. 17:3). serpent This is Media. the way of a ship in the heart of the sea This represents Greece, who was swift with its decrees. the way of a man with a young woman This represents Edom, who said, “I will be a mistress forever.” [גבר is interpreted as גברת, a mistress, and בעלמה as לעולם, forever.] [20] So is the way of an adulterous woman The people of Israel brought this evil upon themselves because they played the adulteress with idolatry, and they deserved that the retribution should befall them. and she says, “I have no committed no sin.” As it says (Jeremiah 2:35), “Behold, I contend with you concerning because you say, I have not sinned.” (Until this point are the words of Rashi [as they appear] in Mikraoth Gedoloth.) [21] the earth quakes This refers to Eretz Israel. under a slave This refers to Nebuchadnezzar, who was the slave and secretary of Merodach-baladan, as appears in Hullin. [To my knowledge, this does not appear in Hullin, but in Helek, the eleventh chapter of Sanhedrin, 96a.] and a wretch who is sated with food This refers to Ahasuerus, who made a banquet for one hundred and eighty days [23] a hated woman who is married Corresponding to Greece. and a maidservant who inherits her mistress This refers to Esau, who should have served Jacob, but the matter was reversed. [25] The ants are a people not strong This refers to Babylon, as it is stated (Isa. 23:13): “Behold the land of the Chaldees, this people has never been.” yet they prepare their food in the summer [This refers to] Nebuchadnezzar, who performed one [act of] honor to the Holy One, blessed be He, in the days of Merodach-baladan, when he sent letters to Hezekiah, and wrote therein, “Peace to King Hezekiah; peace to city of Jerusalem; peace to the great God....

verses.hebrew_full: אִם־נָבַ֥לְתָּ בְהִתְנַשֵּׂ֑א וְאִם־זַ֝מּ֗וֹתָ יָ֣ד לְפֶֽה
verses.translation: If you have been scandalously arrogant,If you have been a schemer,Then clap your hand to your mouth.
verses.rashi: If you have been put to shame, you will be in your ascendency If you were put to shame through your speech, that you derided yourself, you will ultimately ascend in the matter. and if you thought evil in your heart to quarrel, put your hand onto your mouth and remain silent.

verses.hebrew_full: כִּ֤י מִ֪יץ חָלָ֡ב י֘וֹצִ֤יא חֶמְאָ֗ה וּֽמִיץ־אַ֭ף י֣וֹצִיא דָ֑ם וּמִ֥יץ אַ֝פַּ֗יִם י֣וֹצִיא רִֽיב
verses.translation: As milk under pressure produces butter,And a nose under pressure produces blood,So patience under pressure produces strife.
verses.rashi: For pressing milk For, just as butter will come out by pressing milk and blood by pressing the nose too much, so will strife come out of pressing the nostrils of anger. pressing Heb. מיץ, pressing, preindre in Old French, as in (Jud. 6:38): “and wrung (וימץ) dew, etc.” And our Sages explained (Ber. 63b): If you were put to shame because of the words of Torah, by seeking and asking your doubts of your mentor, even if you appear to him as a fool without intelligence, you will ultimately be exalted; ואם זמות, but if you placed a muzzle on your mouth and muzzled it, and you did not ask him anything, your end will be that when they ask you a matter of halachah, you will put your hand to your mouth and you will be dumb, for you will not know to reply anything about it. Just as pressing milk gives out butter, so will pressing anger that your mentor is wroth with you for not understanding readily and you were put to shame because of it, eventually bring out of your mouth after a time many (רבות) halachoth and instructions.

verses.hebrew_full: דִּ֭בְרֵי לְמוּאֵ֣ל מֶ֑לֶךְ מַ֝שָּׂ֗א אֲֽשֶׁר־יִסְּרַ֥תּוּ אִמּֽוֹ
verses.translation: The words of Lemuel, king of Massa, with which his mother admonished him:
verses.rashi: The words of Lemuel the king The words of King Solomon, that he said for the Holy One, blessed be He, because he sinned against the Holy One, blessed be He. Lemuel for God, like (Job 40:4): “to (למו) my mouth.” The words that the king said for the sake of the Holy One, blessed be He. a prophecy that his mother chastised him When he married the daughter of Pharaoh on the day of the dedication of the Temple, she brought in for him many kinds of musical instruments, and he was awake all night and slept on the next day until four hours [after dawn], as is related in Pesikta (unknown, but found in Mid. Mishle and in Num. Rabbah 10:8), and the keys of the Temple were under his head. Regarding that time, we learned (Eduyoth 6:1): “Concerning the daily morning burnt- offering, that it was offered up at [the conclusion of] four hours. Then his mother entered and chastised him with all this prophecy.” a prophecy that his mother chastised him An allegorical prophecy with which his mother chastised him.

verses.hebrew_full: מַה־בְּ֭רִי וּמַֽה־בַּר־בִּטְנִ֑י וּ֝מֶ֗ה בַּר־נְדָרָֽי
verses.translation: No, my son!No, O son of my womb!No, O son of my vows!
verses.rashi: What, my son What is this that you have done and told that you are my son, and [you did] not [tell] that you are the son of your father. Everybody knows that your father was completely righteous, and if you are wicked, they will say, “His mother made him that way.” the son of my womb All your father’s wives, as soon as they conceived, would not return for marital relations, but I pushed and entered in order to have a son well-formed and of strong vitality, because marital relations are beneficial during the last six months. and what, the son of my vows? All your father’s wives would vow that they would have a son fit for the throne, but I vowed that I would have a son bright in Torah.

verses.hebrew_full: אַל־תִּתֵּ֣ן לַנָּשִׁ֣ים חֵילֶ֑ךָ וּ֝דְרָכֶ֗יךָ לַֽמְח֥וֹת מְלָכִֽין
verses.translation: Do not give your strength to women,Your vigor, to those who destroy kings.
verses.rashi: Do not give Do not weaken. your strength to women Heb. חילך, your strength.

verses.hebrew_full: אַ֤ל לַֽמְלָכִ֨ים לְֽמוֹאֵ֗ל אַ֣ל לַֽמְלָכִ֣ים שְׁתוֹ־יָ֑יִן וּ֝לְרוֹזְנִ֗ים (או) אֵ֣י שֵׁכָֽר
verses.translation: Wine is not for kings, O Lemuel;Not for kings to drink,Nor any strong drink for princes,
verses.rashi: It is not for kings, Lemoel This matter is not fit for kings who are for the Holy One, blessed be He. Lemoel is like (Job 40:4): “to (למו) my mouth.” it is not for kings to drink wine It is not proper for them to become intoxicated. neither is strong drink Heb. אי שכר, like אין שכר, there is no strong drink.

verses.hebrew_full: פֶּן־יִ֭שְׁתֶּה וְיִשְׁכַּ֣ח מְחֻקָּ֑ק וִ֝ישַׁנֶּ֗ה דִּ֣ין כׇּל־בְּנֵי־עֹֽנִי
verses.translation: Lest they drink and forget what has been ordained,And infringe on the rights of the poor.
verses.rashi: what was made law What is written in the Torah—and surely the tradition that is committed to memory.

verses.hebrew_full: תְּנוּ־שֵׁכָ֥ר לְאוֹבֵ֑ד וְ֝יַ֗יִן לְמָ֣רֵי נָֽפֶשׁ
verses.translation: Give strong drink to the haplessAnd wine to the embittered.
verses.rashi: to the one who is perishing To him who will ultimately perish—to the wicked. to those of bitter soul Who suffer pain because of their poverty and their mourning. To give them pleasure in this world, so that they receive the reward for the precepts they kept, in this world, [leaving them with nothing in the hereafter.]

verses.hebrew_full: יִ֭שְׁתֶּה וְיִשְׁכַּ֣ח רִישׁ֑וֹ וַ֝עֲמָל֗וֹ לֹ֣א יִזְכׇּר־עֽוֹד
verses.translation: Let them drink and forget their poverty,And put their troubles out of mind.

verses.hebrew_full: פְּתַח־פִּ֥יךָ לְאִלֵּ֑ם אֶל־דִּ֝֗ין כׇּל־בְּנֵ֥י חֲלֽוֹף
verses.translation: Speak up for the dumb,For the rights of all the unfortunate.
verses.rashi: all whose help has passed Heb. בני חלוף. These are the orphans, whose help has passed and gone away.

verses.hebrew_full: פְּתַח־פִּ֥יךָ שְׁפׇט־צֶ֑דֶק וְ֝דִ֗ין עָנִ֥י וְאֶבְיֽוֹן
verses.translation: Speak up, judge righteously,Champion the poor and the needy.

verses.hebrew_full: אֵֽשֶׁת־חַ֭יִל מִ֣י יִמְצָ֑א וְרָחֹ֖ק מִפְּנִינִ֣ים מִכְרָֽהּ
verses.translation: א How precious is a woman of substance!Her worth is far beyond that of rubies.
verses.ibn_ezra: "אשת חיל מי ימצא (Who can find a woman of worth):" "חיל" (worth) means wealth, as in a woman who acquires wealth and possessions through her wisdom. Who can find such a woman? "מכרה (her price):" Derived from "תמכרו" (you will sell) from "תמכרו מאתם" (you will sell from among you).

verses.hebrew_full: בָּ֣טַח בָּ֭הּ לֵ֣ב בַּעְלָ֑הּ וְ֝שָׁלָ֗ל לֹ֣א יֶחְסָֽר
verses.translation: ב Her husband puts his confidence in her,And lacks no good thing.
verses.rashi: and he will lack no gain lit. no plunder, i.e., he will lack no good.

verses.hebrew_full: גְּמָלַ֣תְהוּ ט֣וֹב וְלֹא־רָ֑ע כֹּ֝֗ל יְמֵ֣י חַיֶּֽיהָ
verses.translation: ג She is good to him, never bad,All the days of her life.

verses.hebrew_full: דָּ֭רְשָׁה צֶ֣מֶר וּפִשְׁתִּ֑ים וַ֝תַּ֗עַשׂ בְּחֵ֣פֶץ כַּפֶּֽיהָ
verses.translation: ד She looks for wool and flax,And sets her hand to them with a will.

verses.hebrew_full: הָ֭יְתָה כׇּאֳנִיּ֣וֹת סוֹחֵ֑ר מִ֝מֶּרְחָ֗ק תָּבִ֥יא לַחְמָֽהּ
verses.translation: ה She is like a merchant fleet,Bringing her food from afar.

verses.hebrew_full: וַתָּ֤קׇם בְּע֬וֹד לַ֗יְלָה וַתִּתֵּ֣ן טֶ֣רֶף לְבֵיתָ֑הּ וְ֝חֹ֗ק לְנַעֲרֹתֶֽיהָ
verses.translation: ו She rises while it is still night,And supplies provisions for her household,The daily fare of her maids.
verses.rashi: food Heb. טרף. and an allotted share Heb. וחוק. This too is the food that is allotted to them.

verses.hebrew_full: זָמְמָ֣ה שָׂ֭דֶה וַתִּקָּחֵ֑הוּ מִפְּרִ֥י כַ֝פֶּ֗יהָ (נטע) נָ֣טְעָה כָּֽרֶם
verses.translation: ז She sets her mind on an estate and acquires it;She plants a vineyard by her own labors.

verses.hebrew_full: חָֽגְרָ֣ה בְע֣וֹז מׇתְנֶ֑יהָ וַ֝תְּאַמֵּ֗ץ זְרוֹעֹתֶֽיהָ
verses.translation: ח She girds herself with strength,And performs her tasks with vigor.

verses.hebrew_full: טָ֭עֲמָה כִּי־ט֣וֹב סַחְרָ֑הּ לֹא־יִכְבֶּ֖ה (בליל) בַלַּ֣יְלָה נֵרָֽהּ
verses.translation: ט She sees that her business thrives;Her lamp never goes out at night.

verses.hebrew_full: יָ֭דֶיהָ שִׁלְּחָ֣ה בַכִּישׁ֑וֹר וְ֝כַפֶּ֗יהָ תָּ֣מְכוּ פָֽלֶךְ
verses.translation: י She sets her hand to the distaff;Her fingers work the spindle.
verses.rashi: onto the distaff Heb. כישור that is called vertel, verteil, vertay in Provencal, which prepares (מכשיר) the spindle to spin. the spindle Heb. פלך, fusele in French.

verses.hebrew_full: כַּ֭פָּהּ פָּֽרְשָׂ֣ה לֶעָנִ֑י וְ֝יָדֶ֗יהָ שִׁלְּחָ֥ה לָאֶבְיֽוֹן
verses.translation: כ She gives generously to the poor;Her hands are stretched out to the needy.

verses.hebrew_full: לֹא־תִירָ֣א לְבֵיתָ֣הּ מִשָּׁ֑לֶג כִּ֥י כׇל־בֵּ֝יתָ֗הּ לָבֻ֥שׁ שָׁנִֽים
verses.translation: ל She is not worried for her household because of snow,For her whole household is dressed in crimson.
verses.rashi: She fears not for her house hold For those who live in her house. for snow For the cold. are dressed in crimson Colored clothing.

verses.hebrew_full: מַרְבַדִּ֥ים עָֽשְׂתָה־לָּ֑הּ שֵׁ֖שׁ וְאַרְגָּמָ֣ן לְבוּשָֽׁהּ
verses.translation: מ She makes covers for herself;Her clothing is linen and purple.
verses.rashi: beautiful bedspreads Heb. מרבדים, beautiful spreads for the bed, as in (above 7:16) “I have bedecked my couch with covers (מרבדים רבדתי).”

verses.hebrew_full: נוֹדָ֣ע בַּשְּׁעָרִ֣ים בַּעְלָ֑הּ בְּ֝שִׁבְתּ֗וֹ עִם־זִקְנֵי־אָֽרֶץ
verses.translation: נ Her husband is prominent in the gates,As he sits among the elders of the land.
verses.rashi: Her husband is known in the gates He is recognizable among his peers because of his garments, which are beautiful.

verses.hebrew_full: סָדִ֣ין עָ֭שְׂתָה וַתִּמְכֹּ֑ר וַ֝חֲג֗וֹר נָתְנָ֥ה לַֽכְּנַעֲנִֽי
verses.translation: ס She makes cloth and sells it,And offers a girdle to the merchant.
verses.rashi: to the trafficker Heb. כנעני, a merchant.

verses.hebrew_full: עֹז־וְהָדָ֥ר לְבוּשָׁ֑הּ וַ֝תִּשְׂחַ֗ק לְי֣וֹם אַחֲרֽוֹן
verses.translation: ע She is clothed with strength and splendor;She looks to the future cheerfully.
verses.rashi: at the last day On the day of her death, she departs with a good name. and she laughs all her life about the day of her death, that it should be honored with a good name.

verses.hebrew_full: פִּ֭יהָ פָּתְחָ֣ה בְחׇכְמָ֑ה וְת֥וֹרַת חֶ֝֗סֶד עַל־לְשׁוֹנָֽהּ
verses.translation: פ Her mouth is full of wisdom,Her tongue with kindly teaching.

verses.hebrew_full: צ֭וֹפִיָּה (הילכות) הֲלִיכ֣וֹת בֵּיתָ֑הּ וְלֶ֥חֶם עַ֝צְל֗וּת לֹ֣א תֹאכֵֽל
verses.translation: צ She oversees the activities of her householdAnd never eats the bread of idleness.
verses.rashi: She supervises In her house, she pays attention to all the needs of her household, how they should act with truth and with modesty.

verses.hebrew_full: קָ֣מוּ בָ֭נֶיהָ וַֽיְאַשְּׁר֑וּהָ בַּ֝עְלָ֗הּ וַֽיְהַלְלָֽהּ
verses.translation: ק Her children declare her happy;Her husband praises her,

verses.hebrew_full: רַבּ֣וֹת בָּ֭נוֹת עָ֣שׂוּ חָ֑יִל וְ֝אַ֗תְּ עָלִ֥ית עַל־כֻּלָּֽנָה
verses.translation: ר “Many women have done well,But you surpass them all.”
verses.rashi: “Many women, etc.” In this way, her husband and children call her fortunate.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object

verses.hebrew_full: שֶׁ֣קֶר הַ֭חֵן וְהֶ֣בֶל הַיֹּ֑פִי אִשָּׁ֥ה יִרְאַת־יְ֝הֹוָ֗ה הִ֣יא תִתְהַלָּֽל
verses.translation: ש Grace is deceptive,Beauty is illusory;It is for her fear of GOD That a woman is to be praised.
verses.rashi: Charm is false No one praises a woman of charm or beauty; everything is futility and false, but a God-fearing woman alone is praised.

verses.hebrew_full: תְּנוּ־לָ֭הּ מִפְּרִ֣י יָדֶ֑יהָ וִיהַלְל֖וּהָ בַשְּׁעָרִ֣ים מַֽעֲשֶֽׂיהָ
verses.translation: ת Give her of the fruit of her hands, And let her works praise her in the gates.
verses.rashi: her deeds The skill of her deeds testifies for her so that all passersby praise her in the gate of the city. This is the figure, as I explained it, but according to the allegory, the chapter is explained as referring to the Torah and those who study it. [10] a woman of valor This is the Torah. who can find Fortunate is he who merits to find it. than pearls Heb. מפנינים, [also referred to as] מרגליות. [11] and he lacks no gain He eats the fruit in this world and in the next [13] She seeks wool and flax Since it [the Torah] is compared to a woman, Scripture speaks according to the figure concerning the requirements for the work of women, and the allegory is as follows: The Torah seeks Scripture, Mishnah and Midrash, and searches for them, as they are the requirements of the students. [14] She is like the merchant ships The Torah brings those who study it blessing and sustenance. [15] She rises when it is still night They rise early in the [morning] watch. she gives food to her household The teacher teaches the pupils the lesson allotted to them. [16] She contemplates a field Heb. זממה. The Torah muzzles Esau, the “man of the field,” with a muzzle and a bridle. and purchases it lit. and takes him from the world to destroy him; from the fruit of her deeds she plants a vineyard Israel, to keep them alive for the fire of the world to come. [18] She advised Its speech. her lamp does not go out at night It is written בליל, with the “hey” missing; on the night of watching (ליל שמורים), when the Egyptians were plagued, it shone for Israel and protected them. [19] onto the distaff Heb. בכישור, through the skill of its deeds. support the spindle Heb. פלך, a support, as in (II Sam. 3:29): “one who leans on a staff (מחזיק בפלך).” [20]She spreads out her hand Whoever makes himself like a poor man on its account—in him it [the Torah] endures. [21] She fears not for her household for snow with which they judge the wicked from fire to snow. dressed in crimson The covenant of the blood of circumcision. Another explanation: They are dressed with the commandments expressed in double language. (Deut. 15:10): “You shall surely give (נתון תתן),” (ibid. verse 8): “you shall surely open (פתוח תפתח),” (ibid. verse 14): “you shall surely furnish him liberally (הענק תעניק).” All these save them from the snow of Gehinnom. So it is expounded in Tanhuma. [24] She makes a cloak It grants a glorious [raiment] to the Sages. and she gives a belt to the trafficker To the one who is girded with its merchandise, she gives a belt for their loins. [25] to the last day They need not grieve over the day of judgment because they will be saved from it, and all the days of their life they will rejoice at the day of judgment. An expression of צחוק, laughter, applies to anyone who need not be concerned, as in (Job 41:21): “and he rejoices at the din of a spear.” [27] She supervises the ways of her household The Torah teaches them the good way, to separate from sin. [28] Her children The pup...
